# 16 — Dispatcher Backlog

Working backlog for items extracted from `input/input_raw.md`. Each row
points to the originating line range so context is recoverable.

**Convention:** items are dispatched in rounds; each round runs as one
or more parallel sub-agents on the
`claude/implement-input-raw-changes-2WiOF` branch (or worktree of it).
Status legend:

- `queued` — vetted, waiting for dispatch slot
- `in-progress` — agent is working (note round + agent ID/branch)
- `done` — landed in this branch (commit hash)
- `blocked` — needs user input (note reason)
- `parked` — out of scope for this branch (e.g. dataship work, infra)

## Scope filter

`input_raw.md` mixes work for at least three projects:

1. `dlr-shepard/shepard` (this repo, Java/Quarkus backend + Nuxt 3 / Vue 3 /
   Vuetify 3 frontend + client libs) — **in scope**. (Earlier rounds said
   "Angular"; corrected by the `aidocs/33` agent which verified against
   `frontend/package.json:24,45-46`.)
2. `dlr-shepard/shepard-dataship` (separate Python client; M1–M9 milestones
   on lines 975–1214 reference `src/ui/*.py`, `src/worker.py`, etc.) —
   **out of scope here**, parked.
3. Program-level work (HMC, federation, project website, AAS) — **parked**,
   not implementable from this repo.

## Backlog (this repo only)

| A0 | Admin role mechanism: `instance-admin` role tier, populate `JWTPrincipal.roles` from configurable IdP claim path AND/OR Neo4j `:HAS_ROLE` edge (dual-source), wire `SecurityContext.isUserInRole` → `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")` works. Bundles **C3** + **F8**. | — | M–L | **done** | Backend slice landed (cherry-pick `2901834`). New `Role` entity, `:HAS_ROLE` rel, V13 appId constraint + V14 orphan-permissions backfill (idempotent, refuses if `shepard.permissions.default-owner` unset and orphans exist), bootstrap-token mechanism (`/opt/shepard/.bootstrap-token` mode 0600), `POST /v2/admin/bootstrap`, `GET/POST/DELETE /v2/admin/instance-admins`, `GET /v2/admin/permission-audit`, API-key `roles` field with allowlist + caller-must-have validation. CLI commands deferred to L1 Phase 1 (per `aidocs/51 §11` step 9). 37 new tests; 1268 → 1305 total. |

V14 backfill (`shepard.permissions.default-owner` configured) attaches default Permissions; pre-migration `OrphanPermissionsBackfillContext` aborts startup if orphans + config-unset (post-A1e fail-fast). `GET /v2/admin/permission-audit` surfaces remaining orphans. |
| C5 | Replace string-concatenated Cypher query construction in `Neo4jQueryBuilder.java`, `PermissionsDAO.java`, `DataObjectDAO.java` with parameterised queries | `aidocs/archive/07-security-issues.md` C5, `aidocs/platform/19-architecture-feedback.md` §2, `aidocs/platform/25-neo4j-id-migration-design.md` §5 | M | done | Cherry-pick `e90bfd8`. | C5b | Apply C5's parameter-binding pattern to the second-wave `id()=` / `ID()=` sites — `GenericDAO`, `VersionDAO`, `ShepardFileDAO`, `StructuredDataDAO`, `*ReferenceDAO` family, `SemanticAnnotationDAO` | — | M | done | Cherry-pick `c707e56`. 12 production files + 12 test files; final exit-grep clean of user-reachable injection sites. **L2c fully unblocked.** |
| H4 | Surface RFC 7807-shape error responses (existing high-finding from `07`) | `aidocs/archive/07-security-issues.md` H4, `aidocs/platform/19-architecture-feedback.md` §3 | M | done | Cherry-pick `e526183`. New `application/problem+json` shape via `ProblemJson` record; 10-entry `ShepardErrorCode` catalogue; content negotiation preserves legacy `ApiError` for explicit `Accept: application/json`. **Subsumes M7.** **L2d fully unblocked.** |
| FRONTEND-IMAGE-CVE | Harden the frontend Docker image against the Trivy `node-pkg` HIGH gate (`ci.yml` "Trivy scan — frontend image"). | — | S | **shipped** | `frontend/Dockerfile` now strips the `node:20-slim`-bundled npm + corepack from the runtime layer (the runtime only runs `node server/index.mjs`), removing the `tar` / `glob` / `minimatch` / `cross-spawn` HIGH CVEs that lived solely in `/usr/local/lib/node_modules/npm`. `frontend/scripts/patch-output.mjs` pins the two Nitro-traced runtime deps `uuid` 8.3.2 → 11.1.1 (CVE-2026-41907) and `devalue` 5.6.3 → 5.8.1 (CVE-2026-42570) in the built `.output`. Patch-in-output chosen over a root `overrides` block because npm 9 can't apply a new override to the committed lockfile without a full re-resolve, and that re-resolve drifts dep hoisting enough to break Nuxt auto-import type generation (~95 spurious vue-tsc errors); the committed lockfile stays byte-identical. **Follow-up:** migrate the two pins to `overrides` on the next npm/Nuxt major that re-resolves the lockfile anyway. |
| IMAGE-CVE-2026-06-04 | Clear the next wave of Trivy `CRITICAL,HIGH` findings on both published images (`ci.yml` "Trivy scan — backend/frontend image"). | — | S | **shipped** | OS layer: `backend/Dockerfile` `microdnf -y update openssl openssl-libs` (CVE-2026-28390); `frontend/Dockerfile` `apt-get update && apt-get upgrade -y` (libgnutls30 CVE-2026-33845/-42010 CRITICAL + -33846/-3833/-42009 HIGH; libcap2 CVE-2026-4878). Java libs: `backend/pom.xml` `<dependencyManagement>` pins `org.eclipse.angus:jakarta.mail`+`:smtp` 2.0.3→2.0.4 (CVE-2025-7962) and `org.apache.thrift:libthrift` 0.21.0→0.23.0 (CVE-2026-43869). npm libs: extended `frontend/scripts/patch-output.mjs` to pin `fast-uri` 3.0.6→3.1.2 (CVE-2026-6321/-6322) and `lodash-es` 4.17.21→4.18.0 (CVE-2026-4800) in built `.output` (same lockfile-untouched mechanism as FRONTEND-IMAGE-CVE; finder now walks nested `node_modules`). |

### Architectural / performance (lines 1387–7000)

| ID | Item | input_raw refs | Size | Status | Notes |

| A1 | Async DB init: bounded timeout + exponential backoff in `MigrationsRunner.waitForConnection` | 1395–1440, 1599–1644 | S | done | Round 1, commit `a74d278`. Also fixed second infinite-loop in `NeoConnector`. Config: `shepard.migrations.connection-wait-timeout` (default `PT60S`). |
| A1b | Health checks: distinguish startup readiness vs runtime; per-DB status | 1420–1428 | S–M | done | Round 2, commit `8f40156` |
| A1c | Async DB init: graceful degradation when optional DBs (PostGIS) unavailable | 1408–1414, 1625–1627 | M | done | Round 5, commit `5805363` (salvaged from rate-limited worktree). New `@RequiresDatabase(DatabaseKind...)` annotation + `RequiresDatabaseFilter` JAX-RS provider that consults `DbHealthRegistry` (wraps post-A1b/A1f `DbPinger` ecosystem). Spatial endpoints return **503** with RFC-7807-shaped body + `Retry-After` header when PostGIS is DOWN; **404** when the spatial-data toggle is OFF (matches the readiness short-circuit pattern from A1b). Annotated `SpatialDataPointRest` only in this round; Neo4j/Mongo/Timescale stay implicitly required. 11/11 tests pass. |
| A1f | Automated DB recovery scheduler on top of `DbHealthState` | 1427 | M | done | Round 3, commit `2f80600`. `@Scheduled(every="{shepard.health.recovery.interval}")` default `PT15S`. Adds `quarkus-scheduler` dep. 4 tests passing. |
| A1d | Audit MongoDB / Flyway / Quarkus JDBC startup wait/retry semantics | — | S–M | done | Round 3, commit `e1c3635`. MongoDB and Agroal/JDBC already fail fast within the 60s ceiling — no config added. Flyway's default `connect-retries-interval=120s` exceeded the ceiling; added `quarkus.flyway.connect-retries=10` + `connect-retries-interval=PT5S` (≈50s) for both default and spatial datasources. New `aidocs/archive/17-startup-wait-audit.md`. |
| A1e | `MigrationsRunner.apply()` swallows `ServiceUnavailableException` / `MigrationsException` — surface failed migrations as a startup error rather than continuing | — | S | done | Round 2, commit `0f2f512` |
| A2 | Decompose monolithic `TimeseriesRest` / `FileRest` / `CollectionRest` | 1443–1489 | L | queued | JAX-RS sub-resources, breaking only for code, not API |

Mechanism in place; `versioning` migrated (the only `@IfBuildProperty` use found). Other toggles (`SpatialDataFeatureToggle`, `MigrationModeToggle`) were already runtime via `ConfigProvider`. |

Override is in-process only — does not survive restart. Commit `b9d87cc`. |

Aliasing via a `@Produces` `SpatialDataConfig` bean: both names resolve, new takes precedence, old logs a one-shot deprecation warning. Conflict-on-different-values: new wins, WARN logged (no exception — not breaking). Removal deadline `v6.0`. Migration note in `aidocs/platform/A3c-namespace-migration.md`. 4 new tests passing. Quarkus interpolations `quarkus.flyway.spatial.active=${shepard.spatial-data.enabled}` and `quarkus.hibernate-orm.spatial.active=${shepard.spatial-data.enabled}` keep resolving through the alias chain. |

The input_raw.md "basic Map" critique was stale. Landed change is per-cache TTL/max-size config (`shepard.permissions.cache.ttl` default `PT5M`, `.max-size` default `10000`) overriding the global 3h / 8192 defaults, plus a behaviour-pinning test (hit/miss/TTL/invalidation/LRU). |

| P1 | Parallelize DB connection checks (CompletableFuture / virtual threads) | 1599–1644 | M | **done** | New `DbConnectivityWarmer` (`@ApplicationScoped`, `@Observes StartupEvent`) fires one virtual thread per required `DbPinger` in parallel; wall-clock startup wait is now `max(latencies)` not `sum(latencies)`. `DbRecoveryScheduler.attemptRecovery()` also parallelised (same pattern: virtual thread per stale pinger, `CompletableFuture.allOf` blocks the tick). Both use `RequestContextController.activate/deactivate` per thread so JPA `EntityManager` injections in `TimescalePinger` / `PostGisPinger` work correctly off the scheduler/startup thread. No new config keys — reuses `shepard.migrations.connection-wait-timeout` for the startup warmer ceiling. 7 new unit tests (`DbConnectivityWarmerTest`); 2 new tests added to `DbRecoverySchedulerTest` (parallel timing + request-context lifecycle). Per-DB `DbHealthState` independently tracks each outcome — a Mongo outage still shows DOWN only for Mongo. |
| P2 | Batch permission checks: `checkPermissionsBatch(List<Long>)` | 1672–1678 | S–M | **done** | `22f78b3`. `filterAllowedForUser` (single Cypher). No N+1 site found — stale input assumption. Primitive ships, call-site rewire is a noop. |
| P2b | TimescaleDB continuous aggregates / materialized views | 1690–1698 | M | queued | Already partly tracked in `12-timescaledb-performance-analysis.md` |
| P3 | Migration progress monitoring endpoint | 1720–1737 | S | done | Round 2, commit `7cc74b8`. Side-finding: the existing legacy `migration_tasks` table (V1.1.0) is unreferenced from Java; left untouched as lower-risk than consolidating. |
| P3b | Wire the external `timescale-migration-preparation` image to write `migration_progress` rows (or call `MigrationRunner.migrateContainer` over JDBC) | — | M | queued | Follow-up from P3 — image source lives outside this repo |
| P3c | Tighten authorisation on `/temp/migrations/*` (was: always-allowed for any authenticated user; intended for ops) | — | S | **done** | `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")` at the class level on `MigrationProgressRest`. PermissionsService.isAllowed "temp/migrations" carve-out kept (used by SubscriptionFilter to skip notifying subscribers about migration state changes); the JAX-RS `@RolesAllowed` gate runs separately and is what blocks non-admin callers. 4 new tests (annotation-presence regression + 200/404 happy paths). **AWARE in `aidocs/34`**: scripts that polled migration status as a regular user now get 403. |
| P4 | API versioning routing scaffolding — `/shepard/api/...` (frozen upstream surface) + `/v2/...` (this fork's development surface) live side-by-side under a single Quarkus `Application` | 1760–1764 | S | done | `quarkus.http.root-path` flipped from `/shepard/api` to `/`. Every existing JAX-RS resource (29 classes) carries an explicit `@Path(Constants.SHEPARD_API + "/" + …)` prefix; new `Constants.SHEPARD_API = "shepard/api"` is the single source of truth. New `de.dlr.shepard.v2` package marker reserves the namespace for L2d's incoming `/v2/` resources. `RequestPathHelper.applicationSegments` / `.applicationPath` strip the prefix back off for filters that interpret the path semantically (`PermissionsService.isAllowed`, `SubscriptionFilter.extractEntityId`, `MigrationModeFilter`, `PublicEndpointRegistry`) so first-segment dispatch stays byte-identical. ArchUnit `V2NamespaceTest` fences the convention three ways: classes in `de.dlr.shepard.v2..` must use `/v2/`; classes outside must NOT use `/v2/`; classes outside MUST use `/shepard/api/`. New `@Sunset` annotation (RFC 8594) + `SunsetFilter` skeleton ship for L2e's deprecation window — no method/class uses it yet. User-facing URLs are unchanged. Full suite `Tests run: 1271, Failures: 0, Errors: 1, Skipped: 129` — single error is the pre-existing `oidc.public` baseline (same shape as L2c/C5b).  |
| P4b | OpenAPI client tree-shaking / code splitting | 1765–1774 | S | **done** | `backend-client/package.json` gains `"exports"` conditional-exports map (`"."` + `"./runtime"` with `import`/`require`/`types` branches). `"sideEffects": false` was already set; the `exports` field gives modern bundlers (Vite / Webpack 5 / Rollup) a proper ESM entry point for full tree-shaking support. |
| P4c | OpenAPI emission — split into per-shelf documents (`/shepard/doc/openapi/{v1,v2}.json`) via `OASFilter` | — | S | **done** | Two `OASFilter` implementations (`V1OpenApiFilter`, `V2OpenApiFilter`) classify paths through a shared `OpenApiShelfMembership` utility — v1 keeps everything that is not a `/v2/...` path nor a platform path (`/healthz`, `/openapi`, `/openapi.json`, `/swagger-ui`, `/metrics`); v2 keeps only `/v2/...`. `OpenApiPerShelfRest` (`@PermitAll`, `@Path("/shepard/doc/openapi")`) serves `/v{1,2}.json` and `?format=yaml`, cloning the live `OpenApiDocument.INSTANCE` via `MergeUtil` before running the filter so the combined doc is not mutated. The two new endpoints are registered in `PublicEndpointRegistry` (same posture as the combined `/shepard/doc/openapi.json` smallrye-openapi already serves pre-auth). Combined `/shepard/doc/openapi.json` keeps working unchanged — the split is additive. **AWARE in `aidocs/34`**: admins gain two new public endpoints; no breakage to upstream callers. Generator follow-up (P4b/P17) tracked separately. |

Mapping: Reader → read; Writer → read+update+create+delete; Manager → all + ACL-mod. Best-effort with in-memory retry queue; failures never block user writes. RFC 7807 ProblemJson on admin-endpoint errors. 53 tests. ADR-0020 records the source-of-truth decision. |

Two new DAO methods (`SnapshotDAO.getEntryAppIds` + `SnapshotDAO.filterDataObjectAppIds`) + one new service method (`SnapshotService.listDataObjectAppIds`). New REST class `SnapshotPinnedReadRest`. Gate order: 401 → 404 (collection) → 403 (Read) → 404 (snapshot) → 409 (ownership mismatch) → 200. 9 unit tests (`SnapshotPinnedReadRestTest`). `docs/reference/snapshots.md` updated. `aidocs/34` + `aidocs/42` + `aidocs/44` updated in same PR. |

| PM1 | Plugin manifest SPI + lifecycle (umbrella) | user direction 2026-05-13, ADR-0023 | M-L | **PM1a + PM1b + PM1c + PM1d + PM1e + PM1b2 shipped; PM1b3 (runtime CDI) queued** | `aidocs/47 §2.5` + `aidocs/63 ADR-0023`. The cross-cutting infra every `shepard-plugin-*` JAR compiles against — discovery (`ServiceLoader`), lifecycle (`onRegister`/`onUnregister`), admin surface (`GET /v2/admin/plugins`), runtime toggle (`shepard.plugins.<id>.enabled`), manifest enrichment + dependency resolution (PM1c), CLI extensibility (PM1d), persistent runtime overrides (PM1e), runtime hardening (PM1b2: JAR signature verifier + semver-range enforcement of `shepardCompatibility()` + `DEGRADED` state). PM1b3 (true runtime CDI integration via Vert.x router — see `aidocs/69`) is the remaining queued slice. |
| PM1a | `PluginManifest` SPI + `PluginRegistry` (`@ApplicationScoped`, observes `StartupEvent` post-migrations) + `backend/plugins/` drop-in directory + Dockerfile mount-point + flip UH1a from source-stitched to drop-in JAR (the first real plugin under the new shape). | user direction 2026-05-13, ADR-0023 | M | **shipped** | This PR. Three commits: phase 1 (SPI + registry; 234-line `PluginRegistry`, `PluginEntry`, `PluginContext`, `PluginState`; 15 unit tests covering empty / missing / non-dir / disabled / throwing / duplicate / shutdown / classpath-shadow / smoke-via-ServiceLoader paths). Phase 2 (Dockerfile creates `/deployments/plugins/`, `SHEPARD_PLUGINS_DIR=/deployments/plugins`; `application.properties` documents `shepard.plugins.dir` deploy-time key + per-plugin `shepard.plugins.<id>.enabled` toggle template). Phase 3 (this commit — flip): backend's `build-helper-maven-plugin` source-stitching of `plugins/unhide/src/{main,test}` removed; plugin module produces real `shepard-plugin-unhide-${revision}.jar` with `provided` scope backend dep; backend `<dependency>`-pulls the plugin via the default-active `with-plugins` profile (so Quarkus's build-time CDI scanner picks the `@ApplicationScoped` beans + `@Path` resources from the JAR); `-DnoPlugins` flag breaks the bootstrap circular reference; `quarkus.index-dependency.shepard-plugin-unhide.*` ensures Jandex indexing; CI workflows (`backend-ci.yml` + `build-images.yml`) do the two-pass install dance; Dockerfile copies the JAR into `/deployments/plugins/` (operator visibility) and the PluginRegistry's classpath-then-JAR duplicate check silently shadows the redundant entry. V2 ArchUnit fence extended to treat `de.dlr.shepard.plugins..` as an equal-tier `/v2/` tenant. Trackers (`aidocs/16` PM1a → shipped, `aidocs/34` PM1a row, `aidocs/44` PM1a → shipped, `aidocs/63` ADR-0023 forward-work checklist), reference docs (`docs/reference/plugins.md`), and `docs/admin.md` "shepard plugins" section all updated in the same PR. **No Cypher migration needed** — packaging-only change. |
| PM1b | Admin REST + CLI parity for the PM1a `PluginRegistry`: `GET /v2/admin/plugins` (registry snapshot, full state space including DISABLED + FAILED), `PATCH /v2/admin/plugins/{id}` (RFC 7396 merge-patch — only `enabled` patchable; `plugin.config.read-only-field` 400 + `plugin.not-found` 404 RFC 7807 envelopes); `shepard-admin plugins {list,enable,disable}` CLI verbs with shared `--output={human,json}` / `--url` / `--api-key`. `PluginEntry` gains a `registeredAt` timestamp surfaced in the REST shape so operators see discovery order chronologically. | — | M | **shipped** | This PR. Backend: `PluginsAdminRest` + `PluginEntryIO` / `PluginListIO` / `PluginPatchIO` under `de.dlr.shepard.v2.admin.plugins`. CLI: `PluginsCommand` + `PluginsListCommand` / `PluginsEnableCommand` / `PluginsDisableCommand` + `PluginInfo` / `PluginList` IO mirrors. PROV1a captures each PATCH automatically (admin endpoints opt-in by default; no extra wiring). 12 backend admin-REST unit tests + 10 CLI end-to-end tests covering list (table + JSON + 401 hint), enable / disable (PATCH body shape + 404 → exit 1), no-op PATCH, read-only-field guard, and parent-wiring. `/v2/admin/plugins` (this fork's development surface; upstream `/shepard/api/...` untouched). |
| PM1b2 | `JarSignatureVerifier` for untrusted JARs (four `shepard.plugins.signing.*` config keys, default `required=false`); semver-range enforcement of `shepardCompatibility()` (incompatible plugins land in `FAILED` state with `plugin.compatibility.failed` / `plugin.compatibility.unparseable` failureMessages; `shepard.plugins.compatibility.strict=true` default + operator-override valve); `DEGRADED` lifecycle state + `PluginEntry.markDegraded()` lifecycle hook (forward placeholder for PM1b3 — child-classloader CDI integration deferred to PM1b3 per `aidocs/69`). `VersionRange` parser extended to accept the npm/Composer-style operator-comma syntax (`>=5.2.0,<6`) every existing plugin manifest already uses. | — | M | **shipped** | This PR. Backend: new `de.dlr.shepard.plugin.{JarSignatureVerifier, JarSignatureException}` (`@ApplicationScoped`; JDK's `JarFile(verify=true)` + `KeyStore.getInstance("JKS"/"PKCS12")` for trust-anchor loading; fail-soft on malformed truststore); `PluginRegistry.enforceCompatibility()` runs between discovery + dependency-resolution so compat failures pre-empt dependent's `plugin.dependency.missing` (more accurate root-cause reporting); `PluginRegistry.verifyJarSignature()` + `applySignatureOutcome()` hook into `loadJarPlugin()` so failed-signature entries land in `FAILED` with operator visibility in `GET /v2/admin/plugins`. `PluginState` grows `DEGRADED`. `PluginEntry.markDegraded()` added. Tests: 9 `JarSignatureVerifierTest` (unsigned / self-signed-no-truststore / trusted-anchor / tampered → JarSignatureException / empty-truststore / default-not-required / invalid-truststore-path / isSigningRequired / multi-anchor-any-match — keytool-generated keys + JDK's `jdk.security.jarsigner.JarSigner` API, no internal-class access) + 11 `PluginRegistryCompatibilityTest` (compatible / too-old / too-new / empty constraint / unparseable → FAILED / strict=false override / strict=false unparseable / compat-precedes-dependency / multi-plugin only-incompatible-fails / already-failed-not-reprocessed) + 8 new `VersionRangeTest` cases on operator-comma syntax (>=5.2.0,<6, single-clause ge, single-clause lt, =-operator, empty-clause → IAE, malformed-clause → IAE, semver-numeric compare, bare-clause-as-exact) + 4 `PluginRegistryDegradedStateTest` (markDegraded transitions, list visibility, enum stability, markEnabled clears DEGRADED). Five new config keys (`shepard.plugins.signing.required` / `.trust-anchors` / `.truststore.path` / `.truststore.password` / `shepard.plugins.compatibility.strict`) all default-safe. Trackers (`aidocs/16` PM1 umbrella → PM1b2 shipped + PM1b3 queued; `aidocs/34` PM1b2 AWARE row; `aidocs/44` plugin-runtime-hardening row; `aidocs/63 ADR-0023` forward-work — JarSignatureVerifier + semver-compat enforcement ticked, child-classloader CDI integration deferred to PM1b3) + reference docs (`docs/reference/plugins.md` §"Signing + compatibility enforcement" with the operator runbook + `jarsigner` workflow) + new design doc `aidocs/platform/69-runtime-plugin-cdi.md` for the deferred PM1b3 slice all updated in this PR. `PluginEntryIO.state` schema description extended to mention `DEGRADED`. **No new endpoints** — state flows through existing `GET /v2/admin/plugins`. **No Cypher migration** — runtime-config-only changes. |
| PM1b3 | True runtime CDI integration — a vendor drops `shepard-plugin-foo-x.y.z.jar` into `/deployments/plugins/` at runtime and its REST endpoints + admin CLI subcommand come up **without** declaring the plugin in the backend's `with-plugins` Maven profile. Option B (per the `aidocs/69` analysis): extend `PluginContext` with `registerHttpRoute(pathPattern, handler)` so plugins ship Vert.x handlers instead of `@Path` resources (side-steps Quarkus's build-time CDI scanning); wire `PluginRegistry` to a singleton `PluginRouter` that manages the live Vert.x route table. PM1b2's `DEGRADED` state becomes the auto-detection signal: a JAR-walk plugin without a matching `quarkus.index-dependency.*` config key lands DEGRADED with `plugin.runtime.no-cdi-scan` and the failureMessage points at the runbook. Ship a vendor-style example plugin (`plugins/example-video/`) that's NOT in the `with-plugins` profile, demonstrating the drop-in path end-to-end. | `aidocs/69 §4`, ADR-0023 forward-work | M-L | queued | Quarkus's build-time CDI scanner doesn't expose a runtime bean-addition API (verified against Quarkus 3.27.x source); the structurally correct fix is to give plugins a non-CDI HTTP-handler shape. See `aidocs/69` for the full reasoning + the Option A (per-plugin Quarkus extension) vs Option B (Vert.x router) trade-off. |
| PM1c | Enrich `PluginManifest` SPI with admin-visible metadata (`title()`, `description()`, `homepageUrl()`, `repositoryUrl()`, `licence()`) + plugin-dependency resolution (`dependencies()` returning `List<PluginDependency>`; Maven-style version-range constraints parsed by the in-tree `VersionRange` parser — no `org.apache.maven:maven-artifact` dependency added). Registry topologically sorts the dependency graph at startup via Kahn's algorithm; failure modes (`plugin.dependency.cycle` / `plugin.dependency.missing` / `plugin.dependency.version-mismatch`) mark the affected plugin `FAILED` fail-soft. Wire shape of `GET /v2/admin/plugins` grows six fields (all nullable / arrays — backwards-compatible additive); `shepard-admin plugins list` table grows three columns (TITLE, LICENCE, REPOSITORY, width-capped at 30 / 12 / 40). `UnhidePluginManifest` filled in with title / description / homepage / repository / `Apache-2.0` licence; dependencies left empty (UH1a depends on in-tree SPIs only). | — | S | **shipped** | PR #1112 (commit `0c0279c9`). Backend: new `de.dlr.shepard.plugin.{PluginDependency,VersionRange}` types + 6 default methods on `PluginManifest`; `PluginRegistry.validateAndOrderDependencies()` does the topo-sort between discovery + lifecycle; `PluginEntryIO` + new `PluginDependencyIO` IO record. CLI: `PluginInfo` mirror gains the six new fields + a `PluginDependency` mirror; `PluginsListCommand` grows three columns (with `truncate()` helper). Tests: 8 `PluginManifestTest` + 12 `VersionRangeTest` + 12 `PluginRegistryDependencyTest` + 2 new `PluginsAdminRestTest` + 3+3 helper-unit CLI tests. |
| PM1d | CLI extensibility SPI (`de.dlr.shepard.cli.plugin.AdminCliCommandProvider`) + `CliPluginBootstrap` ServiceLoader walk of the same plugin directory the backend uses (`SHEPARD_PLUGINS_DIR` / sys-prop `shepard.plugins.dir`); migrate the existing `UnhideCommand` from `cli/src/...` to `plugins/unhide/src/...cli/` as the proof-of-shape. Plugin JARs ship their own Picocli subcommands via a `META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.cli.plugin.AdminCliCommandProvider` file pointing at an implementation that returns the {@code @Command}-annotated class. `ShepardAdmin.commandLine()` is the new public entry point that runs the bootstrap before returning — `main` and tests both go through it. CLI's `with-plugins` Maven profile (default-active) copies every `de.dlr.shepard.plugins:*` `provided` dep into `target/plugins/` at packaging time, mirroring the backend's profile shape. CLI's pom also adds a `maven-jar-plugin` `test-jar` execution so plugin modules can pull the shared `StubBackend`/`CliRunner` test fixtures via `<type>test-jar</type>`. | user direction 2026-05-13, ADR-0023 | M | **shipped** | This PR. Two new files in core (`AdminCliCommandProvider`, `CliPluginBootstrap`); `ShepardAdmin` no longer hard-codes `unhide`. Nine files moved from `cli/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/cli/commands/` (the seven `Unhide*Command` files + `UnhideAdminPaths` + `UnhideCommand`) to `plugins/unhide/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/plugins/unhide/cli/`; two IO records moved from `cli/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/cli/io/` (`UnhideConfig`, `HarvestKeyMinted`) to `plugins/unhide/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/plugins/unhide/cli/io/`; `UnhideCommandsTest` moved alongside. Plugin's pom grows two `provided` deps (CLI + Picocli) and one test-scope `<type>test-jar</type>` dep on the CLI module. End-user UX byte-identical — `shepard-admin unhide …` works exactly as before; same verbs, same flags, same JSON shapes, same exit codes; the 12 UnhideCommandsTest cases run unmodified in their new location (only their package + the parent-wiring assertion were updated to call `ShepardAdmin.commandLine()` so the SPI discovery runs). 5 new `AdminCliCommandProviderTest` cases on the JAR-walk path (synthetic plugin JAR built inline via the Java compiler API + a fail-fast smoke through the bootstrap), 4 new `ShepardAdminBootstrapTest` cases on the wiring (core subcommands intact, idempotent, --help, --version). Pre-existing CLI bug fix continuity: the duplicate `delete(String)` overload PM1b already removed stays removed. **Backend untouched**: PM1d is a `cli/`-side + `plugins/`-side refactor only. **Test obligation deferred**: an integration regression that drives `java -jar shepard-admin.jar unhide status` end-to-end against a live Quarkus instance is queued alongside the broader PM1a/PM1b testcontainer story. |
| PM1e | Persist runtime overrides (`PluginRuntimeOverride` Neo4j entity, `HasAppId`) so a `disable` survives a restart without editing `application.properties`. Was the "persist" slot historically labelled PM1c/PM1d; renumbered to PM1e to keep the chain monotonic after PM1c (manifest enrichment) + PM1d (CLI extensibility) shipped. | — | S | **shipped** | This PR. Backend: new `de.dlr.shepard.plugin.{PluginRuntimeOverride, PluginRuntimeOverrideDAO}` (`:PluginRuntimeOverride` `@NodeEntity` with `HasAppId` + `pluginId`/`enabled`/`updatedAt`/`updatedBy`; V32 constraint migration `pluginId_unique_PluginRuntimeOverride` mirrors the V11/V22/V25/V27/V30 idiom). `PluginRegistry.seedOverridesFromDao()` runs first in `discover()` so the in-memory cache reflects the persisted state before lifecycle invocation; `PluginRegistry.setEnabled(id, enabled, actorSub)` writes through the DAO synchronously within the PATCH request (sparse-table semantics — resetting to the deploy-time default DELETEs the row rather than upserting it to match). `PluginsAdminRest.patch` now takes a `@Context SecurityContext` and propagates the caller's `sub` to the registry → `:PluginRuntimeOverride.updatedBy` (redundant with the `:Activity` actor capture from PROV1a, but kept for fast lookups). CLI success messages updated from "the runtime override survives until restart" to "override persisted; survives restart"; `docs/reference/plugins.md` §"Admin REST API" + §"`shepard-admin plugins` subcommand" + §"Enable / disable a plugin" caveats updated. Fail-soft on DAO outage at startup (logs WARN + falls back to deploy-time defaults) or on PATCH write (logs WARN; in-memory cache is current; the override is lost on restart). 13 new `PluginRuntimeOverrideDAOTest` + 11 new `PluginRegistryPersistenceTest` (startup-seed, write-through, sparse-table semantics, simulated restart preserves disable, fail-soft on DAO outage) + 4 new `PluginsAdminRestTest` (actor capture from SecurityContext) + 3 new `PluginsCommandsTest` (CLI success message + simulated restart). | IX1 | External-service minimal-instance infrastructure — prefer running **the real service in a minimal Docker container** over synthetic WireMock stubs; stubs only for services with no self-hostable open-source image. Target list and preferred image for each: (a) **Helmholtz Databus + MOSS** (`R1/MB1`) — Apache Jena Fuseki testcontainer (`stain/jena-fuseki`) is the actual SPARQL store Databus uses; MOSS indexer gets a real minimal MOSS module config pointed at it; validates the full SHACL + DCAT round-trip against real SPARQL semantics. (b) **AAS Registry** (`AAS1-reg`) — `eclipsebasyx/aas-environment:2.x` official image; exercises the real BaSyx REST wire shape including `AssetAdministrationShell` registration payloads. (c) **Matrix home server** (`MTX1a`) — `matrixdotorg/synapse:latest` or `conduit` minimal testcontainer; real `/_matrix/client/v3` routes so the notification flow is exercised against actual Matrix protocol. (d) **Open3D sidecar** (`PC1b`, `VID2a`) — real image we build from `infrastructure/sidecar-open3d/`; testcontainered directly via the built image tag. (e) **SA-Python bridge** (`SA1`) — if the bridge is an HTTP microservice we control, testcontainer it directly; SA COM itself is not CI-testable (Windows-only), so the CI test validates the bridge's HTTP contract against a fixture PLY file. (f) **Helmholtz Unhide HKG harvester** (`UH1`) — no official self-hostable harvester image; use a minimal WireMock container (`wiremock/wiremock:3`) that calls back our feed URL and verifies the JSON-LD shape. (g) **DataCite** (`KIP1d`) — no self-hostable Fabrica; CI falls back to `api.test.datacite.org` OR a WireMock stub set for `/dois` CRUD + `/heartbeat`. (h) **ePIC handle service** (`KIP1c`) — no self-hostable image; WireMock stub for `POST /api/handles/{prefix}/{suffix}`. Pattern: `ExternalServiceStack` Quarkus test-resource (`@QuarkusTestResource`) accepts a `services` init-arg; each enum value starts the correct Testcontainer or WireMock map; `infrastructure-local/docker-compose.external-services.yml` exposes the same images for local dev and manual integration runs without test framework overhead. | user request 2026-05-16 | L | **design sketch** | DX1 (`ShepardTestStack`) handles internal DBs; IX1 handles outbound integrations. Both needed before external integrations land in CI proper. Sub-IDs: IX1a (`ExternalServiceStack` skeleton + Fuseki/Databus), IX1b (BaSyx AAS testcontainer), IX1c (Matrix/Synapse testcontainer), IX1d (Open3D sidecar testcontainer), IX1e (WireMock fallbacks for DataCite + ePIC + Unhide), IX1f (SA-Python HTTP bridge testcontainer). |

| N1a | n10s plugin in Neo4j compose service; `SemanticRepositoryType.INTERNAL` enum value + `InternalSemanticConnector`; n10s bootstrap startup hook (post-A1e); `WHERE NOT n:Resource` audit on shepard's Cypher writes. | — | M | **shipped** | A1e fail-fast already shipped. n10s installed via `NEO4J_PLUGINS=["n10s"]` + procedures-allowlist in `infrastructure/docker-compose.yml`. `N10sBootstrapHook` runs after `MigrationsRunner.apply()`, fail-soft on missing plugin. Audit lives at `backend/src/test/resources/n10s-resource-label-audit.sh` (lint-only, advisory). Testcontainer-level integration test deferred (see `aidocs/34` N1a row + `aidocs/44` 7a). |
| N1b | Pre-seeded common ontologies (PROV-O / Dublin Core / schema.org / FOAF / QUDT / OM-2 / W3C Time / GeoSPARQL) bundled at `backend/src/main/resources/ontologies/`. SHA-256 pinning. Idempotent re-import. | — | M | **shipped** | `OntologySeedService` reads `ontologies-manifest.json` from classpath, verifies SHA-256 per file, calls `n10s.rdf.import.inline` (n10s `import.fetch` does not accept `classpath:` URIs). Wired into `N10sBootstrapHook.run()` after the constraint step. Two new config keys: `shepard.semantic.internal.preseed-ontologies.enabled=true` (default on; ADR-0019 records the rationale) + `shepard.semantic.internal.preseed-ontologies.skip-bundles=` (CSV; e.g. `qudt,om-2`). Fail-soft per-bundle — a missing classpath file, SHA mismatch, or n10s call error is logged and the next bundle is attempted. The bundled `.ttl` files are minimum-viable Turtle stubs carrying each ontology's canonical IRI prefix and a handful of representative classes/properties; full canonical content lands when an operator runs the N1c refresh CLI (or replaces a file and regenerates the manifest entry by hand). ~16 KB of bundled stubs today; the full ~13 MB target lands with N1c. |
| N1c | `shepard-admin semantic refresh-ontologies` CLI per `aidocs/22 §4.x`. | — | S | **shipped** | New backend endpoint `POST /v2/admin/semantic/refresh-ontologies` (instance-admin gated) drives a new `OntologyRefreshService` that walks the bundled-ontology manifest, fetches each bundle's pinned `canonicalUrl`, recomputes SHA-256, and re-imports via `n10s.rdf.import.inline` when the hash differs (or when `force=true`). Best-effort per bundle — partial failures land in the `errors[]` array with 200 OK overall, mirroring the seed service's fail-soft stance from `aidocs/48`. New CLI command `shepard-admin semantic refresh-ontologies [--bundles=prov-o,qudt] [--force]` wraps it; human-table + JSON output; exit 0 on success / 1 on any per-bundle error. No new config keys (reuses the `shepard.semantic.internal.*` namespace). Backend coverage: `OntologyRefreshService` 87% line / 74% branch, `SemanticAdminRest` 100% line / 92% branch; CLI command 92% line / 92% branch. |
| N1c2 | Admin-configurable ontology preseed — runtime enable/disable + custom-bundle upload per `aidocs/65`. | — | M | **shipped** | Five new endpoints under `/v2/admin/semantic/ontologies` (instance-admin gated): `GET` (merged built-in + user list), `POST {id}/enable`, `POST {id}/disable` (409 `semantic.bundle.required` for prov-o / obo-relations), `POST` multipart upload (10 MB cap, SHA-256 + duplicate-id refusal + Turtle parse-shape heuristic), `DELETE {id}` (409 `semantic.bundle.builtin-not-removable` for built-ins). New `:SemanticConfig` singleton (V27) + `:UserOntologyBundle` catalogue (V28) Neo4j entities; `OntologyConfigService` fronts the singleton + first-start seeds from deploy-time `shepard.semantic.internal.preseed-ontologies.{enabled,skip-bundles}` (runtime row wins forever after per CLAUDE.md "Always: surface operator knobs"). Manifest gains a `required: boolean` field — prov-o + obo-relations are required (always seed). Five new CLI subcommands `shepard-admin semantic ontologies {list,enable <id>,disable <id>,upload --file=… --id=… --iri-prefix=… [--canonical-url=…] --license=…,remove <id>}` with the standard `--output={human,json}` + `--url` + `--api-key` flags. New deploy-time exception key `shepard.semantic.internal.user-bundles-dir` (default `/var/lib/shepard/ontologies/`) holds operator-uploaded TTLs on disk. Coverage: `OntologyConfigService` 90.8% line / 78.4% branch; `SemanticAdminRest` 97.9% line / 85.7% branch; `RuntimeConfig` 100/100; CLI 15 new tests against `StubBackend`. |

Auth: any authenticated user. Tries fulltext index `resource_labels` (V44); falls back to CONTAINS scan if absent; returns 200 + empty array when no ontology data loaded — never errors. Limit capped at 50. `AddAnnotationDialog.vue` replaces both free-text `NameInput` IRI fields with `v-combobox` backed by `useTermSearch()` (300ms debounce, min 2 chars); users can still type a raw IRI directly. `TermSuggestionIO` record IO + `SemanticTermSearchRest` REST class. 11 unit tests (`SemanticTermSearchRestTest`). `V44__Add_fulltext_index_Resource_labels.cypher` (idempotent). |
| N1f | (optional) `/v2/semantic/{repoAppId}/sparql` proxy endpoint that wraps n10s SPARQL with shepard auth. | — | M | **shipped** | `GET /{repoAppId}/sparql?query=` + `POST /{repoAppId}/sparql` (form). Auth: any authenticated user (matching v1 SemanticRepositoryRest posture — no per-entity permission check). Read-only gate: SELECT + ASK allowed; CONSTRUCT / INSERT / DELETE / UPDATE / DROP / CREATE / LOAD / CLEAR / ADD / MOVE / COPY rejected 400 `sparql.read-only` via `SparqlQueryValidator`. Type dispatch: `INTERNAL` → n10s HTTP at `POST /rdf/neo4j/sparql` on port 7474 (Basic auth from existing `neo4j.username`/`neo4j.password`; configurable via `shepard.semantic.internal.http-url`); `SPARQL` → proxy external endpoint GET `?query=`; `JSKOS`/`SKOSMOS` → 501 `sparql.not-supported`. Response: upstream body passed through verbatim with `Content-Type: application/sparql-results+json`; non-200 upstream → 502/503 RFC 7807. `SemanticRepositoryDAO.findByAppId` added (mirrors TimeseriesReferenceDAO pattern). Two test classes: `SparqlQueryValidatorTest` (37 cases, keyword + edge-case coverage), `SemanticSparqlRestTest` (21 cases, 401/404/400/501/200 gates). Integration test (actual n10s HTTP round-trip) deferred — tracked in `aidocs/34` N1f row. | ID | Item | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| IMPORT-VIDEO-MP4-SHORTUPLOAD | Large-MP4 video upload retry-loops the stringer importer | M | **FIXED + verified live (2026-06-25 18:0x UTC: deleted-video count flat, frames+Garage climbing)** | **True root cause (third layer):** the v2 reference envelope `ReferenceV2IO.put()` places per-kind fields *flat* in `payload` keyed by the top-level `kind` (so a video exposes `payload.fileSizeBytes` directly), but the importer's `_content_length` only read *nested* `payload.video`/`payload.file` blobs. The singleton-file path happened to work because the file handler nests a `ShepardFile` under `payload.file` with a `fileSize` key; video has no nested blob, so the importer read nothing → `-1` → `stored!=size` retry loop forever, with the correct size (1331387223) sitting unread in `payload.fileSizeBytes`. **Fix:** `_content_length` now scans the flat `payload` first (plus any nested file/video blob) across `fileSize/fileSizeBytes/sizeBytes/size`. Two defense-in-depth backend fixes shipped alongside (next redeploy): (1) video service records the authoritative stored size from `storage.get(locator).sizeBytes()` rather than the declared content-length [`create_recordsAuthoritativeStoredSize_notDeclaredContentLength` test]; (2) the video kind handler emits the canonical `fileSize` flat key for cross-kind consistency (singleton-file uses `fileSize`; video previously only `fileSizeBytes`). Earlier "size-recording" + "non-seekable pipe" hypotheses were both off — the recorded size was already correct and the probe already uses a temp file. | `mffd-stringer-import.py:_upload_file` re-uploads forever when `payload.video.fileSize != source size` (the short-upload guard, lines ~738-748). On run 13's `P02_1.Bahn.MP4` (1.33 GB) the recorded `fileSize` never matched → 50+ DELETE→POST→PUT cycles, **zero HTTP 500s**, ingest wedged. `VideoStreamReferenceService.create`/`attachBytes` set `fileSizeBytes = probe.fileSizeBytes() ?? contentLength` (lines 204/313); ffprobe **exits 1** on these MP4s so `probe` is empty and the recorded size fell back to the declared HTTP `Content-Length`, which does not round-trip authoritatively for large chunked PUTs through Caddy. **Contrast:** the singleton-file path (`FileStorageService.storeViaSpi`) records the *actual* spooled byte count, so it is immune — the video sibling of `STORAGE-SPI-PUT-SIZEFIX`. **FIX (option a, 2026-06-25):** both video upload paths now prefer the authoritative stored size from `storage.get(locator).sizeBytes()` (already fetched for probing) over probe/declared-size, with a 512 KiB-style regression test (`create_recordsAuthoritativeStoredSize_notDeclaredContentLength`). **Correction:** the earlier "non-seekable stdin pipe" theory was WRONG — `VideoProbeService.probe` already spools to a seekable temp file (`Files.createTempFile` + `Files.copy`) and runs ffprobe on the *path*. The ffprobe exit-1 on these MP4s is a **separate open metadata-quality sub-issue** (duration/codec/dimensions come back null); root cause still unknown, tracked below. The size fix unblocks ingest regardless. |
| IMPORT-VIDEO-FFPROBE-EXIT1 | ffprobe exits 1 on the stringer GoPro MP4s → no video metadata | S | open | `VideoProbeService.runFfprobe` runs `ffprobe -v quiet -print_format json -show_format -show_streams` on a fully-spooled seekable temp file (binary present + runnable: `/usr/local/bin/ffprobe` v N-125300). Yet it exits 1 on `P02_*.MP4`, so duration/width/height/codec/frameRate persist as null. NOT a blocker (size now recorded authoritatively, IMPORT-VIDEO-MP4-SHORTUPLOAD). Next step: capture ffprobe stderr (currently logged but empty in the WARN) by dropping `-v quiet` on failure-retry, or probe a sample MP4 in-container to see the actual error. Surfaced 2026-06-25. |
| MFFD-INGEST-OOM | Backend OOM under MFFD-scale ingest — heap was capped at 2G | M | **mitigated 2026-06-26 (heap → 10G); root-cause follow-up open** | After ~8h of healthy ingest the backend wedged: `java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space` storm (164 OOMs) on `.svdx` content PUTs, with the shallow docker healthcheck still reporting "healthy" while every upload 503'd on "spool bytes: Read timed out" (a GC-thrash symptom). Root cause: `infrastructure/docker-compose.yml:6` set `JAVA_OPTS=-Xms2G -Xmx2G` (upstream default) — far too small for MFFD-scale, where the `.svdx` parse path buffers into heap and the importer's warmup re-verification burst (~450 concurrent `.svdx` re-uploads) exhausts 2G fast. **Mitigation (shipped):** override `JAVA_OPTS=-Xms2G -Xmx10G -XX:+ExitOnOutOfMemoryError` in `docker-compose.override.yml` (nuclide; host has 32G) — recreated backend, verified stable + no OOM storm. ExitOnOutOfMemoryError makes a future OOM fail-fast (exit → `restart: unless-stopped`) rather than zombie. **Follow-ups (open):** (a) the docker healthcheck is too shallow — it passed while the JVM was OOM-dead; make it probe a real DB-touching endpoint so the watchdog catches this. (b) the `.svdx` parse + the importer warmup re-verification are both memory-heavy; stream the svdx parse and make warmup re-verify via HEAD/metadata instead of re-PUT. (c) document the heap floor in `aidocs/34` for operators running MFFD-scale ingest. Surfaced + handled during autonomous watchdog 2026-06-26 ~03:00 UTC. |

### Newly surfaced (second-pass analysis of `input_raw.md` lines 1840–8409)

These come from a re-read of regions the first scan missed. `R7` deduplicates with existing `L1`. `R2` (RO-Crate) is **already implemented** in this repo (`ExportConstants.ROCRATE_METADATA`); only the per-payload selection enhancement remains. Most others are blocked on user/maintainer decisions.

| ID | Item | input_raw refs | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| R1 | Databus + MOSS federation layer (`shepard-plugin-databus`) | 8380 | M | **design done** | `aidocs/77`. Full design: `:DatabusConfig` admin entity, admin REST + CLI parity, researcher-facing `POST /v2/collections/{appId}/publish/databus`, PROV-O Turtle generation, `shepard` MOSS module definition (SHACL + indexer). MB1a–MB1g phasing. Gated on PROV1a (`:Activity` capture, `aidocs/55`) and the MOSS module YAML landing in the DLR-internal Databus instance. |
| R2 | Per-payload selective RO-Crate export (refinement of existing export) | 8381 | S–M | **Phase 1 done** | Round 6, commit `be0eb26` (cherry-picked). Existing exporter lives in `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/context/export/` (`ExportService` is the walker entry point, `ExportBuilder` wraps `edu.kit.datamanager.ro_crate.RoCrate.RoCrateBuilder`). Phase 1 adds an optional `ExportSelection` body on a **new** sibling `POST /collections/{collectionId}/export` (the existing GET is preserved unchanged for byte-identical legacy behaviour). Selection has two sections: `payloads` (`include` allow-list of kinds + `excludeIds` deny-list) and `metadata` booleans (permissions / annotations / labJournal / versions / subscriptions). The selection actually applied is recorded as a JSON block on the root data entity in `ro-crate-metadata.json` so consumers can tell what was excluded. 18 tests pass (5 new selection-service + 2 new manifest-injection + 11 pre-existing). **Important:** the exporter today does **not** yet emit `permissions / versions / subscriptions / annotations` documents — the booleans are wired and recorded but bundling those metadata kinds is deferred to **R2d**. |
| R2b | File- and column-level selection on payloads (per-FileReference oid include list; per-TimeseriesReference column / time-window picks) | — | M | done | Round 6, commit `60a3ea1`. Adds `payloads.perPayload: Map<entityId, PerPayloadSelection>` and `payloads.strictPerPayload: boolean`. `PerPayloadSelection` has optional `fileOids`, `columns`, `timeRange{start,end}` — kind-applicability is silent (wrong-kind entries ignored). Existing per-OID file-fetch service reused; existing `fieldFilterSet` parameter on `TimeseriesReferenceService.exportReferencedTimeseriesByShepardId` covers per-column projection; a new 14-arg overload accepts `startNanosOverride` / `endNanosOverride` for time-window without mutating the reference. Stale OIDs / unknown columns silently skipped + recorded in a new `selection.warnings[]` array on the manifest's root selection block; `strictPerPayload=true` flips that to 400. 11 new tests pass (43/43 export-package). P17b lint stays green. |
| R2c | Per-payload metadata-field redaction (e.g. include the annotation but redact its label) | — | M | done | Round 6, commit `f993e8b`. Adds `metadata.redactFields: Set<RedactableField>` to `ExportSelection`. **Closed enum** of 6 fields, each verified against the actual IO type before shipping (one stop-and-report adjustment): `PERMISSION_USERNAME` (replaces `owner` + `reader[]` + `writer[]` + `manager[]`), `PERMISSION_GROUP_IDS` (empties `readerGroupIds` / `writerGroupIds` — renamed from proposed `…_GROUP_NAME` because `PermissionsIO` carries group ids only), `ANNOTATION_LABEL` (replaces `propertyName` — proposed `label` doesn't exist; IRI left intact as stable id), `ANNOTATION_VALUE` (replaces `valueName` — proposed `value` doesn't exist), `VERSION_AUTHOR` (replaces `createdBy` — no `author` field), `LAB_JOURNAL_CONTENT` (replaces `journalContent`). Mutation is direct setter on the Lombok `@Data` IO types before serialisation. Manifest selection block records the **set** but not the replacement values (no leak vector). 11 new tests pass; 54/54 export-package total. |
| R2d | Emit permissions / versions / subscriptions / semantic-annotations documents from the exporter (the metadata booleans from R2 Phase 1 are wired but the exporter currently doesn't bundle these doc kinds) | — | M | **mostly done** | Round 6, commit `d5fa03c`. **3 of 4 kinds wired:** `permissions` (via `PermissionsService.getPermissionsOfEntityOptional`), `versions` (via `VersionService.getAllVersions`; collection-only — only Collections are versioned), `annotations` (via `BasicEntity.getAnnotations()`). Defaults remain `false` so today's behaviour is unchanged. 14 new tests pass on merged branch (10 service-level in `ExportMetadataBundleTest`, 4 builder-level in `ExportBuilderSelectionTest`). **`subscriptions` deferred** — see **R2d2** below. |
| R2d2 | Emit per-entity subscriptions in the RO-Crate export — needs a URL-pattern → entity-URL matcher because subscriptions in shepard are URL-pattern-based, not per-entity-attached | — | M | done | Round 6, commit `967fbf5`. New `SubscriptionMatcher` extracted from `SubscriptionFilter` (pure refactor; `SubscriptionFilterTest` 7/7 pass); new `EntityUrlSynthesiser` produces canonical paths for Collection / DataObject / BasicReference / LabJournalEntry using the same `Constants.*` tokens the JAX-RS resources use. Walker hook calls `subscriptionService.getMatchingSubscriptionsForUrl(url, method)` and writes `<id>-subscriptions.json` per entity. **Documented trade-off:** subscription patterns are full-URL regexes (scheme + host); export only knows the path, so authors of host-anchored patterns may need a `.*` prefix to also match exports. 7 new tests pass; 43+ on merged branch. |
| R3 | Provenance capture in exports (OpenLineage / W3C PROV-O / both) | 8386 | M | **needs decision** | Ontology choice; aligns with `aidocs/archive/semantics/14-semantic-improvements.md` |
| R4 | Evaluate NovaCrate library for RO-Crate metadata editing | 8389 | S | **done (2026-05-26)** | WATCH verdict — NovaCrate is a private Next.js app (not an npm package); no Vue 3 embed path; Describo Vue alternative archived Aug 2025. Recommended: "Open in NovaCrate" external link (shape 4a); revisit when NovaCrate Cloud Frontend track ships a stable backend-adapter contract. Doc: `aidocs/data/r4-novacrate-evaluation.md`. ### API critique series (from `aidocs/23`, 2026-05-05)

The MVP "minimum-viable clunkiness fix" is **P5 + P6 + P7 + P10 + P12 + P16 + P18** (≈ 2 sprints).

| ID | Item | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| P5 | Replace path-segment switching in `PermissionsService.isAllowed` with annotation-based authz on each handler | M | queued | **Same proposal as F1** in `aidocs/24`. Use F1 as the canonical ID; P5 is its API-critique counterpart. |
| P6 | Fold `X-API-KEY` into `Authorization: Bearer`, deprecate the second header | S | queued | Breaking; bundles with P4 versioning. |
| P7 | Ship the unified `POST /search/v2` from `aidocs/13 §2`; deprecate the five legacy search routes | L | queued | Maps to epic E2 in `aidocs/20`. **Gated on C5** (Cypher injection). |
| P8 | Polymorphic `/annotations` endpoint replacing the four per-kind annotation rests | M | queued | Depends on `aidocs/14` model unification. |
| P9 | Single `/entities/{kind}/{id}/permissions` route replacing per-container permissions endpoints | M | queued | Overlaps **A2**. |
| P10 | `POST /sql/timeseries` curated SQL-over-HTTP for bulk reads — **answers the "timeseries to Excel" prompt** | M | **design done** | New paradigm slice; design landed in `aidocs/platform/29-p10-implementation-design.md`. JSON DSL request body (vs raw SQL or templated lang). Cypher-first permission flow via `filterAllowedForUser` (post-P2). Three-format content negotiation (`text/csv` default, `application/json`, `application/x-ndjson`; Arrow deferred to P11). 1M-row + PT60S caps; mandatory time range. **Gated on C5.** Spawns **P10a** (S, JSON-only behind feature flag), **P10b** (M, CSV+NDJSON streaming, caps), **P10c** (S, default-on, wrapper retarget). |
| P10a | Phase 1: `SqlQuerySpec` + `SqlQueryCompiler` + JSON-only endpoint behind `shepard.timeseries.sql.enabled=false` | — | S | **✓ shipped** | `POST /v2/sql/timeseries`. `de.dlr.shepard.data.timeseries.sql` package: `SqlQuerySpec` record, `AllowedTable`/`AllowedColumn` enums, `SqlQueryCompiler` (alias-regex validation, all user values as `?` params), `SqlQueryExecutor` (AgroalDataSource, fetchSize 10k), `JsonWriter`, `PreparedStatementSpec` record, `SqlTimeseriesRest` (with `executeQuery()` package-private method for testability). Feature flag `shepard.timeseries.sql.enabled=false` (default off). 3 test classes: `SqlQueryCompilerTest`, `SqlTimeseriesInjectionTest`, `SqlTimeseriesPermissionTest`; 14 tests pass in standalone JUnit runner. Config keys: `shepard.timeseries.sql.enabled=false`, `…max-rows=1000000`. |
| P10b | Phase 2: `text/csv` + `application/x-ndjson` content negotiation; streaming output with row + duration caps; `Trailer: x-shepard-truncated` | — | M | **✓ shipped** | `CsvWriter` (RFC 4180, CRLF, double-quote escaping, ISO-8601 timestamps, UTF-8 no BOM) + `NdjsonWriter` (one JSON object per line, `\n` delimiter) added. `JsonWriter.write()` return type changed to `WriteResult`. `SqlQueryExecutor` refactored: `executeJson`/`executeCsv`/`executeNdjson` each take `OutputStream` and return `WriteResult`; `Statement.cancel()` called on `IOException` (client disconnect); `statement_timeout` set per request via `SET statement_timeout = N`. `SqlTimeseriesRest` gains content negotiation via `@HeaderParam("Accept")` (CSV default for `*/*`; JSON; NDJSON), `@Context HttpServerResponse` for VertX trailer emission, `@ConfigProperty` wiring for `max-rows` and `max-duration`. New config key: `shepard.timeseries.sql.max-duration=PT60S`. `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="timeseries.csv"` on CSV responses. `Trailer: x-shepard-truncated` announcement header; `x-shepard-truncated: true` trailer via `HttpServerResponse.putTrailer()` on row-cap hit. New test class: `SqlTimeseriesFormatTest` (writer unit tests: RFC 4180 quoting, null fields, row cap, NDJSON per-line, format negotiation, REST-layer routing, trailer emission verified via Mockito-mocked `HttpServerResponse`). `WriteResult` record added. `QueryTimeoutExceptionMapper` (`@Provider ExceptionMapper`) maps SQLState 57014 to HTTP 504. Admin-config deferral resolved in P10c. `docs/reference/timeseries-reference.md` created. |
| P10c | Phase 3: feature flag default-on; `:SqlTimeseriesConfig` + `GET/PATCH /v2/admin/sql-timeseries/config` runtime config for `max-rows` and `max-duration` | — | S–M | **✓ shipped** | `:SqlTimeseriesConfig` Neo4j singleton (A3b pattern) seeded from deploy-time defaults on first start; `GET/PATCH /v2/admin/sql-timeseries/config` `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`; runtime value wins over `application.properties` deploy-time default (`effectiveMaxRows()` / `effectiveMaxDurationIso()` in `SqlTimeseriesConfigService`). `SqlTimeseriesRest` now reads effective caps from `SqlTimeseriesConfigService` (removes direct `@ConfigProperty` injection). `V43__Add_appId_constraint_SqlTimeseriesConfig.cypher` migration adds `REQUIRE n.appId IS UNIQUE`. Feature flag `shepard.timeseries.sql.enabled` flipped to `true` (default-on). 13 unit tests. **CLI parity:** landed in FTOGGLE-CLI-PARITY-1 (PR in-flight). |
| **FTOGGLE-CLI-PARITY-1** | CLI parity for SQL timeseries and HDF runtime toggles. `shepard-admin sql-timeseries {status,enable,disable,set-max-rows,set-max-duration}` targeting `GET/PATCH /v2/admin/config/sql-timeseries`. `shepard-admin hdf {status,enable,disable}` targeting `GET/PATCH /v2/admin/config/hdf` — contributed via PM1d `AdminCliCommandProvider` SPI from `plugins/hdf5/`. | — | S | **✓ shipped (fire-159)** | `cli/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/cli/commands/SqlTimeseries*.java` (5 commands + container) + `cli/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/cli/io/SqlTimeseriesConfig.java`; `ShepardAdmin.java` gains `SqlTimeseriesCommand`. Plugin: `plugins/hdf5/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/plugins/hdf5/cli/{Hdf*.java,io/HdfCliConfig.java}` + `META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.cli.plugin.AdminCliCommandProvider`; `plugins/hdf5/pom.xml` gains `shepard-admin` provided + test-jar deps. Tests: `SqlTimeseriesCommandsTest` (14 cases) + `HdfCommandsTest` (6 cases) — both use `StubBackend`/`CliRunner` harness. `enable`/`disable` for SQL timeseries take effect once FTOGGLE-SQL-ENABLE-1 merges (adds `enabled` to the server-side patch IO); for HDF they take effect once FTOGGLE-HDF-ENABLE-1 merges. |

New `TimeseriesNdjsonService` reads request as `BufferedReader`, validates each `{timestamp, value}` line independently (timeseries identity supplied as query params), batches 5000 lines per `insertManyDataPointsWithCopyCommand` call. Streams response (per-line errors + `{"summary": {"accepted": A, "rejected": R, "durationMs": D}}` final line). Caps: `shepard.timeseries.ingest.ndjson.batch-size` (default 5000), `…max-duration` (default PT5M). Auth check happens **before** stream consumption. 5 `@QuarkusComponentTest` cases pass: mixed valid/invalid, multi-chunk streaming proof, exact-batch-count assertion (12000 lines → 3 batches), pre-stream auth failure. **Follow-up:** once P3's `insertManyDataPointsWithCopyCommandBatched` is preferred, swap the service-layer batching for the repository-layer one. |
| STORAGE-NO-MAGIC-ROUTES-1 | **Rule: no "magic routes" — all binary content (core + plugins) routes through the `FileStorage` SPI (`FileStorageRegistry.activeStorage()`), never a direct GridFS/Mongo-bytes/S3 path.** Verified 2026-06-24: rule was **violated** in two places — (1) core `FileService.createFile` writes straight to a GridFS bucket (`uploadFromStream`); singleton + bundle paths called it directly, bypassing `activeStorage()`. (2) video plugin `VideoStreamReferenceService` injected its own `MongoDatabase` and queried a per-ref GridFS-backed collection for MD5 dedup, bypassing the SPI. **Both code violations fixed 2026-06-24 via STORAGE-SPI-UNIFY-1.** Sanctioned: the gridfs + file-s3 adapters, SPI types, historical migrations, and non-file substrates (structured-data, avatars, mongo infra). ArchUnit guard shipped 2026-06-26 (fire-240): `StorageAdapterIsolationTest.gridFsByteAccessMustStayInsideAdapterZone()` — new `com.mongodb.client.gridfs` imports outside the three sanctioned zones (storage/gridfs adapter, legacy FileService, neo4j migrations) fail the build. | S | ✅ shipped (fire-240) | Operator rule 2026-06-24. Code violations cleared STORAGE-SPI-UNIFY-1. ArchUnit guard: `backend/src/test/java/de/dlr/shepard/architecture/StorageAdapterIsolationTest.java`. |
| STORAGE-SPI-UNIFY-1 | Route the two magic routes above through the SPI. **DONE 2026-06-24 (worktree, pending merge)** — added core `de.dlr.shepard.storage.FileStorageService` as the single registry-routed choke point (`storeFile`/`getPayload`/`deleteFile`; per-row provider resolution so existing GridFS content reads after a provider flip). Migrated `SingletonFileReferenceService` + `FileBundleReferenceRest` off direct `FileService.createFile`/`deleteFile`/`getPayload`, and the thermography plugin's `ThermographyAnalysisService` read path too. Rewrote the video plugin's `storeWithDedup` to drop the direct `MongoDatabase` MD5-dedup query — every video byte now routes through `activeStorage().put`. Grep proof: no `uploadFromStream`/`GridFSBucket`/`@Named("mongoDatabase")` remains in any file/video byte-store path (only the sanctioned `GridFsFileStorage` adapter + non-file substrates). Tests: new `FileStorageServiceTest` proves provider=s3 routes to S3, provider=gridfs routes to GridFS, and a gridfs-stored row reads while active=s3; video `storeWithDedup` test rewritten to assert S3-locator passthrough. **Enabler for S3-default.** | M | shipped 2026-06-24 (worktree) | Implements STORAGE-NO-MAGIC-ROUTES-1. The object-store (S3) single-file *write* path uses the presigned-URL endpoints (direct JVM streaming unsupported for object stores), matching `FileContainerService`. The GridFS-only video MD5 dedup optimization was dropped as the cost of removing the magic route. |
| STORAGE-S3-DEFAULT-DIST-1 | Make **S3 the distribution default**: promote the S3 `FileStorage` to core (or ship file-s3 enabled-by-default), demote GridFS to a legacy plugin (v1-compat pattern), flip the shipped `shepard.storage.provider` default gridfs→s3, and **auto-provision Garage** (layout+bucket+key) on first start in the default compose so S3 works out-of-box. Design-doc + `aidocs/34` **BREAKING** entry + upgrade runbook. Depends on STORAGE-SPI-UNIFY-1. | L | queued (design-first) | Operator vision 2026-06-24: "s3 should be the distribution default; move the s3 plugin to core, gridfs to a legacy plugin like v1 compatibility." |
| STORAGE-GRIDFS-MIGRATE-1 | Migrate existing GridFS file/video content → S3 via the FS1e `FileMigrationService` after STORAGE-SPI-UNIFY-1. Reads stay correct during/after via per-file storage locator. | M | queued | Follow-up to the cutover. |
| TS-CONFLICT-POLICY-1 | Timeseries point-write conflict policy: **reject conflicting points by default**, with an explicit `overwrite` flag to opt into the current `ON CONFLICT (timeseries_id, time) DO UPDATE` behavior. Today every payload write silently last-wins on a `(channel, timestamp)` collision (`TimeseriesDataPointRepository:389`), which masks accidental double-ingest. Default should be reject (return the conflicting count / 409 or per-point reject in NDJSON), overwrite only when the caller asks. | S–M | **shipped 2026-06-27** (PR pending) | Operator ask 2026-06-19. `ConflictException` (HTTP 409) added; `TimeseriesDataPointRepository.insertManyDataPoints(…, boolean overwrite)` uses `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` by default and throws when `affected < submitted`. `TimeseriesService.saveDataPoints(…, boolean overwrite)` exposes the flag. Old 3-arg `saveDataPoints` delegates to `overwrite=true` (v1 compat — no v1 wire change). 3 new `@QuarkusTest` cases. NDJSON path (P14) + COPY path (already rejects) deferred. V2 callers (KRL interpret, SVDX ingest) wire up `overwrite` in their own PRs. |

Dovetails with **P4b**. |
| P17b | CI lint: every IO class has `@Schema(name=…)` | XS | done | Round 4-extra, commit `963d0b0`. Bash script + GitLab CI job; baseline allowlist for incremental cleanup. Predicate: classes under any `*/io/` package (depth-1, excluding `*Mapper`) or files named `*IO.java`. **59 candidates · 32 already annotated · 27 in baseline.** Lint passes on merged branch. **Note:** baseline file currently lives at `backend/src/main/resources/schema-name-baseline.txt` so it ships in the production JAR — `BASELINE_FILE` at the top of the script can be retargeted to `.lint/` or `scripts/` as a one-line follow-up. |
| P18 | RFC 7807 error envelope (`application/problem+json`) | S | **done** | Resolved via **H4** (commit `e526183`). Same proposal; H4 is the canonical implementation. |
| P19 | Cursor pagination on the unified search; offset elsewhere stays for now | M (in P7) | queued | Dovetails with **L6**. |
| P20 | Reactive (Mutiny) migration for the timeseries read path as the first slice | M | queued | Dovetails with **A2**. |
| P21 | Introduce PATCH for partial-update endpoints (currently every update is PUT despite partial DTOs — `DataObjectRest:162`, `TimeseriesRest:472`, etc.) | M | **3 of N follow-ons done** | Surfaced by `aidocs/platform/26-crud-consistency.md` §3 finding #1: **0 PATCH endpoints across 153**. Strategy choice: ship PATCH additively in `/v1/` and deprecate the partial-PUT semantics in `/v2/` (cleanest of three). Done so far: pilot **`Collection`** (`10531bd`), **`DataObject`** (`fd7ea76`), **`LabJournalEntry`** (`f0b3bd3` + canonicalisation). All under `context/`, all use `Constants.APPLICATION_MERGE_PATCH_JSON` (post-P21x), all six IT cases each. LabJournalEntry's PATCH also runs the existing `HtmlSanitizer.isSafeHtml` on the merged result so PATCH cannot bypass HTML safety. **Remaining `context/` candidates:** none with a metadata PUT (everything else hits P21-File-prereq or P21-References-prereq). |
| P21x | Move `APPLICATION_MERGE_PATCH_JSON` constant from per-Rest declarations to `Constants.java` so further P21 follow-ons reference one source of truth | — | XS | done | Round 5-extra, in-line edit. `Constants.APPLICATION_MERGE_PATCH_JSON` is now the single source of truth; `CollectionRest` and `DataObjectRest` updated to reference it. 58 sanity-check tests pass on merged branch. |
| P21-References-prereq | **Architectural finding:** the `*ReferenceRest` family (`BasicReference`, `CollectionReference`, `DataObjectReference`, `FileReference`, `SpatialDataReference`, `StructuredDataReference`, `TimeseriesReference`, `URIReference`) has **no `@PUT` for entity metadata** — same shape as P21-File-prereq. References are create-only-then-delete in the current model. | — | M | **RESOLVED 2026-06-26 (operator): SHIP PUT** | References ARE metadata-editable. Ship `PUT /v2/references/{appId}` (full metadata replace) on the unified v2 reference surface — NOT the frozen v1 `*ReferenceRest` (API-version policy). Tracked as P21-V2-METADATA-EDIT. |
| P21-File-prereq | **Architectural finding (P21-File agent stop-and-report):** the entire `data/` container family (`FileRest`, `StructuredDataRest`, `SpatialDataPointRest`, `TimeseriesRest`) has **no `@PUT` for entity metadata** — only `@PUT /{id}/permissions`. None of the `*ContainerService` classes expose an `updateContainer` method. PATCH on those resources is blocked on a prior decision: should data containers be metadata-editable post-creation, or stay create-only-then-delete? | — | M | **RESOLVED 2026-06-26 (operator): SHIP PUT** | Containers ARE metadata-editable. Ship `PUT /v2/containers/{appId}` (full metadata replace) on the unified v2 container surface the dispatcher is collapsing per-kind containers onto — NOT the frozen v1 `data/` family (API-version policy). Tracked as P21-V2-METADATA-EDIT. |

| P21-V2-METADATA-EDIT | **Ship metadata-edit on the unified v2 surface (operator decision 2026-06-26).** `PUT /v2/containers/{appId}` + `PUT /v2/references/{appId}` — full-replace editable metadata (name, description, attributes/semantic annotations; NOT payload bytes or system fields like checksums/aiGenerated). Belongs in the dispatcher's in-flight `/v2/containers` + `/v2/references` convergence (APISIMP-CONT-* / MISSING-V2-APPID-IN-REFLISTS), so it lands without conflicting with that workstream. Backend PUT + service updateX() + JUnit; frontend edit dialog (per the REF-EDIT-* / done-criteria rule); OpenAPI + aidocs/34 + aidocs/44. Frozen v1 `data/`+`*ReferenceRest` stay create-only (upstream byte-compat). | M | **done** (PR #2113 merged 2026-06-25; PR #2115 merged 2026-06-26) | Resolves P21-File-prereq + P21-References-prereq; unblocks the P21 PATCH follow-on family. Slice 1 (PR #2113, merged 2026-06-25): `PUT /v2/containers/{appId}` + `PUT /v2/references/{appId}` in `ContainersV2Rest`/`ReferencesV2Rest` with full-replace semantics (name required, status cleared if absent for containers); 18 JUnit tests. Slice 2 (PR #2115, merged 2026-06-26): `EditContainerNameDialog.vue` — rename button + dialog wired into timeseries and file container pages; calls `GET /v2/containers/{appId}` on open to preserve status, then `PUT /v2/containers/{appId}` on save; 13 Vitest unit tests in `EditContainerNameDialog.test.ts`. || P22 | SSE proxy-compatibility integration test — confirm `GET /collections/{id}/events` (P13) survives the existing reverse-proxy stack (Caddy / Keycloak) | S | **done (2026-05-26)** | Tripwire from `aidocs/ops/28-paradigms-and-clients-synthesis.md`. Findings: `handle /v2/*` Caddyfile block lacks `flush_interval -1`; a dedicated `handle /v2/collections/*/events` block is required before P13 can be promoted to default-on. See `aidocs/ops/p22-sse-proxy-compat-findings.md`. Test infra: `e2e/scripts/smoke-sse.sh` + `e2e/tests/sse-proxy-compat.spec.ts`. |
| P23 | Presign-vs-cache TTL invariant validator — bound presigned-URL TTL by `shepard.permissions.cache.ttl` so a permission revoked while a URL is live cannot keep granting access | S | **done** | `PresignTtlValidator` (`@ApplicationScoped`): reads `shepard.storage.presign.{upload,download,export}-ttl` (defaults PT15M/PT5M/PT30M) + `shepard.permissions.cache.ttl` (default PT5M); caps each effective TTL to the cache TTL; emits `WARN` at startup when a configured TTL is capped. Wired into `FileContainerPresignedUrlRest` + `CollectionExportUrlRest`. 8 unit tests (`PresignTtlValidatorTest`). New config keys: `shepard.storage.presign.{upload,download,export}-ttl` (all optional, defaults preserve pre-P23 behaviour). |
| P24 | **v2 structured-data reference + payload surface missing** — `/v2/references` has no `structured-data` kind handler and there is no v2 structured-data payload upload; the only path is the frozen v1 trio (`POST /shepard/api/structuredDataContainers`, `…/payload`, `…/dataObjects/{id}/structuredDataReferences`) with numeric ids. | M | **done** (APISIMP-STRUCTURED-DATA-KIND — all 3 slices shipped to main 2026-06-17, fires 94/95/96) | Surfaced by the MFFD bridge-welding import (2026-06-17). `StructuredDataReferenceKindHandler` (`kind=structured-data`) + `StructuredDataContainerKindHandler` both fully implemented in `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/handlers/` and `…/containers/handlers/` respectively. See APISIMP-STRUCTURED-DATA-KIND row in the APISIMP section for implementation details. |
| P25 | **No bulk DataObject + reference create on `/v2/`** — minting 1031 DOs each with multiple references is N requests/DO (create DO → create container → create reference → upload payload → annotate). | M | queued | Also surfaced by the MFFD bridge import (2026-06-17). A `POST /v2/collections/{appId}/data-objects:batch` (and reference batch) would collapse the import fan-out and the per-DO 403-retry window (transient `:Permissions` wiring lag right after DO creation). Dovetails with **BATCH-API** family. |

### Permission-system evolutions (from `aidocs/24`, 2026-05-05)

| ID | Item | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Declarative `@Authz(action, resource)` annotation seam replacing the path-segment switch in `PermissionsService.isAllowed` | M | queued | **Same proposal as P5.** Designed so a future `PolicyDecisionPoint` (OPA / Cedar) is a drop-in (~½-day add). Unblocks the path-segment-switch problem that L2c also needs to solve. |
| F2 | Group / sharing model: `Group` Neo4j node with `(User)-[:member_of]->(Group)-[:has_permissions]->(Permissions)-[:has_permissions]->(Entity)` | L | queued | Major gap for a research-data platform. Includes manager-groups + grant inheritance. |
| F3 | Permission audit log (Postgres table) for grants/revokes | S–M | **✓ shipped** | `V1.10.0__add_permission_audit_log_table.sql` — `permission_audit_log` table (BIGSERIAL PK, TIMESTAMPTZ, entity_app_id, entity_kind, actor_username, action, detail_json). `PermissionAuditLogService` (ApplicationScoped, AgroalDataSource, fire-and-forget). Hooked into all three write paths in `PermissionsService`: `createPermissions` → GRANT, `updatePermissionsByNeo4jId` → UPDATE (before/after JSON), `deletePermissions` → REVOKE. `GET /v2/admin/permission-audit/log` (`@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`, params: entityAppId, actor, from, to ISO-8601, page/size). 13 new unit tests. |
| F4 | Versioned cache invalidation key: `(entityId, AccessType, userSub, jwtIat)` | S | **✓ shipped** | Changed `isAccessTypeAllowedForUser` signature from 3-arg to 4-arg; 4th arg `jwtIat` is the JWT `iat` claim (seconds-since-epoch, default `0L` when no JWT or no iat). `JWTPrincipal` gained an `iat` field; `JWTFilter.parsePrincipalFromAccessToken()` extracts `body.getIssuedAt()`. Services with `AuthenticationContext` pass `currentIat()` (reads `authenticationContext.getPrincipal().getIat()`); REST resources and the cache warmer without live-request context pass `0L`. `CompositeCacheKey` now 4-tuple. 4 new `PermissionsServiceIatCacheKeyTest` tests. |
| F5 | Explicit fail-closed invariant when Neo4j is degraded | S | **✓ shipped** | Added `dbHealthRegistry.isCurrentlyDown(DatabaseKind.NEO4J)` guard at the top of `PermissionsService.isAllowed()`: returns `false` immediately when Neo4j is DOWN without calling the DAO. Null-safe (guards against `@InjectMocks` test environments). 3 new `PermissionsServiceNeo4jGuardTest` tests covering: DOWN returns false without DAO call; UP does not short-circuit; guard is independent of username. |
| F6 | Design F1's seam so a future OPA / Cedar `PolicyDecisionPoint` is a drop-in | XS (within F1) | **✓ shipped (2026-05-26)** | **Verdict from `aidocs/24`:** No to OPA/Cedar adoption now (overkill for a single Quarkus service with small graph-shaped policy); yes to making the seam future-proof at half-day cost. Shipped: `PolicyDecisionPoint` interface (`auth/permission/services/PolicyDecisionPoint.java`); `GraphPolicyDecisionPoint` `@ApplicationScoped` CDI bean delegates to `PermissionsService.isAccessTypeAllowedForUser` (fast path via `EntityIdResolver`) and `isAccessAllowedForDataObjectAppId` (DataObject fallback). Class-level Javadoc added to `PermissionsService`. 3 new `GraphPolicyDecisionPointTest` cases (allowed, denied, DataObject fallback). F1 wires `@Authz` annotation that calls this PDP. |
| F7 | Row-level security in TimescaleDB / PostGIS — denormalise ACLs into the data DBs | L | parked — conditions for unparking listed in `aidocs/24` | **Verdict:** No for v1. Backend is the only enforcement point; replicating ACLs into Postgres adds a second source of truth with no current threat-model justification. Conditions for a flip: more than one client bypasses the backend, or read fanout becomes the dominant cost. |
| F8 | Configurable OIDC roles-claim path (`shepard.oidc.roles-claim-path`, default `realm_access.roles`); `JWTFilter` walks the dot-path so non-Keycloak IdPs (Pocket ID, Authentik, Azure AD) can carry roles in their native claim shape | XS-S | **done** | Bundled with **A0** (`2901834`). `shepard.instance-admin.role` (default empty = no IdP-side admin) names the role-string within the claim that maps to `instance-admin`. CONFIG-status row in `aidocs/34`. |
| F9 | **`POST /v2/annotations` denies annotating a Reference** — the permission gate runs `isAccessAllowedForDataObjectAppId(subjectAppId)` against the annotation subject's own appId, but a Reference appId is not a DataObject, so it always denies. | S–M | **done** | Branch `F9-ANNOTATION-REFERENCE-PERMISSION`. Surfaced by the MFFD bridge import (2026-06-17): ~4963 reference-annotation writes failed (DataObject annotations work fine). Fix: `checkAccessForSubject()` detects Neo4j labels ending with "Reference" and calls `ReferencesV2Service.resolveByAppId()` to walk Reference → DataObject → Collection before gating. Also fixes test NPE from `f9ecbd8f` (`resolveWithLabels` stub). 44/44 tests pass. Supersedes PR #2017. |
