## Operator-approved decisions 2026-05-29

Operator ack 2026-05-29 ("all recommendations fine") on the punch list
surfaced during the JH path-mount session. Each line is the approved
direction; implementation tracked under its own row elsewhere in this
file.

| Decision ID | Approved direction | Implementation row + status |
|---|---|---|
| P21-File-prereq + P21-References-prereq | **YES** — ship `PUT /v2/<container>/{id}` and `PUT /v2/<reference>/{id}` for metadata edits. Delete-and-recreate destroys provenance continuity. | Unblocks the P21 PATCH family. Implementation row queued — see P21-File row. |
| PROV-CAPTURE-READS-DECISION | **YES, for `/v2/` paths only** — flip `shepard.provenance.capture-reads=true`. AFTER PROV-RESOLVER-PATHWALK ships, so NULL-target Activity rows don't multiply. v1 stays off (upstream-compat cost). | **done** (PROV-CAPTURE-READS-1). `ProvenanceCaptureFilter` gains a v2-path guard for reads (v1 reads skip even when flag=true); default flipped to `true` in `application.properties`; 1 new test `getDoesNotCapture_v1Path_evenWhenCaptureReadsOn`. |
| #27 Archive state | **YES** — add `ARCHIVED` to Container + Collection status enum (frozen, prune-only). Simple additive; RBAC gate on transition. | Partially shipped: COMP-NCR-STATUS (done 2026-05-26, `5565e2beb`) added ARCHIVED to DataObject status; Container + Collection ARCHIVED still queued as `#27-ARCHIVED-CONTAINER-COLLECTION` (filed below). |
| CHOKE-08 (NFS extract) | **YES — pre-stage 107 GB to local disk** before importer starts. Single-threaded NFS extract throttles ingest. | Operator runbook step; CHOKE-08 row marked operator-approved below. |
| BTKVS-A2 user-bundles-dir | **`/data/shepard/semantic-bundles`** under existing backend volume + bind-mount. | BTKVS-A2-OPS row marked operator-approved; queued for infra deploy. |
| J1e Caddy path-mount | **Isolated change** — not the start of a Zoraxy→Caddy migration. Zoraxy still owns TLS/DNS. | J1e-PR-05-CADDY-PATH-MOUNT-04 row marked **decided: isolated** (above). |

Lower-priority deferred-until-pulled (operator ack 2026-05-29):
**#22** Collection bundling vocab — defer until cross-Collection grouping
demand. **LANDING-MY-DATA-GRAPH** — defer; recommend temporal heatmap
when picked up. **WIZARD-JSON-INTERPRETABLE** — defer; recommend
FileReference shape. **R8 DLR CD theming** — defer until DLR adoption
push (needs fresh design assets anyway).

## Open user decisions (blocking some items)

1. **Leaked JWTs** at `aidocs/input/input_raw.md:222` and `:360` (issuers
   `bt-au-cube2.intra.dlr.de`, `bt-au-cube3.intra.dlr.de`, sub `kreb_fl`).
   Already on `origin`. Need: rotate tokens, decide whether to redact in
   the file, and whether to rewrite history.
2. ~~**API versioning (P4)** is breaking — confirm strategy
   (`/shepard/api/v1` prefix, dual-serve, deprecation window) before
   dispatching.~~ — **resolved**: shipped non-breaking. Upstream
   `/shepard/api/...` stays byte-frozen via explicit class-level
   `@Path("/shepard/api/...")` prefix; new fork-additive endpoints land
   under `/v2/` (per `CLAUDE.md` API-version policy). User-facing URLs
   unchanged.
3. **Templates system (L3)** is large and design-heavy — needs a written
   design before implementation.
4. **Neo4J ID migration (L2)** is large and touches a lot of code — needs
   a written design before implementation.
5. **Admin role mechanism (A0)** — A3b discovered there is no admin auth
   in shepard today. JWT roles are always `new String[0]`, so
   `@RolesAllowed("admin")` denies everyone. Three paths offered:
   (a) full admin-role mechanism populating roles from OIDC claims
   (~50 lines + config), (b) dev-profile-only exposure of admin
   endpoints, (c) scope-shift admin endpoints to `/temp/admin/*` with
   the same auth as the existing `/temp/migrations/*` carve-out and
   harden later. Affects A3b, P3c.
6. **R-series items (R1–R9)** from the second-pass input analysis are
   mostly blocked on design / framework / asset decisions — see
   "Newly surfaced" table.

## Round log

### Round 1 — 2026-05-05

Dispatched two small, well-isolated agents in parallel worktrees.
Their output lands as separate commits on this branch.

| ID | Agent description | Status | Commit | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | Bounded-timeout DB connection wait with exponential backoff | done | `a74d278` | Replaces infinite loops in `MigrationsRunner.waitForConnection` and `NeoConnector`. Adds `ConnectionWaitTimeoutException`, 5-test `MigrationsRunnerTest` (deterministic via injected clock+sleeper). Spawned follow-ups A1d, A1e. |
| A4 | Caffeine-backed permission cache with TTL/LRU | done | `53996a3` | Cache was already Caffeine via `quarkus-cache`; landed per-cache TTL/max-size config + 5-test behavioural contract. Spawned follow-ups A4c (warming), A4d (metrics). |

**Round 1 outcome:** both items landed cleanly on the dispatcher branch.
A1 + A4 land 357 lines added across 7 files (5 prod, 2 test) with 9 new
unit tests, all passing. Two stale assumptions in `input_raw.md` were
corrected on the way (A1: the analysis flagged Mongo/Timescale/PostGIS
infinite waits, but only Neo4j had them in this codebase; A4: the
permission cache was already Caffeine-backed, not a basic Map).

### Round 2 — 2026-05-05

Dispatched 5 agents in parallel worktrees. P3, A1b, and A1e all sit in
the migrations / startup / health area, so cherry-pick conflicts are
possible — explicit non-overlap clauses included in each prompt:

- A1e owns `MigrationsRunner.java` (just the catch blocks).
- A1b owns health-check classes; explicitly told **not** to touch
  `MigrationsRunner` / `NeoConnector` / Neo4j common.
- P3 owns the InfluxDB→TimescaleDB migration tool (separate module);
  expected to be disjoint from A1b/A1e.

L5 (API keys) and A3 (feature toggles) are independent of all of the above.

**Round 2 outcome:** all 5 items landed on the dispatcher branch.
~3,200 lines added across ~60 files, **102 new unit tests** all passing
on the merged branch (8 `MigrationsRunner` + 21 health-check + 9 API-key
+ 2 versioning toggle + 17 migration progress + 5 from earlier round +
existing regressions). Two cherry-pick conflicts resolved manually:
- `MigrationsRunnerTest.java` (A1 vs A1e — combined test sets).
- `application.properties` (A1 vs A3 — kept both new keys).
Three stale spots in `input_raw.md` were corrected during dispatch:
- A1: only Neo4j had infinite waits, not Mongo/Timescale/PostGIS.
- A4: cache was already Caffeine, not a basic Map.
- P3: orchestrator image source lives outside this repo; only the
  COPY path was instrumentable here.

### Round 3 — 2026-05-05

Dispatched 7 agents in parallel worktrees. Most are small/additive
follow-ups from Rounds 1–2; P2 is medium; L6 is research-only.
Non-overlap clauses included in each prompt:

- A1f owns new files in `common/healthz/` only; cannot modify A1b's surface.
- A4c owns a new `PermissionsCacheWarmer` startup observer; cannot add
  public methods to `PermissionsService` (P2's territory) or modify
  cache config (A4d's territory).
- A4d owns the `metrics-enabled` config key and a smoke test only;
  cannot touch `PermissionsService` or other cache config.
- A3b owns a new `FeaturesAdminRest` resource; cannot modify A3 surface.
- A1d owns `application.properties` config additions + a new
  `aidocs/archive/17-startup-wait-audit.md`; **no source changes**.
- P2 owns the new `filterAllowedForUser` method on `PermissionsService`
  + rewiring **one** call site; cannot warm the cache or change config.
- L6 is research-only, producing `aidocs/archive/18-pagination-inventory.md`
  and an index update; **no source changes**.

|---|---|---|---|---|

Lands as `de.dlr.shepard.integrationtests.PermissionsServiceCacheMetricsIT` to match the project's actual Surefire-exclude convention (`pom.xml:333` excludes `**/integrationtests/*.java` by package, not by `*IT.java` filename). Failsafe picks it up; Surefire skips it. The `cache.gets > 0` assertion runs against the live stack via Prometheus metrics endpoint, matching `HealthzIT`'s pattern. |

Primitive landed (8 new tests; `PermissionsService.filterAllowedForUser` + `PermissionsDAO.findByEntityNeo4jIds`); call-site rewire is a noop until a downstream caller emerges. |

Symmetric to P2's `filterAllowedForUser`. Rewires `SubscriptionFilter.filter` from N-call loop to one batched permission check; reuses `permissionsDAO.findByEntityNeo4jId` (no new DAO method needed). 5 new tests + `SubscriptionFilterTest` updated; 28/28 pass on merged branch. ### Round 4 — 2026-05-05 (analysis layer)

While Round 3 implementation agents ran, four parallel **analysis-only**
agents produced strategic / design docs requested by the maintainer:

| Doc | Purpose | Status | Commit |
|---|---|---|---|
| `aidocs/platform/19-architecture-feedback.md` | Critical review of post-Round-3 architecture | done | `879ffc0` |
| `aidocs/platform/20-epic-roadmap.md` | 14-epic catalogue with Mermaid dependency graph + 2-track 6-month plan | done | `fc4ae3b` |
| `aidocs/archive/21-user-interest-gauge.md` | Demand signals for HDF5/HSDS, tabular, KG interfaces + survey plan | done | `362712e` |
| `aidocs/ops/22-admin-cli-draft.md` | Candidate functions for a future `shepard-admin` CLI | done | `452eded` |

**Key findings from these docs that should drive Round 4 priorities:**

- **`aidocs/19` flags two security fragilities at the top:**
  - **C3 fallback** at `PermissionsService.java:262+` — when the
    permissions node is missing, the fallback grants Reader+Writer+Manager.
    This is a full-access backdoor catalogued in
    `aidocs/archive/07-security-issues.md` C3 — **not new, but not yet fixed**.
  - **C5 Cypher injection** at `Neo4jQueryBuilder.java:198-244` —
    string-concatenated query construction; cross-cuts every search
    endpoint and gates `aidocs/semantics/13-search-improvements.md`.
  - Plus **A0** (admin role) — already in our backlog.
- **`aidocs/20` recommends two parallel tracks:**
  - **Foundations track:** E1 (foundations: A0 + observability + P4 + L6 + S1) → E5 (streaming + ID alignment) → E11 (permissions + admin CLI) → E2 (unified search v2).
  - **User-value track:** E12 (UX & ecosystem) → E6 (annotation generalisation) → E9 (spatial graduate/deprecate) → E4 (triplestore + SPARQL).
  - Three "could start tomorrow" epics: **E1, E5, E12**.
  - Three "blocked on user decision" epics: **E14** (Neo4j ID migration owner), **E9** (spatial graduate vs deprecate), **E4** (default ontology + reasoning profile + n10s deployment).
- **`aidocs/21` ranks the three candidate development directions:**
  - **KG interfaces:** strongest evidence (4 open GitLab issues, partial `SparqlConnector` already, two independent user groups). Ready to act once #274 unblocks.
  - **HDF5/HSDS:** low-medium; one named asker, no GitLab issue, TimescaleDB satisfies the temporal subset. **Maintainer-confirmed hard constraint (2026-05-05):** any HDF5 surface must be compatible with the existing Python ecosystem (`h5py` / `PyTables` / `pandas.read_hdf`) — `h5pyd` over HSDS is the canonical access path; "download original file" is the fallback. Recorded in epic E7 (`aidocs/platform/20-epic-roadmap.md:309-329`) and §3.6 of `aidocs/archive/21-user-interest-gauge.md`.
  - **Tabular/relational:** thin; the ask hides two distinct products (interface vs storage). Defer until separated.

No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. ## New designs landed in the 2026-05-12 batch (post-A0)

Seven design docs landed (`aidocs/52`..`aidocs/58`), each with its own
phasing. Umbrella rows below; per-doc sub-ID detail lives in the cited
aidoc. **`T1` is hereby redirected to the new `aidocs/54` shape**
(`:ShepardTemplate` first-class entity), superseding the older
`aidocs/39` hack; the old T1 sub-IDs T1a–T1h are renumbered per
`aidocs/54 §9`. **`D1` in `aidocs/58 §5` is renamed `CP1`** to avoid
colliding with the existing in-app-docs D1 series. **`AI1` in
`aidocs/58 §8` is renamed `GR1`** (GraphRAG) to avoid colliding with
the existing `aidocs/43` AI1 umbrella.

Captures POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE on 2xx by default; reads opt-in via `shepard.provenance.capture-reads=false`. Casual users see only their own rows; instance-admins see all. New config: `shepard.provenance.{enabled,capture-reads}`, `shepard.instance.id`. |
| PROV1b | Per-entity provenance trail — `GET /v2/provenance/entity/{appId}` + heuristic target-extraction in the capture filter (`TargetEntityResolver` finds the last UUID segment of the request path and maps the preceding plural to a Neo4j label kind) | user request, `aidocs/55 §6` | M | **done** | 11 new tests (TargetEntityResolverTest 10 + filter test row). PROV-O `:USED`/`:GENERATED` edges deferred to PROV1b2 — `targetAppId` on the `:Activity` is enough for the v1 query. Honours V2S1a `?profile=`. |
| PROV1c | On-demand stats aggregation + `GET /v2/provenance/stats?scope=instance\|collection\|user&id=&since=&until=` (totals + sparkline buckets + action-kind histogram + cumulative integral in one payload; auto daily-/weekly-bucket switch at 90-day windows; **single Cypher round-trip** via `ActivityDAO.aggregateStats`) | user request, `aidocs/55 §6` + user follow-up "improve metric calc perf + add cumulative integral" | M | **done** | 12 new tests. Single-pass aggregation (one Cypher → buckets + per-kind totals + distinct-agent count in Java); cumulative integral (running sum) emitted alongside buckets. CodeQL-flagged underflow risk in user-supplied since/until fixed via clamp. Pre-aggregated `:ActivityRollup` deferred to PROV1c2. |
| PROV1c2 | Pre-aggregated `:ActivityRollup` daily/weekly buckets (only if PROV1c per-request aggregation gets slow on big installs) | — | M | parked | gated on observed need; PROV1c shipped at on-demand speed. |
| PROV1c-acl | Per-Collection Read-permission gate on `GET /v2/provenance/stats?scope=collection` — non-admins must hold Read on the target Collection (admins bypass; missing Collection → 404) | `aidocs/55 §6` | S | **done** | Resolves Collection appId → OGM id via `CollectionPropertiesDAO.findCollectionIdByAppId` then `PermissionsService.isAccessTypeAllowedForUser`. Same path CP1b takes. |
| PROV1-content-stats | `contentCensus` field on `/v2/provenance/stats` — at-query-time entity counts (`dataObjects` / `fileReferences` / `timeseriesReferences` / `structuredDataReferences` / `spatialDataReferences` / `labJournalEntries`) per scope. Drives the casual-user dashboard's "what's in here" tiles next to the activity sparkline. Null for `scope=user`. | user request 2026-05-12, `aidocs/55 §5` | S-M | **done** | New `ContentCensusDAO` (single Cypher per scope; aggregates entity counts in one round-trip). 3 new tests. Byte-size totals deferred to PROV1-content-stats-2 (gated on FB1a's stored `fileSize` field). |
| PROV1-content-stats-2 | `byteTotals` field on `/v2/provenance/stats` — at-query-time byte sums by storage kind. v1 ships `fileBytes` (sum of `ShepardFile.fileSize` reachable from the scope). Pre-FB1a rows with `fileSize=null` contribute 0 to the sum — the total is a **lower bound** until those rows are re-uploaded; surfaced as a caveat on the wire schema. Null for `scope=user`. Open-shape: future keys (`timeseriesBytes`, `structuredDataBytes`) get appended without changing schema. | follows from PROV1-content-stats + FB1a-fileSize | S | **done** | New `ContentCensusDAO.byteTotalsForCollection` / `byteTotalsInstanceWide` (single Cypher per scope; `coalesce(sum(coalesce(f.fileSize, 0)), 0)` for null-tolerance). 3 new `ProvenanceStatsServiceTest` rows (collection / instance / user-null). |

| PROV1h | metadata4ing (m4i) content-negotiation on `/v2/provenance/{activities,entity/{appId},count}` — `Accept: application/ld+json` returns plain PROV-O JSON-LD; `Accept: application/ld+json; profile="https://w3id.org/nfdi4ing/metadata4ing/"` (or short `profile=metadata4ing`) returns the m4i flavour with dual `@type: ["m4i:ProcessingStep", "prov:Activity"]`, `m4i:Person` / `m4i:InvestigatedObject` subtype overlays, and `m4i:hasInput` / `m4i:hasOutput` / `m4i:hasMethod` placeholders mapped from the existing PROV-O fields. Unknown profile → RFC 7807 406 `provenance.unsupported-profile`. Plain-JSON callers see no change. Gated on ONT1b (m4i preseed). | `aidocs/64 §3.2`, `aidocs/55 §6` | M | **done** | New `ProvJsonLdRenderer` service (97% line / 85% branch) + content-neg dispatch on the three endpoints (97-100% line on the new methods). Inline `@context` (no external hosting). No new endpoints, no new config keys, no migration, no new dependencies. 36 new renderer tests (`ProvJsonLdRendererTest`) + 20 new resource tests (`ProvenanceRestJsonLdTest`). |

| CG1 | OpenAPI client generators — Kiota new baseline + OpenAPI Generator still-maintained legacy (both indefinitely) | user request, `aidocs/57`, `aidocs/63` ADR-0022 | M | **CG1a + CG1b shipped; CG1c queued** | `aidocs/57`. **Decision 2026-05-13 (ADR-0022)**: ship both. Kiota → `/v2/` shelf → `clients-v2/`; OpenAPI Generator → `/shepard/api/` shelf → existing `clients/` (behaviour-preserving). Neither on a deprecation path. Per-shelf OpenAPI emission (P4c, shipped) gives each generator its own canonical input. Hey API as a TS-only tactical secondary for the Nuxt frontend stays per `aidocs/57 §4.3`. Implementation lands as **CG1a** (Kiota baseline wiring) + **CG1b** (OpenAPI Generator legacy maintenance + dual-posture docs — shipped). |
| CG1b | OpenAPI Generator legacy maintenance — retarget `clients/{java,python,typescript}` to the `/shepard/doc/openapi/v1.json` shelf + dual-posture docs | user request, `aidocs/57 §4.2`, `aidocs/63` ADR-0022 | S-M | **done** | New build-time slicer `scripts/shepard_scripts/scripts/slice_openapi_v1.py` mirrors `V1OpenApiFilter` (Java) on the on-disk `openapi.json` to emit `openapi_v1.json` (drops `/v2/...` + platform paths; keeps every other path). New CI job `.gitlab/ci/jobs/slice-openapi-v1.yml` runs the slicer; the three language clients (`.gitlab/ci/clients/{java,python,typescript}.gitlab-ci.yml`) now `--input-spec` the sliced doc instead of the combined one. Python pipeline chains through `unrequire-attributes-for-python.yml` against the sliced doc. New regression fence `clients/tests/v1-scope-smoke-test.sh` (asserts no `/v2/` leak + positive `Collection`-canary in every generated client) runs per-language after generation. New `clients/README.md` + `clients/java/README.md` explain the "still-maintained legacy" framing; `docs/reference/api.md` adds the "Choosing a client generator" decision section. Behaviour-preserving for downstream `dlr-shepard-clients/*` consumers — same generator, same templates, same Maven coordinates; only the input spec is now v1-shelf-tree-shaken instead of combined. |

Time scrubber (historical) + SSE live mode. Semantic edge types `[:CHARACTERISES]` / `[:VALIDATES]` / `[:INSPECTS]` / `[:PRODUCED_BY]` / `[:COMPANION_TO]` / `[:LOCATES_SENSOR]`. ZLP AFP/welding/robotic data chains. SB1a gated on CAD1b; live SSE (SB1e) gated on PROV1a + sTC. |
| PC1+SB2 | Point cloud integration + live overlay modalities — `PointCloudReference`, CAD–cloud alignment, seven additional display modes | user request 2026-05-16, `aidocs/83` | L | **design done** | `aidocs/data/83-pointcloud-and-live-overlay.md`. `PointCloudReference` payload kind (PLY/E57/LAS/LAZ; Potree tiles). ICP alignment (`Open3D` sidecar, `[:ALIGNED_TO]` edge with 4×4 matrix + RMSE). Deviation map auto-HEATMAP (PC1c). Live streaming via sTC (PC1d). Seven new overlay modes: CAMERA_FRUSTUM+PiP video (SB2a), AE event cloud (SB2b), raster projection for thermography/DIC (SB2c), multi-channel timeline endpoint (SB2d), nominal-vs-actual trajectory (SB2e), FEM frame animation (SB2f), ultrasonic C-scan + interactive A-scan panel (SB2g; data spec pending). SA1 + RDK1 external tool integration notes in §5. PC1a gated on CAD1a. |
| PC-LOD1 | **Point cloud level-of-detail scheme** — large point clouds (AFP PointCloud.csv, LiDAR scans, photogrammetry) need an LOD pipeline so the viewer stays interactive at 10M+ points. Two tiers: (1) server-side tiling via Potree-compatible octree tiles (`shepard-sidecar-potree-convert`; output tiles in Garage S3 keyed by `PointCloudReference.appId`); (2) progressive streaming via `GET /v2/point-cloud-references/{appId}/tiles/{octree-path}` with viewer `screenSpaceError` param. Alternative: COPC (Cloud Optimised Point Cloud) — single-file LOD via HTTP range requests, no tiling server, lower ops complexity. Design decision required (Potree=battle-tested browser perf; COPC=lower infra burden). Quality gate: 50M-point cloud ≥30fps on integrated GPU. Plugin-first: LOD tiling logic belongs in `shepard-plugin-spatial`. | user request 2026-05-26 | M | **design done** | GH #1558. Decision: COPC Phase 1 (PC-LOD1a–c); Potree deferred Phase 2 (PC-LOD1d, `pointCount > 500M`). Sub-IDs in §4 of `aidocs/data/83-pointcloud-and-live-overlay.md §LOD Strategy`. Pairs with PC1+SB2. |
| IL1 | Managed ingest pipelines — user-definable, admin-managed hosted ingest (two-SPI: `IngestTrigger` + `FormatParser`) | user request 2026-05-16, `aidocs/83 §7` | L | **design sketch** | Provisional design in `aidocs/83 §7`; dedicated IL1 design doc not yet written. `:IngestPipeline` Neo4j entity; admin REST `/v2/admin/ingest-pipelines/`; researcher-facing `POST /v2/collections/{appId}/ingest-pipelines`. `IngestTrigger` SPI (FileSystem, S3, SA, RoboDK, Tango Controls). `FormatParser` SPI (CSV, HDF5, Ultrasonic, PointCloud, SA-native, RoboDK). IL1a = `:IngestPipeline` entity + admin REST (manual trigger only). IL1b = `UltrasonicOmniParser` + FileSystem trigger (priority first parser; gated on data spec). IL1c = CSV parser. Superset of upstream "data parsers / data importer tooling." |

Moving-frame `GeometryAnnotation` (`urdfLinkId` + `localPos*`). `DigitalTwinScene` composite entity (multi-URDF, static + dynamic transforms). WebSocket `/v2/digital-twin-scenes/{appId}/live` CBOR StatePacket (delta-encoded joints + sensors + poses + events; I-frame every 10 s). Optional `shepard-sidecar-renderer` (headless Chromium + WebRTC video; interactive via data-channel back-channel for orbit/zoom/click/scrub). | TM1 | Time Reference Model — `timeReference: enum WALL_CLOCK \| EXPERIMENT_RELATIVE` + `wallClockOffset: long` (nanoseconds epoch of t=0 in stored data) on `TimeseriesReference`. Both fields mutable via `PATCH /v2/timeseries-references/{appId}` — offset can be corrected any time a better anchor is discovered (ffprobe, SA sync signal, manual). Translation: `wall_clock_ns(sample) = wallClockOffset + sample_t_ns`. Optional `wallClockOffsetSource: String` tag ("manual" / "ffprobe" / "SA_sync" / "NTP_marker"). V-series Cypher migration backfills `timeReference="WALL_CLOCK"` on all existing rows. Enables SB2a (video+timeseries overlay), SB2d (multi-channel timeline), SB2h (SA 6D tracking sync), and DT1 snapshot timestamp alignment across sources. Sub-IDs: TM1a (model + migration + REST + backfill) + TM1b (frontend time-axis display showing both EXPERIMENT and wall-clock labels). | user request 2026-05-16 | S | **done** | TM1a **shipped** (see Round 6, line ~494): `TimeseriesReference` entity + IO + V37 backfill + V37_R rollback + `PATCH /v2/timeseries-references/{appId}` with EXPERIMENT_RELATIVE/wallClockOffset validation + 10 unit tests. TM1b **shipped** (7713dac16): `frontend/utils/wallClockTime.ts` (`formatDualTime`, `formatRelativeTime`, `formatAbsoluteTime`); `wallClockOffsetMs` prop added to `TimeseriesChart.vue`; tooltip shows "t+Xs (YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss.mmm UTC)" for EXPERIMENT_RELATIVE mode; xAxis ticks show "t+Xs" relative labels; 10 new unit tests in `tests/unit/wallClockTime.test.ts`. Note: TM1b triggers on `timeReference === EXPERIMENT_RELATIVE` (not WALL_CLOCK); WALL_CLOCK data already shows UTC timestamps natively. |
| VID2 | Camera-to-scene calibration — click-based point-correspondence UI lets a researcher click N paired points (image pixel ↔ CAD/scene world point) to solve the camera extrinsic transform via PnP (OpenCV EPNP+RANSAC; reuses the Open3D sidecar from PC1b). `POST /v2/videos/{videoAppId}/align-to/{cadAppId}` body `{ correspondences: [{imageX, imageY, worldX, worldY, worldZ}], cameraIntrinsics?: [9] }`. User can skip features that are occluded, moving, or unclear — sparse correspondences are fine (min 4, RANSAC-robust above 8). Per-correspondence residual colour feedback (green/yellow/red). Stores `[:CAMERA_ALIGNED_TO { matrix:[16], rmse, correspondenceCount, K:[9], solverAlgorithm, alignedAt }]` on `VideoStream→CadReference`. Unlocks: SB2a CAMERA_FRUSTUM mode (real camera matrix in 3D viewer), 3D annotation → video projection overlay (click annotation → highlight pixel on video), DT1 live camera viewport. Sub-IDs: VID2a (backend PnP + Neo4j edge), VID2b (frontend side-by-side picker UI), VID2c (3D viewer: frustum + projection overlay), VID2d (DT1 camera viewport). | user request 2026-05-16 | L | **design sketch** | VID2a gated on VID1a (video upload) + PC1b sidecar (Open3D). VID2b gated on CAD1b (3D viewer). VID2c extends SB2a to use the real camera matrix. Full spatial + temporal alignment = VID2 (spatial) + TM1 (temporal) + SB2a/SB2d (viewer modalities). |

| PERF2a | k6 multi-stage stress script (5 stages, ramps to 50 VUs, exercises upload + ingest + search + permissions) | user request 2026-05-12, `aidocs/59 §3.2` | M | **✓ shipped** | `scripts/perf/k6-stress.js`. 5-stage ramp (0→25→50→0 VUs, 8 min total). Exercises collections-list, search, DataObject reads, 1 KB file upload, 10-row CSV timeseries ingest. Custom Trends + thresholds per endpoint. `setup()`/`teardown()` manage throwaway fixtures. `handleSummary()` writes `last-run.json` for PERF2b recommender. |
| PERF2b | Recommender — `scripts/perf/recommend.py` reads `last-run.json` + Prometheus instant queries → 3-5 ranked suggestions | user request 2026-05-12, `aidocs/59 §4` | M | **✓ shipped** | `scripts/perf/recommend.py`. stdlib-only Python 3.9+. Six rules: `search_threshold_breach` (k6 p95 > 3000ms → Neo4j page-cache), `collections_threshold_breach` (k6 p95 > 800ms → perms-cache), `jvm_heap_too_small` (heap > 85% → -Xmx), `gc_pauses_high` (GC max > 200ms → G1GC), `permissions_cache_cold` (hit rate < 60% → cache size + TTL), `mongo_latency_high` (avg cmd > 50ms → pool size). Prometheus queries for JVM heap, GC pause, perms-cache hit ratio, Mongo latency; falls back gracefully when Prometheus is unreachable. `--json` for machine-readable output; `--output FILE`; `--dry-run` uses canned data. Exit 0 = healthy, exit 1 = suggestions found (CI-friendly), exit 2 = error. |
| PERF2c | k6 soak (5 VUs × 2 h) + Prometheus remote-write integration so k6 runs show up as annotations in Grafana | user request 2026-05-12, `aidocs/59 §3.3` | S | **✓ shipped** | `scripts/perf/k6-soak.js`. 30 s ramp + configurable soak window (`SOAK_DURATION_MINUTES`, default 120) + 30 s cooldown. Read-only workload: `/versionz`, collections list, search. Custom soak Trends + relaxed thresholds (drift detection, not latency budgets). Optional Prometheus remote-write via `K6_PROMETHEUS_RW_SERVER_URL`. `handleSummary()` writes `last-run.json`. |

| KIP1 | HMC Kernel Information Profile integration — umbrella | user request 2026-05-13, `aidocs/66` | L | **KIP1a + KIP1c + KIP1d + KIP1e + KIP1f + KIP1g + KIP1h shipped; KIP1b folded in** | `aidocs/66`. Mint a PID, attach a `:Publication` to the entity, expose the public KIP record at `/v2/.well-known/kip/{pid-suffix}`. Plugin-shaped: `Minter` SPI in core, every minter ships as a plugin (per CLAUDE.md heuristic #3); KIP1h flipped the pre-KIP1h in-core `MockMinter` to `shepard-plugin-minter-local` (renamed `LocalMinter`, versioned PIDs) and made the minter optional (resolver-only deployments are now legitimate). ePIC + DataCite ship as sibling `shepard-plugin-minter-{epic,datacite}` JARs (KIP1c/d) per ADR-0023. KIP1g extracts the resolver + KIP record JSON-LD shape into `shepard-plugin-kip` per CLAUDE.md plugin-first heuristic #2. KIP1f ships the retire endpoint. Phase 2 versioning (full `:EntityVersion` graph) queued as ENT1 umbrella. KIP1a–KIP1h. |
| KIP1a | `Minter` SPI + `MockMinter` + `MinterRegistry` + `:Publication` entity + `POST /v2/{kind}/{appId}/publish` + `GET /v2/.well-known/kip/{pid-suffix}` + `PublicationDAO` + `V29__Add_appId_constraint_Publication.cypher` + `PublishableKindRegistry` (URL-segment dispatch — adding kinds doesn't change URL shape) | user request 2026-05-13, `aidocs/66 §4-§5` | M | **shipped** | 61 new tests (`MockMinterTest` 7 + `MinterRegistryTest` 7 + `PublishableKindRegistryTest` 6 + `PublicationDAOTest` 10 + `PublishServiceTest` 8 + `PublishRestTest` 12 + `KipResolverRestTest` 6 + `PublicationIOTest` 3 + `PublicEndpointRegistryTest` +5). JaCoCo on `de.dlr.shepard.publish.*` + `de.dlr.shepard.v2.publish.*`: **94.5% line / 80.4% branch** — comfortably above the 80%/70% slice targets. KIP1a folds in the KIP1b resolver from the original phasing — the prefix-match shape in `PublicEndpointRegistry` (new `PUBLIC_PATH_PREFIXES` set + `matchesPrefix` helper with the next-char-must-be-`/` foot-gun guard) made shipping both together cheaper than spreading across two PRs. Auth: caller needs Write or Manage on the entity (`AccessType.Write` already admits both per `rolesGrantAccess`). Idempotent: second POST returns existing Publication (200, same PID); `?force=true` mints a fresh row. Active minter resolved via deploy-time `shepard.publish.minter=mock` (default `mock`) — the "cluster identity / topology" CLAUDE.md exception applies (PID-provider switch is a re-bootstrap decision). Plugin-first per CLAUDE.md + ADR-0023: ePIC + DataCite ship as drop-in plugin JARs in KIP1c/d. KIP1e (Vue Publish button + licence picker) + KIP1f (unpublish) queued. |
| KIP1c | ePIC handle service adapter — `shepard-plugin-minter-epic` JAR per ADR-0023. Config keys `shepard.publish.epic.{handle-prefix,api-url,credential-key}` + `:EpicMinterConfig` runtime singleton (admin-runtime per CLAUDE.md). Integration test against ePIC staging. | user request 2026-05-13, `aidocs/66 §5(b)` | M | **shipped** | New module `plugins/minter-epic/` mirrors the KIP1d `plugins/minter-datacite/` shape: `EpicMinterPluginManifest` discovered via `META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.plugin.PluginManifest`, JAR built with `provided`-scope backend dep, declared as a `<dependency>` in the backend's `with-plugins` Maven profile, `quarkus.index-dependency.shepard-plugin-minter-epic.*` ensures Jandex indexing. Activated when an operator flips `shepard.publish.minter=epic` after configuring credentials. Admin-runtime per CLAUDE.md: new `:EpicMinterConfig` singleton (idiom from A3b + UH1a + N1c2). Admin REST under `/v2/admin/minters/epic/` with five endpoints: `GET/PATCH /config`, `POST/DELETE /credential`, `POST /test-connection`. CLI parity: `shepard-admin minters epic {status,enable,disable,set-api-url,set-prefix,set-credential,clear-credential,test-connection}`. **Credential at rest**: AES-GCM with pepper `"shepard:KIP1c:epic:"` (distinct from KIP1d's `"shepard:KIP1d:datacite:"` for key isolation). **Mint flow**: PUT to `<apiBaseUrl>/handles/<prefix>/<uuid>` with B2HANDLE-compatible JSON body; PID = `https://hdl.handle.net/<prefix>/<uuid>`. Network hardening: 10s connect + 30s request timeout; one retry on 5xx/network error. Tests: 50+ cases across 5 unit test classes + CLI tests. Migration: `V45__Add_appId_constraint_EpicMinterConfig.cypher`. Operator runbook: `docs/reference/minter-epic.md`. |
| KIP1d | DataCite REST adapter — `shepard-plugin-minter-datacite` JAR. Config keys for DOI prefix + DataCite Member credentials + `:DataciteMinterConfig` singleton. | user request 2026-05-13, `aidocs/66 §5(c)` | M | **shipped** | This PR. New module `plugins/minter-datacite/` mirrors `plugins/minter-local/` shape: `DataciteMinterPluginManifest` discovered via `META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.plugin.PluginManifest`, JAR built with `provided`-scope backend dep, declared as a `<dependency>` in the backend's `with-plugins` Maven profile, `quarkus.index-dependency.shepard-plugin-minter-datacite.*` ensures Jandex indexing so the `DataciteMinter` `@ApplicationScoped` bean + `DataciteAdminRest` `@Path` resource are discovered at build time. Activated when an operator flips `shepard.publish.minter=datacite` after configuring credentials. Admin-runtime per CLAUDE.md: new `:DataciteMinterConfig` singleton (idiom from A3b + UH1a + N1c2 — single-instance node, `HasAppId`, seed-on-first-start from `shepard.minters.datacite.*` install defaults, runtime PATCH wins). Admin REST under `/v2/admin/minters/datacite/` with five endpoints: `GET/PATCH /config` (RFC 7396 merge-patch, `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`), `POST/DELETE /credential` (set / rotate / clear the DataCite Member password — plaintext never echoed, only first-8 hex of SHA-256 fingerprint surfaced), `POST /test-connection` (probes `<apiBaseUrl>/heartbeat` and reports reachable/statusCode/latencyMs). CLI parity: new `shepard-admin minters datacite {status,enable,disable,set-api-url,set-prefix,set-repository-id,set-publisher,set-landing-page-base,set-state,set-password,clear-password,test-connection}` subcommand group via PM1d's `AdminCliCommandProvider` SPI — twelve verbs in a `MintersCommand` → `DataciteCommand` two-level nesting that lets future minter plugins (ePIC, Crossref, …) slot in alongside without colliding on the top-level name. **`set-password` reads stdin / tty only**, never on the command line, so `ps` + shell history can't leak the credential. **Credential at rest**: AES-GCM (256-bit key derived from `shepard.instance.id` + a fixed pepper, GCM-1 prefixed base64-url-no-padding ciphertext) on `:DataciteMinterConfig.passwordCipher`; SHA-256 hex hash on `:passwordHash` (fingerprint display only). The cipher format includes a `gcm1:` prefix marker so a future cipher upgrade can recognise the legacy shape. **Versioned PIDs (KIP1h respect)**: `DataciteMinter` reads `MintRequest.versionNumber()` directly (KIP1h made it a first-class field), encodes as `version: "v<n>"` in the DataCite request body, and adds `relatedIdentifiers: [{ relationType: IsNewVersionOf, ... }]` for n > 1 — plus a back-fill `PUT /dois/<previous>` that stamps the inverse `HasVersion` so the chain renders both ways in DataCite Commons. Previous-PID lookup is delegated to a `PreviousPublicationResolver` interface that defaults to a no-op (test-friendly; production wires it to the in-core `PublicationDAO` via the manifest hook). **Network hardening**: 10s connect + 30s request timeout on the JDK `HttpClient`; one retry on 5xx / network exception with 1s backoff; `MinterException` on second failure (caught by core `PublishService` → 500 `publish.minter.failed`). 4xx is final (no retry). **Resource-type mapping**: shepard's `digitalObjectType` (Dataset / Collection / Software / Text / Image) maps onto DataCite's `resourceTypeGeneral` vocabulary with `Dataset` as the safe default. **Event mapping**: DataCite `event` field set from `defaultState` — `draft`/`registered`/`findable` → `draft`/`register`/`publish`. New tests: 126 cases across 9 classes (`DataciteMinterTest` 30, `DataciteMinterConfigServiceTest` 24, `DataciteAdminRestTest` 17, `DataciteCommandsTest` 22, `CredentialCipherTest` 12, `DataciteIoTest` 7, `MintersAdminCliCommandProviderTest` 5, `DataciteMinterConfigTest` 5, `DataciteMinterPluginManifestTest` 4). Migration: `V33__Add_appId_constraint_DataciteMinterConfig.cypher` (V31 + V32 are taken by KIP1h backfill + PM1e PluginRuntimeOverride). New deploy-time keys (install defaults that seed the singleton on first start): `shepard.minters.datacite.{enabled,api-base-url,handle-prefix,repository-id,publisher,landing-page-base,default-state}`. Password is NOT a deploy-time key (security posture; gitleaks would flag it). Default `api-base-url=https://api.test.datacite.org` (Fabrica test environment). Operator runbook: `docs/reference/minter-datacite.md`. SpotBugs + findsecbugs clean; backend test suite stays at 2350 (no in-tree code change beyond the application.properties install-default keys + the `with-plugins` profile dep block + the index-dependency declaration — the plugin's 126 new tests live in the plugin module). |
| KIP1e | Vue "Publish" button on Collection / DataObject panes + licence picker (drop-down sourcing from a tracked SPDX list) + post-publish snackbar showing the resolver URL. | user request 2026-05-13, `aidocs/66 §7` | S | **shipped** | This PR. `PublishButton.vue` + `PublishModal.vue` under `frontend/components/context/publish/`; `usePublishEntity` composable wraps the `POST /v2/{kind}/{appId}/publish` endpoint via raw `fetch` (the `@dlr-shepard/backend-client` regeneration for KIP1a hasn't landed yet; the wire shape is stable). Snackbar surfaces the freshly-minted PID with "Copy resolver URL" + "Copy PID" actions. Scope-downs: (1) **existing-publication state display deferred** — KIP1a has no side-channel "fetch current publication" endpoint and `BasicEntityIO` is frozen, so the button reads "Publish" in all states and relies on POST-idempotency to surface the existing PID; the popover + "Re-mint (force)" affordance becomes a follow-up slice when a publications-listing endpoint (or v2 entity-detail shape) exists. (2) **Licence wire-shape deferred** — KIP1a's POST endpoint takes no body, so the SPDX drop-down is informational today; the modal's `submit` event already carries the selected licence id, so when the backend grows a body field the wire-up is one prop forward. (3) **i18n** not wired (Nuxt app has no `vue-i18n`); strings are inlined English. (4) **No frontend test suite exists** (no `*.spec.ts`, no `playwright.config.ts`, no Storybook); ESLint + `nuxt typecheck` clean on the new files (one pre-existing TS error in `utils/auth.ts` unrelated to this slice). |
| KIP1f | `DELETE /v2/{kind}/{appId}/publish` — retain the `:Publication` row (KIP records are append-only by convention) but mark the entity's most-recent Publication as retired (`digitalObjectMutability: "retired"` flag on the row). | user request 2026-05-13, `aidocs/66 §7` | S | **shipped** | Retire semantics confirmed: "retired" is a shepard-extension value (standard vocabulary is `mutable`/`fixed`/`immutable`; "retired" means "no longer the operator's intent" without implying the bit-stream changed). `Publication` entity grows additive nullable `digitalObjectMutability` property (no Neo4j migration — additive nullable). `PublicationDAO.retireMostRecent(entityAppId)` — Cypher `MATCH ... ORDER BY mintedAt DESC LIMIT 1 SET digitalObjectMutability='retired' RETURN count(p) AS n` — returns boolean for 404 detection without a double round-trip. `PublishService.retire(kind, appId)` maintains layering (REST → Service → DAO). `DELETE /v2/{kind}/{appId}/publish` mirrors POST's auth gate (Write on entity), returns 204 on success, 404+problem-json `publish.publication.not-found` when no Publication exists. Idempotent — second DELETE on an already-retired row returns 204. `PublicationIO` grows additive `digitalObjectMutability` field (null → absent from JSON via `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)`; "retired" → present). 20 new tests across 4 classes (`PublicationDAOTest` +5, `PublishServiceTest` +5, `PublishRestTest` +8, `PublicationIOTest` +2). No Neo4j migration needed. `docs/reference/publish-and-pids.md` updated. `aidocs/34` + `aidocs/44` updated. |
| KIP1g | Move `KipResolverRest` + KIP record JSON-LD shape (`KipRecordIO`) into a new `plugins/kip/` plugin module (`shepard-plugin-kip`). The Minter SPI seam, `:Publication` entity, generic `POST /v2/{kind}/{appId}/publish` orchestration and `PublicationIO` stay in core. The `.well-known/kip` public-prefix entry stays registered in `PublicEndpointRegistry` (core) for the auth filter's benefit. | CLAUDE.md plugin-first heuristic #2 ("external integrations → plugin shape"), `aidocs/66`, `aidocs/68 §3` | S | **shipped** | This PR. The endpoint path (`GET /v2/.well-known/kip/{pid-suffix}`), JSON-LD response shape, and RFC 7807 problem responses are **byte-identical** to pre-KIP1g; only the source location moved. New module mirrors `plugins/unhide/` (UH1a + PM1a phase 3): `KipPluginManifest` discovered via `META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.plugin.PluginManifest`, JAR built with `provided`-scope backend dep, declared as a `<dependency>` in the backend's default-active `with-plugins` Maven profile, baked into `/deployments/plugins/` for operator visibility. Index-dep declaration in `application.properties` (`quarkus.index-dependency.shepard-plugin-kip.*`) lets Quarkus's build-time CDI scanner pick up `@RequestScoped` + `@Path` on `KipResolverRest`. CI workflows (`backend-ci.yml` + `build-images.yml`) extended with a third install step for the kip plugin (same two-pass dance as UH1a). 6 `KipResolverRestTest` cases moved to the plugin (counts: backend 2266 + plugin-kip 6 = 2272 total, identical to pre-KIP1g). The package-private `absoluteUrl` helper that the resolver depended on in `PublishRest` is inlined into the resolver rather than promoted to a public core API (keeps the in-core publish orchestration free to refactor). Plugin manifest stays bare (id + version + shepardCompatibility only) per PM1c parallel awareness — PM1c is enriching `PluginManifest` with title/description default methods and KIP1g intentionally doesn't fight that. **Follow-up:** introduce `PluginContext.registerPublicPrefix(String)` so plugins self-declare their public-prefix contributions instead of the core registry hard-coding them (left out of KIP1g to avoid extending PM1a's `PluginContext` SPI mid-PR). |
| KIP1h | Rename `MockMinter` → `LocalMinter` and extract as `plugins/minter-local/` (strict plugin shape per the user's "MockMinter is a plugin" directive + CLAUDE.md heuristic #3). Relax `MinterRegistry` fail-fast to optional posture (no minter wired → 503 RFC 7807 `publish.minter.not-installed`; resolver still works against pre-existing `:Publication` rows). Ship **versioned PIDs Phase 1** — local PID format `shepard:<instance.id>:<kind>:<appId>:v<n>` (was `mock:shepard:<kind>:<appId>:<epoch-millis>`); `:Publication` grows a `versionNumber` property; `?force=true` bumps the version instead of stamping a fresh epoch. V31 backfill stamps `versionNumber=1` on every legacy row. | user request 2026-05-13 | M | **shipped** | This PR. New module `plugins/minter-local/` mirrors `plugins/kip/` shape: `LocalMinterPluginManifest` discovered via `META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.plugin.PluginManifest`, JAR built with `provided`-scope backend dep, declared as a `<dependency>` in the backend's `with-plugins` Maven profile, `quarkus.index-dependency.shepard-plugin-minter-local.*` ensures Jandex indexing so CDI scans the `@ApplicationScoped` bean. CI workflows (`backend-ci.yml` + `build-images.yml`) extended with a fourth install step. **PID-format change is BREAKING for the wire** but **non-destructive for the graph**: V31 backfill only stamps `versionNumber=1` on existing `:Publication` rows; the legacy `mock:` PIDs are preserved verbatim and keep resolving cleanly via the KIP resolver (the resolver does an opaque `findByPid`, the format is irrelevant). Operators must change `shepard.publish.minter=mock` → `=local` in `application.properties` / env (`SHEPARD_PUBLISH_MINTER=local`) before upgrade. New code: `LocalMinter` (12 tests, instance-id namespacing + version segments + stability + null/blank guards + backwards-compat factory); core SPI additions (`MintRequest.versionNumber`, `MinterNotInstalledException`, `MinterRegistry` Optional return, `Publication.versionNumber`); `PublicationDAO.findLatestVersionNumber` (6 cases); `PublishService` consults `findLatestVersionNumber + 1` before minting (5 cases including 503 + version-bump on force); `PublicationIO` + `KipRecordIO` grow `versionNumber` / `digitalObjectVersion` (5 + 2 additive cases). Test counts: backend 2266 → 2322 (+56 net after deleting 7 `MockMinterTest` cases), plugin-kip 6 → 8 (+2), plugin-minter-local 12 (new). Total new test cases ≥ 70, well above the ≥ 15-20 spec target. SpotBugs + findsecbugs clean; coverage gate met. Phase 2 (`:EntityVersion` graph) queued as ENT1 umbrella. `shepard.publish.minter` default flipped from `mock` to `local`; `shepard.publish.minter=none` is now a sentinel that explicitly disables publish (resolver still works). `shepard.instance.id` reused as the `<instance.id>` segment of every local PID — fallback to `local` with a startup WARN guides operators to a namespaced value. |
| ENT1 | Full `:EntityVersion` graph (Phase 2 of versioned PIDs) — umbrella | KIP1h Phase 1 follow-up | L | queued | KIP1h shipped Phase 1 (`Publication.versionNumber` scalar + `:Publication`-count-based version derivation; resolver surfaces `digitalObjectVersion: "v<n>"`). Phase 2 introduces a first-class `:EntityVersion` node with `versionOf` / `previousVersion` / `nextVersion` edges, lets a Publication cite the specific `:EntityVersion` it captured (rather than the entity's "current" snapshot), and surfaces `m4i:hasIdentifier` `isNewVersionOf` semantics in the KIP record. Wire-shape compatible with Phase 1 — the `versionNumber` scalar persists as a denormalised read-side optimisation. Triggered when (a) KIP1d (DataCite DOIs) needs `isNewVersionOf` for re-publish, or (b) an operator demands "show me what changed in this Collection between v2 and v3" UI affordances. GH #1544. |
| UH1 | Helmholtz Unhide publish plugin (umbrella) | user request 2026-05-13, `aidocs/67` | M-L | **UH1a + UH1b + UH1c + UH1d + UH1e shipped** | `aidocs/67`. First `shepard-plugin-*` module ever. Exposes `/v2/unhide/feed.jsonld` (schema.org + metadata4ing JSON-LD) for the Helmholtz Knowledge Graph (HKG / Unhide) harvester. Admin-configurable at runtime per the CLAUDE.md "operator knobs" rule: `:UnhideConfig` singleton + `/v2/admin/unhide/config` GET/PATCH + `/v2/admin/unhide/harvest-key/{rotate,revoke}` + `shepard-admin unhide ...` CLI parity. Cites ADR-0023 (drop-in JARs via ServiceLoader). UH1a ships as a real `shepard-plugin-unhide-${revision}.jar` post-PM1a phase 3 (the PluginManifest SPI flip removed the build-helper source-stitch). |
| UH1a | `shepard-plugin-unhide` module skeleton + `:UnhideConfig` singleton (Neo4j + V30 constraint + first-start seed) + admin REST (GET/PATCH `/v2/admin/unhide/config`, POST `/harvest-key/{rotate,revoke}`, DELETE `/harvest-key`) + feed REST (GET `/v2/unhide/feed.jsonld` schema.org + metadata4ing) + CLI parity (`shepard-admin unhide {status,enable,disable,set-feed-public,set-contact-email,rotate-harvest-key,revoke-harvest-key}`) + RFC 7807 envelopes (`unhide.feed.disabled` 503, `unhide.harvest-key.absent` 401, `unhide.config.read-only-field` 400) | user request 2026-05-13, `aidocs/67 §5–§6` | M | **shipped** | This PR (initially) + PM1a phase 3 (flipped to drop-in JAR). Plugin ships as `shepard-plugin-unhide-${revision}.jar`; backend pulls it via the `with-plugins` Maven profile (so Quarkus's build-time CDI scanner picks the `@ApplicationScoped` beans + `@Path` resources). Harvest-key plaintext returned exactly once at mint, never logged, never enters `:Activity` (PROV1a's `ProvenanceCaptureFilter` captures request method/path/status only). Auth-fallback simplification (call-out): the design's "OR instance-admin role" private-feed fallback is omitted in Phase 1 — the harvest key is the sole non-public auth path, operationally cleaner. Phase 1 feed entries: every non-deleted Collection (per-Collection `publishToHelmholtzKG` toggle deferred to UH1d). 53 plugin-unit + 12 CLI + 14 PublicEndpointRegistry tests passing locally. V30 (V29 taken by sibling slice KIP1a; constraint-only migrations are rebasable). |
| UH1b | Extend feed body with metadata4ing provenance fragments — `m4i:hasProcessingStep` array inlines the most-recent N `:Activity` rows (default window 5) targeting each Collection, rendered via the PROV1h `ProvJsonLdRenderer.renderActivityAsM4iNode(...)` entry point so the m4i shape stays in one place | user request, `aidocs/67 §9` | S | **shipped** | Commits `3b1b4cf4` (production) + tests + trackers + docs. 12 new `ProvJsonLdRendererTest` cases on the per-activity renderer plus expanded `UnhideFeedServiceTest` coverage of the `buildProcessingSteps` path (window clamping, empty/null Collection appId, DAO-failure fail-soft, sort-order preserved from `ActivityDAO`). Deploy-time-only knob `shepard.unhide.feed.provenance-window` (default 5, hard cap 100) — buffer-sizing exception per CLAUDE.md "operator knobs" rule (no operator demand for per-window runtime tuning). Collections without activities omit the field entirely (absent-key, not `[]` or `null`). PROV1h dependency on main (#1101). |
| UH1c | Feed cites KIP record (PID + locator) per entity — adds `schema:identifier` (schema.org `PropertyValue` with `propertyID="pid"`) + `schema:url` (the `/v2/.well-known/kip/{pid-suffix}` resolver URL) + `m4i:hasIdentifier` (the same PID, m4i-flavoured) per published Collection | user request, `aidocs/67 §9` | S | **shipped** | Commit `febbdd45` (production) + tests + trackers + docs. Reuses `PublicationDAO.findByEntityAppId` from KIP1a; plugin never reads `:Publication` nodes directly. Most-recent Publication wins by explicit `max(mintedAt)` (defends against future DAO sort-order changes). DAO failure logs + omits fields (fail-soft, same posture as UH1b's provenance fetch). Collections without a Publication omit all three fields entirely (schema.org "no identifier" semantics). KIP1a dependency on main (#1102). |
| UH1d | Per-Collection "Publish to Helmholtz KG" toggle in UI + admin `/admin` page tile for instance-wide toggles (mirrors A3b1 layout) | user request, `aidocs/67 §9` | S-M | **shipped** | This PR. `CollectionProperties.publishToHelmholtzKG` boolean (default `true`, opt-out). No Neo4j migration needed — additive schema-less property; null on legacy nodes treated as `true` by the feed filter. `CollectionPropertiesDAO.ensureFor` Cypher extended to seed the field on CREATE. `CollectionPropertiesIO` + `PatchCollectionPropertiesIO` expose the field; `CollectionPropertiesRest.patch` applies it. `UnhideFeedService` injects `CollectionPropertiesDAO` and filters out Collections where `publishToHelmholtzKG=false` (absent node → include). Frontend: new `useCollectionProperties.ts` composable (raw fetch, no generated client yet) + `CollectionPropertiesPane.vue` component with a `v-switch` wired to the toggle, surfaced as an "Publishing" `<ExpansionPanelItem>` on the Collection detail page (gated on `isAllowedToEditCollection`). 4 new `UnhideFeedServiceTest` cases (toggle-false excluded, absent-node included, explicit-true included, pagination counts filtered set). 1 new `CollectionPropertiesRestTest` case (`patchUpdatesPublishToHelmholtzKG`). 1 new `CollectionPropertiesIOTest` case + updated existing cases. DAOs query `DataObject -[:has_reference]->()-[:has_payload]-> Container` with `RETURN DISTINCT do`. Services gate behind `getContainer(id)` read-permission check. ### MCPGW — 2026-05-22 (MCP gateway as sync SSOT)

User directive 2026-05-22: *"move syncservice into mcpgateway … config ui? complete sync — mcpgateway explicit source of truth. additional services on lobe require explicit confirmation (one time on gui)"*.

Consolidates sync into the MCP gateway plugin (`shepard-plugin-mcp`) and gives operators an explicit confirmation gate when client-side LLM hosts (LobeChat, Claude Desktop, etc.) request to add new shepard-backed services.

| ID | Slice | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| MCPGW-SYNC1 | Move existing SyncService into `shepard-plugin-mcp`; the MCP gateway becomes the explicit source of truth for what's syncable + what each client has subscribed to. Remove the separate SyncService deployment surface. | M | queued | Cross-cutting refactor — SyncService likely currently lives outside the plugin module. Need to enumerate which collections/containers it sync-watches today + map to the MCP gateway's resource model. Depends on shepard-plugin-mcp being the canonical surface (MCP1d already shipped per task #30). |
| MCPGW-SYNC2 | Config UI for sync: list of services + their sync state + add/remove buttons. Lives under `/admin/mcp/sync` (instance-admin only) and `/me/mcp` (per-user subscriptions). | M | queued | Mirrors A3b1 admin-tile layout. Reuse the `:McpConfig` singleton if one exists (or stand one up per the A3b/N1c2/UH1a pattern). |
| MCPGW-SYNC3 | "Additional services on lobe require explicit confirmation" — when LobeChat / Claude Desktop / any client requests a new service binding via the MCP `tools/discover` flow, the gateway holds the request in a `pending-approval` state. An operator clicks **Confirm once** on the GUI (per-client, one-time) and the service is then exposed thereafter. | M | queued | Anti-abuse posture: a compromised LobeChat instance cannot silently expand the surface area it can reach. One confirmation per client, persisted in a `:McpClientApproval` Neo4j node. Audit-logged via PROV1a. |

### ID-MIG — 2026-05-23 (appId → shepardId transition migration — operator story)

User directive 2026-05-23: *"backlog appId to shepardId transition migration"*. Pre-existing context (consulted per `feedback_backlog_consult_context.md`):

- **Design done:** `aidocs/platform/25-neo4j-id-migration-design.md` (L2 chain), `aidocs/platform/87-timeseries-appid-migration.md` (TS variant), `aidocs/platform/91-appid-uri-scheme.md` (URI scheme).
- **Code-rename rule:** `feedback_appid_to_shepardid.md` in agent memory — single coordinated rename pass, NOT ad-hoc.
- **Shipped:** L2a (`fec7979`) additive `appId` property; L2b (`796bc11`) backfill; L2c (`f3ca003`) Cypher rewrites. L2d (v2 surface exposes appId) **gated on H4** (now shipped — L2d ready to land).
- **Sibling backlog rows:** task #58 (TS 5-tuple→shepardId), task #123 (Java internal rename, deferred until SHACL substrate lands), task #125 (rename PRs 3-10 sweep).

The rows below cover what's **missing** — the operator-facing transition story (deprecation window, dual-emit, comms, migration tooling).

| ID | Slice | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ID-MIG1 | **Dual-emit window** — every `/v2/` response that today emits `appId` also emits `shepardId` (same UUIDv7 value, both keys present). Both are accepted on inbound. Compat surface `/shepard/api/...` stays appId-only (byte-frozen per CLAUDE.md). Adds a `Deprecation: true` + `Sunset: <date>` header on responses still using `appId`. | M | queued | Builds on L2d. Cite `aidocs/91 §URI-scheme` for the canonical `shepard:<instance>:<kind>:<id>` resolution. Dual-emit is the bridge before ID-MIG2. |
| ID-MIG2 | **Code-side rename (the field, not the value)** — Java field/method/JSON-name pass `appId` → `shepardId` across backend + frontend + MCP + generated clients. SAME UUIDv7 value; just the property name moves. Bundle into ONE coordinated PR train per `feedback_appid_to_shepardid.md`. Cite #123 + #125 as the pre-existing rows. | XL | queued | Single coordinated pass. Pre-conditions: ID-MIG1 dual-emit shipped + L2d shipped + SHACL substrate split decision made (`feedback_shacl_single_source_of_truth.md`). Do NOT start this before SHACL stabilises or the rename happens on top of a moving target. |
| ID-MIG3 | **`shepard-admin migrate-appid` CLI verb** — operator-side data export / re-key tool. Reads a Collection's full graph, re-emits with `shepardId`-only JSON, optional dry-run + diff against the original. Useful for operators with downstream consumers (analysts, Jupyter notebooks, custom dashboards) that read responses verbatim — gives them a clean upgrade path. | M | queued | Mirrors A0 admin-CLI shape. Not strictly needed for the wire migration (dual-emit covers that) but reduces operator anxiety. |

Banner on GitHub Pages site and release-note ladder deferred to ID-MIG1 dual-emit ship. |

### DB-OPT — 2026-05-23 (database optimisation against real-world data — POST-IMPORT)

User directive 2026-05-23: *"after import we should do the database optimisation case we have real word data"*. The MFFD ingest is about to populate the multi-substrate stack with the first **real-world-scale** dataset (≈8k DataObjects + their reference graph + tapelaying TS + bridgewelding TS). That's the perfect baseline for benchmark-driven optimisation. Sequencing: import lands → snapshot the post-import state → run the DB-OPT pass against real data → ship the wins. Builds on `### DB — 2026-05-22` inventory + anti-pattern hunt; this section is the **action layer**.

| ID | Slice | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| DB-OPT1 | **Capture the post-import baseline** — once MFFD ingest completes, snapshot every store: Neo4j node + relationship counts per label, Postgres table sizes + index hit-rates, TimescaleDB chunk count + compression ratio, MongoDB collection sizes, PostGIS geometry counts, Garage bucket size. Output: `aidocs/agent-findings/db-baseline-post-mffd.md`. This is the reference point every optimisation is measured against. | S | **done** (doc `db-baseline-post-mffd.md`, 2026-05-26) | Blocks DB-OPT2..5. Should run automatically as the final step of the import script's `--verify-imported` flag in a future v15.3. |
| DB-OPT2 | **Hot-path query analysis** — `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` every Postgres query the backend issues on `/v2/collections/{appId}`, `/v2/timeseries-containers/{appId}/timeseries`, `/v2/collections/{appId}/data-objects` against the real MFFD data. Same for Neo4j `PROFILE` of the Cypher analogs. Output: ranked list of queries by latency + plan + suggested index. Wins land as a `V##__add_index_hot_path.{sql,cypher}` migration. | M | **done** (2026-05-26) | Shipped V75 (`Activity.startedAtMillis` RANGE — 593,637 → O(log n) DB hits) + V76 (`BasicEntity.appId` RANGE — 21,571 → 12 DB hits per auth'd request, 1,800× win on permissions hot path). No Postgres migrations needed — TimescaleDB chunk indexes + existing compound index on `channel_metadata` already cover hot paths. Findings: `aidocs/agent-findings/db-opt2-hot-path-analysis.md`. |
| DB-OPT3 | **Timeseries-specific tuning** — TimescaleDB chunk interval vs MFFD's actual ingest cadence (likely 1ms TCP thermal trail → micro-batches), compression policy on chunks older than N days, continuous-aggregate candidates for the dashboard endpoints. | M | **done** (2026-05-26) | Chunk interval (1h, V1.13.0) and compression threshold (7d, V1.8.0) are correct — 23.5× ratio measured on 132M-row MFFD dataset. Two CAgg bugs fixed and shipped in `V1.17.0__fix_cagg_integer_now_and_backfill.sql`: (1) CAgg compression job 1046 was failing since V1.12.1 (`integer_now` not set on materialized hypertable); (2) full history never backfilled (CAgg created WITH NO DATA + 25h refresh policy). After V1.17.0: CAgg has 32 534 rows, LTTB hot path is 0.4 ms (775× vs 148 ms TS-OPT1). Findings: `aidocs/agent-findings/db-opt3-timescaledb-tuning.md`. |
| DB-OPT4 | **Anti-pattern remediation pass** — the DB-AP1 / DB-AP2 hunt rows in the `### DB —` section produced findings; this row ships the fixes on the real data. Per-substrate: rebuild missing indices, drop dead ones, fix dual-source-of-truth fields, add TTLs where data is ephemeral. | L | queued | Depends on DB-AP1+AP2 completing. Real data may surface different priorities than the synthetic baseline did. |
| DB-OPT5 | **Frontend payload diet** — once query latency is honest, the next round of wins is on response-shape. Use the actual `/v2/collections/{appId}/data-objects` payload size on MFFD-Dropbox (≈8k DOs × ~2 KB each = 16 MB) and figure out which fields the frontend actually reads. Slim the response by `?fields=` or default-trim heavy nested objects. | M | **done** (2026-05-28) | Shipped: `?fields=foo,bar` flat-CSV allow-list (GitHub REST convention) + default-trim that drops `description`, `attributes`, and the three deprecated `int` sibling counts. `?include=full` opts back in. `id`/`appId`/`name` always present as identity. Unknown field → 400 before any DB hit. New `X-Shepard-Payload-Diet` response header surfaces the active mode. Per-request `ObjectMapper.copy()` + `SimpleBeanPropertyFilter` with the IO annotated `@JsonFilter("doListV2")`; allow-list reflected from the class hierarchy. Frontend `usePagedDataObjects` composable sends the panel-shaped 13-field allow-list. Measured: single-DO synthetic payload (600-char description + 12-attr) → default-trim 75.8% smaller than `?include=full`, panel `?fields=` 86.6% smaller, minimal-fields 93.8% smaller. 5-DO page end-to-end → default-trim 77.8% smaller. 10 + 8 new unit tests. Diagnostic: `examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/diagnostics/measure-payload-diet.sh`. See `aidocs/34` DB-OPT5, `aidocs/44` row, `docs/reference/data-objects.md`. Follow-up: DB-OPT5-NESTED for dotted-path field selection (e.g. `attributes.bench`) — see `aidocs/16` DB-OPT5-NESTED row below. |
| DB-OPT5-NESTED | **Dotted-path field selection on `?fields=`** — follow-up to DB-OPT5. Current implementation does flat top-level fields only; some callers may want `attributes.bench,attributes.propellant` to keep only specific map keys instead of opting all of `attributes` back in via `?include=full`. Trade-off: extra Jackson plumbing (`PropertyFilter` + dotted-path tokenizer) for what's currently a rare need. Default trim already drops the heavy nested maps so this is genuinely an optional capability. | S | queued | Not blocking. Surface when a real caller asks. |

Templated `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` queries + interpretation cheat-sheet. | S | **done** (2026-05-26) | Shipped: `docs/ops/db-optimisation-runbook.md`. Mirrors `docs/ops/garage-activation-runbook.md` shape. |

### BATCH-API — 2026-05-23 (bulk endpoints for metadata-heavy passes)

User directive 2026-05-23 after v16 deploy block landed: *"backlog batch API"*. v16's Pass 1 + Pass 2 issue tens of thousands of individual POST/PATCH calls (one per source DO) — the metadata work that's cheap individually but expensive in aggregate. v16's 3-pass design is mainly bottlenecked here, not at the per-DO file/TS/SD write level.

A batch API on the dest reduces N round-trips to N/B (B = batch size, e.g. 100). For an MFFD-scale ingest with ~30K source DOs this is the difference between 30K HTTP calls and 300 — likely **10-30× speedup on Pass 1 + Pass 2** combined.

| ID | Slice | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| BATCH-API-1 | **Design + spec** — endpoints under `/v2/` (the fork's development surface): `POST /v2/collections/{appId}/data-objects:batch` (bulk create, body = array of DataObjectIO with name/desc/attrs/parentAppId/predecessorAppIds, transactional or best-effort per `?mode=`); `PATCH /v2/collections/{appId}/data-objects:batch` (bulk update); same shape for `/v2/structured-data-containers/{appId}/payloads:batch` (bulk SD writes for tight ref groups). Body cap configurable (e.g. 1000 items / 10MB whichever smaller). Response: per-item `{index, id, appId, status, error?}`. | M | **done** | Shipped ahead-of-row via MFFD-BATCH-01 (commit `82f37fc1e`). `POST /v2/data-objects/batch` at `DataObjectBatchV2Rest`, MAX_BATCH_SIZE=500, HTTP 207, per-item permission memoization, 562-line test suite. |
| BATCH-API-2 | **Backend implementation** — Quarkus REST resource + service. Transaction shape per `?mode=`: `mode=atomic` rollback on any failure; `mode=besteffort` partial success returns per-item status (default). Reuses existing OGM + persistence logic; just amortizes session+transaction cost across N items. Optimistic-locking for PATCH (revision check per item). Permission check applies per item. | L | **done** | Shipped ahead-of-row via MFFD-BATCH-01 (commit `82f37fc1e`). Best-effort (207 partial) is the shipped mode; atomic-rollback noted as `@Disabled` placeholder in tests pending future slice. |
| BATCH-API-3 | **Importer adoption** — v16.5 importer uses batch endpoints when available. Pass 1: chunk all_src into batches of 100, POST `/v2/data-objects/batch`; map src_do_id → dest_appId from response array; resolve ogm id via GET for Pass 2/3 compatibility. Pass 2 stays per-DO PATCH (no batch PATCH yet). Auto-fallback to per-DO on 404. `--use-batch-api`/`--no-batch-api` flags. Telemetry: `batch_pass1_start`, `batch_pass1_chunk_success`, `batch_pass1_complete`. | M | **done** | Shipped in `mffd-import-v15.py` v16.5 (commit on main). BATCH-API-5 tests in `tests/test_v16_batch.py` (7 tests). |
| BATCH-API-4 | **Per-substrate batch primitives audit** — see if Mongo `bulkWrite`, PostgreSQL `INSERT INTO ... VALUES (...), (...), ...`, TimescaleDB `COPY`, Neo4j `UNWIND` are being used optimally underneath BATCH-API-2's per-item loop. Eke out the last 2-3× by collapsing the inner loop into a single substrate-native bulk op. | L | **done** (2026-05-27) | Findings: `aidocs/agent-findings/batch-api-audit-2026-05-27.md`. Key findings: (1) MongoDB has zero `insertMany`/`bulkWrite` calls — highest headroom substrate; (2) Neo4j DO batch endpoint is sequential OGM loop — UNWIND write path not wired (pattern proven in DAO reads); (3) BulkTrace* IO classes are dead code (`/channels/bulk` path in javadoc, no `@Path` wires it); (4) No batch presigned URLs; (5) No batch annotation create. Priority queue in findings doc. New backlog: DEADCODE-BULKTRACE (see below). |
| BATCH-API-5 | **Tests** — `DataObjectBatchV2RestTest.java`: added 4 tests (atomic-mode `@Disabled` placeholder, oversized-batch-returns-400 documents actual behavior vs spec's 413, duplicate-names-no-constraint, all-failed-still-returns-207). Importer: `tests/test_v16_batch.py` (7 tests) covering chunking 250→3×(100+100+50), fallback on 404, happy path, partial failure, resume skip, `--no-batch-api`, `_batch_supported=False` pre-set. | M | **done** | Shipped. Backend: 19 tests (1 `@Disabled`), 0 failures. Python: 7 tests, 0 failures. |

**Acceptance**: re-running a 30K-DO MFFD import with BATCH-API in place completes Pass 1 + Pass 2 in <5 min (vs ~50 min today). Pass 3 unchanged (workers=4 worker bound stays the bottleneck for payload-heavy work).

| ID | Slice | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| DEADCODE-BULKTRACE | **Remove orphaned BulkTrace IO classes** — `BulkTraceRequestIO`, `BulkTraceResultIO`, `BulkTraceChannelIO` all carry javadoc referencing `POST /v2/timeseries-containers/{id}/channels/bulk` but no `@Path` annotation wires this path. The shipped bulk read endpoint is `/channels/data/bulk` using `BulkChannelDataRequestIO`. Delete or wire. Confirmed by `grep -rn 'channels/bulk' backend/src/main/java/` returning only javadoc strings. | XS | **done** (2026-05-27) | All three files deleted; dead imports removed from `TimeseriesContainerChannelsRest.java`. Surfaced by BATCH-API-4 audit (2026-05-27). |

### IMPORT-PERMS — 2026-06-17 (importers must seed :Permissions at create time)

| ID | Item | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| IMPORT-PERMS-1 | **MFFD importers create entities without a `:has_permissions` edge.** The 2026-06-17 MFFD import left **27,323 orphan `BasicEntity` nodes** (10,282 DataObjects + 4,696 SingletonFileReferences + 1,031 StructuredDataReferences + ~11k Activities/annotations) with **no Permissions**, plus ~3,108 created with `Private` admin-owned perms. Consequence: (a) the `OrphanPermissionsBackfillContext` startup guard **aborts the backend** on the next restart if `shepard.permissions.default-owner` is unset (V14 backfill only runs once — it ran on the empty DB during the reset, so it never covers post-reset imports); (b) non-admin users get 403 on imported content until perms are manually set. | M | **bug — needs fix** | Root cause: the importers (esp. v1-path bridge/wiki: `/shepard/api/collections/{}/dataObjects`, structured-data v1 trio) and the v2 bulk DataObject/reference create paths do **not** call `PermissionsService.createPermissions` at create time — same defect class as BUG-148/#207 but for the import code paths. Fix: every importer seeds a `:Permissions` (owner = import user, type per a `--permission-type` flag defaulting to `Private` or `PublicReadable`) on each created DataObject/reference/container; OR the backend create paths seed it server-side (preferred — closes it for all callers). **Workaround applied 2026-06-17** (manual, not committed to code): re-set `SHEPARD_PERMISSIONS_DEFAULT_OWNER=7eead942` to clear the boot guard + Cypher backfill of all 16,013 orphans + 3,102 StructuredDataContainers + 6 collections → `PublicReadable` owned_by admin (`legacyBackfill='pubread-2026-06-17'`). Future imports will recreate the orphan situation until the create-time seeding lands. Refs: `OrphanPermissionsBackfillContext`, `V14__Backfill_orphan_permissions.cypher`, `PermissionsService.createPermissions`, BUG-148/#207. |

| ID | Item | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|

Reuse `plugins/spatial-importer/cli/linescan.py` for the PNG→trace decode. Keep the 40 GB disk-floor guard. ### V16-PRESERVE-HIERARCHY — 2026-05-23 (replace v15's flat shape with full source-DO tree replication)

User directive 2026-05-23 after v15 hit steady-state ingest: *"so under one DO many DOs could be and then another... DOs to the bottom — along the way references ... is the script doing this"* + decision **B** when offered the trade-off. v15 flattens cube3's hierarchical DO tree (Step → Layer → Ply Group → Track) into a single step DO with thousands of refs whose names encode the source-DO identity. v16 ships the proper-fidelity design: replicate the source tree as actual dest DOs with parent/child + predecessor/successor edges + per-DO attributes.

**Fidelity gaps v15 has that v16 closes**:

- Source DO tree → flattened to single step DO (loses Layer / Ply Group / Track structure)
- Per-source-DO attributes → only step DO carries attrs (loses `layer_index`, `ply_orientation`, `track_idx`, `material_batch`, etc.)
- Per-source-DO predecessor/successor edges → not replicated (Track N→N+1 chain lost)
- Per-source-DO timestamps → only step DO carries import-time stamp

**What stays from v15**:

- Predefined SD containers (645000 + 645003) — these are SHARED across all leaf DOs of a step. Each leaf's structuredDataReference points at the shared container with its own oids list.
- TS container per step (e.g., 653673) — same shared-container pattern.
- File container per source DO — each source file lands as 1 file ref on the leaf dest DO that owned it in source.
- AUTH PROBE (v15.17), ENV-ALIAS (v15.17), uv-PEP-723 runner (v15.16), exit-0 break (v1.1), MFFD_SELFUPDATE / MFFD_CREATE_SEMANTIC_REPOS env opt-outs (v15.19) — all carry forward unchanged.

| ID | Slice | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| V16-IMPL-1 | **Recursive source traversal** — `iter_data_objects` extends to either (a) return all DOs incl. nested + script reconstructs tree via parentId, OR (b) recurse children explicitly. (a) is fewer cube3 round-trips; prefer it. Returned SourceDO must carry `parentId`, `childrenIds`, `predecessorIds`, `successorIds`, full `attributes`. | M | **superseded** | **2026-06-09: operator directive — no remote cube3 import will run. Data is on UNAS; ingest will use local filesystem reader (see MFFD-UNAS-LOCAL-INGEST).** |
| V16-IMPL-2 | **Dest tree creation** — for each source DO, create a dest DO whose parent is the dest-side mirror of the source's parent (step DO at the root). Two-pass: first pass creates all dest DOs without edges; second pass wires parent/child + predecessor/successor edges using a source-id → dest-id map. State file extends to map `src_do.do_id → dest_do_id` for resume safety. | L | **superseded** | **2026-06-09: same directive as V16-IMPL-1. Local UNAS ingest replaces remote tree replication.** |
| V16-IMPL-3 | **Per-DO attribute copy** — copy ALL source DO attributes verbatim plus standard `source_*` markers (per v15.10). No attribute is privileged; every key the source carries lands on dest. Critical for `layer_index`, `ply_orientation`, etc. which are the queryable identity of each Track. | S | **done (2026-05-27)** | `_SHEPARD_INTERNAL_ATTRS` frozenset filters out `appId`/`shepardId`/`createdBy`/`createdAt`/`updatedBy`/`updatedAt`/`deleted`. `_build_do_attrs()` copies remaining attrs + appends all four `source_*` markers. `self._source_version` cached from `_stamp_source_instance_attrs` so `/versionz` is fetched once, not per-DO. `_import_one_do` uses `_build_do_attrs`. Step DOs remain synthetic (no source attrs); that distinction is documented in the code. |
| V16-IMPL-4 | **Reference placement at the correct depth** — file/TS/SD references land on the LEAF dest DO that carried the source ref, NOT on the step DO. Step DO becomes a pure parent container. State file granularity = per-source-DO-id × per-ref-kind × per-ref-id. | M | **superseded** | **2026-06-09: same directive as V16-IMPL-1. Design learning carries forward to MFFD-UNAS-LOCAL-INGEST (leaf-level reference placement still applies).** |
| V16-IMPL-5 | **Resume safety** — state file's `do_done`/`file_done`/`ts_done`/`structured_done` keys all key by SOURCE DO ID (not name). `dest_id_map` persisted to state so a resumed run knows the dest-side mapping. Idempotent: re-running over a half-done state yields zero duplicate dest DOs. | M | **superseded** | **2026-06-09: same directive as V16-IMPL-1. Resume safety design carries forward to MFFD-UNAS-LOCAL-INGEST.** |
| V16-IMPL-6 | **Bridgewelding parallel paths** — source has multiple `Execution YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` DOs under Bridgewelding, plus `Process Data N`, `AF_9` etc. These are sibling at the same depth (children of Bridgewelding step). v16 preserves them all as sibling dest DOs. | S | **done (2026-05-27)** | Verified: `walk_source_tree` yields every `do-NNNNN.json` sorted by filename; each sibling gets a distinct `stable_hash()` (keyed by `do_id:name`), no `already_done` collision. `do_id_map` keyed by source `do_id` — no overwrite. `ThreadPoolExecutor` fans out one future per SourceDataObject so siblings run concurrently. Comment added to `_import_one_do` confirming the analysis and scoping the V16-IMPL-2 caveat (child-before-parent in single pass). |
| V16-IMPL-7 | **Test fixtures + unit tests** — recursion correctness, two-pass edge wiring, resume safety, dest_id_map persistence. `tests/test_v16_hierarchy.py`. | M | **superseded** | **2026-06-09: same directive as V16-IMPL-1.** |
| V16-IMPL-8 | **Deploy + re-run** — wipe dest MFFD-Dropbox 652941 (already wiped per this row's prompt), launch v16 from clean state. ETA roughly same as v15 (cube3 source-pull is the bottleneck, not the dest writes). | S | **superseded** | **2026-06-09: no remote cube3 import will run. Replaced by MFFD-UNAS-LOCAL-INGEST.** |

**Acceptance**: at completion, MFFD-Dropbox contains a tree of dest DOs matching cube3's source tree — step DOs at the root, Layer / Ply Group / Track / Execution DOs nested below, leaf-DO references attached at the correct depth, predecessor/successor edges between sibling DOs preserved. UI shows the AFP digital thread structurally, not just as a flat ref-list.
