### TSDB-DELETE-LIMIT — 2026-05-23 (TimescaleDB DML decompression limit blocks TS container delete)

Surfaced during the MFFD-tapelaying triage (2026-05-23 ~13:36 UTC). `DELETE /shepard/api/timeseriesContainers/{id}` returns HTTP 500 for any TS container whose FK-cascade chain touches >100K rows in compressed chunks. The backend code is innocent — TimescaleDB substrate config knob `timescaledb.max_tuples_decompressed_per_dml_transaction = 100000` (default) is the gate.

Stacktrace from backend log (ref `10295074-4c77-4319-92c7-406efac48288`):
```
JDBC exception executing SQL [ERROR: tuple decompression limit exceeded by operation
  Detail: current limit: 100000, tuples decompressed: 3487063
  Hint: Consider increasing timescaledb.max_tuples_decompressed_per_dml_transaction or set to 0 (unlimited).
  Where: SQL statement "DELETE FROM ONLY \"_timescaledb_internal\".\"_hyper_1_38_chunk\" WHERE $1 OPERATOR(pg_catalog.=) \"timeseries_id\""]
[delete from timeseries te1_0 where te1_0.container_id=?]
```

| ID | Slice | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| TSDB-DDL-1 | **Raise the decompression limit in the TimescaleDB container env.** Three placement options: (a) `ALTER SYSTEM SET timescaledb.max_tuples_decompressed_per_dml_transaction = 0` baked into a migration; (b) `command:` arg on the `timescaledb` service in `infrastructure/docker-compose.yml` (`-c timescaledb.max_tuples_decompressed_per_dml_transaction=0`); (c) `SET timescaledb.max_tuples_decompressed_per_dml_transaction = 0` per-session in the Hibernate / JDBC connection-pool initializer. Recommendation: **(b)** — declarative, visible to operators in compose. `0` = unlimited; safe because Shepard's typical delete is admin-initiated. | S | **done** | Pairs with `feedback_db_audit_includes_stack.md` (compose env / driver knobs are in scope for DB-audit). Sister issue: explicit FK cascade trigger could be replaced by `drop_chunks()` for whole-container deletes — see TSDB-DDL-2. |
| TSDB-DDL-2 | **Chunk-aware container delete path** — for `DELETE /timeseriesContainers/{id}`, the right substrate primitive is `SELECT drop_chunks('timeseries_data_points', older_than => now())` scoped to `timeseries_id IN (SELECT id FROM timeseries WHERE container_id = ?)`, NOT row-by-row DELETE. Drops compressed chunks without decompression. Order-of-magnitude faster + bypasses the decompression-limit issue entirely. Replace the JPA cascade for whole-container TS deletes. | M | queued | This is the structural fix. TSDB-DDL-1 is the immediate unblock. |
| TSDB-DDL-3 | **Apply retroactively: today's TS-tapelaying consolidation** — once TSDB-DDL-1 ships, the deferred 589657 + 599903 delete (528098 is strict superset, zero data loss) should succeed via the existing REST DELETE endpoint. Acceptance test for TSDB-DDL-1. | S | queued | Cross-references PRE-MUT-SNAP5 (which would gate this through a snapshot pair when that mechanism ships). |

### PRE-MUT-SNAP — 2026-05-23 (pre-mutation snapshot mechanism — gate on every cleanup / purge / migration)

User directive 2026-05-23, during the MFFD TS-tapelaying-duplicate triage: *"thats why we need provenance .. and snapshots on mutations"*. The trigger was a sloppy "duplicate-looking" call on 3 TS containers (528098/589657/599903) where a row-level spot-check turned out to be required to confirm safe-to-delete; the REST DELETE 500'd before any harm was done, but the **structural fix is to gate every mutation behind a snapshot pair**, not rely on backend failure as the safety net.

Builds on `project_snapshot_boundaries.md` (existing memory: "every larger transformation gets a snapshot pair") + `feedback_referenced_data_infinite_retention.md` (referenced data has infinite retention; only orphans are deletion-eligible) — this section ships the mechanism so those rules are enforced, not just documented.

| ID | Slice | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| PRE-MUT-SNAP1 | **Pre-mutation snapshot contract** — design doc `aidocs/ops/<NN>-pre-mutation-snapshots.md`. Defines: every mutation pathway (cleanup / purge / migration / storage rebalance / FS1e move / SM1 orphan-collect) MUST take a pre-mutation snapshot of the affected scope BEFORE the first destructive operation. Scope = the substrate slice that would be mutated (e.g. `timeseries_data_points WHERE container_id IN (...)` + the corresponding Neo4j subgraph + Mongo collections + Garage objects). Snapshot artefact = a `:Snapshot` DataObject in a dedicated `snapshots-system` collection with PROV-O attribution of WHO triggered it + WHAT it covers + the artefact hash. Snapshots are retained for a configurable grace window (default: 90 days). | M | queued | Companion to `project_snapshot_boundaries.md`. Cite `feedback_referenced_data_infinite_retention.md` as the kill-switch criterion. |
| PRE-MUT-SNAP2 | **Substrate-native snapshot primitives** — implement the per-substrate snapshot operations the contract requires. TimescaleDB: `INSERT INTO snapshots.<table>_snap SELECT * FROM <table> WHERE <scope>`. Neo4j: `apoc.export.cypher.query(scope-cypher, file, {format:'plain'})` to a snapshot artefact. Mongo: `db.<coll>.aggregate([{$match: scope}, {$out: 'snapshots.<coll>_<session>'}])`. Garage: `garage bucket snapshot` (if available) or `garage block download` + tar. Each primitive returns a `SnapshotResult{substrate, scope, rowCount, sizeBytes, artefactUri, sha256, durationMs}`. | M | queued | Lives in backend (Java) `de.dlr.shepard.ops.SnapshotService` + a Quarkus REST surface `POST /v2/admin/snapshots`. |
| PRE-MUT-SNAP3 | **Gate every mutation pathway** — wire SnapshotService into every existing cleanup / migration / purge entry point: the orphan-wipe scripts under `examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/`, the SM1 `purgeOrphans` operation (task #74), the FS1e migration runner, the `mffd-completeness-check.py` consolidation suggestion (when it gets that), and the `shepard-admin <feature> set-*` commands that touch data. Gate behaviour: `--no-snapshot` flag REQUIRED to skip; default = take snapshot, refuse to proceed if snapshot fails. | L | queued | Touches ≥5 entry points. Land incrementally per pathway — don't block ship of the mechanism on full coverage. |
| PRE-MUT-SNAP4 | **Rollback runbook** — `docs/admin/runbooks/rollback-from-snapshot.md`. Single-page operator procedure: given a snapshot id, restore the affected scope. Per the `feedback_admin_runbooks_pattern.md` contract: numbered steps, host indicator per step, expected stdout, end-state verification (a fresh `SELECT COUNT(*)` matching the pre-snapshot count). Includes the cross-substrate restore order (Neo4j → TimescaleDB → Mongo → Garage) so referential integrity holds. | M | queued | Pairs with `feedback_admin_runbooks_pattern.md` (runbook contract) + `ADMIN-RUNBOOKS-LIBRARY` (the systematic library this row contributes to). |
| PRE-MUT-SNAP5 | **Apply retroactively: today's TS-tapelaying triage** — once the mechanism ships, the deferred MFFD-tapelaying consolidation (528098 keep, 589657 + 599903 delete) goes through the new path: snapshot first, then DELETE. Closes the loop on the situation that motivated the row. | S | queued | This is the **acceptance test**: if PRE-MUT-SNAP1..4 are right, this consolidation should be a two-line operator script. |

### DB — 2026-05-22 (database inventory + best-practices + anti-pattern hunt)

User directive 2026-05-22: *"database inverntiry best pracices review, antipattern avaidance on backlog"*. Survey the full multi-substrate landscape (Neo4j + Postgres + TimescaleDB + MongoDB + PostGIS + Garage S3) and codify what's good, what's drifting, and what's actively anti-pattern. Per `feedback_db_review_all_stores.md` — never single out one substrate.

| ID | Slice | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| DB-INV1 | **Inventory pass** — every Cypher migration (`V##__*.cypher`), every Flyway SQL migration (`db/migration/V*.sql`), every Mongo collection seed, every PostGIS geometry table, every Garage bucket. Output: `aidocs/data/<NN>-db-inventory.md` listing schema name + owner module + indices + active write-paths. | M | **done (2026-05-26)** | `aidocs/data/05-db-inventory.md` shipped. Snapshot in time; refresh annually. Cross-references CLAUDE.md "always ship migrations idempotent + fail-fast". |
| DB-BP1 | **Best-practices catalogue** — what the fork does right per store. Examples: parametrised Cypher (post-C5/C5b shipped), JPA `@Entity` + Panache repository pattern (importer plugin), PostGIS index-on-create, Mongo collection naming convention. Each entry cites a representative commit. Output: `aidocs/data/<NN>-db-best-practices.md`. | M | queued | Living catalogue. Authors of new code copy from here. |
| DB-AP1 | **Anti-pattern hunt** — find every site that violates a known good practice. Candidates: string-concat'd Cypher (C5/C5b killed them but regressions grow back), N+1 query patterns (Neo4j AND Postgres), missing indices on hot-path foreign keys, GridFS used for files when Garage S3 is active, missing `ON DELETE CASCADE` on Postgres FKs where the parent is mutable, missing constraints on `:HasAppId` Neo4j labels, Mongo collections without TTL where the data is ephemeral. Output: `aidocs/agent-findings/db-antipatterns.md` listing every flagged site with severity (CRITICAL/MAJOR/MINOR) and remediation. | L | queued | Dispatched as multi-agent fan-out (one agent per substrate). Per `feedback_db_review_all_stores.md` MUST cover all six substrates. |
| DB-AP2 | **Cross-cutting findings** — patterns that span substrates. E.g. dual-source-of-truth (same field written to Neo4j AND Postgres), substrate-mismatch (timeseries lives in Timescale but the Neo4j `:Timeseries` label still carries denormalised metadata that can drift), schema-evolution gaps (migration added the field but the IO class doesn't surface it). Highest-value findings per SSOT discipline (`feedback_shacl_single_source_of_truth.md`). | M | queued | DB-AP1 enables DB-AP2 — run AP1 first per substrate, then AP2 reads the union. |
| DB-DOC1 | Roll the catalogues into operator-facing docs: `docs/reference/database-overview.md` (one-screen "what data lives where") + `docs/help/troubleshooting-databases.md` (operator-readable common issues + fixes). | S | **done** | Shipped. `docs/reference/database-overview.md` + `docs/help/troubleshooting-databases.md` + `docs/admin/index.md` links. |

### IMPORT-FIX — 2026-05-23 (v15.3 surgical fixes + v15.4 keypress diagnostics)

User directives 2026-05-23: *"the script should not perform the snapshot at the end ... the human will"* · *"if you need that inital snapshot bake it in collection / object creation"* · *"script could send debug info automatically on keypress"* · *"is a decicive action by human"* · *"verify against v5 surface — fileReference only contain oids which have to resolved again to get the payload"*.

| ID | Slice | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| IMPORT-NS1 | Remove the 3 script-side `create_snapshot` call sites in `mffd-import-v15.py` (L2231 bootstrap-t0, L3512 pre-import G1, L3550 post-import G4). Keep the `create_snapshot` method — humans may still call it via curl/UI. Replace each removed call with `print("Reminder: snapshot is a human decision. Fire when ready.")`. | S | **done** | Per the user's snapshot-policy. Closes the false-positive "import crashed" reading from 2026-05-23 log lines 49-50 + 271-272. |
| IMPORT-NS2 | Backend feature — atomic baseline-snapshot-on-create for Collection + DataObject (`?createBaselineSnapshot=true` query flag emits one t=0 snapshot in the same transaction as the create). | M | queued | Replaces the bootstrap t=0 snapshot pattern; lets the backend own the boundary atomic-with-create. |
| IMPORT-SR1 | Fix `get_or_create_semantic_repo` defaults in `mffd-import-v15.py` (L1287-1308). Backend requires non-blank `endpoint` for ALL repo types (INTERNAL too). Replace conditional `if endpoint: body[...]=...` with unconditional `body["endpoint"] = endpoint or f"urn:shepard:repo:{name}"`. | S | **done** | Surfaces from 2026-05-23 log lines 70-72 + 203-205 + 273-274. Ships in v15.3 alongside IMPORT-NS1. |
| IMPORT-DBG1 | **Keypress-driven debug surface in `mffd-import-v15.py`.** Background thread reads stdin non-blocking via `select`; keys: `?`/`h` help, `s` status, `d` full diagnostic (last 3 HTTP req+resp + state-file + env, literal creds per `feedback_no_redactions.md`), `l` tail log, `D` dump-to-file per `feedback_diagnostic_artefact.md`, `p` pause/resume, `q` graceful quit. | M | queued | v15.4. Separate from v15.3 so each round is small + cleanly bisectable. |
| IMPORT-Q7-VERIFY | One-curl probe on cube: `wc -c` the response of `GET .../fileReferences/{frid}/payload/{oid}` against a known source OID. > 0 → script bug. == 0 → source-data-state bug (task #145 Q7 confirmed). | XS | **superseded** | **2026-06-09: operator directive — no remote cube3 import; data already on UNAS. Cube-side validation no longer applicable.** |
| IMPORT-Q7-FIX | **Code-fix shipped 2026-05-28** for task #145 / Q7. Two compounding bugs in `download_file_ref`: (1) **forgotten-oid** — the worker fan-out at L4170 called `download_file_ref(src_coll_id, do_id, fref_id, tmp_file, fref.size)` with 5 positional args, dropping `oid=fref.oid`. Per v5.4.0 `FileReferenceRest.getFiles`, the bare `/payload` endpoint returns a JSON array of `ShepardFile` metadata, NOT bytes — so the importer wrote JSON-as-binary and uploaded it to dest. (2) **Silent stream truncation** — `iter_content()` exits without raising when the upstream connection closes mid-transfer (psf/requests#2275, #4227, #6512); v15 never compared `dest.stat().st_size` to `Content-Length`. Fix: keyword-pass `oid=fref.oid, corr=corr_id` from the worker; post-stream byte-count guard in `download_file_ref`; unlink partial files; emit `file_truncated` / `file_unverified` diag events (IMPORT-DBG1 surface). Regression test: `examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/test_fileref_truncation.py` (10 cases, all pass; truncation cases verified to FAIL pre-fix). Validation deferred to next v16 import (no live cube JWT + 2026-05-28 full-reset wiped 661923). | M | **done** | Branch `145-fileref-truncation-fix`. Findings: `aidocs/agent-findings/q7-fileref-parser-bug-2026-05-28.md`. |

See `aidocs/agent-findings/v15.2-implementation.md`. |
| IMPORT-W2 | Unexpected-reply abort + structured diagnostic report. On any non-2xx or wire-shape drift during warmup or main loop: stop, emit a structured diagnostic block (verb + url + expected schema + got + where to look + what to try next), exit with a distinct integer per failure class (auth=2, source-unreachable=3, garage-down=4, operator-interrupt=5, wire-shape-drift=6, write-permission-denied=7). Caller scripts and the Makefile can react. | S | **done** | Shipped 2026-05-22 alongside IMPORT-W1. `WarmupReport.to_text()` is the diagnostic artefact (one fact per line); `WarmupAborted` carries the report + exit code. Tests assert each documented exit-code constant. Main-loop instrumentation (carrying the same structured shape to in-flight failures, not just warmup) DEFERRED — separate slice. |
| IMPORT-W3 | OpenAPI-driven wire-shape comparator helper (Python) — `tools/openapi_shape_check.py`: given `(spec_path, endpoint, method, response_body)` returns `(ok, missing_fields, extra_fields, type_mismatches)`. Used by IMPORT-W1 warmup + by `V1WireFidelityIT` Java side via a parallel implementation. | S | **done** | Shipped 2026-05-22 as `compare_against_openapi()` in `_smart_warmup.py` (not a separate `tools/` file — co-located with the only Python consumer). ### P1 — 2026-05-16 (parallel DB connectivity)

| ID | Slice | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| P1 | Parallel startup DB warm + parallel recovery | done | New `DbConnectivityWarmer` (`@ApplicationScoped`, `@Observes StartupEvent`) fires one `Thread.ofVirtual()` per required `DbPinger` in parallel — total startup wait is now `max(latencies)` not `sum(latencies)`. Bounded by `shepard.migrations.connection-wait-timeout` (reused from `MigrationsRunner`; no new config key). `DbRecoveryScheduler.attemptRecovery()` now collects stale pingers then probes each in its own virtual thread (`CompletableFuture.allOf` blocks the tick). Both use `RequestContextController.activate/deactivate` per thread so JPA `EntityManager` injections in `TimescalePinger` / `PostGisPinger` work correctly off the caller thread. Per-DB `DbHealthState` independently tracks each outcome. 7 new unit tests in `DbConnectivityWarmerTest` (including timing proof: 3 × 300 ms pingers finish in < 950 ms wall-clock); 2 new tests added to `DbRecoverySchedulerTest`. |

Nuxt module. This is a correction to `aidocs/agent-findings/trace3d-spike.md`
which assumed `@tresjs/nuxt` was available — see survey §1.2 for the rationale.

|---|---|---|---|---|
| VIS-S1 | Shared cross-cutting platform piece: `POST /v2/shapes/render` `ViewRecipeRenderer` SPI dispatcher (in-tree, plugin authors register a renderer keyed by SHACL shape IRI from the template body's `viewRecipeShape` field). Garage signed-URL minter helper (`SignedUrlIssuer`) + presigned-URL access-control filter SPLIT OUT to a follow-up (VIS-S1b) — the dispatcher alone unblocks VIS-T1 sprint-2, the URL minter is wanted by VIS-V1 / VIS-X1 but not Trace3D. | M | **shipped 2026-05-28 (VIS-S1a — SPI dispatcher only; VIS-S1b URL minter still queued)** | In-tree, not a plugin. **Predecessor for all VIS-* plugins.** SPI shape `de.dlr.shepard.spi.view.{ViewRecipeRenderer, ViewRecipeRendererRegistry, RenderRequest, RenderResponse, RenderException}` — ServiceLoader-loaded POJO impls mirroring the `PayloadKind` P-series pattern; fail-fast at startup on duplicate shape IRI; renderer-thrown `RenderException` → HTTP 422 (RFC 7807); unexpected `RuntimeException` → HTTP 500; no renderer registered → in-tree TPL2b `DECLARED`-status fallback (wire shape preserved for clients whose plugin isn't installed). 32 unit tests (14 registry + 18 dispatcher). Three-audience docs: `docs/reference/view-recipe-spi.md` + `docs/help/plugin-renderer-not-loaded.md`. `aidocs/34` + `aidocs/44` rows landed in same PR. Already partially scoped in `aidocs/agent-findings/trace3d-spike.md §3` and `aidocs/agent-findings/persona-review-api-scrutinizer.md`. |

| VIS-C1 | `shepard-plugin-vis-cad` module skeleton + `CadViewShape` SHACL allow-list extension + `CadResolver` (File payload → signed Garage URL for `occt-inline` track, or triggers optional `step-to-gltf-converter` sidecar for `gltf-cached` track, or constructs iframe URL for `online3dviewer-iframe` fallback) + `CadView.vue` (`occt-import-js` WASM browser-side STEP/IGES/BREP read + tessellation → Three.js `BufferGeometry` inside shared `<TresCanvas>` from `vis-trace3d`) + `AssemblyTree.vue` (Vuetify treeview from occt-import-js hierarchy result; click-to-isolate) + `SectionPlaneControls.vue` (three-slider Vuetify dialog applying Three.js `Clipping Plane`) + `CadIframe.vue` (Online3DViewer iframe wrapper for exotic formats — `.CATPart`, `.prt`) + plugin docs trio (`reference`/`quickstart`/`install`) | L (~2 sprints) | queued | `occt-import-js` is LGPL-2.1 — **not** in the CLAUDE.md GPL/AGPL/SSPL ban list; dynamic-linking clause satisfied as WASM blob loaded at runtime; documented in `plugins/vis-cad/docs/install.md`. **xeokit-sdk rejected** (AGPLv3 — blocked by licence gate). **Forge Viewer rejected** (commercial). `Online3DViewer` (MIT, kovacsv) — widest format coverage on the web (3dm, 3ds, brep, dae, fbx, fcstd, gltf, ifc, iges, step, stl, obj, off, ply, wrl) — ships as static SPA at `cdn.shepard/online3dviewer/v1/` for the iframe fallback track. | VIS-F1 | `shepard-plugin-vis-fem` module skeleton + `FemResultViewShape` SHACL allow-list extension + `FemResolver` (File payload → signed Garage URL for VTU/XDMF/PVD pass-through; triggers `fem-converter` sidecar for `.odb`/`.rst`/`.op2`/`.med`/`.h3d`; or constructs iframe URL for `pyvista-trame-iframe`/`paraview-glance-iframe` render targets) + `FemView.vue` (VTK.js v35 unstructured-grid reader + field colour-mapping + glyph/threshold/contour filters inside shared `<VtkScene>` from `vis-vector`) + `FemFieldPicker.vue` (Vuetify dropdown listing fields from VTU header; field + component selector) + `DeformedShapeControls.vue` (Vuetify slider for `deformationScale`; undeformed-wireframe toggle) + `IsoContourEditor.vue` (drag-add iso-values on a field histogram) + `PyVistaTrameIframe.vue` (server-side-render escape hatch) + `fem-converter` Python sidecar (FastAPI + `pyNastran` BSD-3 for `.op2` + `meshio` MIT for `.med`/`.inp`/`.bdf` mesh + opt-in `ansys-dpf-core` commercial for `.rst` + opt-in bind-mounted Abaqus + `odb2vtk` MIT for `.odb`; declared per PM1f) + `pyvista-trame-renderer` sidecar (PyVista + Trame, BSD-3, optional for large-dataset escape hatch) + plugin docs trio | L (~3 sprints) | queued | **The most-sidecar-heavy plugin in the family — and the one where the sidecar pattern most clearly earns its keep.** Proprietary FEM binaries (`.odb`, `.rst`) require vendor SDK linkage that can't run in a browser; pure browser parsing is unrealistic. Operators bring their own Abaqus/Ansys licences via env-var opt-ins (`FEM_CONVERTER_ENABLE_ABAQUS`, `FEM_CONVERTER_ENABLE_ANSYS`); open-source formats (`.med`/`.op2`/`.vtu`/`.xdmf`) work out-of-the-box with no extra licences. **Salomé partly blocked** (AGPL on Web modules); ParaView Glance available as `paraview-glance-iframe` track if needed. Vendor-isolation pattern is Shepard's structural answer to a gap most competing RDM platforms punt on entirely (Kadi4Mat / SciCat typically ship no FEM viewer). `cadOverlay` slot composes vis-cad's resolved glTF into the same `<VtkScene>` — MFFD's "as-designed vs as-deformed" comparison case. Acceptance: MFFD CF/LMPAEK ultrasonic-weld T-joint Abaqus `.odb` (~150 MB, ~80k C3D10 elements, 50 timesteps) → VTU+PVD in < 60 s; browser von-Mises render at > 30 FPS at 1080p; deformation-scale slider responsive; time-scrubber > 24 FPS; iso-contour at material yield works; cadOverlay composes correctly. Backup public dataset: NASA Common Research Model wing-box Nastran `.op2`. Depends on VIS-V1 (`VtkScene` reuse) + VIS-C1 (cadOverlay path). Survey: `aidocs/agent-findings/vis-plugin-survey-addendum-cad-fem.md` §6. |

| RDK-PARSE-1 | **`RdkTextScrapeParser` tier-1 — readable strings out of `MFZ.rdk`**. New module `plugins/fileformat-robotics/` (declared as a `FileParserPlugin` per `aidocs/integrations/110-file-format-parser-plugin.md §3`). Decompresses the `.rdk` zlib payload (4-byte custom magic header skipped), walks `[uint32 LE byte-length][UTF-16LE bytes]` records, and classifies each via predicate-specific regex; emits eight SemanticAnnotations on the parent FileReference under `urn:shepard:rdk:*`: `appVersion`, `platform`, `programSource`, `cadRef[]`, `stepRef[]`, `apiEndpoint`, `robotController`, `companionSpatialAnalyzer` (the last when a `<base>.xit`/`.xit64` sibling exists in the same FileContainer). | S | **done 2026-05-28** | MAJOR | user request 2026-05-28 | `plugins/fileformat-robotics/` standalone Maven module + three-audience docs trio. 17 JUnit tests green, including `RdkTextScrapeParserMFZFixtureTest` against the real 12.1 MB `examples/mffd-showcase/raw-data/mffd-data/cell/MFZ.rdk` — reproduces aidocs/110 §4.3 expected output verbatim. Aggregator wire-up filed as **RDK-WIRE-AGGREGATOR-1** (same `OTVIS-WIRE-AGGREGATOR-1`-class Jandex blocker). Namespace decision documented in `RdkAnnotations` javadoc: `urn:shepard:rdk:*` supersedes the `chameo:*` / `m4i:*` sketch in aidocs/110 §4.3 — the doc is `stage: concept` and updated in this PR. Note: walker is single-pass structured-string extraction over zlib-decompressed UTF-16LE length-prefix records — literal printable-ASCII run extraction discarded after empirical eval (754K raw runs vs. 72 structured records on `MFZ.rdk`); chose option (a) (regex-per-predicate) over option (b) (multi-pass classifier) for tier-1. |
| RDK-WIRE-AGGREGATOR-1 | **Wire `fileformat-robotics` (and the sibling `fileformat-thermography`) into the aggregator + canonical SPI.** Today both modules are standalone (build via `cd plugins/<name> && ../../backend/mvnw test`) because pulling them into `plugins/pom.xml` worsens the Quarkus/Jandex `CompositeIndex.getClassByName` hang in the backend. When that hang clears (root-caused per `aidocs/agent-findings/jandex-hang.md`): (1) add modules to `plugins/pom.xml` `<modules>`; (2) introduce the canonical `de.dlr.shepard.spi.fileparser.FileParserPlugin` interface in the backend module; (3) replace the local shim interfaces in both modules with `@Override`s against the canonical SPI; (4) add `META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.spi.fileparser.FileParserPlugin` descriptors pointing at `RdkTextScrapeParser` + `OTvisParser`; (5) wire `FileContainerService.upload(...)` to dispatch via `FileParserRegistry`; (6) plumb `ParseContext.siblingFiles()` from the FileContainer DAO listing. | M | queued | MAJOR | spawned from RDK-PARSE-1 / OTVIS-PARSE-1. Reuse the OTvis path verbatim — same shape, same blocker. |
| RDK-PARSE-2 | **`RdkKinematicExtractor` tier-2 — full kinematic tree out of `MFZ.rdk`.** New sidecar (declared per `feedback_plugins_declare_sidecars.md`) running RoboDK Python API headless: loads station, walks `RDK.Items()`, extracts joints (axes, limits, types), tool frames (TCP), targets (named poses), reference frames (cell base, table, fixture). Emits a single `:DigitalTwinScene` Neo4j entity with `CoordinateFrame` children, each with `:HAS_PARENT_FRAME` edges, plus `:JOINT_*` properties on robot links. Stored under the parent FileReference as a child `:DigitalTwinScene`. Acceptance: MFZ.rdk → 1 :DigitalTwinScene + ≥ 2 robot subtrees (R10, R20) with 6 joints each + named targets for Ply 1–15. Requires RoboDK SDK licence on the sidecar host (operator opt-in via `FILEFORMAT_RDK_ENABLE=true` env). Failsafe: if sidecar unavailable, tier-1 strings remain. | L | queued | MAJOR | user request 2026-05-28 | RoboDK Python API: `import robolink; rdk = robolink.Robolink()`. `aidocs/data/85-coordinate-frame-tree.md` (target schema). Pairs with VIS-S3 (Isaac WebRTC) and Trace3D. |
| URDF-WEBVIEW-1 | **in progress (this PR — phase 1: in-tree renderer adapter, plugin extraction deferred).** **`shepard-plugin-vis-urdf` — URDF browser viewer + animator + Foxglove iframe fallback.** **URDF is Shepard's lingua franca for robot kinematics** — ROS RViz, Isaac, MuJoCo, Foxglove, Gazebo all consume it. Plugin ships: (a) `UrdfViewShape` SHACL allow-list extension; (b) `UrdfResolver` returns signed Garage URL for `.urdf` + mesh assets; (c) `UrdfView.vue` using `urdf-loader` (gkjohnson, MIT, npm `urdf-loader`) — Three.js-based, mounts into the shared `<TresCanvas>` from `vis-trace3d`; supports STL/Collada/glTF meshes; (d) **`UrdfAnimator.vue` — trajectory playback**: takes a `:DigitalTwinScene` URDF + a `TimeseriesReference` carrying joint-angle channels (one channel per movable joint, `urn:shepard:urdf:joint` annotation maps channel → joint name; preselection rule applies), drives `urdf-loader`'s per-joint setters against a time cursor. Vuetify play/pause/scrub/speed controls (1x, 0.1x, 10x, reverse). Optional Trace3D TCP-trail overlay shown synchronized in the same `<TresCanvas>`. Frame-rate budget: ≥ 30 FPS at 100 Hz channel rate; downsample with LTTB-vis-toggle if needed (TS-LTTB-VIS-TOGGLE-01 pattern); (e) `UrdfJointPanel.vue` manual Vuetify slider per movable joint (static-view mode); (f) `UrdfRecordButton.vue` — record current animation timeline as a `TimeseriesReference` with the joint-name channel-annotation mapping; (g) Foxglove Studio iframe fallback (`foxglove-studio` Apache-2.0, self-hosted at `cdn.shepard/foxglove/v2/`) for ROS-bag replay against the same URDF — covers the "operator already lives in RViz" use case. Conversion: `RdkToUrdfExporter` sidecar wraps RoboDK Python API → URDF via `robolink.Item.UpdateRobotURDF()` (cell-level info lost; document the lossy boundary in `plugins/vis-urdf/docs/reference.md`). Plugin docs trio. Acceptance: MFZ.rdk → URDF render of R10 in browser; AFP layup recorded joint trajectory plays back at 30+ FPS with TCP-trail synced; static joint sliders work in manual mode; same URDF loads in Foxglove iframe playing a ROS bag. | L (~2 sprints) | **in progress** (phase 1 in-tree; plugin extraction deferred) | MAJOR | user request 2026-05-28 "research other (compatible webviewers, rvis or similar - urdf should be our standard)" + "urdf viewer and animator!". Survey results inline below the table — `urdf-loader` won the eval. Pairs tightly with RDK-PARSE-2 (output of RDK extractor), SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 (the animator can save/load animations as `:Activity`-tracked edits), Trace3D, and the annotation-driven preselection principle (joint channel mapping via annotation, no manual hookup). License clean (MIT for `urdf-loader`, Apache-2.0 for Foxglove Studio). **Phase 1 shipped this PR:** `urdf-loader` dep + `UrdfCanvas.vue` + `UrdfView.vue` + `UrdfAnimator.vue` + `UrdfJointPanel.vue` + `UrdfChannelPicker.vue` + shapes/render delegation + ViewRecipeBuilderDialog renderer toggle + sample two-link-arm URDF + 47 Vitest tests. Design doc: `aidocs/integrations/113-urdf-viewer.md`. Phase 2 deferred: UrdfRecordButton, Foxglove iframe, RdkToUrdfExporter sidecar, Trace3D+URDF composition. |
| URDF-MFFD-SHOWCASE-1 | **MFFD RDK→URDF end-to-end showcase** — `examples/mffd-rdk-urdf-showcase/`. | DT1-PHASE-0 | **Substrate scaffold: `:DigitalTwinScene` + `:CoordinateFrame` + `:Joint` Neo4j entities + V95 migration.** GATING ROW for SCENEGRAPH-REST-1, RDK-PARSE-2, KRL-INTERPRETER-05-REST, URDF-WEBVIEW-1 phase 2 animation persistence, and ISAAC-USD-EXPORT-1. **In-flight 2026-05-29 (branch `dt1-phase-0-scaffold`):** (a) `DigitalTwinScene` `@NodeEntity` with `appId`, `name`, `description`, `sourceFileAppId?`, `rootFrameAppId?` (separate `:Joint` entity ships `homeAngle`; `revision` deferred — entity is not yet `VersionableEntity`); (b) `CoordinateFrame` `@NodeEntity` with `appId`, `name`, `parentFrameAppId?`, scalar transform `(x,y,z,rx,ry,rz)` doubles, `kind` ∈ {FRAME,JOINT,TOOL,BASE,TCP} (smaller enum than design doc's {WORLD,LINK,JOINT,TOOL,TCP,SENSOR} — additive expansion deferred); (c) `Joint` `@NodeEntity` with `appId`, `name`, `parentFrameAppId`, `childFrameAppId`, axis `(axisX,axisY,axisZ)`, `limitMin`/`limitMax`, `type` ∈ {REVOLUTE,PRISMATIC,FIXED,CONTINUOUS}, `homeAngle`; (d) `V95__DT1_Phase0_DigitalTwinScene_scaffold.cypher` + `V95_R__DT1_Phase0_rollback.cypher`. Three `@ApplicationScoped` DAOs extend `GenericDAO` unchanged (the CHOKE-03 fresh-session refit is deferred — no startup-time caller exists). **No REST surface, no IO classes, no MCP tools, no Activity wiring, no frontend UI ship in this row** — all deferred to SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 (now unblocked). Planned graph edges (`:HAS_FRAME`, `:HAS_PARENT_FRAME`, `:HAS_JOINT`, `:JOINT_PARENT`, `:JOINT_CHILD`) documented in V95 top-comment as the shape that SCENEGRAPH-REST-1's service layer will write. 14 DAO unit tests + 1 V95 regression test in `TestNeo4jMigrations`. Backlog row spawned: DT1-DAO-FRESH-SESSION (override `createOrUpdate` per JupyterConfig pattern when SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 introduces startup-time callers). | S | **in-flight (PR open 2026-05-29)** | CRITICAL | sub-row spawned 2026-05-29 during SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 dispatch: substrate audit found zero entity classes. | SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 | **`/v2/scene-graphs` REST surface — read + write the DigitalTwinScene.** Backend exposes the `:DigitalTwinScene` graph (produced by RDK-PARSE-2 or hand-built) as a REST resource: `GET /v2/scene-graphs/{appId}` returns the frame tree (JSON-LD or plain JSON), `GET …/export.urdf` returns URDF XML, `GET …/export.usd` returns a 503 stub (queued under ISAAC-USD-EXPORT-1), `POST /v2/scene-graphs` creates an empty scene, `POST /v2/scene-graphs/{appId}/frames` adds a frame, `PATCH /v2/scene-graphs/{appId}/frames/{frameAppId}` mutates a single frame's transform + parent + kind, `DELETE /v2/scene-graphs/{appId}/frames/{frameAppId}` removes a subtree, `POST/DELETE /v2/scene-graphs/{appId}/joints[/…]` register / remove joints. Mutations generate `:Activity` with PROV1j `sourceMode`/`agentId` from `X-AI-Agent` and a supplementary `:WAS_DERIVED_FROM` edge to the previous activity for the same scene. Seven MCP tools (`scene_graph_get`, `_create`, `_add_frame`, `_patch_frame`, `_delete_frame`, `_register_joint`, `_delete_joint`, `_export_urdf`). URDF exporter emits `<link>` per frame + `<joint>` with axis + limits; visual / collision / inertial blocks queued for URDF-WEBVIEW-1 phase 2. Frontend placeholder at `/scene-graphs/[appId]` (placeholder kit); real graph browser UI tracked as `SCENEGRAPH-REST-1-UI`. Auth: `@Authenticated` only — the scaffold has no owning-collection pointer; `SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1` row tracks the eventual permission anchor. **`UrdfExporterTest` (6) + `SceneGraphServiceTest` (12).** | M | **in-flight 2026-05-29 (PR open, branch `scenegraph-rest-1`)** | MAJOR | user request 2026-05-28 "also expose a scenegraph via rest (including modifications from api)". Cross-ref: DT1-PHASE-0 (substrate row, ✓ shipped), RDK-PARSE-2 (producer), URDF-WEBVIEW-1 (consumer), `aidocs/data/85-coordinate-frame-tree.md` (entity schema), MCP-V2 (tool exposure). Spawned follow-ons: `SCENEGRAPH-REST-1-UI` (graph browser), `SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1` (owning-collection anchor). |
| SCENEGRAPH-REST-1-UI | **Real graph browser UI for `/v2/scene-graphs/{appId}` — replaces the placeholder shipped with SCENEGRAPH-REST-1.** Page at `/scene-graphs/{appId}` with: recursive `:HAS_PARENT_FRAME` tree (left column, kind/joint-count/descendant-count chips, orphan rescue group), sticky frame inspector (right column, six-scalar transform + parent picker + kind selector + Save with optimistic PATCH + revert + Copy-as-JSON-LD power-tool), sibling joints table (parent/child frame names, type chip, axis, limits, home, Add + Delete), Add-frame dialog (parent picker hidden for first frame → becomes root), Add-joint dialog (parent/child required), Delete-frame confirm with descendant-count surfacing, URDF export download via Blob (filename sanitised), 404 + empty + loading states, write gating on `canWrite` (today = true under `@Authenticated`; per-scene perms queued as SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1). Recursive tree built as a tiny `<SceneGraphTreeNode>` SFC rather than v-treeview / vis.js / dagre per the "no heavy graph lib for a tree" guard. Tests: 5 Vitest files (53 cases) on pure helpers (`sceneGraphErrorMessageForStatus`, `urdfDownloadFilename`, `indexFramesByParent`, `countDescendants`, plus extracted dirty-detect / parent-items / canSubmit / body-builder / frame-label helpers); Playwright 4K spec `scenegraph-rest-1-ui.spec.ts` covers 404 graceful path + tree+inspector layout + add-frame dialog viewport fit + delete-confirm. | M | **in-flight 2026-05-30 (branch `scenegraph-rest-1-ui`)** | NICE | spawned 2026-05-29 by SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 dispatch. Cross-ref: SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 (backend), URDF-WEBVIEW-1 (renderer for the URDF preview iframe), SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1 (per-scene permission walk — blocker for real perm gating). |
| SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1 | **Per-scene permission anchor for `:DigitalTwinScene`.** SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 shipped with `@Authenticated`-only auth because the DT1-PHASE-0 scaffold has no owning-collection pointer. Permanent shape: scene inherits permissions from `sourceFileAppId → FileReference → DataObject → Collection` walk; for hand-built scenes (no source file), add an explicit `ownerCollectionAppId` field with the same Collection-permission walk. The walk needs to short-circuit if `sourceFileAppId` resolves but the FileReference→Collection edge is missing (delete-cascade race), defaulting to "owner-only" in that case. | S | **shipped 2026-05-31 (this PR)** | MAJOR | spawned 2026-05-29 by SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 dispatch — the scaffold's design intentionally left the permission anchor open. **Landed 2026-05-31**: new `SceneGraphPermissionService` walks `scene.sourceFileAppId → SingletonFileReferenceService.getByAppId → ref.getDataObject().getAppId() → PermissionsService.isAccessAllowedForDataObjectAppId(parentDoAppId, AccessType, caller)` (re-uses the existing helper that already walks DataObject → Collection). `SceneGraphRest` now gates: read endpoints (`GET /v2/scene-graphs/{appId}`, `GET …/export.urdf`) on Read; write endpoints (POST create/frame/joint, PATCH, DELETE) on Write; list endpoint (`GET /v2/scene-graphs`) post-filters the page (short-page acceptable; `total` envelope reports the unfiltered total). Hand-built scenes (no `sourceFileAppId`) fail closed unless caller carries `instance-admin`. Create gate: when `sourceFileAppId` is supplied in the body, the caller must hold Write on its parent Collection. The eventual `ownerCollectionAppId` field for first-class hand-built-scene anchoring stays queued for a follow-up. 25 JUnit cases (11 SceneGraphPermissionService + 5 RestList incl. permission post-filter + 9 RestPerms). **MCP layer (`SceneGraphMcpTools`) bypasses the REST gate** by calling `SceneGraphService` directly — follow-up row `SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1-MCP` queued. Cross-ref: SCENEGRAPH-REST-1, DT1-PHASE-0. |
| SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1-MCP | **MCP scene-graph tools bypass the SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1 gate.** `SceneGraphMcpTools.java` calls `SceneGraphService` directly (find/add/patch/delete/export) without consulting `SceneGraphPermissionService`. The REST surface enforces the walk; the MCP surface doesn't, so an AI agent driving the MCP tools sees / mutates every scene regardless of the caller's Collection permissions. Fix: thread the MCP-layer caller identity (already extracted via `McpContextFilter` for other tools) through every tool body, call `scenePermissions.isAllowed(...)` before the service call, return a domain error on deny. | S | **shipped 2026-05-31 (this PR)** | MAJOR | spawned 2026-05-31 by SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1 ship. Cross-ref: SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1, SCENEGRAPH-REST-1, MCP-V2. **Landed 2026-05-31**: `SceneGraphMcpTools` now injects `SceneGraphPermissionService` + resolves caller identity through the existing `AuthenticationContext` injection. Per-tool gates: read tools (`scene_graph_get`, `scene_graph_export_urdf`) call `scenePermissions.isAllowed(sceneAppId, AccessType.Read, caller, isAdmin)`; write tools (`scene_graph_add_frame`, `scene_graph_patch_frame`, `scene_graph_delete_frame`, `scene_graph_register_joint`, `scene_graph_delete_joint`) call the same with `AccessType.Write`. `scene_graph_create` mirrors the REST `create` shape: hand-built scenes (no `sourceFileAppId`) require `instance-admin`; sourced scenes require `canCreateFromSourceFile`. `scene_list` post-filters the page to readable rows and reports the unfiltered total (matches REST). `scene_create_from_urdf` was already gated downstream by `ScenegraphFromUrdfService` so its existing `ForbiddenException → -32002` mapping suffices. Denied tool calls raise `jakarta.ws.rs.ForbiddenException`, which `McpToolSupport.run` maps to MCP error code `-32002` (Permission denied). 14 new JUnit cases on `SceneGraphMcpToolsTest` (read-allow + read-deny per read tool; write-deny per write tool; hand-built admin-only + sourced write gate on create; list post-filter + unauthenticated list rejection). |
| DT1-DAO-FRESH-SESSION | **Override `createOrUpdate` + finders on `DigitalTwinSceneDAO` / `CoordinateFrameDAO` / `JointDAO` to refetch a live OGM session per call (CHOKE-03 / JupyterConfig pattern).** Deferred from DT1-PHASE-0 because that PR ships scaffold-only — no startup-time caller exists. When SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 (or any plugin that boots before `SessionFactory`) starts driving the DAOs, the inherited `GenericDAO` constructor's cached `session` field can be null, and writes will NPE. Mirror `JupyterConfigDAO`: override `createOrUpdate` and any high-traffic finder with `Session live = NeoConnector.getInstance().getNeo4jSession()` + null-guard + manual `AppIdGenerator.next()` mint + `live.save(entity, 1)`. Add JUnit coverage that exercises the fresh-session path. | XS | **BLOCKED — moot until DT1-PHASE-0 merges** | MINOR | spawned 2026-05-29 by DT1-PHASE-0. Cross-ref: `JupyterConfigDAO.createOrUpdate` for the canonical pattern; CHOKE-03 origin context. **fire-251/fire-257**: attempted to ship DAO fresh-session overrides in commit `33eb75ba`, but the entity classes (`de.dlr.shepard.v2.scenegraph.entities.*`) don't exist on main — V2CONV-B4 dissolved the scenegraph scaffold before `dt1-phase-0-scaffold` merged. The broken DAO/service files were removed in `a777f71a` on MFFD-BATCH-01. Implementation prereq: DT1-PHASE-0 must land first (branch `dt1-phase-0-scaffold` exists, needs CI + rebase). |
| ISAAC-USD-EXPORT-1 | **`GET /v2/scene-graphs/{appId}/export.usd` — USD round-trip with Isaac Sim.** Per `aidocs/data/85 §7`: Isaac cannot embed as canvas, integration is round-trip via USD. Endpoint generates a USD stage from the `:DigitalTwinScene` (one prim per CoordinateFrame; mesh prims from CadReferences via STEP/DAE → USD conversion). Isaac loads → researchers add physics/sensors → results flow back via sTC collector or REST ingest. Gated behind `shepard.isaac-sim.enabled` feature toggle (Omniverse + RTX is ~20 GB, not a default dep). | M | queued (deferred — Isaac is post-reset) | NICE | user directive 2026-05-28 "(we leave isaac as a backlog)". `aidocs/data/85-coordinate-frame-tree.md §7 NVIDIA Isaac Sim`. Depends on SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 (the producer endpoint). |

### TOOLS-NAV-* / SCENEGRAPH-NAV-* — top-nav reachability reconciliation (2026-05-30)

Reconciler walked the deployed UI on 2026-05-30 and found exactly one strict
violation of the CLAUDE.md "every shipped feature reachable from the top-nav
before beta" rule: **SCENEGRAPH-REST-1-UI** was 4 clicks from any page
(avatar → /me → Semantic tile → Scene graphs). The cheapest structural fix
is a top-level `Tools` menu that aggregates six research-tooling routes —
which simultaneously lifts five other surfaces from 4-click to 1-click depth.

| ID | Item | Size | Status | Severity | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| TOOLS-NAV-01 | **Top-level `Tools` menu in `HeaderBar.vue` + `/tools` landing page.** Adds a desktop nav button + mobile drawer entry between Containers and the admin gate. Landing page is a 6-tile grid: Vocabularies (`/semantic/vocabularies`), SPARQL playground (`/semantic/sparql`), Shape validator (`/shapes/validate`), Snapshot diff (`/snapshots/diff`), Scene graphs (`/scene-graphs`), Shapes render playground (`/shapes/render`). Tile inventory + appId-shape helper in `frontend/utils/toolsLanding.ts` (testable without mount). The `SemanticPane.vue` hub-tile pattern at `/me#semantic` stays for one release cycle; removal queued as a follow-up. Vitest coverage on the tile-inventory invariants + the appId plausibility check. | XS | **shipped 2026-05-30 (this PR)** | MAJOR | dispatched 2026-05-30 from the topnav reachability reconciler. Cross-ref: CLAUDE.md "every shipped feature reachable from the top-nav before beta", SCENEGRAPH-REST-1-UI (was the unreachable surface), SEMA-NAV-PLACEMENT-DECISION (the prior /me#semantic decision this re-evaluates). Files: `frontend/components/layout/HeaderBar.vue`, `frontend/pages/tools/index.vue`, `frontend/utils/toolsLanding.ts`, `frontend/tests/unit/toolsLanding.test.ts`. |
| SCENEGRAPH-NAV-01 | **`/scene-graphs/index.vue` — list-page-shaped landing with "Open by appId" form + "Create new" guidance.** Sibling to `/scene-graphs/[appId]`. Loud info-state notice that the backend list endpoint is not yet exposed (queued as SCENEGRAPH-LIST-1); until then the page supports (a) opening any scene by its appId (UUID shape check via `isPlausibleAppId` short-circuits typos before round-tripping the backend), (b) a help card naming the three entry points to mint a scene (URDF upload, RDK importer, `POST /v2/scene-graphs`). Reuses the existing `useSceneGraph().fetchScene` — no new composable methods, no backend changes. | XS | **shipped 2026-05-30 (this PR)** | MAJOR | dispatched 2026-05-30 alongside TOOLS-NAV-01 (prerequisite: the Tools-menu Scene-graphs tile needs a destination that isn't a placeholder). Cross-ref: SCENEGRAPH-REST-1-UI (the per-scene UI), SCENEGRAPH-LIST-1 (the missing list endpoint). Files: `frontend/pages/scene-graphs/index.vue`, `frontend/utils/toolsLanding.ts::isPlausibleAppId`. |
| SCENEGRAPH-LIST-1 | **Backend: `GET /v2/scene-graphs` — list scenes the caller can read.** Discovered missing during SCENEGRAPH-NAV-01 dispatch: `SceneGraphRest.java` exposes `GET /{appId}`, `POST`, `POST/PATCH/DELETE /{appId}/frames…`, but no list endpoint. SCENEGRAPH-NAV-01 ships a list-page-shaped landing that compensates with an "Open by appId" form + a loud notice, but a real list (paginated, filterable by `sourceFileAppId` and `name`-substring) is the right shape. Permission walk lands on top of SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1: until that ships, the list returns every scene to every authenticated caller; after, it scopes to scenes the caller can read via the owning-collection chain. Frontend follow-up: swap the `/scene-graphs/index.vue` info-card for a real `v-data-table` once the endpoint exists. | S | **shipped 2026-05-30 (this PR)** | MAJOR | spawned 2026-05-30 by SCENEGRAPH-NAV-01 dispatch — the worktree agent confirmed the endpoint does not exist via primary-source read of `SceneGraphRest.java` and chose to compensate frontend-side rather than silently add a backend endpoint. **Landed 2026-05-30 as C1**: `SceneGraphRest.list()` returns `{ items[], total, page, size }` envelope; ordered by `updatedAt DESC, appId ASC`; per-row frame + joint counts aggregated in one Cypher round-trip; `createdAt` + `updatedAt` shipped as nullable `@Property` fields on `DigitalTwinScene` (additive, no migration). Per-scene permission gate deferred to SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1 (matching the existing `SceneGraphRest` scaffold posture — every authenticated caller sees every scene). Frontend follow-up still queued: swap `/scene-graphs/index.vue` info-card for a real `v-data-table`. Cross-ref: SCENEGRAPH-NAV-01 (the compensating frontend), SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 (sibling endpoints), SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1 (permission walk dep). |
| SCENEGRAPH-NAV-02 | **"Open in scene-graph editor" / "Create scene from this URDF" button on the FileReference detail page.** Auto-detects scene-graph-eligible FileReferences via any of: `urn:shepard:scenegraph:scene-appId` back-annotation (definitive — scene already minted), any `urn:shepard:rdk:*` annotation (RDK file detected by `RdkTextScrapeParser`), any `urn:shepard:urdf:*` annotation (URDF parser plugin signal — forward-compat), or `.urdf`/`.rdk` filename fallback. When the back-annotation is present, the button routes to `/scene-graphs/{sceneAppId}`. The right shape is a one-click backend flow: parse the URDF from the FileReference content (reusing the script's `parse_urdf` logic in Java — likely an in-tree port until the planned `shepard-plugin-vis-urdf` lands and inherits it), POST one frame per `<link>`, register one joint per `<joint>`, and write the `urn:shepard:scenegraph:scene-appId` back-annotation on the FileReference. This row flips that modal into a real one-click flow: confirm button calls the endpoint, on 201 routes to `/scene-graphs/{newSceneAppId}`, on 409 routes to the existing scene's appId (the response body carries `existingSceneAppId`), on 4xx surfaces the error. Cross-ref: URDF-WEBVIEW-1 (renderer), SCENEGRAPH-REST-1-UI (the editor it augments), COLL-SCENE-2-UI (sibling — renders `export.urdf` on the Collection landing). The play envelope already carries `jointTimeseriesReferenceAppId` + `jointChannelBindings[]` when a TimeseriesReference is bound. Options: (a) backend `UrdfResolver` returns a manifest of signed mesh URLs keyed by `package://` path; (b) serve the bundle's mesh siblings from the FileBundleReference under a stable per-bundle prefix and point urdf-loader's `packages` there. Until this lands, multi-file robot bundles render frames-only (with the visible SCENEGRAPH-CANVAS-MESH-1 warning); the static kr210 public sample at `frontend/public/urdf-samples/kr210_r2700_2/` resolves because it's a static-asset path. | M | queued | MAJOR | spawned 2026-06-03 by SCENEGRAPH-CANVAS-1 — surfaced when feeding the kr210 source FileReference (which carries `package://` STL refs) through a blob URL. Cross-ref: SCENEGRAPH-CANVAS-1, URDF-WEBVIEW-1 (`UrdfResolver`), "UI never asks for paths/URLs" rule (the resolver owns URL resolution). |
| COLL-SCENE-1 | **Backend: Collection-level scene-graph link — `:Collection.sceneGraphAppId` + `/v2/collections/{appId}/scene-graph` (GET/PUT/DELETE).** Today a `:DigitalTwinScene` is reachable only via the scene's own appId on `/v2/scene-graphs/{appId}`. The Collection landing page has no hero-scene affordance, so the MFFD `MFZ.rdk`-derived URDF (the physical robot cell every track was made in) doesn't surface on the Collection that contains those tracks. This row adds a scalar `sceneGraphAppId` property on `:Collection` (additive nullable; V104 NOOP migration + rollback twin) + a dedicated REST surface. **Permission gate is two-sided** (per the 2026-06-02 advisor): GET requires Read on Collection; PUT requires Write on Collection AND Read on the target scene (writer-on-A must not blind-link a private scene B they cannot read); DELETE requires Write on Collection. Provenance is filter-driven (no handler-side `record()` — this is a cross-cutting mutation without domain-specific Activity enrichment). `CollectionIO` exposes `sceneGraphAppId` with `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` so v1 byte-fidelity is preserved. **The Collection→Scene link does NOT widen scene readability** — `SceneGraphPermissionService`'s walk (scene → FileReference → DataObject → Collection) stays the source-of-truth for the scene side. | S | **shipped 2026-06-02 (this PR)** | MAJOR | spawned 2026-06-02 from GAP-6 in `aidocs/agent-findings/mffd-feature-gaps-2026-06-02.md`. Files: `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/context/collection/entities/Collection.java` (+`sceneGraphAppId` field), `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/context/collection/io/CollectionIO.java` (wire exposure), `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/collection/{daos,io,resources}/CollectionSceneGraphLink*` (new), `backend/src/main/resources/neo4j/migrations/V104__Collection_scene_graph_link.cypher` + `V102_R__*` rollback. 18 JUnit cases on `CollectionSceneGraphRestTest` (GET 401/404/403/no-link/200/dangling + PUT 400/404/403-coll/403-scene/404-scene/200 + DELETE 401/404/403/204/idempotent). Cross-ref: SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1 (scene-side walk that stays canonical), GAP-6 (originating gap), COLL-SCENE-2-UI (paired FE landing). |

Writers see a "Link scene-graph" CTA when no scene is linked yet (opens a v-select picker pulling from `GET /v2/scene-graphs?size=200`), plus "Unlink" + "Replace" overlay actions when a scene is rendered. Readers on a collection with no link see nothing. Dangling pointer (link resolves but scene is gone) renders the existing `EntityNotFound` for the band. Wired into `frontend/pages/collections/[collectionId]/index.vue` at the very top of the page, above the heroImageUrl band. | S | **shipped 2026-06-02 (this PR)** | MAJOR | spawned 2026-06-02 alongside COLL-SCENE-1. ### M4I — 2026-05-23 (metadata4ing deepening)

User directive 2026-05-23: *"how to deepen metadata4ing (m4i) integration"*.
Design lands at `aidocs/semantics/94-metadata4ing-integration-design.md`.
**Critical finding** surfaced during research: the current
`ProvJsonLdRenderer` emits **non-canonical predicates**
(`m4i:hasMethod` / `m4i:hasInput` / `m4i:hasOutput`) that do
not exist in canonical m4i 1.4.0 — see M4I-b. Latest m4i
version is **1.4.0 (2025-12-08)**; the shipped bundle pins it
already, no version bump needed.

| ID | Slice | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| M4I-a | Promote `metadata4ing.ttl` stub → fetch canonical TTL on first seed via existing `canonicalUrl` + `fetchOnSeed` flag (NASA Thesaurus N1e pattern). Stub stays in JAR as offline fallback. Acceptance: ≥ 100 m4i `:Resource` rows in n10s after fresh start. | S (1 d) | **✓ shipped (2026-05-26)** | `aidocs/semantics/94 §4.1`. `fetchUrl: "https://w3id.org/nfdi4ing/metadata4ing/1.4.0/"` + `fetchFormat: "Turtle"` added to `ontologies-manifest.json` metadata4ing entry. w3id.org permanent URL 303-redirects to `nfdi4ing.pages.rwth-aachen.de` (~202 KB canonical TTL; ≥ 150 terms). Stub unchanged (SHA-256 pin intact) as offline fallback. New test `realManifest_metadata4ing_hasFetchUrl_andTurtleFormat` added to `OntologySeedServiceTest`. |
| M4I-b | **CRITICAL bug-shaped fix.** Rewrite m4i branch of `ProvJsonLdRenderer.render(...)` to emit canonical predicates: `m4i:hasMethod` → **`m4i:realizesMethod`**; `m4i:hasInput` → **`obo:RO_0002233`** (OBO Relations Ontology, already preseeded via ONT1a); `m4i:hasOutput` → **`obo:RO_0002234`**. Extend `@context` with `"obo"` prefix when profile=m4i. Add `m4i:investigates` from activity to investigated entity on read actions. | S | **done (2026-05-26)** | `aidocs/semantics/94 §4.2`. Verified against canonical OWL at `https://nfdi4ing.pages.rwth-aachen.de/metadata4ing/metadata4ing/` on 2026-05-23. Both code paths fixed (`renderActivityAsM4iNode` + `render(activities, m4i)`). `obo:` prefix added to `@context` when profile=m4i. 8 new canonical-predicate tests added; 5 existing tests updated to assert new predicates. SHACL self-test deferred to M4I-c. AWARE in `aidocs/34` flagged BREAKING for any m4i consumer that depended on the old non-canonical names (low real-world risk; no known consumer; discipline is the point). |
| M4I-c | `m4i:DataObjectShape` SHACL contract + `M4iDataObjectRenderer` + content-neg `Accept: application/ld+json; profile=metadata4ing` on `GET /v2/data-objects/{appId}` (mirrors PROV1h on `/v2/provenance/*`). Mandatory triples: `m4i:InvestigatedObject` type, `dcterms:identifier`, `dcterms:title`, `schema:dateCreated`. Optional: `m4i:hasIdentifier` (KIP1a PID), `obo:RO_0002233/4` (predecessor / successor), `prov:wasGeneratedBy` (most-recent Activity), `m4i:realizesMethod` (when actionKind known), `schema:keywords` (annotations). Refactor UH1b renderer to consume same shape. | M (3–4 d) | **✓ shipped (2026-05-28)** | `aidocs/semantics/94 §4.3`. Shape file at `backend/src/main/resources/shapes/m4i-dataobject-shape.ttl`; renderer at `v2/m4i/M4iDataObjectRenderer`; content-neg on the existing nested path `/v2/collections/{cid}/data-objects/{doid}` (flat shelf is L2 follow-up). UH1b convergence via shared `@context` constants + namespace alignment (see §12.3 of the design doc). pyShacl acceptance script at `examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/validate_m4i_shape.py` validates the LUMEN + MFFD seeds. 26 new `M4iDataObjectRendererTest` cases. |
| M4I-d | Promote `:Activity.actionKind` / instruments / annotations to first-class m4i individuals. Three sub-slices: **(d-1)** mint stable `shepard:method/<kind>` IRIs typed `m4i:Method` on first use; **(d-2)** mint `shepard:tool/<typename>` IRIs typed `m4i:Tool`, link via `m4i:hasEmployedTool`; **(d-3)** annotation keys matching SHACL `m4i:hasNumericalVariable` slot render as typed `m4i:NumericalVariable` blank nodes with QUDT unit refs. | M (3 d) | **✓ shipped (2026-05-28)** | `aidocs/semantics/94 §4.4`. All three resolvers ship as inner classes of `M4iDataObjectRenderer` (lazy, read-time). d-1: `MethodResolver.iriFor(actionKind)` → `shepard:method/<kind>`. d-2: `ToolResolver.iriFor(targetKind)` → `shepard:tool/<kind>`. d-3: `NumericalVariableResolver.toNode(SemanticAnnotation)` consumes the QUDT unit IRIs written by the channel-axes-and-units recovery script (2026-05-28) — but only for **direct DataObject annotations**. Channel-level numeric variables (the 95 `:AnnotatableTimeseries` annotations addressable via a multi-hop DO → ref → container → channel walk) are deferred to M4I-d-3-followup below. |
| M4I-d-3-followup | Channel-level `m4i:hasNumericalVariable` walk: enumerate each DataObject's TimeseriesContainers via the existing `findContainersByDataObjectAppId` Cypher, fan out to channel rows, resolve `:AnnotatableTimeseries` bridge nodes, and inline each channel's QUDT unit annotation as a nested `m4i:NumericalVariable` blank node under the parent DO. Acceptance: every MFFD channel with a `urn:shepard:unit` annotation (95 channels per the 2026-05-28 recovery script) appears as a typed variable in the parent DO's m4i projection. | M (2 d) | queued | `aidocs/semantics/94 §4.4 (d-3)`. Cost: one Cypher hop per DO at render time + per-channel batch fetch. Deferred from M4I-c/d v1 ship to control read-path complexity (see §12 board consultation). Pairs with the L2d flat-shelf migration when that ships. |
| M4I-e | Operator runbook `docs/reference/nfdi4ing-federation.md` + companion task page `docs/help/register-with-nfdi4ing.md`. Walk through: m4i bundle enable verification → Terminology Service self-registration → Unhide harvester pointing → (forward-looking) NFDI4Ing Archetype Doris registration for HPMC use cases. Pure docs, no code. | S (1 d) | **✓ done (2026-05-27)** | `aidocs/semantics/94 §4.5`. Pairs with `aidocs/integrations/67 §7`. Both pages shipped: `docs/reference/nfdi4ing-federation.md` + `docs/help/register-with-nfdi4ing.md`. |
| M4I-f | Add `metadata4ing-hpmc` bundle to preseed manifest — m4i HPMC sub-ontology for high-performance measurement + computing workflows. SHA-256-pinned TTL stub + canonical URL + `required=false`, default-on. Stub covers headline classes (`m4i:HpmcWorkflow` etc.); N1c admin refresh pulls full canonical OWL. | S (1 d) | **done (2026-05-27)** | `aidocs/semantics/94 §4.6`. Source: <https://nfdi4ing.de/5-23-2/>. Targeted (HPMC-using operators only — LUMEN hot-fire DAQ, MFFD AFP telemetry). Parallel to M4I-a. Transitive dependencies (NFDI-MatWerk / MaRDI) documented as forward reference in `sourceRationale` field of manifest entry. 5 classes + 3 properties in stub. `OntologySeedServiceTest` count assertions updated (17→18) + 2 new per-bundle tests added. |

Recommended landing order (per `aidocs/semantics/94 §9`):
**M4I-a → M4I-b → M4I-c → M4I-e → M4I-d → M4I-f**. Total ~10–11 days.
M4I-b lands first regardless of slice ordering — correctness, not deepening.

### DOC-STAGE — 2026-05-23 (doc-lifecycle taxonomy + tagging)

Cross-cutting documentation hygiene. The lifecycle taxonomy lives at
`aidocs/00-doc-stages.md`; the grouped index at
`aidocs/01-doc-stage-index.md` (auto-generated). CLAUDE.md carries the
"Always: tag every aidocs doc with a lifecycle stage" rule.

| ID | Slice | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| DOC-STAGE1 | Taxonomy doc (`aidocs/00-doc-stages.md`) + generator (`scripts/regenerate-doc-stage-index.py`) + tagger (`scripts/tag-aidocs-stages.py`) + retro-tag pass over ~210 `aidocs/**/*.md` files + CLAUDE.md rule + this backlog section + memory rule. Zero UNTAGGED leftovers; histogram printed at commit time. | M | done (this PR) | All `aidocs/**/*.md` carry YAML `stage:` front-matter. Retro-tags use existing `**Status.** …` lines + path heuristics; owner can refine any best-guess `fragment` defaults in follow-on. |
| DOC-STAGE2 | Pre-commit hook + CI step that runs `python3 scripts/regenerate-doc-stage-index.py --check` and fails on drift; same hook fails when a new `aidocs/**/*.md` lands without a `stage:` field. Wire into `.github/workflows/` (Pages workflow is the natural home — it already runs on doc PRs). | S | **done** | `.git-hooks/pre-commit` (tracked, `chmod +x`) + `scripts/install-hooks.sh` installer; `.github/workflows/doc-stage-check.yml` (triggers on `aidocs/**` + script path changes); `--check` mode already existed; added missing-stage detection with per-file `Missing stage: field in: <path>` stderr output + exit 1; fixed the one untagged file (`aidocs/data/46-gridfs-to-s3-migration-runbook.md` → `stage: concept`). |
| DOC-STAGE3 | Per-stage filter view in the Pages site — a `/doc-stages/<stage>` route listing every doc at that lifecycle stage with last-stage-change date. Driven by the same front-matter the generator parses. Useful for "show me everything ready for persona review" / "everything deployed but not yet covered by tests". | M | queued | Builds on `aidocs/ops/49-in-app-user-docs.md` (in-app `/help` route). Generator can emit a JSON sidecar (`aidocs/01-doc-stage-index.json`) to power the route without re-parsing markdown client-side. |
| DOC-ALIGN-STANDING | **Standing Documentation Aligner role** — periodic sweep over `aidocs/**`, `docs/**`, `plugins/*/docs/**` to fight doc sprawl: dead cross-references after path renames, shipped features still described as planned/queued, redundant SSOTs, stale prefixes, `stage:` frontmatter drift. **Cadence: ad-hoc when sprawl is suspected; quarterly minimum.** Each pass produces `aidocs/agent-findings/doc-alignment-<date>.md` covering edits applied + UNSURE proposals + ESCALATE items. Durable rules: `feedback_continuous_doc_maintenance.md` + `feedback_ssot_per_concept.md`. **First pass: 2026-05-28** (`aidocs/agent-findings/doc-alignment-2026-05-28.md`, branch `doc-align-2026-05-28`) — 32 dead-link incidence fixed, AI1b + SPATIAL-V6-001 + FS1b + M4I-a/b/f status flipped from "designed" to "shipped" across `roadmap.md` / `40-ecosystem.md` / `44-fork-vs-upstream-feature-matrix.md` / `docs/reference/plugins.md`. | recurring (~half-day per pass) | **first pass done 2026-05-28** | Next pass cadence trigger: when more than 50 dead-link incidence accrue or when an aidocs/16 ID is shipped but still appears as `📐 designed` in three or more live status docs. |


### SWEEP — 2026-05-23 (deferred items surfaced from repo grep)

Sweep result from a cross-cutting `grep -E 'TODO|FIXME|deferred|follow-up|out of scope|queued'` pass across `backend/`, `frontend/`, `plugins/`, `examples/`, `docs/`, `aidocs/agent-findings/`, `aidocs/{platform,integrations,ops,semantics,strategy,ux,workflows,reference}/*.md`, `.github/workflows/`, `Makefile`, and `backend/pom.xml`. 320 existing row IDs were extracted from this file and used as a dedup filter — every row below is **not** already on the backlog. Full inventory + source citations: `aidocs/agent-findings/repo-task-sweep-2026-05-23.md`.

Rationale: per `feedback_backlog_for_later.md` ("things-for-later go on a maintained backlog, not in prose docs") and `feedback_backlog_consult_context.md` (every row cites its source). The risk being closed is the slow rot where a `TODO(IMP1)` in `ImportV2Rest.java` or a "this is a follow-up" footnote in a design doc never becomes a row, and the deferral is forgotten.

Skew: 5 CRITICAL, 49 MAJOR, 17 MINOR, 14 NICE-to-have. Highest-priority item to ship: **`MFG5`** — enforce DataObject `status` as a closed enum at the persistence boundary. Quoting `aidocs/agent-findings/manufacturing-quality.md §11.2`: *"the single largest gap between Shepard's current design and aerospace quality record requirements"* — one backend PR, no migration on shipped data beyond a value-set check.

| MFFD-INFLUX1 | InfluxDB → Shepard timeseries export bridge for the MFFD v5 source side. | M | queued | MAJOR | `aidocs/integrations/92-mffd-real-data-import-strategy.md:244` "Track the gap as a follow-up MFFD-INFLUX-EXPORT item" | Today the 4 627 TS-reference stubs are imported empty; this row fills them. |
| MFFD-IMPORT-PERF1 | **Real worker fan-out for `run_source_mode_workers`.** Extract the per-DO loop body (`mffd-import-v15.py` L2495..2598) into a callable `_process_one_source_do(...)` and submit one future per source DO. Bound at 4 concurrent cube3 GETs + 4 dest POSTs per `aidocs/93 §5`. Today the wrapper is a fake fan-out — it spawns the pool, runs `lambda: True` probes, then calls `run_source_mode` SEQUENTIALLY (`mffd-import-v15.py` L2983..2999). `--workers N` does nothing for throughput. | M | **done (v15.8)** | MAJOR | `aidocs/agent-findings/mffd-import-slowness-diagnose-2026-05-23.md §5 Hypothesis #1` | Shipped 2026-05-23 in v15.8 (manifest published to FileContainer 473932). Per-DO body extracted into `_process_one_source_do(src_do, do_bar)` closure inside `run_source_mode`. Driver submits one future per source DO via `ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=workers)` with `as_completed`-based drain + refill keeping the pool saturated. `tqdm.update(1)` happens only in the driver thread. `ImportState` gains `_lock: RLock` covering all read+write ops. `existing_names` hoisted to per-step snapshot (no N parallel calls for the same dest list). `run_source_mode_workers` simplified to a thin forwarder; tests in `tests/test_worker_pool.py` updated. |
| MFFD-IMPORT-PERF2 | **Lazy enrichment in `iter_data_objects`.** Yield a stub `SourceDO(do_id, name, attrs, ...)` with `file_refs=None / ts_refs=None / sd_refs=None`; fetch refs only when the per-DO `state.is_*_done` skip check would not fire. Today (`mffd-import-v15.py` L707..711) the script fires 3 cube3 GETs per source DO BEFORE the L2504/L2541/L2570 state-skip checks. On a fully-resumed run that's 25 371 wasted WAN round-trips for an 8 457-DO collection. | S | **done (v15.8)** | MAJOR | `aidocs/agent-findings/mffd-import-slowness-diagnose-2026-05-23.md §5 Hypothesis #2` | Shipped 2026-05-23 in v15.8 (manifest published to FileContainer 473932). `SourceDO.file_refs / ts_refs / structured_refs` now default to `None`; `iter_data_objects` yields stubs (no enrichment GETs). New `ShepardClient._load_file_refs / _load_ts_refs / _load_structured_refs` helpers fetch + cache on first access via `SourceDO._src_coll_id`. Per-DO loop body calls these AFTER per-ref state-skip would short-circuit. Fully-done DO on resume: 0 cube3 GETs (was 3). |
| MFFD-IMPORT-PERF3 | **Preempt-friendly self-update apply.** After each completed source DO, check `if updater.update_event.is_set(): break` so the script gracefully exits to the `finally`-block self-update path (`mffd-import-v15.py` L4154..4157). Today the updater detects v15.7 at 60-s cadence (137 events) but the apply is gated by `run_source_mode` returning — operator sees "stuck on 15.6" for 2 h 17 min while the loop runs. Observability win, not a throughput win. | XS | done (2026-05-27) | MINOR | `aidocs/agent-findings/mffd-import-slowness-diagnose-2026-05-23.md §5 Hypothesis #3` | Implemented: `update_event: threading.Event | None = None` param on `run_source_mode` + `run_source_mode_workers`; break checks in sequential DO loop, worker drain loop, and outer step loop; passed as `updater.update_event` from `main()`. |
| MFFD-IMPORT-PERF4 | **Add `gauge_cube3_inflight_requests` + `gauge_cube3_p95_latency_ms` to the `Telemetry` class.** Today the operator's only signal that the script is "alive" is heartbeat cadence + `counter_dos_processed`. With these two gauges the OBS-MFFD1 chart UI surfaces whether the bottleneck is cube3-side WAN or dest-side write path. Pairs with `feedback_shepard_measures_itself.md` — observability lives IN Shepard's own TS substrate. | XS | done (2026-05-27) | MINOR | `aidocs/agent-findings/mffd-import-slowness-diagnose-2026-05-23.md §6 row 5` | Implemented: `Telemetry.gauge_inc()` + `record_latency_ms()` (p95 sliding window, deque maxlen=200); `ShepardClient.attach_telemetry()` + `_telemetry` field; `_request_with_retry` wrapped in try/finally: inc on entry, dec on exit, latency recorded per attempt; attached to `dest_client` + `src_client` in `main()`. |
| MFFD-IMPORT-PERF5 | **Workers=N footgun warning at startup.** Until MFFD-IMPORT-PERF1 ships, `--workers 4` is observably slower than `--workers 1` (it pays pool-spawn cost without buying parallelism). Print a stderr warning at L2964 ("⚠ workers > 1 is currently a no-op fan-out; see MFFD-IMPORT-PERF1") so the next operator doesn't repeat the diagnostic. | XS | **obsolete (v15.8)** | MINOR | `aidocs/agent-findings/mffd-import-slowness-diagnose-2026-05-23.md §6 row 6` | Dropped — MFFD-IMPORT-PERF1 (real fan-out) shipped in v15.8, so `--workers N>1` is no longer a no-op. The footgun is gone. |

| TPL-SEED-MFFD-1 | Built-in **MFFD process-step ShepardTemplates** — eight DATAOBJECT_RECIPE templates, one per step of the MFFD AFP→assembly chain (`AFP Layup`, `Ultrasonic Welding`, `Resistance Welding`, `Stud Welding`, `NDT Inspection`, `Frame Welding`, `Stringer Connection`, `LBR Cleats Assembly`). Each carries the per-process unique field set (TCP temperature + consolidation force for AFP; frequency/amplitude/energy for ultrasonic + frame; current/voltage/electrode-force for resistance; stud-type + pull-test for stud; inspection-method + result enum for NDT; joint-type + stringer-id for stringer connection; cleat-count + lbr-program-id + force-torque-profile-ref for LBR cleats) plus the common fields (process-type / step-number / equipment-id / operator-id / started-at / ended-at / notes / parent-collection-app-id). Body JSON embeds an `annotations[]` array with `urn:shepard:domain="aerospace-manufacturing"`, `urn:shepard:mffd:process-type=<canonical>`, `urn:shepard:mffd:step-number=<1..8>`, and (for CF/LMPAEK steps) `urn:shepard:material="CF/LMPAEK"` — pre-fill seeds for a future template-aware Create dialog. | S | **✓ done (2026-05-31)** | NICE | CLAUDE.md §"MFFD domain context"; project_mffd_domain_context.md; project_mffd_seed_demo.md | `V100__Mffd_process_templates_seed.cypher` (8 idempotent MERGEs keyed `{name, version: 1}`; `source: 'V100-mffd'` rollback handle). Annotations live in the body JSON for now; wiring them through `TemplateInstantiationRest` is tracked separately (TPL-INSTANTIATE-ANNOTATIONS-1). |

| AI1v-Phase-1 | **Channel unit auto-inference, deterministic floor.** New `ChannelUnitInferenceService` (in-tree CDI bean) maps a channel `field` name to a QUDT canonical IRI via three resolution tiers: SUFFIX (`_mm`, `_N`, `_bar`, …), PREFIX_HEURISTIC (`tc_`, `pc_`, `j1_…j7_`, …), WELDING_CAP (`CM_I` → Ampere, `W1_U` → Volt, …). Wired into the channel-creation write path inside `TimeseriesSemanticDualWriteService` so every new channel gets a `:SemanticAnnotation` with `propertyIRI='urn:shepard:unit'`, `valueIRI=<QUDT>`, `sourceMode='ai'`, `source='ts-channel-unit-suffix'`, and a tier-derived `confidence` in {1.0, 0.9, 0.85}. Ambiguous fields (`BridgePosition`, `valve_*`, welding-cap `_p` / `_t`) are logged at WARN and skipped. | S | **✓ shipped 2026-05-28** | MAJOR | user direction 2026-05-28; `examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/recovery/annotate-channel-axes-and-units.py` (the Python reference impl, 95 unit + 18 axis annotations on container 1772) | 59 unit tests (suffix, prefix, welding-cap, ambiguous-tail, edge cases) + 4 dual-write integration tests, sub-second. Backfill on existing un-annotated channels handled by the Python recovery script (already run against the live `shepard.nuclide.systems` MFFD data); a Java backfill migration is intentionally deferred -- new installs get the auto-inference from the first write. |
| AI1v-Phase-2 | **LLM resolution of the ambiguous tail.** When `ChannelUnitInferenceService.infer()` returns `Tier.AMBIGUOUS` (≈18% of LUMEN+MFFD channels today: `BridgePosition`, `valve_fuel`, `valve_lox`, welding-cap `_p` / `_t` variants), the AI plugin TEXT capability is invoked with the parent DataObject context (process step, instrument family, sibling channels) to propose a QUDT IRI. Output writes a `:SemanticAnnotation` with `sourceMode='ai'` and a real (non-1.0) `confidence`; UI accept-flip promotes to `collaborative`. | M | queued | MAJOR | this dispatch; AI1v-Phase-1 commit | **Gated on AI1a** (AI plugin plumbing). Phase 1 already emits a structured WARN per AMBIGUOUS channel, which is the input signal — once AI1a is live, Phase 2 consumes that warning stream (or re-scans periodically). |
| AI1v-Phase-3 | **SHACL `UNIT_MISMATCH` validator on `ShapesRenderResponseIO`.** View recipes can declare expected units (e.g. Trace3D wants `MilliM` on its x/y/z channels); the SHACL engine cross-checks the channel's `urn:shepard:unit` annotation against the recipe expectation and emits a typed violation when they disagree. Closes the loop on the "unit is just data" critique: once Phase 1+2 populate the annotation, Phase 3 makes it _validatable_. | M | queued | MINOR | this dispatch; M1-VIEWS-AS-SHAPES-WAVE | Gated on AI1v-Phase-2 + the M4I-d renderer pass that derives QuantityKind from the unit IRI. |
| STAGE1 | **Per-user data dump / staging area** — every authenticated user gets a personal staging space (a private, draft-shaped Collection or a sibling concept) where they can drop files, archives, raw exports, or scratch data without committing to a curated Collection yet. AI analysis plugins (FVF on micrographs, anomaly detection on TS, m4i auto-extraction on PDFs, channel-unit inference) fire against staged data automatically when an AI capability is registered. The user reviews results, then **promotes** items into shared Collections — staged items either move (one-shot) or remain as the read-only origin with provenance edges. The microsections-showcase is the canonical use case: researcher drops `Schliffbilder.7z`, FVF analysis runs per TIF, user accepts results into a sample-series Collection. Pairs with task #26 (ingest data from git) + AI1t (PDF auto-annotation) + AI1v (channel-unit inference) + J2 (JupyterHub plugin); all of those become "things that run inside the staging area" rather than separate top-level integrations. Operator-visible bits: `:UserStagingArea` Neo4j entity (one per `:User`); `GET /v2/me/staging` returns the area; `POST /v2/me/staging/files` uploads; `POST /v2/me/staging/promote` moves items into a Collection with PROV-O `wasDerivedFrom`. UI: a top-bar "Inbox" affordance + a /me/staging page. | M-L | queued | MAJOR | user request 2026-05-29 (microsections showcase) | The strategic positioning: shepard moves from "researchers create well-shaped Collections" to "researchers dump data and the platform helps them shape it." Reduces the cold-start friction the Reluctant Senior persona surfaced in their re-walk. Cross-cuts AI1 family + J2 + RDM-005. Open question for design: is staging area-shaped or collection-shaped? — first design dispatch resolves. |
