### GH-INFRA — 2026-05-23 (GitHub features integration)

Full survey: `aidocs/strategy/83-github-features-leverage.md`. The
guiding rule (also pinned in `feedback_github_features.md`): default is
"don't add a new GitHub feature unless solo-dev cost is near zero AND
external-contributor / security-posture value is high"; `aidocs/16`
stays SSOT — Issues are NOT a backlog mirror.

Decision matrix per feature lives in the survey doc above.

| ID | Slice | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| GH-INFRA1 | Templates + CODEOWNERS + Dependabot + canonical labels + release-notes scaffolding + PR template mirroring CLAUDE.md "Always:" rules + CONTRIBUTING.md "Filing issues" / "PR discipline" sections + the survey doc + this row. Pure config-as-code; no API calls; no live changes to the GitHub repo until merged + the operator actions below are taken. | M | done | Shipped 2026-05-23 on the worktree branch in one commit. Files: `.github/release.yml`, `.github/workflows/release-notes.yml`, `.github/workflows/sync-labels.yml`, `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/{config,bug-report,feature-request,docs-improvement,security-finding}.yml`, `.github/pull_request_template.md`, `.github/CODEOWNERS`, `.github/dependabot.yml`, `.github/labels.yml`, `docs/ops/cut-a-release.md`, `docs/ops/github-projects-board-setup.md`, `aidocs/strategy/83-github-features-leverage.md`, CONTRIBUTING.md additions. |
| GH-INFRA2 | Manually create the Projects v2 board following `docs/ops/github-projects-board-setup.md`. Operator-only; no code. Sets up columns (Backlog / In Progress / In Review / Blocked / Done), custom fields (Severity / Area / Effort / Stage / Aidocs ID), auto-add-to-project rule, and 5 saved views. | S | queued | Manual one-time setup. Picking up after GH-INFRA1 merges to main. |
| GH-INFRA3 | Cut `v6.0.0-rc.1` once the MFFD real-data import lands and aidocs/34 has the corresponding row. First release that uses `.github/workflows/release-notes.yml`. | S | queued | Gated on MFFD import landing. Follows `docs/ops/cut-a-release.md`. |
| GH-INFRA4 | aidocs/16 ↔ GitHub Issues sync tooling. **Gated on external-contribution velocity** — don't build until 5+ external contributors are filing issues per month. The duplicate-bookkeeping cost only pays back when there's real bilateral flow. | M | blocked | Blocking condition: external-contributor velocity threshold not yet met. Re-evaluate quarterly. |
| GH-INFRA5 | GitHub Environments for `nuclide.systems` and `DLR boxes` with required-reviewer deploy gates. Replaces the implicit "I just ran `make redeploy` in tmux" deploy step with a Github-tracked, audit-trailed deploy. | M | queued | Lower priority than GH-INFRA2/3. Pairs with the deploy-after-feature rule from `feedback_deploy_after_visible_features.md`. |

### GH-PM — 2026-05-23 (GitHub project-management policies)

Full policy: `aidocs/strategy/85-github-project-management-policies.md`.
The shape: `aidocs/16` is the SSOT backlog; GitHub Issues are the narrow
4-gate subset (external-pickup / security / repro-bug / agent in-flight);
sync direction is one-way (aidocs/16 → Issue); the unified state machine
maps doc front-matter ↔ aidocs/16 Status ↔ GH `status:*` / `stage:*`
labels ↔ Projects v2 columns byte-for-byte (see `aidocs/00-doc-stages.md`).
Releases cut on coherent-feature-set boundary with the §5 pre-flight
checklist. The end-state test is `scripts/trace-feature.sh <ID>` — nine
surfaces resolved per feature.

| ID | Slice | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| GH-PM1 | The policy doc itself + `scripts/trace-feature.sh` helper + CLAUDE.md "Always: file Issues + cut releases per `aidocs/strategy/85`" amendment + this aidocs/16 section + the `feedback_github_pm_policies.md` memory rule. Sets out the unified state machine, the 4-gate Issues filter, branch + commit + PR + milestone + release + label conventions, the §14 traceability query, and the §15 anti-patterns. | S | done | Shipped 2026-05-23 on the worktree branch in one commit. Files: `aidocs/strategy/85-github-project-management-policies.md`, `scripts/trace-feature.sh`, `CLAUDE.md` (one new "Always:" section), `aidocs/16-dispatcher-backlog.md` (this section), `/root/.claude/projects/-opt-shepard/memory/feedback_github_pm_policies.md`, MEMORY.md index entry. Validated end-to-end against `FS1b` — all nine surfaces resolved. |
| GH-PM2 | Wire `scripts/build-traceability-index.py` (TRACE-A) into `.github/workflows/pages.yml` so the rendered `docs/reference/traceability.md` updates on every push to `main` and ships on the GitHub Pages site. Operator-visible traceability — anyone can answer "what shipped under FS1b?" from a public URL. | S | queued | Gated on TRACE-A landing on `main` + `.github/workflows/pages.yml` being audit-trail-ready. Single-file workflow edit + one `python3` invocation. |
| GH-PM3 | Pre-commit hook (`.git/hooks/commit-msg` template under `scripts/hooks/`) that validates PR / commit message titles against the Conventional-Commits + aidocs/16 row-ID convention from §7. Rejects `feat(fs1b): …` (lowercase ID), `feat(FS1B): …` (wrong case), `feat: …` (no scope), and `feat(unknown-id): …` (ID not in aidocs/16). Opt-in (operator runs `make install-hooks`); recommended via CONTRIBUTING.md. | M | queued | After GH-PM2. The strict-uppercase rule is in §7 anti-pattern #4; the hook turns the soft rule into a hard gate locally before CI catches it. |
| GH-PM4 | Nightly drift check: cron-driven `gh issue list --search "in:title <ID-pattern>"` ↔ `aidocs/16` status diff. Surfaces orphan Issues (open with aidocs/16 row marked `done`) and orphan rows (`in-progress` with no Issue when one is expected per §3). Pure read-only; produces a daily report in `aidocs/agent-findings/issue-drift-<date>.md`. Gated on **external-contribution velocity** picking up (same gate as GH-INFRA4) — at solo-dev cadence the drift surface is empty and the cron noise isn't worth it. | M | blocked | Blocking condition: external-contributor velocity threshold not yet met. Re-evaluate quarterly alongside GH-INFRA4. |
| FOCUS-2026-05-23-2 | **Pages page: "Advisory board" — the 10 personas summarised as the project's standing AI-driven expert board.** One card per persona (Strategy Aligner, API Scrutinizer, RDM, IME/AQE, Ontologist, Ecosystem Advocate, AI Specialist, Reluctant Senior, Digital Native, Core Tech/UX). Each card: brief from CLAUDE.md (1 paragraph), the lens (1 sentence), the kinds of questions this persona fires on, last-fired date + the findings doc they produced, and the standing-reasoning-resource invocation pattern per `feedback_persona_audit_triggers.md §Addendum`. Lives at `docs/advisory-board.md` (Jekyll); cross-link from index footer alongside `/bibliography` and `/origin-myth`. First-references: CLAUDE.md §"Specialized agent roles", `feedback_persona_audit_triggers.md`, `aidocs/agent-findings/persona-*-gh-pm-2026-05-23.md`, `feedback_collaboration_process_rules.md`. | S | **✓ done (2026-05-27, found-already-shipped)** | `docs/advisory-board.md` fully populated with all 10 persona cards; `stage: deployed`, `last-stage-change: 2026-05-23`. Backlog row was stale. |
| FOCUS-2026-05-23-1 | **Backfill other project features in the same pattern as GH-PM5.** When the GH-PM5 backfill lands, the pattern (synthetic-but-transparent + captured-as-Shepard-dataset) generalises. Candidates: (a) persona findings on past designs (backfill personas reviewing already-shipped code, each with transparency marker); (b) bibliography entries for past commits where a cited source applies; (c) `:Activity` PROV-O rows reconstructed from git log; (d) release notes for shipped-but-untagged work pre-v6.0.0-rc.1; (e) backfilled snapshots on past collection states (G1 backfill on existing collections). Each batch follows: (1) transparency marker on every artefact, (2) capture-into-Shepard as a synthetic-batch Collection, (3) `fair2r:syntheticBackfill true` triple per DataObject. First-references: `feedback_no_synthetic_provenance.md`, `feedback_synthetic_artefacts_are_datasets.md`, `GH-PM5`, `GH-PM-DATASET`. | L (depends per candidate) | queued | **Captured during focus mode on "import script" 2026-05-23.** Off-topic from current focus subject; explicit row so the pattern doesn't get lost when focus ends. Each sub-candidate (a-e) gets its own row when focus on it begins. |
| GH-PM-DATASET | **Capture the GH-PM5 backfill batch INTO Shepard as a synthetic-artefact dataset.** Per `feedback_synthetic_artefacts_are_datasets.md`. Creates a Collection `gh-pm-backfill-synthetic-2026-05-23` on `shepard-api.nuclide.systems` with `synthetic_batch=true`, one DataObject per filed Issue + per Milestone, predecessor links to source aidocs/16 rows, `fair2r:modeOfProduction "ai"` + `fair2r:syntheticBackfill true` annotations on every DataObject, pointer to the generation-rule plan doc. Recursive: Shepard storing the audit trail of its own backfill operation inside itself, with the same provenance discipline as research data. | M | queued | Fires AFTER `GH-PM5` (#179) lands the live Issues + Milestones; the dataset captures the resulting URL + Issue numbers. Pattern generalises to future synthetic batches (next backfill, next persona-audit-batch, next bibliography regen). EU AI Act Art-50 alignment as a side effect. |
| GH-PM5 | **Backfill from `aidocs/16` ↔ Issues + Milestones + Releases on policy adoption, with mandatory per-artefact TRANSPARENCY MARKER.** Every backfilled Issue body opens with `🤖 **BACKFILL** — created retroactively YYYY-MM-DD as part of GH-PM5 adoption. Original work: commit <hash>, date <date>. Audit trail: <aidocs row + persona findings>.` Every Milestone description carries the BACKFILL marker. Persona findings backfilled review the SHIPPED artefact only; never claim retroactive prescience. Per `feedback_no_synthetic_provenance.md` in agent memory. | M | **done** | Executed 2026-05-23 alongside the §85 stage-flip. Backfill plan + execution log at `aidocs/agent-findings/gh-pm-backfill-plan-2026-05-23.md`. Milestones created: `v6.0.0-rc.1`, `v6.0.0-rc.2`, `v6.0.0`, `v6.x — backlog (no milestone yet)` — first three carry BACKFILL marker on their description. Per-Issue body opens with BACKFILL marker per §85 §8.1. Operator approval gate replaced by **artefact-level transparency-marker discipline** — the synthetic nature is disclosed in the artefact itself per `feedback_no_synthetic_provenance.md` refinement. Adds `meta:backfill` label to `.github/labels.yml`. |

### OBS — 2026-05-23 (recursive self-observability)

The principle is documented in `feedback_shepard_measures_itself.md`:
Shepard's own ingest-rate metrics live INSIDE Shepard's TimeseriesContainer
substrate, not in a parallel observability stack. The MFFD-Dropbox import
session was the first concrete instance — the chart updates by the same
TS chart UI a researcher already uses.

| ID | Slice | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| OBS-MFFD1 | `scripts/mffd-import-stats-collector.py` polls `/v2/collections/{appId}/data-objects` every 5 min, walks per-DO refs for file/TS/SD counters, runs `docker exec shepard-garage /garage bucket info shepard-files` for the object-store size, and POSTs 11 channels per tick to `TimeseriesContainer` 590324 linked to `DataObject` 590344 in the MFFD-Dropbox collection. Bootstrap is idempotent; the `TimeseriesReference` is lazily created after the first tick materialises channels (the v5 `Bug A` minItems:1 constraint, learned from v15.2). Ships a systemd timer pair (`infrastructure/systemd/mffd-import-stats-collector.{service,timer}`) so operators can replace the ad-hoc background process. Plottable via the existing TS chart UI — no new frontend code. | S | done | Shipped 2026-05-23 (this commit). Files: `scripts/mffd-import-stats-collector.py`, `infrastructure/systemd/mffd-import-stats-collector.{service,timer}`, `docs/help/observing-an-import.md`, this row, `aidocs/data/00-model-inventory.md §8` row, `feedback_shepard_measures_itself.md`. Verified: first tick at 23:07:24Z wrote 11/11 channels (`DO=8, files=0, garage=160300 B`); second tick at 23:08:19Z saw `TSrefs=1, channels=11` confirming the lazy reference creation. |
| OBS-MFFD2 | Generalise the collector to ANY import job: lift the MFFD-specific name-pattern table into a per-collection config + accept any collection-appId. Land as `scripts/import-stats-collector.py` (drop the `mffd-` prefix) plus an admin UI for picking which collections to instrument. | M | queued | Promotes the recursive-observability pattern to a first-class tool. Pairs with the `OBS-MFFD3` admin dashboard. |
| OBS-MFFD3 | Dashboard view summarising all in-flight imports: list every `ImportStats-*` DataObject across all collections + a `dos_total` sparkline + a delta-since-last-tick. Plugs into the existing Collections list or lands as `/v2/admin/imports`. | M | queued | "What's actually being ingested right now?" admin view. Dep on `OBS-MFFD2`. Pairs with the `GH-INFRA3` release gate. |

distinct audiences (admin, user, plugin) with the same-PR maintenance shape,
served from BOTH the Pages site AND Shepard's in-app `/help` route. The user
+ plugin audiences already have policies in CLAUDE.md; the admin audience
needs explicit consolidation.

| AGENT-WT1 | Standing tier-1 agent-dispatch protocol: when 2+ agents may touch overlapping aidocs paths (esp. parallel persona-audits or design-doc edits), use `EnterWorktree` for true isolation. Today's batch (LOGSTORE/PROMPT/ADMIN-STALE-CH/ONT-AI-MAP persona audits, dispatched in parallel) landed 3 deliverables under one commit because agents staged concurrently in the main worktree. Content correct, commit-message scope misleading. **Action:** update agent prompt templates to require `EnterWorktree` for tier-1 parallel dispatches; document in `feedback_autonomous_mode_protocol.md`. **Structural fix (2026-05-27):** agents launched with `isolation: worktree` get separate working trees — but if the agent then writes to absolute `/opt/shepard/...` paths rather than paths relative to its worktree root, the writes bypass worktree isolation and land directly in the main tree, defeating the purpose. Agent prompt templates must warn: use relative paths within the worktree, OR stage + commit before reporting. The `feedback_autonomous_mode_protocol.md` tier-1 checklist and `aidocs/strategy/85 §3` now carry this note. | S | ✓ done (2026-05-27) | Surfaced by `aidocs/agent-findings/persona-audit-admin-stale-ch-2026-05-23.md` E-Caveat (commit `681309a2` swept 3 audits + ontology-survey into one). Structural fix note added to backlog row, §85, and autonomous-mode protocol memory. |

| PROV-USER-ENRICH | **Wire `X-Source-User-*` headers → `:MirroredUser` in `ProvenanceCaptureFilter`.** SHIPPED 2026-05-26 (#234). When the MFFD v15+ importer forwards `X-Source-User-{Username,DisplayName,Email,Instance}` headers, `ProvenanceCaptureFilter` now (a) upserts a `:MirroredUser` node via `MirroredUserDAO.createOrUpdateBySourceKey`, (b) caches the result for 5 min in new `@ApplicationScoped` `MirroredUserEnrichmentCache` to avoid per-request DB round-trips, (c) stamps the resolved appId as `mirroredUserAppId` on the `:Activity` node via new 11-param `ProvenanceService.record(...)` overload, (d) backfills blank `firstName`/`lastName`/`email` on the local `:User` node from the display-name header. All prov enrichment is best-effort (WARN-logged, never fails the request). New: `auth/users/services/MirroredUserEnrichmentCache.java`; extended: `Activity.mirroredUserAppId` property, `ProvenanceService` 11-param overload, `ProvenanceCaptureFilter`. Tests: `provenance/filters/MirroredUserLookupTest.java` (11 cases). | M | **done (2026-05-26, #234)** | Source: 2026-05-23. Pairs with PROV-USER-MIRROR-ENDPOINT (#229), MFFD-IMPORT-USER-CAPTURE (header-forward v15.9). |

| MFFD-IMPORT-USER-CAPTURE | **v15.9 — script captures source-side user identity for prov enrichment.** SHIPPED 2026-05-23 in v15.9 (read-side + header-forward halves). Three forwarding channels: (a) per-DO attributes `source_user_username` + `source_user_displayName` + `source_user_email` + `source_user_instance` (visible/searchable in UI without dest plugin); (b) `X-Source-User-*` headers on every dest write (consumed by ProvenanceCaptureFilter PROV1a — no per-call-site threading, set once on the requests.Session); (c) telemetry event `source_user_resolved` (gauge 0/1) into runlog. Dest-side `:User` mirror step deferred — see PROV-USER-MIRROR-ENDPOINT (dest exposes no user-mint REST endpoint). Pattern card: `aidocs/integrations/95 §Pattern 17`. Tests: `examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/test_user_capture.py` (19 cases). Pairs with PROV-USER-ENRICH (dest-side header → PROV-O triple). | M | done (v15.9, 2026-05-23) | Read side ✓ + headers ✓ + telemetry ✓. Mirror channel split out as PROV-USER-MIRROR-ENDPOINT. v5 `/users/{u}` validated working on cube3 (kreb_fl shape) + nuclide (UUID-sub shape). Graceful degradation tested (5 fallback paths). |

REST: `…v2/admin/users/MirroredUserRest.java` (`@Path("/v2/admin/users/mirror")`, `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`). IO: `MirroredUserCreateIO` + `MirroredUserIO`. | LANDING-MY-DATA-GRAPH | **Personal landing page — "my data" graph view (distinct from the system-wide graph).** User flag 2026-05-23: *"different graph for users my data on landing page"*. The personal landing page (task #43, shipped) already carries Collections digest + sparklines + last-change + contributor list. Add a graph view scoped to **the current user's data** — distinct from any system-wide or per-Collection graph. Open shape questions for the design pass: (a) **what's "my data"?** — author / `createdBy=me`? `:CONTRIBUTED_TO` via Annotation / Lab-Journal entry? `:OWNED_VIA_PERMISSIONS`? Probably the union, with toggles in the UI; (b) **what kind of graph?** — lineage / predecessor-successor scoped to my-touched DOs (matches the §142 Trace3D family), or a treemap of payload-kind footprint (collections × kinds), or a temporal heatmap of my activity, or a forced-directed network of "DOs I created" linked via shared Collections / annotations / co-authors? My first instinct: **temporal heatmap (axis: days × Collections, intensity: activity count) + small lineage graph on hover** — sees more than a pie chart, scales to MFFD volume without breaking like the current dagre layout (memory: `feedback_reuse_trusted_code.md` flagged dagre as the wrong choice for MFFD-scale). Reuse the existing personal-landing-page substrate (#43 shipped); the new graph is one additional panel. Cross-references: shipped #43 (personal landing page), #21 (fun plugin: leaderboard), #43-style sparklines pattern, #25 (graph displays scale linearly — break at MFFD volume; this work must NOT regress that), #142 (Trace3D — shares the lineage-graph rendering substrate). | M | queued | Design decision needed before implementation: which "my data" definition + which graph kind. Reuse trusted graph libraries per `feedback_reuse_trusted_code.md` — d3.js or visx (not dagre at MFFD scale). The temporal-heatmap variant doesn't need a graph library at all (vue-cal + density colouring is enough), which is the leanest first ship. |

Rule shape captured in memory `feedback_admin_runbooks_pattern.md`: top metadata block, numbered steps, host indicator per step, expected stdout per step, rollback per step, end-state verification, version+sha256+commit-hash references. **Same-PR discipline**: when a new scenario gets fixed ad-hoc in a session, the runbook lands in the same commit. Honest-reconstruction: every block MUST have been tested or be a direct copy of an in-session command that worked. **First three (highest leverage)**: (a) generic cube hot-patch — covers every future v15.x deploy; (b) orphan-payload wipe + redrive — covers BUG-E/F/G class of failures going forward; (c) JWT rotation — every operator hits this at least once. | M | **done (2026-05-26)** | 10 runbooks shipped in `docs/admin/runbooks/01-10-*.md` + README index + `docs/admin/index.md` updated + `docs/ops/cut-a-release.md` converted to redirect stub. | WIZARD-JSON-INTERPRETABLE | **Process wizards emit a JSON manifest of every action taken.** User-flagged 2026-05-23 *"make process wizards jason interpretable - whatever that means"*. Today's UI wizards (Create-from-template, ImportManifest helper, AnomalyDetection setup, future spatial-region annotator) capture user intent in form-state, translate it into REST writes (POST Collection / POST DataObject / POST Annotation / link refs / set attrs), and produce no durable per-action record other than the resulting Shepard entities + the PROV1a `:Activity` audit trail. The gap: an external agent (Claude / MCP / another Shepard instance / a downstream automation) can't re-read what the wizard *intended* — only what it *produced*. **Spec:** every wizard run emits a `wizard-run.json` artifact attached to the resulting top-level entity as a FileReference (`name="wizard-manifest.json"`, attribute `kind:wizard-manifest`). Manifest shape: `{wizard_id, wizard_version, user, started_at, finished_at, form_inputs: {...verbatim form state...}, computed_decisions: [{predicate, value, source: "user|default|inferred"}], created_entities: [{type, appId, summary}], skipped: [{type, reason}], next_actions_suggested: [...]}`. Consumers: (a) MCP tools can read the manifest to answer "how was this Collection set up?" without reverse-engineering from the graph; (b) replay automation can re-run the same wizard with the same form state on another instance for templating; (c) provenance-export (RO-Crate / BagIt) carries the manifest as a first-class artifact; (d) AI assistants get a clean view of "the user clicked through this flow" without parsing the activity log. **Pairs with:** task #20 (Create-from-template — entry point already shipped), task #42 (template UX), task #51 (containerless basic mode), Importer's AgentContextIO / ImportManifestIO patterns from v15 (the wizard manifest is the UI-side dual of the importer-side manifest). | M | queued | Design decision needed: does the wizard manifest live as a FileReference on the entity (durable, queryable) or as a separate `:WizardRun` Neo4j node linked to the entity (graph-traversable)? Recommend file-attached: easier to round-trip, cheaper to read. Effort sized for one canonical wizard (Create-from-template) as proof-of-concept; subsequent wizards follow the same shape. |
| SD-WIPE-AND-REGET | **Wipe today's orphan SD containers + re-import SD payloads from source (preferred over redrive).** Operator decision 2026-05-23: rather than re-POST payloads to the existing 4 193 containers (the redrive path), DELETE them and let v15.13 + v15.14 re-create one container per process-step with the source-side SD content. Confirmed orphan (sample check 2026-05-23): containers 633520 / 634910 / 637000 all show `payload[]: 0` + `references[]: 0` + no back-link from any DO via the canonical retrieval path. Additionally confirmed BUG-F: containers 633520 + 634910 carry identical names (`Execution 2023-05-30 19:22:51/StepMetaProcessExecution`) created 33 min apart — same Execution, two containers, retry-induced duplication. **Workflow**: (1) deploy v15.13 (BUG-E fix) + v15.14 (BUG-F fix) on cube together; (2) freeze the importer (stop the wrapper); (3) DELETE all SD containers created on/after 2026-05-23T01:05 in dest (~4 193) via `DELETE /shepard/api/structuredDataContainers/{id}` in batches; (4) reset the SD-related state in the importer's `mffd-import-state.json` so SD entries re-run; (5) restart the importer — it now creates ONE SD container per step + posts payloads correctly + links references. **Safety**: the wipe is risk-free because the orphan containers have no back-references; if any container DID have a reference, the DELETE would 409/refuse (verify in dry-run first). **Cleaner than redrive because**: re-fetch from source is cheap (the source-side SD payloads are small JSON); the redrive script would need state to track which containers map to which (original-source-DO, original-source-SD-name) tuples, and v15.14 changes the container-id strategy anyway making the mapping invalid. | M | queued | Pairs with MFFD-IMPORT-BUG-E (v15.12 wrap fix) + MFFD-IMPORT-BUG-F (v15.14 container-reuse fix). Cleanup volume: ~4 193 SD container DELETEs + the Mongo collections they own. Operator gate: confirm "all four-thousand-something are orphan with no back-refs" before running the batch DELETE; the canonical-retrieval-paths memory note has the verification query. Supersedes the prior `SD-REDRIVE-AFTER-BUG-E` plan in MFFD-IMPORT-BUG-E. |
| MFFD-IMPORT-BUG-F | **v15.14 — SD container reuse: one SD container per process-step, not per-Execution.** Surfaced 2026-05-23 by operator clarification + dest-side count probe: MFFD-Dropbox has **4 193** structuredDataContainers created today vs **2 expected** (one for Tapelaying, one for Bridgewelding). The data model intent (operator-stated): one SD container per process-step; per-Execution distinction comes from `structuredDataOids[]` on the Reference, not from container proliferation. **Distinct from BUG-E** (v15.12 fix wraps array payloads to clear the `Document.parse` 500). BUG-F is a separate write-side logic bug: `upload_structured_payload(container_id, ...)` is called with a fresh `container_id` per Execution instead of reusing a per-step container. **Comparison data:** file containers (6 total, 1 today) and TS containers (12 total, 3 today) match the per-process model — the file/TS-reuse logic in `link_*_to_do()` is correct; the SD path doesn't reuse. **Fix sketch:** lookup/create-once a per-step SD container at the start of `run_source_mode` per source_map step, cache its id in the run scope, pass that id to every `upload_structured_payload(...)` for that step. After v15.14 + BUG-E redrive: each Execution's SD payload becomes a new oid on the same per-step container's `structuredDataOids[]` Reference on the per-Execution DO. **Cleanup work:** ~4 191 surplus orphan SD containers in Mongo from today's ingest — DELETE after redrive lands new oids on the correct per-step containers. | M | queued | Pairs with: MFFD-IMPORT-BUG-E (v15.12 wrap fix), SD-REDRIVE-AFTER-BUG-E (the payload redrive), MFFD-DB-AUDIT-REAL-DATA (cleanup of Mongo orphan-container surplus is part of that audit). Separately tracked because (a) BUG-F is a model bug, not a wire-shape bug; (b) the fix has a different shape (container-id lookup + caching) than BUG-E (payload-wrap); (c) the cleanup volume is much larger than BUG-E suggested. |
| LUMEN-DATASET-ENGINEERING-INTEGRATE | **Integrate the 4 LUMEN eLib publications' engineering content into the LUMEN showcase dataset (`examples/lumen-showcase/`).** Per operator request 2026-05-23 after the LUMEN eLib research landed in commit `248059c1`. The synthetic dataset currently models a generic LOX/LH2 hot-fire campaign; the real LUMEN program is **LOX/LCH4 expander-bleed cycle** per Traudt 2019 [@traudt_2019_lumen_status] and the 2025 test-bed paper [@hardi_2025_lumen_testbed]. Concrete enrichment work: (1) Update `data/generate.py` channel set to reflect LOX/LCH4 test-bed instrumentation patterns named in Hardi 2025 (oxygen-methane combustion stability metrics, expander-bleed cycle telemetry shape, turbopump fatigue indicators per Gulczynski 2023 + Riccius 2022). (2) Update propellant attribute on every TR-00x DataObject from `LOX/LH2` to `LOX/LCH4` to match real LUMEN. (3) Update the README + seed.py docstrings to cite the 4 eLib publications (bib keys: `traudt_2019_lumen_status`, `riccius_2022_lumen_turbine`, `gulczynski_2023_lumen_fatigue`, `hardi_2025_lumen_testbed`). (4) Add a `lumen_engineering_anchor` annotation on each TR-00x linking back to the relevant eLib record. (5) Keep the synthetic disclaimer load-bearing — the numbers stay deterministic from `numpy.random.default_rng(2024)`, but their *shape* now reflects published LUMEN engineering rather than a generic hot-fire abstraction. **Pairs with**: `aidocs/strategy/95-damast-space-transport-data-management.md §1` (the strategy doc where the LUMEN paragraph names these 4 publications); `project_lumen_is_damast.md` memory (LUMEN showcase = DaMaST operational demo). | M | **done** | Shipped in commit `feat(lumen-showcase): LOX/LCH4 engineering integration from eLib publications`. Propellant was already LOX/LCH4; added `turbopump_vibration_rms_g`, `turbopump_bearing_temp_degC`, `lch4_temperature_K` channels (Gulczynski 2023 + Hardi 2025). TR-004 anomaly extended to both vibration channels. Docstring citations added to `data/generate.py` and `seed.py`. README data-provenance table added. RNG seed unchanged. |
| MFFD-IMPORT-PROGRESS-ETA-CONTAINED-COMPLETENESS | **v15.13 — pre-flight totals banner + contained completeness pass at end of run_source_mode.** SHIPPED 2026-05-23 in v15.13. Three additions: (a) Pre-flight banner — before iteration, the importer counts every source collection and prints "PRE-FLIGHT TOTALS" with per-step + grand totals. Operators see denominator before any work starts; emitted as `warmup_step:pre_flight_totals` diag event with the full `source_map`. (b) Contained completeness pass — at end of `run_source_mode` the importer aggregates the per-step counters it already maintains (files uploaded/skipped/failed, ts imported/skipped/failed, sd imported/failed, duration_s) into a verdict struct (`_build_completeness_verdict`) and emits a `migration_completeness` diag event plus prints a human-readable banner (`_print_completeness_banner`) showing verdict (PASS / FAIL-PARTIAL / FAIL-EMPTY), per-kind totals, computed rate (DOs/min), and detected flags. Recognised flag shapes include `BUG-E-SHAPE-SD-100PCT-LOSS` (the v15.9-v15.11 signature — file uploads OK but every SD failed), `SD-PARTIAL-LOSS-NEEDS-REDRIVE`, `DO-COUNT-MISMATCH:<step>`, `PARTIAL-KIND-FAILURE:<step>`. (c) Artifact persistence — `mffd-completeness-<session>.json` written to `MFFD_COMPLETENESS_OUT_DIR` (default cwd), and best-effort upload as a `MIGRATION-COMPLETENESS-<SESSION>` DataObject in the destination collection (lineage anchor). No external script needed — completeness lives inside the importer process and uses its own runtime knowledge, no extra source/dest queries. Smoke-tested with synthetic 1400-DO BUG-E shape → correctly classified `FAIL-PARTIAL` + `BUG-E-SHAPE-SD-100PCT-LOSS` flag. **Pairs with:** standalone `mffd-completeness-check.py` (still available for forensic re-verification post-hoc). Triggered by user requests 2026-05-23 *"can you fold total info in importer as well"* + *"also completeness check"* + *"contained process"*. | M | done (v15.13, 2026-05-23) | Compile gate + version canary + verdict-aggregator smoke test ✓. Pure-function aggregator unit-testable. Best-effort upload (does not block on failure). Sibling: post-ingest DB audit MFFD-DB-AUDIT-REAL-DATA. |
| MFFD-DB-AUDIT-REAL-DATA | **Post-ingest DB audit with real MFFD data — antipatterns, bad design, best-practice gaps.** Queued 2026-05-23 by user: *"when we finally ingested data we will run databases audit again with the real data and look for antipatterns, bad design and implement best practices."* Triggered AFTER (a) v15.13 import completes the bridgewelding + tapelaying ingest, (b) MFFD-COMPLETENESS verdict reaches PASS or operator accepts PARTIAL, (c) destination warmed with real-data volumes (thousands of DOs, files in Garage, SDs in Mongo, TS samples in TimescaleDB, semantic annotations in Neo4j). Cross-substrate sweep: Neo4j + PostgreSQL + TimescaleDB + MongoDB + PostGIS + Garage. Builds on prior empty/synthetic-data audit pass (`aidocs/agent-findings/db-schema-research-*.md` + `db-antipatterns-*.md`). Look for: hot-path query patterns that need indexes (Neo4j label-property indexes, Mongo compound indexes, Timescale chunk pruning); Cypher patterns that don't scale (cartesian joins, expanding-bag traversals); SD container collection proliferation in Mongo (one Mongo collection per SD container — at MFFD volume that's potentially 1000+ collections); Garage object naming + bucket layout under real load; PG↔Neo4j duplication seams (where the same fact lives in both); SHACL-shape vs runtime-query mismatch (cf. `feedback_shacl_single_source_of_truth.md`); TS chunk + retention policy fit for MFFD cadence; permission lookup cost at depth. Output: `aidocs/agent-findings/db-audit-post-mffd-real-data.md` + per-substrate sub-findings. Pairs with `feedback_db_review_all_stores.md`. | L | queued | Dependency: v15.13 ingest complete + verdict PASS/PARTIAL accepted. Use the live data — no fixtures, no synthetic seeds. Cross-cutting findings (touch all six substrates) are the highest-value output. |
| MFFD-IMPORT-BUG-E | **v15.12 — fix SD payload BSON-array reject (root cause of 100% StepMetaProcessExecution failure rate).** SHIPPED 2026-05-23 in v15.12. Backend `StructuredDataService.java:53` calls `Document.parse(payload.getPayload())` which MongoDB's BSON model rejects with `readStartDocument...not when CurrentBSONType is ARRAY` when the inner JSON has an array at root. The v15.9-v15.11 importer was hitting this on every `StepMetaProcessExecution` SD upload because that payload IS a list. **Fix:** `upload_structured_payload()` wraps non-dict payloads in `{"items": [...], "_wrapped": "v15.12-bug-e"}` before `_json.dumps()`. Readers consuming MFFD SD payloads must unwrap the single `items` key when `_wrapped` marker present. Diagnosed via destination backend log grep (`f1a0a74d-7436-41a7-8dc6-9d13dc2d883e` reference) → exact exception type → traced to `StructuredDataService.java:53` `Document.parse()` call. **Sibling:** MFFD-IMPORT-COMPLETENESS-CHECK (post-migration verifier so we can confirm the wrap actually landed everything). **Operator action needed:** redrive the SD references already created with empty payloads (ID range 632242..current → all bridgewelding SDs that hit 500 since import started). Either DELETE + recreate or POST again to same /payload (since prior call never landed). Per-DO recovery scriptable from the source 5-tuple (collection + DO + container name → original SD payload). | S | done (v15.12, 2026-05-23) | Wrap fix + version canary + py_compile gate ✓. Cube hot-patch + main commit + manifest publish pending. Backend-side alternative (detect array-root + wrap before Document.parse) tracked as MFFD-SD-BACKEND-WRAP-FALLBACK. |
| MFFD-IMPORT-COMPLETENESS-CHECK | **Post-migration completeness verifier — `mffd-completeness-check.py` v1.0.** SHIPPED 2026-05-23. Standalone Python script (no dependency on the importer module — uses its own thin Client) that compares source vs destination Shepard instances per DataObject and emits a structured completeness report. Per-DO row: existence-in-dest + per-kind ref counts (files / TS / SD / fileBundle / video / image / anomaly / semantic / children) + blocking kinds. Output: machine-readable `mffd-completeness-<session>.json` + human-readable `.md` + uploaded as a first-class `MIGRATION-COMPLETENESS-<session>` DataObject in the destination Collection (lineage anchor for the verification artefact itself). Exit codes 0=PASS / 1=PARTIAL / 2=MISSING-DOS / 3=CONN-FAIL — usable in CI / cron / wrapper-finish hook. Match strategy: `attributes.source_id` → `attributes.source_appId` → `appId` fallback (handles current + future enrichment shapes from MFFD-IMPORT-SOURCE-BY-ATTRS). Failure-class histogram (top 20) + first-50-failing-DOs table in MD output for fast triage. Triggered by the user request 2026-05-23 *"implement some kind of completeness check to verify migration when done (log this artifact)"*. Logs `SUMMARY: {...}` JSON line on stdout for downstream log scraping. | S | done (v1.0, 2026-05-23) | Path: `examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/mffd-completeness-check.py`. Designed to run from wrapper after `mffd-import-v15.py` declares "done" — wire into `mffd-runner.sh` post-loop. Sibling backlog rows: SD-REDRIVE-AFTER-BUG-E (replay affected SD payloads), COMPLETENESS-CHECK-AS-CI (run nightly on prod for drift detection). |
| MFFD-IMPORT-DIAG | **v15.11 — structured diagnostic instrumentation for self-diagnosable failures.** SHIPPED 2026-05-23 in v15.11. Adds `DiagSink` class emitting one JSON line per event to stderr (atomic via single `os.write(2, ...)` under module-level lock, ≤4096 bytes = Linux PIPE_BUF), tee'd into the existing log file and mirrored into the existing `Telemetry` channel (best-effort, never breaks emit). New `--diag-mode={quiet,normal,verbose,http-trace}` flag (default `normal`; env `MFFD_DIAG_MODE`). 18 event kinds (startup, warmup_step, source_user_resolved, iter_start/end, do_start/skip/done/error, http_request/response, worker_pool, jwt_status, manifest_poll, state_save, telemetry_flush, summary, shutdown). Auto-classified diagnostic hints from a pure-function `classify_error(status, body)` → 9 hint codes (jwt-expired-or-rotated, permission-denied, not-found, conflict, rate-limited, bean-validation, client-error, server-side, timeout-or-network) with human-readable actions. Per-DO correlation id (`<step>:<src_do_id>`) ties every related event together. Non-2xx http responses ALWAYS emit regardless of mode — failure signal must not be filterable. Credential masking (`mask_credential` → `sha256:<first-12-hex>`) with explicit plaintext-leak guard test. Shutdown event carries the final 60s rolling-window summary. 36 new pytest cases in `tests/test_diag.py` (wire shape, 8-thread atomic-write concurrency, mode gating, all 9 error classes, JWT decode well-formed + malformed, credential mask + leak guard, summary aggregation, payload truncation, PIPE_BUF overflow marker, telemetry-mirror best-effort, version constant). Pattern card: `aidocs/integrations/95 §Pattern 19`. Closes the "2 AM debugging requires code spelunking" failure mode by making logs alone sufficient to diagnose any of: wire-shape errors, 401/403/404/409/429, rate limits, worker deadlock, JWT expiry, lock contention, state-file inconsistency, source-side missing content, Quarkus bean-validation violations. | M | done (v15.11, 2026-05-23) | DiagSink ✓ + 18 event kinds ✓ + classifier ✓ + masking ✓ + atomic-write concurrency test ✓. Sibling row: LOG-AS-PROV-ARTEFACT (dest-side consumption of diagnostic events into PROV-O `:Activity`). |

One DO per importer version (v15.0..v15.9..) with `git_commit_sha` + `git_blob_url` attrs + Predecessor chain. One FileContainer with conversation-chunk fragments (`convo-chunk-NN-for-v15X.md`). Spec in `/root/.claude/projects/-opt-shepard/memory/project_importer_arc_as_dataset.md`. | UX-REVERT-MUTATIONS | **First-class "undo" for data mutations — user-facing revert of changes / mutation passes.** Companion to the Goldilocks-shaping principle (`project_mffd_dest_backfill_pass.md`, `project_dataset_forging.md`): if mutations are iterative ("juuuuuuuust right"), then UNDO must be first-class. Every mutation that lands via the v2 API is recorded as a typed `:Activity` (PROV-O) with input/output state references; the UI exposes a "history" panel per DataObject / Collection / Container showing N most recent mutations with one-click "revert this change" (returns to the pre-mutation state) and bulk "revert N passes" (back to S(N-k)). Plays nice with the snapshot chain S0→S1→...→Sn that the back-fill pattern produces. Pairs with PV1a payload version history (already shipped — extends from "file payload versions" to "attribute + reference + structure mutations"). Probably needs a v2 endpoint `POST /v2/{resource}/{appId}/revert?to=activityAppId` + `GET /v2/{resource}/{appId}/mutations` (list). | L | queued | Source: 2026-05-23 user flag ("for backlog, easy way to revert changes / mutations for users"). Companion to MFFD-IMPORTER-DATASET-CAPTURE + the dest backfill pattern. Critical for trusting AI-driven mutation passes — user can experiment + back out safely. EN 9100 audit angle: the revert trail IS the audit trail. |

| TPL2c | **Live channel resolution for `POST /v2/shapes/render`.** When the backend processes a render request it walks the focus DataObject's Neo4j graph (`DataObject → TimeseriesReference → TimeseriesContainer`) then probes Postgres via `TsChannelResolver` to match each declared `channelSelector` against real channel rows. Each binding returns `RESOLVED` (+ `containerAppId` + `channelShepardId`), `UNRESOLVED` (+ reason: `CHANNEL_NOT_FOUND` / `NO_TS_CONTAINERS` / `INVALID_SELECTOR`), or `AMBIGUOUS` (+ list of matching `containerAppIds`). Frontend `render.vue`: manual container-ID field becomes `v-if="someBindingsNeedManualContainer"` (hidden when all resolved); `fetchBulkTrace` prefers resolved pointer; AMBIGUOUS bindings surface a disambiguation picker. New classes: `ChannelResolutionService` + `TimeseriesReferenceDAO.findCandidateContainers()`. Updated IO: `ResolvedChannelIO` gains `containerAppId + channelShepardId + channelTuple?`; `ChannelBindingProjectionIO` gains `unresolvedReason? + ambiguousContainerAppIds?`. Status vocabulary: `DECLARED` \| `RESOLVED` \| `UNRESOLVED` \| `AMBIGUOUS` \| `DIRECT` (frontend-only). | NETCDF1 | **Plugin: shepard-plugin-netcdf — NetCDF / CF-conventions payload kind.** Self-describing multidimensional arrays with embedded metadata; dominant in atmospheric science, oceanography, climate, AFP thermal-trace exports, CFD post-processing. Build as `shepard-plugin-netcdf` (plugin-first per CLAUDE.md §2 PayloadKind heuristic). Library: UCAR netCDF-Java (BSD-3-Clause) for read, h5netcdf or netCDF4-python for sidecar tooling. Browse: dimensions + variables + attributes per file via REST. Stream: HDF5-S3 access pattern (netCDF-4 is HDF5-based — same chunked-streaming substrate as A5). CF-conventions metadata maps cleanly to `m4i:numericalVariable` + QUDT units (cf-units library). Render hooks: per-variable timeseries extraction + grid-section view for spatial vars. CF-compliance check on upload (warn don't reject). Companion to HDF5 plugin (A5 shipped) — NetCDF is "HDF5 with conventions"; in many cases the same file unpacks via either lens. SP1 spatial plugin + Trace3D view recipe (#142) are downstream consumers. | L | queued | Source: 2026-05-23 user flag ("backlog netcdf integration"). Pairs with A5 HDF5, SP1 spatial. Plugin-first per CLAUDE.md §2. m4i NFDI4Ing alignment via cf-conventions ↔ metadata4ing variable types. CF-conventions is the de-facto standard the scientific community already uses — adopting it directly = ADOPT-VOCAB per the reuse-before-reimplement rule. |

**🛈 Clarification 2026-05-23 (user flag "none are for direct reuse - informational only"):** The UNAS code-drop projects are **informational only** — patterns + design references + field-confirmation that ZLP needs the integration shape. Do NOT propose direct code-reuse / dependency adoption from these zips. They inform the *shape* of what to build, not the *substrate* to build on. Treat the listed projects as "see how others solved this" rather than "import this library." Reuse-before-reimplement still applies — but the reuse decision goes through fresh upstream OSS evaluation, not the UNAS snapshot.

| PERM-SYSTEM-REVIEW | **Permission system review — consistency, inheritance, ACL vs RBAC vs alternatives.** Today's Shepard permissions are a per-entity ACL bolted on: each entity (Collection, FileContainer, DataObject, ...) carries `{owner, permissionType: Private\|Public, reader[], writer[], readerGroupIds[], writerGroupIds[], manager[]}`. **Live failure modes observed 2026-05-23** (RoboDK dropbox setup): (a) FileContainer created via REST defaults to `Private` despite being inside a `Public` Collection — no inheritance; (b) DataObject has no per-entity permissions endpoint (`GET /collections/{id}/dataObjects/{did}/permissions` 404) so write authorization is unclear / inconsistent with FileContainer pattern; (c) `createFileReference` fails opaquely when either DO or FC denies write — error toast literally reads "Error while createFileReference:" with empty message after colon (the UX-FRIENDLY-ERRORS symptom); (d) ~~per task #148, DataObject creation doesn't seed a Permissions record at all, causing 403 cascades~~ **CORRECTED 2026-05-24** (see `aidocs/agent-findings/bug-148-do-perms-seeded-2026-05-24.md`): DataObjects are **designed** to not seed own Permissions; access inherits from parent Collection via `PermissionsService.isAccessAllowedForDataObjectAppId` (lines 291-329 of `PermissionsService.java`). Live verified all 17,149 DOs lack `:Permissions` and all writes succeed via the parent-Collection walk (HTTP 200/201, zero 403s). The RoboDK 403 cascades trace to sub-bullet (a) — FileContainer not inheriting parent Collection's `Public` setting — not anything DO-related. **BUG-148 / task #148 → close as works-as-designed.** **Design question to resolve**: (1) **Pure ACL** (current shape, fix inheritance + add DO endpoint) — minimal change, retains owner-centric model, but every entity carries its own list; doesn't scale to project-level orgs. (2) **RBAC** (roles: viewer, contributor, curator, admin; scoped to Collection) — projects assign roles, members inherit. Clean for institutional use; loses fine-grained per-DO override. (3) **RBAC + ABAC overrides (PBAC, policy-based)** — RBAC as default, per-entity policy lets `embargo_until: 2027-01-01` or `accessRights: restricted access` short-circuit role grants. Best fit for FAIR + EN 9100 needs (embargo, license-gated access, NCR-confidentiality), but complex. (4) **Capability-based / Macaroons** — bearer tokens carry scoped capabilities (read coll-X-until-2027 + write fc-626776). Composable, delegatable, but breaks the "user-in-list" UI assumption. **Recommendation to investigate** (audit before implementation): RBAC core + ABAC overlay (option 3). Default role grants from Collection; per-entity policies layer on license / embargo / access-rights from LIC1. Pairs with TPL10 DQR (DataQualityRequirement) which can carry policy. The 5 acceptance lenses: (A) auditor-ready audit trail per access decision (PROV1a captures the decision; the role + policy at decision time); (B) onboarding a new contributor takes ≤ 2 clicks (assign role, done); (C) embargo enforcement is a property not a one-off ACL entry; (D) inheritance: child inherits parent unless explicitly overridden; (E) all four UI badges (🌐 Public / 👁 Restricted / 🔒 Closed / ⏱ Embargoed) compute deterministically from role+policy without per-entity ACL lookup. Audit deliverable: `aidocs/platform/<n>-permission-system-review.md` with reuse survey (existing FAIR-aware ACL libraries: Keycloak Authorization, OPA, Cedar (AWS, Apache-2.0) — Cedar particularly notable as it's purpose-built for ABAC-on-RBAC), prior art (CKAN dataset access, Coscine + RWTH; Dataverse role assignments; openBIS spaces+projects), and decision matrix. | L | queued | Source: 2026-05-23 user flag with screenshot evidence ("permission system review for more consistency - inheritance, discuss ACL or RBAC or alternatives") + live failure on RoboDK FC. Pairs with #148 (Permissions bug) + UX-FRIENDLY-ERRORS (the opaque error) + LIC1 (license field needs policy enforcement) + TPL10 (DQR). GH #1557. |

| BUG-UI-FILEREF-EMPTY-OIDS | ✓ shipped (eba9be1a9) — **UI bug: createFileReference POSTs with empty `fileOids` array.** Fixed in `fix(frontend): file upload — await createFileReference + surface empty-oids honestly`. Guards in both `useCreateFileReference.ts:20-30` and `DataObjectFileUploadDialog.vue:113-123`. Live repro 2026-05-23: user opens DataObject → "Add data reference" → picks a 12.1 MB .rdk file → picks existing FileContainer → clicks Upload → 400 `fileOids must not be empty`. The 2-step backend flow is: (a) POST file content to `/shepard/api/fileContainers/{cid}/payload` returning fileOid, (b) POST `{name, fileContainerId, fileOids: [oid]}` to `/collections/{cid}/dataObjects/{did}/fileReferences`. Either step (a) is failing silently and step (b) fires anyway, OR step (b) doesn't await the step (a) Promise. Frontend file: search `frontend/components/dialog/AddDataReferenceDialog.vue` or similar; also `frontend/composables/api/useFileReferences.ts`. Likely fix: chain `await uploadToContainer(file) → const oid = response.oid → createFileReference({fileOids: [oid]})`. UX-FRIENDLY-ERRORS would surface this honestly (the error toast literally truncates to "Error while createFileReference:"). | M | queued | Source: 2026-05-23 screenshot evidence + curl-traced 400 reproduction. Workaround: 2-step curl-upload (literal command in commit body). Pairs with UX-FRIENDLY-ERRORS + #135 file upload progress + PERM-SYSTEM-REVIEW (this is the kind of failure mode the permission audit must include). |

| LOG-ENHANCEMENT | **Enhance logging on Shepard for debuggability.** Today most v2 endpoints log at minimal verbosity; constraint-violation rejections + 4xx responses don't include actionable context (which user, which collection, which payload shape). Audit shape: (a) structured-log every request boundary (method, path, user appId, http status, latency, body-shape-hash) at INFO; (b) every Activity-mutating call gets DEBUG-level pre/post-state diffs (the data needed for the prov-artefact angle below); (c) admin-facing log viewer under `/v2/admin/logs?since=...&level=...` (per UX-aided diagnose-by-self) — gates behind instance-admin role; (d) wire to VictoriaLogs sidecar (LOGSTORE design `aidocs/integrations/94`); (e) sensible defaults: INFO in prod, DEBUG togglable per-request via `X-Debug-Token` header for support sessions. Pairs with #115 (comprehensive logging pass for createGitReference). | M | queued | Source: 2026-05-23 user flag ("backlog item to enhance logging on shepard so debugging becomes easier"). Pairs with UX-FRIENDLY-ERRORS + LOGSTORE design. |
| BUG-148-DESIGN-ASSERT-TEST | **Lock the DataObject-permissions inheritance contract into a regression-anti-test.** BUG-148 (2026-05-24) was a misdiagnosis ("DO creation should seed a Permissions record"); the real design is that DOs inherit from parent Collection via `PermissionsService.isAccessAllowedForDataObjectAppId` (lines 291-329) and the 3-arg fallback overload (lines 276-289). Anti-regression test added to `DataObjectServiceTest.dataObjectHasNoDirectPermissionsNode_inheritedFromCollection`: asserts `permissionsDAO.createOrUpdate` is never called during `createDataObject` — the test fails if a future agent adds per-DO Permissions seeding. Pairs with PERM-SYSTEM-REVIEW (the test asserts the *current* contract; PERM-SYSTEM-REVIEW is free to change it). | XS | done (2026-05-26) | Source: `aidocs/agent-findings/bug-148-do-perms-seeded-2026-05-24.md` §Test results. Test: `DataObjectServiceTest#dataObjectHasNoDirectPermissionsNode_inheritedFromCollection`. |
| V14-WHERE-CLAUSE-TIGHTEN | **V14 orphan-permissions backfill WHERE clause is too broad.** `V14__Backfill_orphan_permissions.cypher` line 44-45 matches `(e:BasicEntity) WHERE NOT (e)-[:has_permissions]->(:Permissions)` — but DataObject + BasicReference ARE BasicEntity nodes designed to have NO direct Permissions (they inherit from parent Collection). V90__Tighten_orphan_permissions_backfill.cypher removes any such incorrectly-attached Permissions (legacyBackfill='A0-V14') from DataObject/BasicReference nodes, then DETACH DELETEs the orphaned Permissions node if no other incoming edges remain. Rollback V90_R is a documented no-op (restoring incorrect Permissions would re-break the inheritance walk). | S | done (2026-05-26) | Migration: `V90__Tighten_orphan_permissions_backfill.cypher` + `V90_R__Rollback_Tighten_orphan_permissions_backfill.cypher`. Pairs with PERM-SYSTEM-REVIEW + BUG-148-DESIGN-ASSERT-TEST. |
| LOG-AS-PROV-ARTEFACT | **Shepard logs (processed) as PROV-O artefact describing mutations.** Distinct from LOG-ENHANCEMENT but compose-able with it: every Shepard log record that describes a mutation (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) becomes a typed `:Activity` in the prov substrate — `prov:wasAttributedTo` (user), `prov:used` (input refs), `prov:generated` (output refs), `prov:atTime` (timestamp), `shepard:operation` (verb+path), `shepard:resultStatus` (http). Processing pipeline: VictoriaLogs sidecar ingests raw logs → a normaliser (plugin? sidecar? in-tree filter?) extracts mutation events → writes :Activity nodes to Neo4j. **Recursion**: every Shepard mutation thus gets two trails — the raw log (operational) and the typed prov node (semantic). Activity diff = log diff projected through PROV-O. The case-study value: a queryable "what actually changed and why" surface that survives long after the log retention window. Pairs with PROV1a (ProvenanceCaptureFilter — already captures mutations, but as filter-time records, not log-processed) — open question: dual-write vs. consolidate to one path. | L | queued | Source: 2026-05-23 user flag ("shepard logs (maybe processed) as prov artefact describing mutations"). Pairs with LOG-ENHANCEMENT + LOGSTORE design + PROV1a + the f(ai)²R recursion principle. |

| MOBILE-PWA | **Shepard frontend as a Progressive Web App (PWA) for mobile.** Researchers + auditors increasingly check Shepard from phones / tablets — esp. on the shop floor (per the manufacturing-quality persona's ruggedized terminal requirement). PWA scope: (a) web app manifest with Shepard icons + theme colours (DLR branding works on dark mobile splash); (b) service worker for offline-first cache of recently-viewed Collections + DataObjects (read-only when offline); (c) install-to-homescreen prompt for repeat visitors; (d) push notifications wired to NTF1 (in-app + email + now mobile push); (e) responsive layout audit on existing pages — DataObject detail page already broken at 4K (#139), small-screen also needs attention; (f) the 5 task pages from docs/help/ get a "save for offline" capability so the help is available when network is dodgy. Reuse: Nuxt 3 has a `@vite-pwa/nuxt` module that wires most of this. Constraint: don't break SSR; PWA must work alongside Nuxt's existing auth + nitro routes. | L | queued | Source: 2026-05-23 user flag ("note for later can we make Shepard mobile a pwa"). Pairs with #36 card-style theme + #117 a11y + NTF1 notifications + manufacturing-quality shop-floor IME requirements. |
| VIEW-RECOMMENDER | **AI-assisted view recommender: "what should I look at next?" by context + user needs.** A Collection page surfaces dozens of DataObjects + payload kinds; a researcher arriving cold doesn't know where to start. Recommender shape: (a) signals captured — current page context (Collection / DO / Container kind), recent activity (last 10 viewed across sessions), user role + project tags, time-of-day pattern; (b) candidate views generated from `aidocs/semantics/98 §2` VIEW_RECIPE templates filtered by what the data supports; (c) ranking by relevance — semantic-embedding similarity (pgvector already in stack) between candidate view + user-history embedding; (d) UI: a "Suggested next" carousel above the fold on Collection landing pages; (e) feedback loop — user clicks → boost; user dismisses → demote; per-user learning kept in their profile. Plugin shape: ships as **shepard-plugin-ai** extension (per project_ai_data_arranger.md). For Claude/agent clients, exposed as an MCP tool `suggest_next_view(collectionId, userContext)`. EU AI Act Art-50 transparency: every recommendation labelled `🤖 suggested` with a "why" tooltip. | M | queued | Source: 2026-05-23 user flag ("view recommender for data ? by context and user needs"). Pairs with M1-VIEWS-AS-SHAPES-WAVE (VIEW_RECIPE substrate) + shepard-plugin-ai + project_ai_human_collab_provenance.md. |
| VIS-GANTT | **Gantt chart view for process timelines.** User flagged 2026-05-23 ("gannt was mentioned before very useful") — confirmed. Use cases: MFFD AFP layup process steps (sequential ply layup → consolidation → NDT → rework loops → sign-off) rendered as a Gantt with timing bands; LUMEN test campaign sequence (TR-001 → TR-015 with the TR-004 anomaly investigation branch); EN 9100 audit timeline ("show me everything that happened to this NCR between 2024-03 and now"). Ships as a `templateKind: VIEW_RECIPE` per `aidocs/semantics/98 §2.1` — same shape as Trace3D (#142). Reuse: dhtmlx-gantt (open-source GPLv2 variant; commercial license required for proprietary), Frappe Gantt (MIT — preferred for the license-compatibility policy), Vis-Timeline (Apache-2.0), or build on Vuetify's existing date-range primitives. Recommend Frappe Gantt for license; needs to handle: dependencies (Predecessor/Successor edges → arrows), milestones (DataObject status transitions), rework loops (arrows that go backwards), and audit annotations (badges on bars). | M | queued | Source: 2026-05-23 user flag (confirmation of earlier mention). Pairs with #142 Trace3D, M1-VIEWS-AS-SHAPES-WAVE, manufacturing-quality EN 9100 NCR audit trail. |

Scope: (a) audit what AAS code still sits in `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/aas/*` (if any) vs `plugins/aas/`; (b) propose extraction PR sequence; (c) coordinate with AAS-REUSE-AUDIT (which picks the underlying stack — BaSyx vs FAAAST). Pairs with AAS-REUSE-AUDIT + AAS1 umbrella + the plugin-first CLAUDE.md rule. |

| HTML-TO-MD-MIGRATION | **Migrate all rendered fields from HTML to Markdown — combined with block-editor (editor.js / BlockNote) research.** User flags 2026-05-23: ("move from html rendering to markdown in all fields /migrate") + ("combine with editor js research"). The migration target is not "markdown text in a textarea" but a **shape-driven block editor** where each block is a typed Shepard individual, serialised to markdown for storage. **Phase A — data migration**: (a) inventory HTML-bearing fields backend (`description`, `notes`, attributes, lab-journal entries, ref comments) + frontend (`v-html`, contenteditable, rich-text); (b) HTML → markdown via `turndown` on read OR one-shot Cypher/SQL; (c) markdown sanitiser tests (XSS, scripts, styles, malicious links); (d) export round-trip test. **Phase B — block editor**: (e) pick base — **editor.js** (MIT, flexible blocks) vs **BlockNote** (MIT, Notion-flavour UX) — full reuse-survey per `feedback_reuse_before_reimplement.md`; (f) each block type maps to a typed Shepard individual (heading / paragraph / table → existing TableContainer / code / image → file-reference / ts-chart → embedded TS view / shepard-ref → any artefact); (g) mode A form-shapes from SHACL templates + mode B free-text; (h) per-block `fair2r:modeOfProduction` + author + timestamp attribution; (i) lossless markdown round-trip; (j) admin-configurable block-type registry. Subsumes `project_block_editor.md`. Migration mode: backward-read → forward-write → drop HTML. | L | queued | Source: 2026-05-23 user flags. Pairs with PV1a (markdown diffs cleanly), UX-FRIENDLY-ERRORS, LABJOURNAL-SCIENTIFIC-TWITTER (the labjournal IS the canonical consumer of the new block editor). |

| LABJOURNAL-SCIENTIFIC-TWITTER | **Lab journal as scientific twitter — direct references to Shepard artefacts of all types; render specifics supplied by plugins; AI agents can join the convo.** User flags 2026-05-23: ("labjournal should be a scientific twitter with direct references to shepard artifacts (of all types - specifics should be supplied by plugins responsible)") + ("maybe even agents ca join the convo"). The shape: every lab-journal entry is a short post (twitter-style, but no length limit) authored by either a human OR an AI agent (both with f(ai)²r attribution). Entries can **directly reference** any Shepard artefact — DataObject, Collection, FileReference, TimeseriesReference, StructuredDataReference, GitReference, AAS submodel, ImageBundle frame, anything a plugin contributes as a `PayloadKind`. The **render specifics for each reference type come from the plugin responsible** for that PayloadKind (per the PayloadKind SPI — image plugin renders thumbnail-with-link, TS plugin renders live-sparkline-snippet, GitReference renders commit-shape, etc.) — labjournal core knows nothing about how to render a TS chart; it asks the TS plugin. **Threading**: entries can reply to other entries (twitter-style); branch into discussion sub-threads; pin pinnable posts; mention users (`@kreb_fl`) + agents (`@claude-opus-4-7`). **Agent participation**: AI agents post into the labjournal under their model identity with mandatory `🤖 Posted by` transparency line (per `feedback_role_agents_file_issues.md` extended pattern); the agent's reasoning per post is captured + cite-able. **Agent capabilities in-thread** (per user flag 2026-05-23 "assisting in research questions - ordering thoughts - reformatting extracting semantic anchors"): (1) **research-question assistance** — agent surfaces related artefacts, related literature (via paper-search MCP), open-question gaps; (2) **thought-ordering** — agent suggests reorderings of a chain of posts, identifies missing transitions, surfaces implicit assumptions; (3) **reformatting** — agent polishes markdown formatting, suggests block-type replacements (e.g. "this list reads better as a table"), maintains house-style; (4) **semantic-anchor extraction** — agent reads free-text posts and proposes typed annotations from the active ontology (CHAMEO / Material OWL / m4i / domain-vocabulary), surfacing them as user-acceptable suggestions per the f(ai)²r five-rung acceptance ladder. Each agent suggestion lands as a NEW post in the thread (not an inline edit) — preserving the conversational shape + the human's final-decision authority. **Composition**: built on top of HTML-TO-MD-MIGRATION's block editor — each post IS a block-editor document with first-class artefact-reference blocks. Replaces the existing lab-journal entry shape (which is plain markdown today per J1 chain). | XL | queued | Source: 2026-05-23 user flags. Pairs with HTML-TO-MD-MIGRATION (block editor substrate), TPL9 (f(ai)²r AI provenance), PERSONA-ISSUES-FROM-AUDIT (the AI-can-post-with-transparency pattern), J1d (lab-journal entry history panel + diff viewer — the new shape needs new viewers), NTF1 (notifications: who got mentioned). Cross-references `project_block_editor.md` (Twitter-hybrid attribution per block — already in memory; this row makes it concrete for labjournal). |

## FAIR4-* — FAIR4ML × FAIR-for-AI × f(ai)²r alignment

Rows opened by the 2026-05-23 comparative survey
`aidocs/semantics/fair4ml-fair4ai-fai2r-2026-05-23.md`. Each one closes
a specific predicate-level integration gap between f(ai)²r (this fork's
PROV-O extension; `noheton/f-ai-r`) and the RDA FAIR4ML schema.org
extension (`w3id.org/fair4ml`). Sequence them after PROMPT1 (the
PromptLog substrate is the canonical f(ai)²r consumer in the fork).

| ID | Description | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FAIR4-SURVEY | **Comparative survey done — `aidocs/semantics/fair4ml-fair4ai-fai2r-2026-05-23.md`.** Nine-dimension comparison (authority / scope / maturity / lead institutions / predicate count / RDF / SHACL / EU policy / lifecycle stage). Conclusion: f(ai)²r and FAIR4ML are complementary along orthogonal axes (authoring vs distribution); the thesis posture is A+B (gap-filling + lifecycle-stage complementarity), not C (competition). | S | **feature-defined 2026-05-23** | Source doc landed. Ten follow-on rows below close the predicate-level alignment work. |
| FAIR4-NS-FREEZE | **Freeze the canonical f(ai)²r namespace to `https://noheton.org/f-ai-r/ns#`.** The fork uses `https://noheton.github.io/f-ai-r/ns#` in `aidocs/semantics/99-promptlog-design.md` line 579 (and downstream SHACL shapes); the canonical repo uses `https://noheton.org/f-ai-r/ns#` in `doc/provenance.ttl`. Diverged namespace = future merge cost. Coordinate with VIEWS-AS-SHAPES-CANONICAL-IRIS (#1137) — same shape of fix, different vocab. | S | **✓ done (2026-05-27)** | Bridges with: VIEWS-AS-SHAPES-CANONICAL-IRIS (#1137), FAIR4-SHACL-COMPLIANCE. Fixed in `99-promptlog-design.md`, `synergy-2026-05-23-openlineage-fair2r.md`, and `plugins/analytics-ts/.../mffd-anomaly-annotation.shacl.ttl`. |
| FAIR4-BRIDGE-ONTOLOGY | **Ship a small bridge ontology at `aidocs/semantics/fair4ml-bridge.ttl`** that imports `fair4ml:`, `fair2r:`, `m4i:`, `prov:`, `schema:` and declares the composition rules: `fair4ml:MLModel rdfs:subClassOf prov:Entity`; an MLModel can be the `prov:generated` of an `m4i:ProcessingStep` (training run) which `prov:wasInformedBy` a `fair2r:AuthoringPass` (LLM hyperparameter conversation); `shp:MLModelCollection rdfs:subClassOf fair2r:Manuscript, schema:CreativeWork`. This is the alignment artefact a peer reviewer reads. | M | queued | Pairs with M4I-d (m4i × f(ai)²r bridge), `aidocs/semantics/94 §M4I-d`. |
| FAIR4-SHACL-COMPLIANCE | **SHACL shape `shp:Fair4mlMLModelShape`** validating a Shepard Collection-tagged-as-MLModel against FAIR4ML v0.1.0 minimum-metadata expectations (`fair4ml:mlTask`, `fair4ml:trainedOn`, `fair4ml:intendedUse`, `fair4ml:modelRisksBiasLimitations`, `schema:author`, `schema:license`). FAIR4ML v0.1.0 ships predicate types but no shapes; this is a contribution back to the RDA FAIR4ML IG. Validated via existing `n10s:validateShapes` substrate (per SHACL changeover #127). | M | queued | Pairs with #127 SHACL changeover, COMP-LICENSE-FIELD (required for `schema:license` to mean anything). |
| FAIR4-DASHBOARD-INTEGRATE | **AI-accountability dashboard reads BOTH `fair2r:` (authoring trail) AND `fair4ml:` (model card) predicates.** Per `aidocs/agent-findings/persona-strategy-aligner-gh-pm-2026-05-23.md` the dashboard projects the f(ai)²r authoring trail; extend it to also project FAIR4ML model-card metadata for any Collection tagged as an MLModel. This is the **single UI surface that proves f(ai)²r + FAIR4ML compose at runtime**, not just on paper. | L | queued | Pairs with PROMPT1, S-08 synergy, FAIR4-BRIDGE-ONTOLOGY. |

Author is the same person (Krebs), so the canonical-repo PR is low-friction. **Alternative if not lifted: move to a fork-private namespace** `http://semantics.dlr.de/shepard-fair2r-ext#` to avoid namespace squatting. **Recommendation: lift.** | M | queued | Pairs with FAIR4-NS-FREEZE; coordinate with the `noheton/f-ai-r` next-release cycle (likely tied to FAIR4-PUB-VENUE). |
