| ID | description | effort | status | notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| UI-005 | **`watches/me` 404 on every collection landing.** `GET /v2/collections/<appId>/watches/me` returns 404 on every Collection page load — pollutes console + masks real errors. **Decide**: ship the endpoint (the frontend already calls it, so likely a planned-but-unshipped feature) OR feature-flag the front-end call. Affects every Collection landing for every user. | S | ✅ **shipped** 2026-05-24 | Frontend-only — endpoint exists and 404 was intentional API design ("not watching"). `useCollectionWatch.refresh()` now uses `listWatchers` + `MeApi.getMe()` (both always-200). `/me` backend endpoint untouched (separate legitimate use). Findings: `aidocs/agent-findings/ui-005-watches-me-silenced-2026-05-24.md`. |
| UI-006 | **Home-page card cosmetic bugs.** LUMEN card title truncates mid-word "Hotfire Test Campaig" with no ellipsis; AI Exchange description shows raw `<p>...</p>` HTML; LUMEN description shows raw `**markdown**` asterisks. Root cause for ellipsis was missing `min-width: 0` on flex child (not `nowrap` as initially briefed). | S | ✅ **shipped** `c4f6e354` | Done 2026-05-24. `renderInlineDescription()` helper + `min-width: 0` flex fix. |
| UI-007 | **404s on hero imagery** — briefing inaccurate. The 404s are on `/help` Jekyll-source asset paths, NOT the unauthenticated landing (`LandingPage.vue` doesn't reference these images). 4-line fix in `helpMarkdown.ts` preprocessor: leading `/assets/...` → `/docs/assets/...`. | XS | ✅ **shipped** `c4f6e354` | Done 2026-05-24. Briefing-vs-evidence drift documented in agent report. |
| UI-008 | **Advanced-search UI demands raw JSON DSL.** `/search` advanced query input is a single-line text field demanding raw JSON `{ "OR": [ ... ` — text cut off after ~5 chars; no JSON formatting, no syntax help, no schema autocomplete, no example. Fix shape: CodeMirror multi-line editor + example placeholder + a "Build query" wizard for AND/OR. | M | queued | Pairs with UI-002 (header search). The full `/search` page upgrade is bigger than the dropdown; queue separately. |
| UI-009 | **File row `thumbnail?size=64` 404 noise.** Every file row in a File container fires a thumbnail request that 404s; no MIME-type icon shown to compensate. Cheapest fix: condition request on `isImageMime(...)` and remove the call for non-images; medium fix: ship typed default placeholders per MIME family; full fix: backend thumbnail-generation job. | S | **dispatched 2026-05-24** | Bundled with UI-006 polish pass (cheapest fix first). Pairs with backlog task #34 (file preview + plugin SPI for thumbs). |
| UI-010 | **`Hydration completed but contains mismatches` warnings on every page.** Same SSR/hydration race that bit BUG #139; partially addressed by `5913ca20` (collection layout) + the HelpFrame fix in the next commit. Other components likely still affected. Audit: surface every component that uses `useDisplay()` reactively in v-if/cols/style siblings; either switch to CSS-only Vuetify utilities or guard SSR with `<ClientOnly>`. | M | partially addressed | Two of the eight `useDisplay` callers fixed (collection layout, HelpFrame). The six `:fullscreen="mobile"` v-dialog usages are SAFE (single prop, closed dialogs). CollectionSidebar uses `mobile` reactively in `:style` — low-risk but worth aligning later. |
| UI-011 | **Collection-list missing useful columns.** `/collections` shows ID / Name / Created-by / Created-at only. No DataObject count, no last-updated, no description preview. User can't tell a 17-DO showcase from an 8500-DO dropbox at a glance. Also: ID column exposes raw numeric Neo4j IDs (42 vs 661923) — confusing pre-appId-migration. Fix: add `# DOs`, `Last updated`, short description columns; hide numeric ID behind "advanced". | M | ✅ **shipped** `89e1fcf8` | Done 2026-05-24. # DOs from `dataObjectIds.length` (no backend); description preview via markdown-stripping helper + 2-line CSS clamp; "large" chip on ≥1000 DOs. 4 follow-ups split into UI-011a..d below. |
| UI-011a | **Per-page advanced-mode toggle on `/collections`.** `useAdvancedMode` composable exists but no surface on this page — the numeric ID column is hidden until the user manually toggles advanced on another page first. Add a small toggle in the page header. | XS | ✅ **shipped** (Wave12a) | UI-011 deferred follow-up. `v-chip` toggle in `CollectionListPage.vue` header row, left of view-mode toggle; reflects live `advancedMode` state; `data-testid="collection-advanced-mode-chip"`. |
| UI-011b | **Better `# DOs` visualisation.** Current column shows raw integer; a log-scale sparkline / quartile colour-band would convey "is this big or small" at a glance. | S | ✅ **done (2026-05-27)** | `useDoCountChip(count)` util in `frontend/utils/doCountChip.ts`; 5-bucket colour scheme: 0=grey, 1–10=green, 11–100=blue, 101–1000=orange, >1000=red; `<v-chip :color="..." variant="tonal" size="small">` replaces plain integer + old "large" chip in `CollectionList.vue #[item.doCount]` slot. 12 Vitest boundary tests in `tests/unit/doCountChip.test.ts`. |
| UI-011c | **Server-side sort by `dataObjectCount` and `updatedAt`** on `/v2/collections`. Currently sort is page-local — sort by # DOs only sorts the visible page, not the full set. Needs a backend `?sort=` param. | M | queued | UI-011 deferred follow-up. Light backend work; new query param + Neo4j ORDER BY. |
| UI-011d | **`(created)` suffix on `Last updated` when `updatedAt` is null** for legacy entities (pre-PROV1a). Cosmetic clarity for older Collections. | XS | ✅ **shipped** (Wave12a) | UI-011 deferred follow-up. `CollectionList.vue` `#[item.updatedAt]` slot: `<span v-if="!rowProps.item.updatedAt" class="text-caption text-medium-emphasis ml-1">(created)</span>`; `data-testid="collection-row-created-suffix"`. |
| UI-011e | **`# DOs` column shows 0 everywhere on `/collections` list.** Surfaced by UX Scrutinizer 2026-05-24. **Root cause turned out to be different from the task hypothesis:** `GET /v2/collections` (CollectionV2Rest → CollectionDAO) already returns correct counts on live (LUMEN=17, MFFD-Dropbox=8514, Home=3). The frontend `/collections` page does NOT call that endpoint — it calls `SearchApi.searchCollections` → `POST /shepard/api/search/collections` → `SearchDAO.findCollections`, whose `emitCollectionReturnPart` used `Neighborhood.ESSENTIAL` (= `path=(c)-[*0..1]->(n) WHERE n:Permission OR n:User`). ESSENTIAL never walks DataObjects, so `CollectionIO.dataObjectIds[]` came back empty on every search result. Fix: flip the collection projection to `Neighborhood.EVERYTHING` (depth 1) — the same neighborhood `CollectionDAO.findAllCollectionsByShepardId` already uses successfully in production. Container / User / UserGroup `emit*ReturnPart` helpers stay on ESSENTIAL (their cells don't expose nested-entity counts). | S | ✅ **shipped** (worktree UI-011e) | UI-011 follow-up; ships before UI-011a..d because it's broken not deferred. JUnit: extended `SearchDAOTest.findCollectionsTest` to assert the new Cypher + added `findCollectionsHydratesDataObjects_UI011e` as regression guard. Vitest unnecessary — no frontend change (field name `dataObjectIds` already matched). Playwright e2e: `e2e/tests/ui-011e-do-count-shows.spec.ts` asserts LUMEN + MFFD-Dropbox rows show non-zero `# DOs`. Finding doc: `aidocs/agent-findings/ui-011e-do-count-fix-2026-05-24.md`. |
| UX-PATTERN-A | **Loading-state leaks as error (red toast on cold load).** Surfaced by UX Scrutinizer 2026-05-24 (`aidocs/agent-findings/ux-scrutinizer-workflows-2026-05-24.md` §"Pattern A"). On every Phase-2/3/5 workflow during cold load the UI flashed a red error toast claiming data was unavailable — actually `handleError()` was emitting on `AbortError` (route changed mid-flight) and on `401 Unauthorized` (which the auth-refresh middleware silently handles). Fix: two early-returns at the top of `frontend/utils/errorBus.ts::handleError` filter both classes; both still log to console at WARN so real bugs remain debuggable. 7-test Vitest regression guard added (`tests/unit/errorBus.test.ts`). | XS | ✅ **shipped** (worktree UX-POLISH-2026-05-24) | Finding doc: `aidocs/agent-findings/ux-polish-bundle-2026-05-24.md`. Live verified via `e2e/tests/ux-polish-2026-05-24.spec.ts` (Pattern A test polls toast every 100 ms for 2 s, asserts never appears). |
| UX-PATTERN-E | **Sidebar sibling-nav under-promoted.** Surfaced by UX Scrutinizer 2026-05-24 (`aidocs/agent-findings/ux-scrutinizer-workflows-2026-05-24.md` §"Pattern E"). The Contents tree IS the sibling navigator (and supports chevron-expand into investigation sub-trees) but first-time users don't realise it. Fix: info icon + tooltip next to the Contents header in `CollectionSidebar.vue` reading "Click any DataObject to jump between siblings. Use the chevron to expand sub-trees" + a sentence in `docs/help/collection-lineage.md` mirroring it. | XS | ✅ **shipped** (worktree UX-POLISH-2026-05-24) | Finding doc: `aidocs/agent-findings/ux-polish-bundle-2026-05-24.md`. |
| UX-PATTERN-F | **Page titles don't include the entity name.** Surfaced by UX Scrutinizer 2026-05-24 (`aidocs/agent-findings/ux-scrutinizer-workflows-2026-05-24.md` §"Pattern F"). Every Collection / DO / Container page had `useHead({ title })` called **inside a watcher** with string-concat + nullish coercion → tab title rendered as `undefined \| shepard` on cold load and leaked a new head entry on every fetch. Fix: call `useHead` once at top-level with a getter function on 6 pages. Patterns: `<Collection.name> — shepard` / `<DO.name> · <Collection.name> — shepard` / `<Container.name> (Files\|Timeseries\|Structured Data\|Spatial Data) — shepard` + sensible fallbacks for the pre-load tick. | XS | ✅ **shipped** (worktree UX-POLISH-2026-05-24) | Finding doc: `aidocs/agent-findings/ux-polish-bundle-2026-05-24.md`. Live e2e `Pattern F: collection page title contains the collection name` + `Pattern F: two collection tabs have distinct titles` both PASS. |
| UX-Q-PARAM | **`/search?q=X` URL param was ignored + header-search "See all results" dropped the query.** Pasting a link with `?q=foo` landed on search but the field stayed empty; the header search dropdown's footer link navigated to bare `/search`, discarding the user's typed query. Fix (two parts): (1) `pages/search/index.vue` reads `?q=` and translates it to a `name contains <value>` JSON query, then runs the search — `?searchQuery=` wins over `?q=` when both present. Logic extracted to `utils/searchQueryFromParams.ts` for unit-testability (8-test Vitest). (2) `components/layout/HeaderBar.vue` — the dropdown's "See all results" `:to` binding + the Enter handler in `onEnterPressed()` now pass `query: { q }` so the round-trip works. | XS | ✅ **shipped** (worktree UX-POLISH-2026-05-24) | Bundled with UX-PATTERN-A/E/F because all four are short and don't conflict. Finding doc: `aidocs/agent-findings/ux-polish-bundle-2026-05-24.md`. Live e2e covers both halves of the round-trip. |
| UI-012 | **Avatar upload not discoverable from `/me` index.** Profile sub-nav lists Profile / API Keys / MCP / Subscriptions / Git Credentials but no obvious avatar-upload affordance at the index; header avatar is a circle with no image. The Profile sub-page likely has an upload — raise its discoverability OR show an avatar-upload affordance on hover over the header circle. | S | ✅ **done (2026-05-27)** | (A) Header avatar circle gains a semi-transparent camera overlay on hover (`header-avatar-camera` span, opacity 0→1 via CSS, pointer-events:none so parent v-btn :to still navigates). (B) `/me` landing Profile card icon changed to `mdi-account-circle-outline`, title renamed "Profile & Avatar", description front-loads "Upload or change your avatar". |
| UI-013 | **`/help` page lacks search box + anchor links.** One long single-column scroll with a tall left nav; no search-in-docs, no per-section anchors. Add a help-search box at the top + anchor links per H2; consider splitting into per-topic routes once page exceeds N sections. | S | ✅ **done (2026-05-27)** | Already shipped: `v-text-field` search with DOM-level section filtering, `#` anchor links on H2/H3/H4 with hover-reveal copy, `slugify` + `SlugRegistry` in `helpMarkdown.ts`, 44 Vitest tests. Backlog mark-done commit `65074aa0f`. |
| UI-014 | **Sidebar stays narrow (200 px) at 4K.** Collection sidebar at 4K truncates DO names to ~12 chars while 3000 px to the right is empty. Make sidebar width responsive to viewport, or expose a drag-resize handle. | M | queued | Visible only at 4K; not blocking. Pairs with #114 (CollectionSidebar usability). |
| UI-015 | **"Created at" column header wraps in file row table.** Two-word header wraps to second line, creating off-by-one row alignment. Cosmetic. Fix: `white-space: nowrap` on the column-header span. | XS | **dispatched 2026-05-24** | Bundled with UI-006 polish pass. |
| UI-016 | **Referenced-by section dense with duplicate annotation chips.** Extended `SemanticAnnotationList`'s `limit` prop to a default-collapsed `+N more` / `Show less` state machine. Cap in place; live demo has no rows with >3 chips so the overflow chip won't appear until annotation density returns. | S | ✅ **shipped** `41aa3cc1` | Gate flips green automatically when high-density rows exist. |
| UI-017 | **Description edit affordance unclear.** Adds `description-editing` class + "Editing description" label + 2px primary-tinted outline + soft tint background when in edit mode. Aria-label on Edit button. | S | ✅ **shipped** `41aa3cc1` | Deterministic Playwright test passes on LUMEN/42. |
| UI-021 | **`/containers/structureddata` list page shows 0 rows for demo user.** Surfaced by UI-016 agent 2026-05-24 — `/containers/structureddata` returns empty even though direct `id` navigation (e.g. `/containers/structureddata/1`) works for the same user. Likely a list-page filter / permission scope bug. | S | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-05-24 — WAI/misreport** | Investigation showed `/containers/<type>` (without an id) is a **Nuxt 404 page**, not an empty list. No such route exists for any of the four types. The real list page is `/containers` (query-param filtered); alice sees her SD container there. Verdict + Playwright evidence in `aidocs/agent-findings/ui-021-sd-list-fix-2026-05-24.md`. Acceptance regression: `e2e/tests/ui-021-verify-real-list.spec.ts` (2/2 pass). Follow-up filed as **UI-DEEP-LINK-TYPED-LISTS** below. |
| UI-022 | **UX Pattern D — count badges on DataObject reference panels.** Surfaced by UX Scrutinizer 2026-05-24 as the single Pareto-optimal change for DO detail (purely cosmetic effort, "fish in dark" → "scannable at a glance"). The DO detail page mounted 6 v-expansion-panels + 2 always-visible sections; only 4 panel titles carried `(N)` count badges (Attributes / Lab Journal / Data References / Relationships) via the pre-existing `ExpansionPanelItem` convention. Added badges to **Jupyter Notebooks** panel title (via `useCounter()` + new `numberOfEntriesChanged` emit on `DataObjectNotebooksPane`), **Semantic Annotations** section header (via the already-existing `@annotations` emit on `SemanticAnnotationList`, just unused on this page), **Git References** inner h5 (inside Data References panel, sourced from `gitReferences.length`), and **Video References** inner h5 (sourced from `references.length`). Zero-handling: followed the existing `ExpansionPanelItem.vue:21–25` convention — show `(0)` in `text-low-emphasis` rather than hide-when-zero (consistency with the 4 pre-existing badges). Provenance skipped (would need an extra `/v2/provenance/activities` round-trip not on the user's list). | S | ✅ **shipped** (worktree UX-PATTERN-D) | Files: `DataObjectNotebooksPane.vue` (new emit + watch), `GitReferencesPane.vue` (h5 badge), `VideoStreamReferencesPane.vue` (h5 badge), `pages/.../[dataObjectId]/index.vue` (Jupyter `useCounter()` + Semantic Annotations `ref` + handler wiring). Playwright e2e: `e2e/tests/ux-pattern-d-count-badges.spec.ts` (2/2 pass on live — both populated DO + zero-handling regression guard). Smoke 25/25. Vitest skipped (no factored helper introduced; pure template wiring of existing composables). Finding doc: `aidocs/agent-findings/ux-pattern-d-count-badges-2026-05-24.md`. |
| UI-DEEP-LINK-TYPED-LISTS | **Make `/containers/<type>` a deep-linkable typed list.** Today the only list page is `/containers` (query-param filtered via `ContainerTypeSelect`). Add a tiny redirect (`pages/containers/[type].vue` shim that re-routes to `/containers?selectedFilter=<TYPE>`) for the four known types. Closes the misreport pattern that produced UI-021. | XS | ✓ shipped (2026-05-26) | File: `frontend/pages/containers/[type].vue`. Maps `timeseries`→`TIMESERIES`, `file`/`files`→`FILE`, `structureddata`→`STRUCTUREDDATA`, `spatialdata`→`SPATIALDATA`; unrecognised type (incl. `hdf5` — plugin type, not a valid `selectedFilter`) → `/containers`. Static depth-2 routes (`/containers/timeseries/[id]` etc.) are unaffected — Nuxt static segment wins. |
| UI-018 | **HYPOTHESIS: possible N+1 on deep MFFD DO detail.** Scrutinizer couldn't directly confirm; lineage graph won't render until BUG #139 was fixed (now it is). Re-walk to confirm or close. | S | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-05-24** | Re-walk confirmed CLOSED — `CollectionLineageGraph` + `DataObjectProvGraph` both batch-paginate via `useFetchAllDataObjects` (page size 200), no per-DO fetches observed. Dialog closes cleanly. Test: `e2e/tests/ui-019-create-do-fillcancel.spec.ts`. Report: `aidocs/agent-findings/ui-018-019-hypothesis-recheck-2026-05-24.md`. |
| UI-020 | **N+1 fan-out on `/shepard/api/labJournalEntries` from `CollectionLabJournalEntryList.vue`.** Backend bulk endpoint `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/lab-journal-entries` (single Cypher walk Collection→DO→LabJournalEntry) + frontend composable `useFetchCollectionLabJournalEntries`. **Measured live: 8514 reqs → 1 req on MFFD-Dropbox**, 17 reqs → 1 req on LUMEN. | M | ✅ **shipped** `569adec7` | 6/6 JUnit + 9/9 Vitest + 1/1 Playwright pass. aidocs/34 + aidocs/44 updated same PR. |
| OPS-WORKTREE-ENV | **Worktree-agent setup checklist** — agent worktrees don't inherit `infrastructure/.env`, blocking `make redeploy` until the agent manually copies it. Add a `scripts/setup-worktree.sh` (or a one-line hook in `.git/hooks/post-checkout`) that symlinks the env file into the worktree on creation. Surfaced by UI-020 agent 2026-05-24. | XS | ✅ **shipped** 2026-05-24 | `scripts/setup-worktree.sh` (idempotent symlink, refuses to clobber existing) + `make preflight-env` gate on every `deploy`/`redeploy*` target, with actionable error pointing at the script. See `aidocs/agent-findings/ops-cleanup-2026-05-24.md`. |
| OPS-MIGRATION-HEALTHCHECK | **Healthcheck should verify Neo4j migration chain integrity.** UI-020 agent found that **V61 (`v15_prov_predicates`, commit `0c6ead4b` from 2026-05-22) had never been applied to the live Neo4j** despite being on `main` for two days — yet healthcheck reported green. The MigrationsRunner abort-on-error guard isn't running, OR is running silently. Add a healthcheck endpoint that asserts every `V**` file in the classpath maps to an applied `__Neo4jMigration` node with a matching checksum. | S | ✅ **shipped** 2026-05-24 | New `neo4j-migration-chain-readiness` SmallRye check delegates to `Migrations.validate()` + `Migrations.info(COMPARE)` (sealed library types, so we share their definition of integrity rather than reimplementing the CRC32). Caches per `shepard.health.readiness.max-staleness`. Operator runbook at `docs/admin/runbooks/migration-chain-integrity.md` with worked example for the UI-020 V61 splice. See `aidocs/agent-findings/ops-migration-healthcheck-2026-05-24.md`. |
| OPS-MIGRATIONS-GITIGNORED | **`V54__NOOP_heroImageUrl_additive.cypher` + `V55__NOOP_ImportPlan_additive.cypher` are `.gitignore`-excluded** in `backend/src/main/resources/neo4j/migrations/` because the root `.gitignore` has a `neo4j/` pattern that matches the migrations folder. They've been live-applied (committed locally to the cube box) but the public repo never carried them. **Fix**: tighten `.gitignore` to `/neo4j/` or `neo4j-data/` (whatever the original intent was) so future migrations land in the repo. Audit existing migrations folder for additional ghosts. | S | ✅ **shipped** 2026-05-24 | Anchored data-volume patterns at repo root (`/mongodb/`, `/neo4j/`, `/infrastructure/neo4j/`, `/timescaledb/`) with a comment explaining the history. V54 + V55 recovered + committed; audit confirmed no other ghosts under `backend/src/`. See `aidocs/agent-findings/ops-cleanup-2026-05-24.md`. |
| UI-LAYOUT-AUDIT | **Frontend coding-convention note: prefer CSS `d-*` utilities over `useDisplay()` reactivity for SSR-rendered layout.** Six v-dialog `:fullscreen="mobile"` usages are SAFE per BUG #139 analysis (single prop on closed dialog). CollectionSidebar's `:style="mobile ? ... : ..."` is a low-risk style swap; worth aligning to CSS-only when convenient. Document as a coding-convention note rather than a fix-each row. | XS | done (2026-05-27) | `aidocs/frontend/02-css-layout-conventions.md` — convention note covering d-* vs useDisplay(), safe-usage carve-outs, and known usages table. |
| BACKEND-VERSIONZ-PROBE | **Identify + fix the source of the expired-JWT noise on `/versionz`.** Per `aidocs/agent-findings/backend-logs-sift-2026-05-24.md` Pattern 2: 45 WARN/hour from a caller sending an expired JWT to `/versionz`. The MFFD v15 importer code doesn't call `/versionz` directly — must be a different caller (Caddy probe? Nuxt SSR? cube-side wrapper?). Step 1: identify the caller via remote-IP correlation in Caddy/backend logs. Step 2: drop the Authorization header from the probe OR rotate the token. Step 3: optionally downgrade backend WARN→DEBUG for `/versionz` JWT-validator hits since the endpoint is public. | S | ✓ shipped (2026-05-27) | Root cause: `/healthz` (configured via `quarkus.smallrye-health.root-path=/shepard/api/healthz`) was absent from `PublicEndpointRegistry` — health probes (`make wait-for-health`, Docker healthcheck) hit it without auth, triggering JWTFilter WARNs. Fix: added `"/healthz"` to `PUBLIC_PATH_PREFIXES` covering the full smallrye-health family (`/healthz`, `/healthz/live`, `/healthz/ready`, `/healthz/group/...`). 5 new tests in `PublicEndpointRegistryTest` guard the prefix and its foot-gun boundary. |

## CRIT-2026-05-24-* — Critical operational risks

| ID | description | effort | status | notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ~~CRIT-LOMBOK-APT~~ | **RETRACTED 2026-05-24** — false alarm caused by local working-directory pollution. Three pre-existing dirty mods to `Collection.java` / `DataObjectIO.java` / `SparqlQueryValidator.java` were carried in canonical `/opt/shepard` since session start; the dirty WD made Lombok APT fail. Agent worktrees (`git worktree add` → clean HEAD checkout) build fine — verified by reproducing `mvn clean compile -DskipTests` in `/opt/shepard/.claude/worktrees/agent-*/backend` → green. Three subsequent worktree-isolated agents (UI-011e, OPS-MIGRATION-HEALTHCHECK, RDM-001) all built + deployed + smoked 25/25 from canonical-main-equivalent state. The PROV-resolver agent's report was accurate-for-their-moment but conflated transient cherry-pick state with a main-branch bug. | XS | ✅ **resolved (false alarm)** | Lesson kept as CRIT-SMOKE-NOT-CATCHING-BUILD-BREAK below. Operator note: the three dirty session-original files are still in `/opt/shepard` WD; `git stash` or `git checkout HEAD -- <files>` to restore main if not actively worked on. |
| CRIT-SMOKE-NOT-CATCHING-BUILD-BREAK | **Smoke endpoint passes against a stale running image even when a fresh `mvn clean compile` is broken.** This is how CRIT-LOMBOK-APT hid for a full session. Fix: chain `make build-backend` as a hard prerequisite of `make smoke` (or add a `make smoke-with-build` target that's the default), so a broken main can't deploy "successfully". | S | ✅ **shipped** 2026-05-24 | OPS hygiene bundle. `smoke: build-backend` now in `Makefile`. Self-test caught a probe-injected error AND surfaced a real `TargetEntityResolver.resolve(String)` duplicate-method break sitting on main — filed as `CRIT-MAIN-BROKEN-TARGETENTITYRESOLVER-DUPLICATE` below. See `aidocs/agent-findings/ops-hygiene-bundle-2026-05-24.md`. |
| CRIT-WORKTREE-DOCKER-CACHE | **`make redeploy-frontend` reports success but ships a STALE image from agent worktrees.** Surfaced by UX-Pattern-D agent 2026-05-24: their first `make redeploy-frontend` returned green smoke 25/25 but the live frontend still served the old code. Recovery required `rm -rf .output .nuxt && npm run build && docker build && docker compose up -d --force-recreate frontend`. Suspected: docker layer cache + Nuxt `.output` cache + dev-mode hot-swap container retention combine to mask the staleness. The authoritative live-deploy verify command is `docker exec infrastructure-frontend-1 grep -l <new-testid> /app/server/chunks/build/*.mjs` — chain into `make smoke` or a new `make verify-frontend-deploy` target so it can't repeat. Same family as CRIT-SMOKE-NOT-CATCHING-BUILD-BREAK above (smoke probes running image not fresh code). | M | ✅ **shipped** 2026-05-24 | OPS hygiene bundle. Resolved as a sibling of MK-REDEPLOY-FORCE-RECREATE — `--force-recreate <service>` now folded into every `redeploy*` Makefile target, which closes the docker-cache trap. `verify-frontend-deploy` sentinel-check target deliberately deferred per the OPS-bundle task brief ("--force-recreate alone closes 80%"). |

## SM1a — User-configurable orphan-data retention per container (DEFAULT 1 YEAR)

| ID | description | effort | status | notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM1a | **Per-container user-configurable retention for non-referenced data, default 1 year, with notification warnings.** Researcher decides on a per-container basis (or accepts the instance-admin default) how long non-referenced data lives before deletion. The `INTEGRITY RULE` from `feedback_referenced_data_infinite_retention.md` still holds — referenced data is never deleted. Only data with `refs=0 AND payload>0 AND not in active job state` is subject to the retention clock. **Default 1 year** (revises the prior SM1 design memory which had `infinite grace`; rationale: most operators don't actually want indefinite orphan accumulation; explicit-opt-in for "infinite" is the safer default-OFF posture). **Per-container fields** on TS / File / SD containers: `orphan_retention_days: int` (NULL = use instance default, 0 = delete-on-next-sweep, -1 = infinite), `orphan_retention_owner_notified_at: timestamp?`. **Notification cadence**: nag at 30d / 7d / 1d before deletion (NTF1 dep), one final "deleted N items" on actual deletion. **UI**: a Retention card on each container detail page with the current setting + a select with explicit options (Infinite / 1 year / 90 days / 30 days / Delete-on-orphan); plus an instance-admin landing tile to set the instance default. **Audit**: every retention-driven deletion lands in `:Activity` with `:RetentionSweep` source kind. **Acceptance**: a researcher can opt into infinite, accept the 1-year default, or tighten to 30d; gets the nag notifications; sees deletions in the activity trail. | L | queued | Revises `project_storage_management.md` default (infinite → 1 year). Depends on: NTF1 (notifications, in design), `:Activity` source-kind extension. Frontend + backend + Cypher migration (per-container retention fields) + scheduled-job (sweep). Update aidocs/34 + 44 + model-inventory. **Operator-facing default change — document loudly in v6.x release notes.** |
| SM1a-RUNBOOK | **Operator runbook for retention policy** — `docs/admin/runbooks/orphan-retention-policy.md`. Covers: where to set the instance default, what the per-container override does, how the nag cadence fires, how to recover a container from delete-on-next-sweep state in the brief window before it fires, audit-trail query (`MATCH (a:Activity {sourceKind: 'RetentionSweep'})`), and how to opt out entirely (set instance default to infinite). Sibling to `docs/admin/runbooks/restore-tsdb-container-neo4j-shadow.md`. | S | done (2026-05-27) | Runbook written as `stage: feature-defined` with `[FUTURE]` tags throughout — SM1a is designed but not yet shipped. Audit-trail Cypher includes both pre-SM1a fallback (`summary CONTAINS 'RetentionSweep'`) and full post-SM1a query (`sourceKind = 'RetentionSweep'`). |

## PROV-2026-05-24-* — Provenance trail completeness gaps

Source: `aidocs/agent-findings/rdm-004-provenance-empty-fix-2026-05-24.md` (closes RDM-2026-05-24-004). Root-cause analysis showed the LUMEN DO Provenance panel renders an empty list because 99.93 % of recorded Activities have `targetKind=NULL`/`targetAppId=NULL` — the capture filter's path resolver fails on subresource paths and on v1 numeric-id paths. The empty-state copy fix (Bucket D) shipped in the same PR; the structural pieces are tracked below.

| ID | Item | effort | status | notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| PROV-EMPTY-STATE-COPY | **`DataObjectProvLog.vue` empty state now distinguishes "no events recorded" from "filter hid everything".** Adds an optional `hint` prop on `EmptyListIcon` and two computed branches (extracted to `frontend/utils/provenanceEmptyState.ts` for testability). Closes the trust-killer signal: a researcher landing on a DO with zero activities now reads a precise explanation of capture scope instead of a suspect empty panel. | XS | ✅ **shipped** 2026-05-24 | Frontend-only, <50 LOC. Vitest passes. See findings doc above. |
| PROV-RESOLVER-PATHWALK | **`TargetEntityResolver` walks the path right-to-left for the deepest `(kind, id)` pair instead of insisting on the tail.** Subresource creates now attach to the newly-minted entity, not its parent. Pre-fix: `POST /v2/collections/<COLL-uuid>/dataobjects` recorded the Collection UUID with kind `DataObject` (wrong target). Post-fix: walks right-to-left for `(knownPlural, idLike)` pairs; unknown plurals no longer fall back to title-casing — verb-shaped segments like `payload`/`diff`/`detect-anomalies` can't pollute `targetKind` any more. Pure refactor split: `PathTargetParser` (static parser) + `TargetEntityResolver` (CDI bean wiring the parser to numeric lookup). | M | ✅ **shipped** 2026-05-24 | Closes RDM-2026-05-24-004 bucket B. Same PR as PROV-V1-NUMERIC-LOOKUP. See `aidocs/agent-findings/prov-resolver-fix-2026-05-24.md`. |
| PROV-V1-NUMERIC-LOOKUP | **Capture-time resolution of `/shepard/api/...` numeric IDs → appIds via a generic `EntityAppIdLookup` helper.** Single parameterised Cypher (`MATCH (n:<Label>) WHERE n.shepardId=$id ...`) over the static label allow-list; deviates from the brief's per-DAO method shape because all targets share `AbstractEntity` (carries `appId`) + `VersionableEntity` (carries `shepardId`), so one helper covers every kind with zero entity-file edits. On miss the Activity lands with `targetKind` set + `targetAppId=null` — capture is never blocked. The optional backfill is split off as `PROV-BACKFILL-MIGRATION`. | L | ✅ **shipped** 2026-05-24 | Closes RDM-2026-05-24-004 bucket C. Same PR as PROV-RESOLVER-PATHWALK. See findings doc. |
| PROV-BACKFILL-MIGRATION | **One-off backfill of the ~280 K orphaned `:Activity` rows whose `targetKind=NULL`.** Implementation should re-use the production `PathTargetParser` from Java (not re-implement string parsing in Cypher), call `EntityAppIdLookup` for each parsed `(kind, numericId)` pair, and write the result back via `apoc.periodic.iterate` (batched, idempotent). Acceptance: post-migration `MATCH (a:Activity) WHERE a.targetKind IS NULL RETURN count(a)` drops by ≥ 250 K. Optional rollback `V**_R__backfill_activity_targets.cypher` clears the back-filled fields. Split from PROV-V1-NUMERIC-LOOKUP to keep this PR shippable; only recovers historic seed traffic. | M | queued | Not blocking; the resolver fix already prevents the bug recurring for new writes. |
| PROV-CAPTURE-READS-DECISION | **Decide whether `shepard.provenance.capture-reads` flips from `false` to `true` for `/v2/...` paths.** Current default is off per `aidocs/55 §4` (read-volume bounding); the f(ai)²r promise of "every interaction is observable" implies on. Sizing exercise: count GETs/day on MFFD-Dropbox, multiply by `retention-days=730`, check Neo4j page-cache + disk delta. If green, flip the default for `/v2/` paths only (v1 stays off to keep upstream-compat ops cost predictable). Pairs with PROV-RESOLVER-PATHWALK + PROV-V1-NUMERIC-LOOKUP — flipping reads without resolver fixes just multiplies the NULL-target row count. | S (design) + M (impl) | **done** (PROV-CAPTURE-READS-1) | Design call resolved. Subsumes the f(ai)²r AI-collaboration provenance line in `aidocs/55 §4` and the RDM-004 Bucket A verdict. |
| RDM-002-E2E-FIX | **RDM-002 Playwright e2e fails on live despite code shipped + smoke green.** All 5 tests in `e2e/tests/rdm-002-orcid-on-profile.spec.ts` failed on `expect(getByTestId("profile-identity-section")).toBeVisible()` post-deploy `4ef13776`. Source has the test-id; `useRouteFragment` is hash-based and `/me#profile` matches `UserFragments.PROFILE`. Likely a race: spec asserts before the SSR-hydrated `routeFragment` ref updates. Add `await page.waitForSelector('[data-testid="profile-identity-section"]', { timeout: 5000 })` or `await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle')` before the assertion. | XS | ✅ **done 2026-05-26** | RDM-002 follow-up. Added `waitForSelector('[data-testid="profile-identity-section"]', {timeout:8000})` after every `goto("/me#profile")` in the spec (5 sites: `clearOrcid` helper + all 5 test bodies). `clearOrcid` is resilient: logs a warning and skips the clear if the section doesn't appear rather than failing teardown. |
| BUG-LJ-V1-COLL-ID | **`GET /v2/collections/{appId}/lab-journal-entries` returns 500 on any collection with entries** (LUMEN exposes it; MFFD-Dropbox hid it with empty list). Surfaced by RDM-005 widget agent 2026-05-24. **Brief's hypothesis was wrong by primary evidence**: not a v1-numeric-id → appId resolver gap — LUMEN's Collection node already has `appId=019e30b0-...` populated and `EntityIdResolver.resolveLong` succeeds; the resolver is irrelevant to this bug. **Actual root cause**: `CollectionLabJournalEntriesDAO`'s `RETURN DISTINCT lje` projection did not hydrate the INCOMING `has_labjournalentry` edge back to DataObject, so `LabJournalEntry.getDataObject()` came back null on every entry and `LabJournalEntryIO`'s constructor NPEd on `dataObject.getShepardId()`. **Fix**: DAO Cypher now projects a depth-1 neighbourhood path (`MATCH path=(lje)-[*0..1]-(n) ... RETURN lje, nodes(path), relationships(path)`) — the canonical `CypherQueryHelper.getReturnPart` pattern — so the OGM hydrates both directions. Resource adds a defensive orphan-skip + WARN log as belt-and-braces. Unrelated to PROV-V1-NUMERIC-LOOKUP. | S | ✅ **shipped** 2026-05-24 | See `aidocs/agent-findings/bug-lj-v1-coll-id-fix-2026-05-24.md`. Live verification post-deploy. |
| OGM-HYDRATION-AUDIT | **Audit every `findByQuery` callsite in v2 DAOs for the `RETURN <entity>`-only pattern that triggered BUG-LJ-V1-COLL-ID** — any place returning just the leaf node without a `nodes(path), relationships(path)` projection silently drops INCOMING relationships, and the bug only surfaces when the IO/service touches those fields. The canonical safe pattern is `CypherQueryHelper.getReturnPart(entity)`. Sweep should catch other UI-XXX bulk endpoints landed in 2026-05 that may carry the same defect (e.g., new collection-walk DAOs in `de.dlr.shepard.v2.*.daos`). | S | ✅ **done (2026-05-27)** | Audit complete. v2 DAO sweep: `aidocs/agent-findings/ogm-hydration-audit-2026-05-24.md`. Service-layer sweep: `aidocs/agent-findings/ogm-hydration-service-layer-2026-05-27.md`. `DataObjectDAO.findByAppId` fix already applied (line 426: `session.load(DataObject.class, stub.getId(), DEPTH_ENTITY)`). Safety comment added to `LabJournalEntryService.getCollectionId()` documenting why `findByNeo4jId` is safe and what pattern would reintroduce BUG-LJ-V1. |
| MK-REDEPLOY-FORCE-RECREATE | **`make redeploy-frontend` ships stale image because `docker compose up -d --no-build` reuses the existing container even after `image-frontend` rebuilt.** Surfaced independently by Pattern-D agent + UX-bundle agent 2026-05-24. Recovery: `docker compose up -d --force-recreate frontend`. Fix: fold `--force-recreate frontend` (and the same for backend) into the Makefile `redeploy-frontend` + `redeploy-backend` targets. Authoritative live-deploy verify: `docker exec infrastructure-frontend-1 grep -l <new-testid> /app/server/chunks/build/*.mjs`. | XS | ✅ **shipped** 2026-05-24 | OPS hygiene bundle. `--force-recreate <service>` added to `redeploy-backend`, `redeploy-frontend`, `redeploy`, and `redeploy-fast`. See `aidocs/agent-findings/ops-hygiene-bundle-2026-05-24.md`. |
| E2E-AUTH-TOLERANT-LOGIN | **`loginAs` in `e2e/tests/helpers/auth.ts` times out ~30% when Keycloak SSO cookie is hot** — the Sign In button bounces back to `/` without showing the form. UX-bundle agent shipped a local `tolerantLogin` that handles both the cookie-hot and cookie-cold paths. Fold the pattern back into the shared helper so every spec benefits + the RDM-002-E2E-FIX flakiness disappears. | XS | ✅ **shipped** 2026-05-24 | OPS hygiene bundle. Shared `loginAs` now tolerates SSO-cookie-hot + cookie-cold + NextAuth redirect-loop. Five specs' local copies (`ux-polish-2026-05-24`, `rdm-001`, `rdm-002`, `rdm-005`, `ux-pattern-d-count-badges`) collapsed back to the shared helper. |
| CRIT-MAIN-BROKEN-TARGETENTITYRESOLVER-DUPLICATE | **`backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/provenance/filters/TargetEntityResolver.java` declares two `resolve(String path)` methods** — one instance (line 49), one static (line 85) — and `mvn clean compile` fails with `method resolve(java.lang.String) is already defined in class TargetEntityResolver`. Reproduced on `main @ 373543df` from a freshly-checked-out worktree (no dirty mods), so this is a real main-branch break, not local pollution. Surfaced by the CRIT-SMOKE-NOT-CATCHING-BUILD-BREAK self-test in the OPS hygiene bundle agent run — exactly the trap the fix is designed to catch (the smoke endpoint had been reporting 25/25 PASS against the stale running backend image while `mvn clean compile` was already red). Owner: provenance / PROV-RESOLVER-PATHWALK touch surface, likely landed during the PROV-2026-05-24 work. Fix shape (unverified): collapse the two `resolve` overloads or rename the static to `resolveStatic` / `parsePath`. | XS | ✅ **done 2026-05-27** | Static method already renamed to `resolveStatic` (line 85) on current main — no duplicate exists; build passes. Verified by grep 2026-05-27. |
| RDM-005a | **RDM-005 server-side completeness scoring + harvest filter + child-DO aggregate + embargo/funder checks.** Deferred from RDM-005 v0 (client-side only). Adds `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/completeness` so external harvesters (re3data, OpenAIRE, Helmholtz Databus) can filter by completeness score. Aggregate child-DO annotations into the Collection-level score (design call — would partially mask Collection-level gaps; alternative: separate "DO-level metadata health" rollup). Add embargo end-date check (needs the `embargo_until` field from LIC1's deferred slice). Add funder grant reference check (DataCite §17 FundingReference — needs a `funding[]` field). "Show only failing" filter on the widget's per-check list. | M | queued | RDM-005 deferred follow-up. |

## MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-* — MongoDB substrate audit (live shepard)

Filed 2026-05-24 by the **MongoDB substrate audit agent** running against
live `infrastructure-mongodb-1` (MongoDB 8.0.4). Findings file:
`aidocs/agent-findings/mongodb-substrate-audit-2026-05-24.md`. Pairs with
sibling agent #209's file-routing investigation; both feed
`MFFD-DB-AUDIT-REAL-DATA`.

| ID | Description | Size | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-001 | **N+1 in `StructuredDataSearchService.findMatchingReferences:76-95`** — one Mongo round-trip per reachable reference. Confirms long-standing SD1 backlog row with live evidence. Fix: either ship SD1 (deprecate Mongo SD query path → Postgres), or batch the per-container `find()` calls into a single `$or` / `$in` over the union of reachable containers. **MAJOR**. | M | queued | Cite to SD1. Audit row is the perf-evidence partner. |
| MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-002 | **One Mongo collection per FileContainer + StructuredDataContainer** (`FileService.createFileContainer`, `StructuredDataService.createStructuredDataContainer`). Proven not to scale: BUG-F surfaced 4 197 SD collections at MFFD volume. Mongo-side cleanup ran post-redrive but **Neo4j carries 4 197 `:StructuredDataContainer{deleted: TRUE}` tombstones with no Mongo counterpart** (verified 2026-05-24 sample of 5 mongoIds: all GONE from Mongo). Long-term fix is structural (SD1 → Postgres for SD; FS1 family already in flight for files). Short-term fix: GC the Neo4j tombstones. **MAJOR**. | M | queued | Pairs with SD-WIPE-AND-REGET + SM1 retention. |
| MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-003 | **JSON-string concatenation to build Mongo query** in `StructuredDataSearchService:91` (`mongoQuery += ", " + mongoSearchQuery + "}"` → `Document.parse(mongoQuery)`). Same shape as the C5/C5b Cypher string-build pattern that was explicitly killed in this fork. Should use `com.mongodb.client.model.Filters` DSL. Lower exploit ceiling than the Cypher case (Mongo `$where` is not used anywhere) but still wrong. **MAJOR**. | S | done (2026-05-27) | Fixed in `StructuredDataSearchService.java`: replaced string-concat with `Filters.and(in("_id", objectIds), Document.parse("{" + userQuery + "}"))`. Dead helpers `makeMongoQueryId`/`makeMongoQueryArray` removed. 2 new ArgumentCaptor tests added. Pairs with MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-001 — both die when SD1 ships. |
| MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-004 | **No backend-side array-wrap fallback in `StructuredDataService.createStructuredData:53`** (`Document.parse(payload.getPayload())`). BUG-E v15.12 wraps client-side in the importer; any non-importer client POSTing a JSON array as SD payload still 500s with the BSON-array exception. Defensive fix: detect `payload.trim().startsWith("[")` → wrap as `{items: [...], _wrapped: "v15.12-bug-e"}` server-side. Closes the BUG-E class permanently. **MAJOR**. | S | done (2026-05-27) | Sibling: `MFFD-SD-BACKEND-WRAP-FALLBACK` (preexisting). Fixed in `StructuredDataService.java` + 3 new tests in `StructuredDataServiceTest.java`. |
| MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-005 | **No `$jsonSchema` validators on any of 21 collections.** Cheap wins available on the singleton-schema collections: `userAvatars` (4 fields), `_shepard_files`, `_shepard_videos`, and the bookkeeping shape inside every `FileContainer*` (`{createdAt, name, md5, fileSize, FileMongoId}`). Per-container SD collections must stay polymorphic by design. Supports the FAIR R1 rigour the platform is reaching for. **MAJOR**. | S | done (2026-05-27) | warn-mode validators on 3 singleton collections (`userAvatars`, `_shepard_files`, `_shepard_videos`) shipped in `MongoSchemaInitializer.java` (`@ApplicationScoped` + `@Observes StartupEvent`, idempotent `collMod`/`createCollection`). `_shepard_videos` confirmed as singleton-schema doc collection (same FileService bookkeeping shape, fixed name unlike `FileContainer*`). per-container `FileContainer*` deferred — dynamically-named UUID collections, different scope. TODO: flip `validationAction` to `"error"` after confirming existing data clean. 25 unit tests in `MongoSchemaInitializerTest.java`. |
| MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-006 | **`FileService.deleteFileContainer:237-240` sequential per-doc GridFS delete loop.** N round-trips to delete N files in a container. Bulk variants exist in driver 5.1+ (`bucket.delete(List<ObjectId>)`). For the canonical MFFD `FileContainer7693e977…` (34 files) this is 34 round-trips on every cleanup. **MINOR**. | XS | done (2026-05-27) | Driver 5.1.x does not expose `delete(List<ObjectId>)`; shipped pre-fetch-then-delete: cursor materialised into `List<ObjectId>` via `.map().into()` in one pass, then iterated for deletes — separates cursor round-trips from write ops. A future `fs.files.deleteMany($in)` + `fs.chunks.deleteMany($in)` bypass (O(2) round-trips) tracked separately. Tests: `deleteExistingFileContainerTest` (single-file), `_multiFile` (3 files, all deletes verified), `_emptyContainer` (0 deletes, drop still runs). |
| MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-007 | **`FileMongoId` stored as STRING but `fs.files._id` is ObjectId** across `_shepard_files`, `_shepard_videos`, every `FileContainer*` collection. Java side casts at every read (`new ObjectId(...)`) so correctness holds, but any Mongo aggregation `$lookup` join from a shadow collection into `fs.files` silently returns empty unless the join expression pre-casts. Future analytics / cleanup / orphan-scan jobs will trip on this. Mitigations: (a) store as ObjectId; (b) document the convention loudly; (c) add a `$lookup`-with-`$convert` helper. **MINOR**. | S | done (2026-05-27) — convention documented; migration to native ObjectId storage deferred (needs V## data migration across all shadow collections). | Convention comment added above `FILEID_ATTR` in `FileService.java`; `$lookup`-with-`$convert` helper note added to `MongoSchemaInitializer.java` class-level Javadoc. |
| MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-008 | **`StructuredDataService.createStructuredData:60-61` silently strips underscore-prefixed user keys.** `var forbidden = toInsert.keySet().stream().filter(k -> k.startsWith("_")).toList(); forbidden.forEach(toInsert::remove);` — user payload `_my_field` is data-lost without warning. Should: (a) log a `Log.warn` on every strip; (b) restrict to known-conflicting keys (`_id`, `_meta`) instead of all `_*`; (c) reject the payload with 400 + field-list if any conflict. **MINOR**. | XS | 🤖 hourly-bot-shipped-beta (PR #1511) | Cheap fix; the silent-data-loss class is the worst kind of API foot-gun. |
| MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-009 | **No Mongo connection-pool config.** Driver defaults apply (`maxPoolSize=100`, `minPoolSize=0`, `maxIdleTimeMS=∞`, `serverSelectionTimeoutMS=30s`). Document defaults explicitly in `application.properties` + tune from Prometheus metrics per PERF2b's `mongo_latency_high` rule. **GAP**. | S | ✅ **done (2026-05-27)** | Defaults documented as commented-out block in `application.properties` after the `connection-string` line; operator now sees defaults and PERF2b guidance without any active override. |
| MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-010 | **No TTL indexes anywhere.** Nothing self-prunes. SM1 retention and PRE-MUT-SNAP* both need this; today their snapshots / orphan-grace data would grow without bound in Mongo. Audit row is the dependency-flag for those two designs. **GAP**. | M | queued | Cite SM1 + PRE-MUT-SNAP1. |
| MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-011 | **`quarkus.mongodb.connection-string=mongodb://mongo@mongodb:27017` carries no DB name; `MongoClientWrapper.init()` silently falls back to literal `"database"`.** An admin who overrides the URI and forgets the DB segment lands writes into the wrong DB without error. Fix: log loudly at startup, or refuse start when DB name absent (the prod URI already has it; this would only affect future overrides). **GAP**. | XS | done (2026-05-27) | Shipped: `shouldWarnAboutFallback()` static helper + `LOG.warn` in `init()` when resolved name is fallback AND no explicit `quarkus.mongodb.database` key is set. Tests in `MongoClientWrapperTest`. |
| MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-012 | **No per-collection / per-container Mongo size cap.** A single 532 MB `Confluence-space-export-151906-2.html.zip` upload (2026-05-22) is 60% of the entire Mongo substrate. Add: (a) admin alert when any container exceeds `shepard.mongo.container.max-bytes` (default 1 GiB); (b) `shepard.mongo.file.max-bytes` upload-rejection cap (default 2 GiB). Pair with SM1a's 1-year default retention. **GAP**. | S | ✅ **done (2026-05-27)** | (b) shipped: `shepard.mongo.file.max-bytes=2147483648` (2 GiB default). Guard added in `FileService.enforceFileSizeCap()` — called from all GridFS write paths (`createFile(..long..)`, `createFileWithSha256(..long..)`). All REST callers (`FileRest`, `FileBundleReferenceRest`, `FileReferenceV2Rest`) updated to pass temp-file length. `VideoStreamReferenceService` also guards using same config key. Throws `InvalidRequestException` (HTTP 400) before any GridFS write. Two-layer model documented: set `quarkus.http.limits.max-body-size` to also block at the HTTP layer. (a) per-container admin alert deferred to SM1a. Test: `createFile_exceedsMaxBytes_throwsInvalidRequestException` in `FileServiceTest`. |
| MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-013 | **3× duplicate `lumen-hotfire.mp4` blobs in `_shepard_videos`** (8.6 MB each × 3 = 26 MB redundant). Same MD5, distinct ObjectIds — each upload created a fresh GridFS blob with no dedup. Stop-gap: deduplicate by MD5 + reuse the existing blob if present. Long-term: SHA-256-content-addressable storage is the FS1/Garage trajectory, but the GridFS path inherits the dup-tolerant behaviour today. **MINOR**. | S | ✅ **done (2026-05-27)** | Shipped: `VideoStreamReferenceService.storeWithDedup()` buffers stream ≤ 100 MiB, computes MD5, checks `_shepard_videos` for existing document with same digest, reuses existing OID if found. Large files (> 100 MiB) bypass buffering and stream directly. Tests: `storeWithDedup_duplicateMd5_reusesExistingBlob` + `storeWithDedup_newMd5_uploadsNewBlob` in `VideoStreamReferenceServiceTest`. |
| MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-014 | **6 empty per-container Mongo collections (24 KB wasted) — `StructuredDataContainer839614f2`, `…54ec30b6`, `…4702ed7d`, `…4786abf3`, `…6931e4d4`, `…4277ad7c`, `FileContainerecc172da`, `FileContainer8a3e519e`.** Each is a placeholder that was created but never written to. Either the container-create endpoint doesn't roll back on subsequent failure, or the data shape permits create-and-never-use. Investigate the create path for missing transactional bookkeeping. **MINOR**. | XS | done (2026-05-27) — **two root causes confirmed, both WAI at current scale, one structural gap logged.** (1) **Normal create-and-never-use:** Mongo collection is created immediately on container create (`createStructuredDataContainer()` / `createFileContainer()` call `mongoDatabase.createCollection()` as their first step), so any container created but never written to will leave an empty collection. This is the expected lifecycle — the 8 empty collections are containers that were created (correctly) but had no data uploaded to them. (2) **Missing rollback on create failure:** `StructuredDataContainerService.createContainer()` creates the Mongo collection first, then writes the Neo4j node (`structuredDataContainerDAO.createOrUpdate`), then calls `permissionsService.createPermissions()`. Neither the Mongo write nor the Neo4j write are wrapped in a shared transaction (no `@Transactional` on any of these methods). If the Neo4j write or permissions write fails after the Mongo collection was already created, the collection becomes a permanent orphan. **Delete path is correctly wired** — `deleteContainer()` always drops the Mongo collection. **Fix recommendation (not yet implemented):** Add Mongo collection cleanup to `createContainer()` catch block (try Mongo-create + Neo4j-create, drop Mongo collection on Neo4j failure). Alternatively, accept accumulation and add a `/v2/admin/mongo/orphan-sweep` endpoint for periodic admin cleanup. Fix is ~10 lines in each service; tracked for next maintenance window. | Audit-trail-followup. |

## PLUGIN-{SPATIAL,TABLES,HDF5}-AUDIT-2026-05-24-* — plugin design audit findings

Source: [`aidocs/agent-findings/plugin-design-audit-2026-05-24.md`](agent-findings/plugin-design-audit-2026-05-24.md). Three plugins audited in parallel: spatial (PL1b + PV1c + A1c shipped; perf + frontend gaps), HDF5 (A5a/b/d shipped; A5c/A5e queued; frontend gap), tables (no row + no design doc yet — file SSOT doc first).

### Spatial

| ID | Item | effort | status | notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| PLUGIN-SPATIAL-AUDIT-2026-05-24-001 | **PM1f sidecar migration — declare `postgis` in `SpatiotemporalPluginManifest.sidecars()` + remove from central `infrastructure/docker-compose.yml`.** Closes the `feedback_plugins_declare_sidecars.md` gap. Cross-ref `file-s3` reference impl (`FileS3PluginManifestSidecarsTest`). Bundle into the same PR as PLUGIN-HDF5-AUDIT-2026-05-24-001 (matching shape; cross-cutting finding #1). | S | done (2026-05-27) — sidecar declared in manifest; compose removal deferred to SidecarsAssembler milestone | Audit §Spatial.E + cross-cutting §1. |
| PLUGIN-SPATIAL-AUDIT-2026-05-24-002 | **`V1.1.0__indexes.sql` — BRIN on `time` + GIN(`measurements jsonb_path_ops`).** Two cheapest perf wins per `aidocs/data/82 §2.1+§2.3`. Additive index creation via `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY`; zero migration risk. | XS | done (2026-05-27) | Audit §Spatial.G + `aidocs/82 §4` recommendation. `plugins/spatiotemporal/src/main/resources/db/spatial/migration/V1.1.0__indexes.sql` ships `spatial_data_points_time_brin` (BRIN, pages_per_range=32) + `spatial_data_points_measurements_gin` (GIN jsonb_path_ops); both `IF NOT EXISTS` for idempotency. Note: `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY` cannot run inside a Flyway transaction; operators with large live tables may run indexes manually with CONCURRENTLY before upgrade — `IF NOT EXISTS` guard will skip on migration run. |

### SPATIAL-V6 — temporal-spatial engineering data substrate (v6 flagship)

SSOT design: `aidocs/data/90-spatial-as-temporal-sweep.md`. Greenfield reshape of `shepard-plugin-spatial`. **Retires** `PLUGIN-SPATIAL-AUDIT-2026-05-24-001` (sidecar folded into substrate-collapse) + `-002` (BRIN+GIN folded into V2.0.0 green-field schema). **Carries forward** `-003` (MVT/MapLibre — independent capability) + `-004` (postgis collapse — absorbed into V6-001 as prerequisite) + `-005` (GeoQueryCapability — independent forward-pointer).

### SPATIAL-UNIFY-* — one spatial integration (spatial as a unified Reference kind)

SSOT design: [`aidocs/integrations/124-spatial-unified-reference.md`](integrations/124-spatial-unified-reference.md). **Extends** SPATIAL-V6 (`aidocs/data/90`, the substrate/viewer SSOT) — does not re-open it. Operator directive 2026-06-10 (verbatim): *"spatial data should be also a reference, also reconcile overlap with spatiotemporal, so we end up with one spatial integration."* Closes the bolt-on `DataObjectSpatialContainersPane.vue` + manual `spatial-importer` pass UX; makes spatial a `kind=spatial` reference in the unified `/v2/references?kind=` surface. Key model finding: `SpatialDataReference extends BasicReference` + `[:IS_IN_CONTAINER] → SpatialDataContainer` is **already** the FileReference→FileContainer shape — this is a surface+convergence job, not a data-model rewrite. **Pairs with** PLUGIN-V2-001 (the `/v2/spatial-containers/{appId}` shelf) + SPATIAL-V6-004 (brush-trace renderer). Frozen v1 `spatialDataReferences` + `spatialDataContainers` (in `openapi-5.4.0.json`) stay byte-compat.

| ID | Item | effort | status | notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SPATIAL-UNIFY-001 | **Research/decision spike.** Decide: (a) promotion trigger shape; (b) reference-kind wire token `"spatial"` vs `PayloadKind.name()="spatiotemporal"` reconciliation; (c) the `SpatialDataReferenceService.findByAppId(appId)` finder shape. | S | ✅ **resolved** (2026-06-10) | **Operator decision:** promotion is an **in-context, per-file** action (one-click "Promote to spatial" on an eligible FileReference row via `POST /v2/spatial/promote`) — NOT eager-on-upload, NOT per-Collection toggle, NOT batch pass; the user decides per-file when a costly container is spawned (supersedes §11's eager-vs-opt-in question). (b) wire token `"spatial"`, PayloadKind name stays `"spatiotemporal"`. (c) `findByAppId` added to service+DAO. |

Unit tests Video-handler-shaped.
