### V2UI-CONFORMANCE — strict v2 annotation sweep (filed 2026-06-13)

Findings SSOT: `aidocs/agent-findings/v2-ui-conformance-2026-06-13.md`. Drove the
operator-reported live annotation crash to ground: `annotated.ts`'s
`AnnotatedCollection`/`AnnotatedDataObject`/`AnnotatedReference` were keyed on the
v1-singular `SemanticAnnotationApi` + numeric `collection.id`/`dataObject.id` (dropped
from v2 entities → `undefined` → `getAllCollectionAnnotations` 400). Converged the
whole `Annotated` family onto the typed `SemanticAnnotationsApi` (`/v2/annotations`,
`useV2ShepardApi`) keyed by `subjectAppId` + `subjectKind`. **Shipped this PR** (scope
`V2UI-CONFORMANCE`): `annotated.ts` rewrite + all ~16 construction sites repointed to
appId; `dataTableElementMappingUtil.ts` carries reference `appId` onto legacy meta;
`relationshipTableElement(.ts)` carries `referenceAppId`/`referenceKind`;
`OpenIn3dViewButton`/`CreateDataReferenceDialog`/`AddRelationshipDialog` take
`dataObjectAppId`/`fileReferenceAppId`. Tests: `annotatedSubject.test.ts` (10),
`useAnnotations.test.ts` + `AnnotatedChannel.test.ts` green.

| ID | Item | Size | Status | First references |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **V2UI-CHANNEL-ANNO-CLIENT** | `AnnotatedChannel` in `composables/annotated.ts` hits `/v2/timeseries-containers/{containerId}/channels/{channelShepardId}/annotations` (TS-SEMANTIC-REST) via raw `fetch` — the generated client has no typed class for this nested channel-annotation resource. Named v2 fallback (on the `/v2/` surface, NOT `/shepard/api/`), so not a 404-class bug. Generate a `TimeseriesChannelAnnotationsApi` client + repoint `AnnotatedChannel`. | S | **✓ shipped 2026-06-14 (PR #1900, sha dad7ec0a9)** | `composables/annotated.ts` migrated to typed `TimeseriesChannelAnnotationsApi` client; `SemanticAnnotationIO.java` gains `appId` field; latent delete-by-numericId bug fixed; 7 Vitest tests rewritten. |
| **V2UI-TS-ANNO-V2** | `AnnotatedTimeseries` in `composables/annotated.ts` is the only remaining v1 annotation caller — `useShepardApi(TimeseriesContainerApi)` keyed on the per-timeseries-channel numeric `id` + `containerId` (the upstream `/shepard/api/.../timeseries/{id}/annotations` route). The `TimeseriesEntity` carries its own numeric id from the still-v1 timeseries channel-content surface (named-fallback set). Migrate once channel content has a v2 appId twin. | S | queued | `composables/annotated.ts` (AnnotatedTimeseries); `components/container/timeseries/TimeseriesMeasurementsTable.vue`; `components/context/display-components/timeseries/ShowTimeseriesReferenceDialog.vue`. Blocked-by: TS channel-content v2 (per `aidocs/platform/87`). |
| **V2UI-MFFD-NDT-ANNO-V2** | `MffdNdtGridCard.vue:75` + `useMffdNdtGridProbe.ts:67` call the v1-singular `SemanticAnnotationApi.getAllDataObjectAnnotations({collectionId, dataObjectId})` on numeric ids resolved from the loaded v2 collection (`collectionNumericId` — documented v1-fallback shape, NOT a crash). The probe also uses `DataObjectApi.getAllDataObjects({collectionId})`. Opportunistic migration to `SemanticAnnotationsApi.listAnnotations({subjectAppId, subjectKind:"DataObject"})` once a per-collection DataObject-by-appId enumeration is available. Deferred: works today; part of the MFFD NDT cluster. | S | **✓ shipped fire-349** | Migrated: `DataObjectsApi.listDataObjects({collectionAppId})` + `SemanticAnnotationsApi.listAnnotations({subjectAppId, subjectKind:"DataObject"})` in both card and probe; `DataObjectWithAnnotations.id:number` → `appId:string`; probe cache now keyed by string appId; `collectionId` prop renamed to `collectionAppId`; 30 unit tests updated and passing; typecheck green. |
| **V2UI-REF-CREATE-V2** | `CreateDataReferenceDialog.vue` + `AddRelationshipDialog.vue` still take numeric `collectionId`/`dataObjectId` props for their reference-create calls (`useShepardApi(FileReferenceApi/TimeseriesReferenceApi/StructuredDataReferenceApi/UriReferenceApi/...)`). This is **dispatcher-owned REF-API-MIGRATION** (V2-SWEEP-004) territory — do NOT migrate here. This PR only added a `dataObjectAppId` prop to drive the v2 template-prefill annotation pull; the numeric create plumbing stays for the dispatcher. | M | deferred-to-dispatcher | Owned by V2-SWEEP-004-REF-API-MIGRATION; `components/context/data-references/create-dialog/CreateDataReferenceDialog.vue`; `components/context/display-components/relationships/add-dialog/AddRelationshipDialog.vue`. |
| **V2UI-REFERENCEDBY-LIST-V2** | `ReferencedByRow.vue:50` lists timeseries references via `useShepardApi(TimeseriesReferenceApi).getAllTimeseriesReferences` (v1 list). The annotation surfaces on this row are now v2 (appId); the reference LIST itself remains a dispatcher REF-API-MIGRATION concern. | S | **✓ shipped fire-384 (inline, no PR)** — already migrated during BUG-DO-DETAIL-A-TOAST-2026-06-29: raw fetch to `/v2/references?kind=timeseries&dataObjectAppId=` (v2 unified endpoint, comment at line 77). No v1 call remains. | Owned by V2-SWEEP-004-REF-API-MIGRATION; `components/container/timeseries/ReferencedByRow.vue`. |

### RESEED findings — deploy gaps surfaced by the feature seeds (2026-06-09)

The reseed ran LUMEN + 15 `feat-*` seeds against the live deploy and surfaced real "merged-but-broken-on-deploy" gaps — the high-value output of testing features via reseed.

| ID | Item | Size | Status | First references |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **RESEED-FIND-FILEPARSER-DISPATCH** | **SHIPPED.** `FileParserPlugin` SPI now wired into the singleton file-upload path. Canonical `de.dlr.shepard.spi.fileparser.FileParserPlugin` + `FileParserRegistry` (CDI `Instance<FileParserPlugin>`) discovered at startup; `SingletonFileReferenceService` fires `registry.runAll(...)` as a fire-and-forget secondary write after the GridFS store — a matching parser's `urn:shepard:*` annotations land on the new FileReference's appId. All 4 format plugins (`fileformat-{cad,thermography,svdx,robotics}`) migrated to the canonical SPI + register via `META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.spi.fileparser.FileParserPlugin`; robotics wired into the `with-plugins` profile + `quarkus.index-dependency` + `scripts/install-plugins.sh`. Tests: backend `FileParserRegistryTest` (10, dispatch+persist mechanics) + cad `CadFileParserDispatchTest` (real `.step` → `urn:shepard:cad:format=step` + `step_schema=AP242…` through the registry dispatch loop). | M | shipped | PR (see branch `reseed-fileparser-dispatch`); was branch `wip-fileparser-spi`; `feat-cad-plybook`/`feat-ndt-thermography` findings |
| **RESEED-FIND-RENDER-PNG** | **No PNG `ViewRecipeRenderer` installed** → `/v2/shapes/render` `Accept: image/png` falls back to JSON (only SceneGraphPlay executor ships). Blocks `feat-render-media` + MFFD NDT-grid PNG. Needs a renderer with `producibleMedia()⊇image/png`. ✓ **DONE** — `Trace3DPngRenderer` (`plugins/vis-trace3d/.../render/Trace3DPngRenderer.java`) claims the `Trace3DViewShape` IRI (`http://semantics.dlr.de/shepard-ui/trace3d#Trace3DViewShape`, the IRI `feat-trace3d`/`feat-render-media` seeds target), declares `producibleMedia()⊇image/png`, and rasterises a labelled view-recipe card pure-JVM via `BufferedImage`+`Graphics2D`+`ImageIO` (no headless browser). Registered via `META-INF/services/...ViewRecipeRenderer` (ServiceLoader). Unit test asserts valid PNG signature (`Trace3DPngRendererTest`, 6 tests green). | M | done | `feat-render-media` finding; V2CONV-A1; `plugins/vis-trace3d/` |
| **RESEED-FIND-RENDER-PNG-LOG** | **PNG render still 422s live despite `Trace3DPngRenderer` shipping (regression on RESEED-FIND-RENDER-PNG).** `POST /v2/shapes/render` `Accept: image/png` returns `422 {error=media render failed for image/png, renderer=Trace3DPngRenderer}` on the deployed image. Root cause (backend log): `Trace3DPngRenderer.rasterise()` throws at runtime, then its catch block at `Trace3DPngRenderer.java:132` calls `io.quarkus.logging.Log.warnf(...)` which itself throws `java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException: Using io.quarkus.logging.Log is only possible with Quarkus bytecode transformation; make sure the archive is indexed` — the plugin jar is not Jandex-indexed (no `beans.xml` / `quarkus.index-dependency` entry for vis-trace3d). The secondary-write `Log` failure (violates "secondary writes are fire-and-forget") masks the primary fault and surfaces 422 instead of the intended JSON fallback. Fix: (a) replace `io.quarkus.logging.Log` in the plugin with `org.jboss.logging.Logger` (or add the Jandex index for vis-trace3d), AND (b) determine why `rasterise()` throws — likely headless AWT (no `-Djava.awt.headless=true` on the backend JVM; `BufferedImage`/`ImageIO`/font init). `feat-render-media` seed degrades gracefully (reports the gap; JSON path is green). | S | **✓ shipped 2026-06-11** | **(a)** Replaced `io.quarkus.logging.Log` with `org.jboss.logging.Logger` (private static final field) in all 3 vis-trace3d source files (`Trace3DPngRenderer`, `VisTrace3DPluginManifest`, `SceneGraphPlayTransformExecutor`) — the catch block now logs correctly and falls back to JSON instead of propagating an `UnsupportedOperationException`. **(b)** Added a `static { System.setProperty("java.awt.headless", "true"); }` initialiser in `Trace3DPngRenderer` — must run before AWT toolkit loads; since this class is ServiceLoader-discovered at startup, the property is set before any font/image creation. All 42 vis-trace3d unit tests green. First-refs: `plugins/vis-trace3d/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/plugins/vistrace3d/render/Trace3DPngRenderer.java`; `VisTrace3DPluginManifest.java`; `scenegraph/SceneGraphPlayTransformExecutor.java`. |
| **RESEED-FIND-KRL-CDI** | **KRL executor live-broken: `KrlTrajectoryService` CDI bean unresolved** on the deployed image → materialize 422 internal-error before the sidecar. Dispatch/registry sound (vis-trace3d executor works same image); krl-interpreter bean activation is the gap. | S | queued | `feat-krl-transform`; `plugins/krl-interpreter/` |
| **RESEED-FIND-KRL-CDI** | **KRL executor live-broken: `KrlTrajectoryService` CDI bean unresolved** on the deployed image → materialize 422 internal-error before the sidecar. Dispatch/registry sound (vis-trace3d executor works same image); krl-interpreter bean activation is the gap. Root cause: Quarkus ARC removes `KrlTrajectoryService` as "unused" because no static `@Inject` references it — only a runtime `CDI.current().select(KrlTrajectoryService.class).get()` inside the ServiceLoader SPI `KrlTrajectoryTransformExecutor`. Fix: `@io.quarkus.arc.Unremovable` on `KrlTrajectoryService`. | S | **✓ shipped 2026-06-10** | `@Unremovable` + `@ApplicationScoped` retention contract test added. Merged PR #1815 (`a8e1573285`). `plugins/krl-interpreter/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/transform/krl/services/KrlTrajectoryService.java`; branch `reseed-find-krl-cdi` |
| **RESEED-FIND-APIKEY-ROLES** | **API-key JWTs carry no `roles` claim** → role-gated endpoints (NCR `status` needs `quality-engineer`) 403 even for admin keys. apikey-mint must embed realm roles. ✓ **DONE** — `ApiKeyService.createApiKey` now inherits the owner's *effective* Neo4j `:HAS_ROLE` grants (same source as `JwtTokenAuthService.resolveDualSourceRoles`) intersected with `shepard.apikey.role-allowlist` (default widened to `instance-admin,quality-engineer`); explicit-roles path unchanged. `@RolesAllowed` now passes for API-key auth identically to interactive tokens. | M | done | `feat-ncr-disposition` finding; `ApiKeyService`; allowlist `application.properties:382` |
| **RESEED-FIND-N10S-SPARQL** | **SPARQL/RDF projection down post-wipe: n10s `_GraphConfig` not initialised** (init needs empty graph; bootstrap populates first → `/rdf/neo4j/sparql` 404). Freshly-wiped instances have broken SPARQL. Needs graphconfig init ordered first or a re-init tolerant of existing nodes. | M | ✅ merged (fire-465, PR #2391) — replaces `n10s.graphconfig.init()` (empty-graph guard) with `MERGE (gc:_GraphConfig) ON CREATE SET …` + `n10s.graphconfig.set(…)` two-step; 12 tests updated/added | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/context/semantic/N10sBootstrapHook.java`; `feat-semantic-sparql`; OPS-MIGRATION-HEALTHCHECK |
| **RESEED-FIND-MISC** | (a) `GET /v2/annotations?subjectAppId=<Collection>` 403s to owner (DataObject subject 200) — perm asymmetry; (b) metadata-completeness frontend-computed (no backend endpoint); (c) `unhide` not bundled (`/v2/admin/unhide/config` 500). | S | (a) ✅ done (fire-464) — `SemanticAnnotationV2Rest.java:957–966` confirmed already uses 3-arg `isAccessTypeAllowedForUser()` overload; the 4-arg form with `jwtIat=0L` that produced stale cache keys was fixed with an explanatory comment; (b) queued; (c) queued | `feat-fair-publish`/`feat-semantic-sparql` findings |

## V2-SWEEP — frontend v2-exclusivity rework (filed 2026-06-10)

Worklist SSOT: `aidocs/agent-findings/frontend-v2-exclusivity-audit.md`.
Waves 1–4 shipped 2026-06-10 (sidebar v2/appId-native; URDF render link
carries FileReference appId; detail-page mutations on v2 merge-patch;
data-object list composables v2-only). Rows below are the documented
v1 exceptions left behind (each cited at its call site) + backend gaps.

| ID | Item | Size | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **SIDEBAR-V2-CREATE** | Migrate `CreateDataObjectDialog`'s blank form off v1 `createDataObject` onto `POST /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects`; needs appId-capable Parent/Predecessor inputs (today they ride the v1 SearchApi + numeric ids resolved from loaded v2 entities). | M | **✅ shipped fire-384 (PR pending)** — `createDataObject()` now calls `useV2ShepardApi(DataObjectsApi).createDataObjectV2({collectionAppId, ...})`; `DataObjectToCreate` type base updated to `CreateDataObjectV2Request["createDataObjectV2"]`; `ParentInput`/`PredecessorInput` numeric ids remain wire-compatible (v2 body accepts `parentId: number` + `predecessorIds: number[]`); `useShepardApi` import dropped from this file. | Call sites: `CollectionSidebar.vue`, `CollectionSidebarItemContextMenu.vue`, `CreateDataObjectDialog.vue` |
| **SEARCH-V2** | v2 search surface — `GET /v2/search` already shipped (global collection+DO search); sub-rows below migrate the remaining v1 `SearchApi` callers onto v2. | L | 🔄 in-flight | Named exception in CLAUDE.md |
| **SEARCH-V2-1** | Add `?collectionAppId=` optional scope param to `GET /v2/search` so DataObject results can be narrowed to a single collection using the stable UUID v7. Resolves appId → Neo4j id internally via `CollectionDAO.findByAppId()`; when active, collection items are omitted from results (scoped DO-only search). Unblocks SEARCH-V2-3 (`useDataObjectSearch` migration). | S | **✅ shipped (PR #2264)** | Backend only; 3 new unit tests in `SearchV2RestTest` |

Cascades into `CollectionPrefillableInput.vue` and `CollectionAutocomplete.vue` — both currently bind/emit numeric `collectionId` to parents; migrate to appId where possible, resolve numeric id at call time for any remaining v1-only ops. | M | **✅ shipped (PR #2265, merged 2026-07-03)** | Unblocks LINEAGE-V2 |
| **SEARCH-V2-3** | Migrate `useDataObjectSearch` composable from v1 `POST /shepard/api/search` → `GET /v2/search?collectionAppId=` (requires SEARCH-V2-1). Update `DataObjectAutocomplete.vue`, `DataObjectPrefillableInput.vue`, `AddRelationshipAutocomplete.vue` to pass `collectionAppId` string instead of numeric `collectionId`. | M | **✅ shipped (PR #2266)** | Requires SEARCH-V2-1 |

New `searchByText(String)` on `UserGroupSearchService` returns `List<UserGroup>` entities. Jackson used for safe JSON query build. No numeric id exposed. Unit tests in `UserSearchV2RestTest` (4) and `UserGroupV2RestTest` (+2). | S | **✅ shipped (PR #2273, fire-394)** | Unblocks SEARCH-V2-4 |
| **SEARCH-V2-4** | Migrate remaining v1 search callers: member/permission user search composables (`useMemberSearch`, `usePermissionUserSearch`) off `useShepardApi(SearchApi)` onto `GET /v2/users?q=` and `GET /v2/user-groups?q=`. `backend-client/UserApi` extended with `searchUsersV2` method; `ListUserGroupsRequest` extended with `q?` param. Groups mapped from `UserGroupV2` to `UserGroup` with `id=0` sentinel; equality checks in `EditPermissionsDialog`, `MemberPermissionList` migrated to `appId`. `MemberAutocomplete` label updated to drop now-meaningless numeric id. Frontend: SDR detail page now watches `referenceV2.appId` and fetches `GET /v2/references/{appId}/content` to populate `structuredDataDataTableItems`. Table no longer renders empty for UUID-routed users. | Pairs with REFS-V2-PANELS-2 (fire-397). |

| **CONTAINER-V2-ROUTE** | Container detail routes (`/containers/{type}{numericId}`) stay numeric because the container detail pages fetch via the v1-generated `getXContainer({ containerId })`, whose path resolves ONLY the numeric Neo4j id (the frozen v1 container GET 404s on an appId). Migrate the container accessors (`TimeseriesContainerAccessor`, `FileContainerAccessor`, `StructuredDataAccessor`, `SpatialDataContainerAccessor`) to a v2 appId-keyed GET, then flip the route segments to appId. | M | **✓ shipped 2026-06-12** (`53638d69`) | V2-SWEEP-003-2 (branch `V2-SWEEP-003-container-get-accessor`, 2026-06-12): `FileContainerAccessor`, `TimeseriesContainerAccessor`, `StructuredDataAccessor` all have dual-path `fetchData()` — UUID param → raw fetch `GET /v2/containers/{appId}` (V2 path); numeric param → v1 `getXContainer({containerId})` (V1-EXCEPTION for HeaderBar search). `ContainerRouteParams.containerId` changed from `number` to `string` (parser no longer parseInt's UUIDs). `ContainerAccessor` base class TId changed from `number` to `string`. Route builders in `CollectionContainersPanel.vue` and `ContainerList.vue` use `c.appId`. `ContainerTitleAndMetadataDisplay` prop renamed `id → appId`. `numericId` getter resolves Neo4j id for remaining v1 call sites. FE typecheck + 1961 Vitest tests green. `SpatialDataContainerAccessor` still numeric (spatial has no v2 GET yet — queued). |
| **COLLREF-V2-APPID** | A `CollectionReference`'s `referencedCollectionId` is the numeric id of ANOTHER collection and carries no appId sibling on the v2 wire (the `BasicReference` payload omits it), so the relationship-table mapper can't build an appId route to it (sync mapper, no async resolve). Add `referencedCollectionAppId` (+ `referencedDataObjectAppId`) to the CollectionReference / DataObjectReference v2 payload (additive + nullable), then route on it. Until then the cross-collection link renders as a non-navigable label. | S (backend) | **✓ shipped 2026-06-11** | Backend (V2-SWEEP-004-1): `CollectionReferenceKindHandler` + `DataObjectReferenceKindHandler` on main. Removes the non-navigable label fallback documented at `relationshipTableElementMappingUtil.ts:100`. | S (frontend) | **✓ shipped 2026-06-10 (`b467a246`)** | `useRelatedEntities` now accepts `dataObjectAppIdInput?`; when a UUID v7 is present (hyphens) calls `GET /v2/references?kind=collection&dataObjectAppId={appId}` and maps to `CollectionReferenceV2`; mapper adds `isCollectionReferenceV2` branch → `/collections/{referencedCollectionAppId}` navigable link. `CollectionReferenceTypeCell` shows "Collection" when appId present. `DataObject detail index.vue` passes `dataObjectIdStr` (route UUID). |

### APISIMP — v2 surface simplification (2026-06-10 sweep)

Findings SSOT: `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-2026-06-10.md`. Ordered by
effort×value. Pairs with V2CONV (`aidocs/platform/191`). Scope correction: the
Collection/DataObject/BasicReference SA resources the brief named are on the
**frozen `/shepard/api/` v1 surface** — OUT of scope; only the 3 container-SA
resources are bespoke `/v2/`.

| ID | Item | Size | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **APISIMP-SA-CONT-DELETE** | Delete the 3 bespoke `/v2/` container-SA resources (`FileContainerSemanticAnnotationRest`, `TimeseriesContainerSemanticAnnotationRest`, `StructuredDataContainerSemanticAnnotationRest`) — strict subset of `/v2/annotations` (subjectKind=`*Container` + container appId), and the only callers of the DB-internal-id-leaking legacy `SemanticAnnotationIO`. AC: the 3 resources gone; `GET /v2/annotations?subjectAppId={containerAppId}&subjectKind=TimeseriesContainer` returns the same rows; mvn verify green. | S | **✓ shipped 2026-06-10** | The 3 bespoke resources deleted; container annotations flow through `/v2/annotations?subjectAppId={appId}` (the polymorphic SEMA-V6 surface). e2e probe `test_v2_features.py::test_container_annotations_endpoint` repointed off the numeric-id path. Removed the legacy `SemanticAnnotationIO` Neo4j-id leak from the wire. Superseded by `.../v2/annotations/resources/SemanticAnnotationV2Rest.java`; apisimp-sweep §P1 |
| **APISIMP-SA-CONT-PROBE-REPOINT** | Repoint the one live caller of the deleted container-SA path — `e2e/api/tests/test_v2_features.py:35` (`GET /v2/timeseries-containers/{cid}/annotations`) — onto `GET /v2/annotations?subjectAppId={containerAppId}&subjectKind=TimeseriesContainer`. No FE/MCP/seed caller exists. AC: e2e probe green against `/v2/annotations`. | XS | **✓ shipped 2026-06-10** (done inside APISIMP-SA-CONT-DELETE: `f94b3db`) | `test_container_annotations_endpoint` already calls `GET /v2/annotations?subjectAppId={appId}` with no `subjectKind` filter (accepts any subject kind matching the appId — which is correct); the per-kind filter was unnecessary. Verified in `e2e/api/tests/test_v2_features.py:40`. |
| **APISIMP-PV-UNIFY** | Collapse `PayloadVersionRest` + `StructuredDataPayloadVersionRest` (identical `GET /v2/{kind}-containers/{appId}/files/{name}/versions`, differ only by kind) into a generic `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/files/{name}/versions` via a `ContainerKindHandler.listVersions` default (415 for kinds without file-payload versioning), mirroring the shipped A7-HDF-UNIFY `downloadFile` generic. AC: one resource; both kinds resolve; non-file kind → 415. | S | **✓ shipped 2026-06-11** | `ContainerKindHandler.listVersions` default → `Optional.empty()` (415); `FileContainerKindHandler` + `StructuredDataContainerKindHandler` override via `PayloadVersionDAO.findByContainerAndName`. `PayloadVersionRest` + `StructuredDataPayloadVersionRest` + their tests deleted. 6 new unit tests in `ContainersV2RestTest`. Merged (#1833). |
| **APISIMP-TSCONT-APPID-KEY** | Re-key the entire `/v2/timeseries-containers/{containerId}/…` cluster off the numeric `Long containerId` (raw Neo4j OGM id, the largest numeric-id leak left in `/v2/`) onto `{containerAppId}` String, resolving the OGM id in the service. Resources: `TimeseriesContainerChannelsRest`, `TimeseriesChannelAnnotationRest` (also leaks `channelShepardId`), `TimeseriesContainerTemporalAnnotationRest`, `TimeseriesContainerChartViewRest`. Wire break — pre-prod, land FE+backend in lockstep; unblocks appId-native container routes. AC: no numeric `@PathParam` in the cluster; FE panes call appId paths; mvn verify + FE typecheck green. | M–L | **✓ all 4 slices shipped 2026-06-11** — S4 merged `bdf4090` (#1846) | Slice 1 (2026-06-11, ✓ merged #1843): backend-only for `TimeseriesContainerStatsRest` + `TimeseriesContainerLinkedDataObjectsRest` + DELETE — `@PathParam Long containerId` → `@PathParam String containerAppId`; 8 unit tests. Slice 2 (2026-06-11, ✓ merged #1844): FE composable migration — `useFetchTimeseriesContainerStats`, `useTimeseriesContainerLinkedDataObjects` now accept `string | Ref<string|null>` (reactive appId); `safeDeleteContainer` accepts `string|number` for timeseries; `useContainerReferencedByCollections` accepts `string|number`; callers in `pages/containers/timeseries/[containerId]/index.vue`, `TimeseriesContainerAccessor.delete()`, `ContainerListPage.vue`, `ContainerList.vue`, `ContainerReferencedByCell.vue`, `TimeseriesAllChannelsChart.vue` updated; test updated. Slice 3 (2026-06-11, ✓ merged #1845, commit `bf67632`): `TimeseriesContainerChannelsRest` 5 endpoints re-keyed to `containerAppId`; `TimeseriesReferenceKindHandler.toIO()` adds additive `timeseriesContainerAppId` field; FE: `useFetchV2Channels`, `useFetchChannelPreview` (+ `useChannelPreviewLazy`), `ChannelPreviewChart`, `TimeseriesMeasurementsTable`, `ChannelAnnotationsPane`, `Trace3DChannelPicker`, `ViewRecipeBuilderDialog`, `Trace3DEditChannelsDialog` migrated to `containerAppId: string`; `useFetchTimeseriesReferences` extracts `timeseriesContainerAppId` from v1 container wire response; timeseries-reference detail page wired; tests updated. **Slice 4 (2026-06-11, PR #1846 in review):** `TimeseriesChannelAnnotationRest` + `TimeseriesContainerTemporalAnnotationRest` + `TimeseriesContainerChartViewRest` all re-keyed from `@PathParam Long containerId` → `@PathParam String containerAppId`; `AnnotatedChannel` frontend class updated to `containerAppId: string`; `ChannelAnnotationsPane`, `TemporalAnnotationDialog`, `TimeseriesAllChannelsChart`, `useTimeseriesContainerChartView`, `useTimeseriesContainerAnnotations` all updated; `TimeseriesContainerChartViewRestTest` (new, 4 tests), `TimeseriesChannelAnnotationRestTest` + `TimeseriesContainerTemporalAnnotationRestTest` updated to string containerAppId; `AnnotatedChannel.test.ts` updated. first-refs: `backend/.../v2/timeseriescontainer/resources/{TimeseriesContainerChannelsRest,TimeseriesChannelAnnotationRest,TimeseriesContainerTemporalAnnotationRest,TimeseriesContainerChartViewRest}.java`; pairs with `CONTAINER-V2-ROUTE`/`V2-SWEEP-003`; apisimp-sweep §(a) |
| **APISIMP-PAGINATION-UNIFY** | Standardise pagination across all v2 list endpoints on `page`+`pageSize` (capped default, e.g. 50/max 200 — the SEMA-V6 choice with the most adopters). Today four vocabularies coexist (`page`+`pageSize`, `page`+`size`, bare `limit`, `limit`+`offset`-ish) with defaults 20/50/100/200. | **APISIMP-BASICENTITY-DROP-ID** | Drop `private Long id` from `BasicEntityIO` — the shared base for all v2 entity responses (Collection, DataObject, Reference, Container, …). | **APISIMP-KIND-DISCRIMINATOR** | The unified `/v2/references?kind=` surface (`ReferencesV2Rest`) handles all non-binary reference kinds. Two bespoke endpoints remain outside it: `POST /v2/files` (singleton FileReference — multipart binary upload; `GET|PATCH|DELETE /v2/files/{appId}`, `GET /v2/files/{appId}/content`, `GET /v2/files/by-data-object/{dataObjectAppId}`) and `/v2/bundles` (FileBundleReference CRUD + FileGroup sub-resources). Per 191 §2, the target is to dissolve both under `/v2/references?kind=file` and `?kind=bundle`. **Design verdict (fire-76): Option C — two-step JSON create + binary PUT.** Step 1: `POST /v2/references?kind=file` with body `{name}` → creates metadata-only node. Step 2: `PUT /v2/references/{appId}/content?filename=…` with octet-stream body → stores bytes. **Slice 1 (fire-83, merged #1963):** SPI `uploadContent()` default method, `PUT /v2/references/{appId}/content` endpoint, `FileReferenceKindHandler.create()` metadata phase, `SingletonFileReferenceService.createSingletonMetadata()` + `attachContent()`, 51 passing tests. **Slice 2 (fire-83, PR open):** frontend composable repointed to two-step pattern; `POST /v2/files` returns 410 Gone; MCP error message updated; 18 FE tests + 60 BE tests green. **Slice 3 (fire-100, PR open):** `FileBundleReferenceKindHandler` (`kind=bundle`); `FileBundleReferenceDAO.findByDataObjectAppId()`; 17 unit tests. Slice 4: tombstone `/v2/bundles` CRUD (done via APISIMP-FILE-PATH-RETIRE-2, PR #1978, fire-99). AC (overall): `POST /v2/files` and `/v2/bundles` return 404/410; all callers use `/v2/references?kind=`; `mvn verify` + FE typecheck green. | L | ✅ done (all slices on main: slice1+2 fire-83 #1963, slice3 fire-100 FileBundleReferenceKindHandler.java, slice4 FILE-PATH-RETIRE-2 fire-99 #1978; fire-306 audit) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/file/resources/FileReferenceV2Rest.java`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/bundle/resources/FileBundleReferenceRest.java`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/spi/ReferenceKindHandler.java`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/resources/ReferencesV2Rest.java`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/context/references/file/services/SingletonFileReferenceService.java`; aidocs/platform/191-v2-surface-convergence.md §2; apisimp-sweep-2026-06-15-fire60 §Finding1 |

| **APISIMP-FILECONTAINER-THUMBNAIL-BARE-STRING** | `FileContainerKindHandler.getThumbnail()` at `:183` returns HTTP 503 with `.entity(sue.getMessage())` — a bare String body, no `Content-Type: application/problem+json`. Fix: replace `.entity(sue.getMessage())` with a `ProblemJson` call (`type=/problems/files.thumbnail-unavailable`, `status=503`, `detail=sue.getMessage()`), keeping the `Retry-After` header. The server silently caps `pageSize` at 50 (line 220, `MAX_LIMIT`) — a caller supplying `?pageSize=200` will receive at most 50 results with no indication why. The `@Operation` description mentions the cap in prose only. Fix: add `@Parameter(required=true, description="Search string. Must be at least 2 characters.")` to `q`; add `@Parameter(description="Maximum results to return (default 20). Server-side cap: 50 — values above 50 are silently clamped.")` to `pageSize`; add 2 reflection regression tests. AC: OpenAPI schema for both params is documented; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ done (PR #1996, merged 2026-06-24) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/semantic/resources/SemanticTermSearchRest.java:199-200`; apisimp-sweep-2026-06-18-fire114 §Finding4 |
| **APISIMP-VOCAB-BROWSE-SCOPE-UNDOCUMENTED** | `VocabularyBrowseRest.listVocabulariesUsedBy()` at line 179 accepts `@QueryParam("scope") @DefaultValue("data-object") String scope` with no `@Parameter` annotation. The DAO normalises silently: any value other than `"collection"` (case-insensitive) maps to `"data-object"`. The class Javadoc documents the two values in prose; the OpenAPI param schema is bare. Fix: add `@Parameter(description="Annotation walk scope. 'data-object' (default): only the entity's own annotations. 'collection': walks [:HAS_DATAOBJECT*0..] descendants too. Any other value is treated as 'data-object'.")` to the `scope` param; import `org.eclipse.microprofile.openapi.annotations.parameters.Parameter`; add 1 reflection regression test (`listVocabulariesUsedBy_scopeParamIsDocumented`). AC: OpenAPI schema carries a description for `scope`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | **APISIMP-DO-IO-NUMERIC-ID-LEAK** | `DataObjectDetailV2IO` and `DataObjectListItemV2IO` both use `@JsonIgnoreProperties({"id"})` but inherit 7 numeric Neo4j id arrays from the frozen `DataObjectIO`: `collectionId` (long), `referenceIds[]` (long[]), `successorIds[]` (long[]), `predecessorIds[]` (long[]), `childrenIds[]` (long[]), `parentId` (Long), `incomingIds[]` (long[]). All 7 still flow through the v2 wire today. The v2 IO already adds typed appId replacements (`timeseriesReferenceAppIds`, `fileReferenceAppIds`, `predecessorSummaries`, etc.) making the numeric arrays redundant for v2 callers. Fix: extend `@JsonIgnoreProperties` on both classes to `{"id", "collectionId", "referenceIds", "successorIds", "predecessorIds", "childrenIds", "parentId", "incomingIds"}`; verify no v2 caller reads these numeric fields (grep `dataObject\.(collectionId\|referenceIds\|successorIds\|predecessorIds\|childrenIds\|parentId\|incomingIds)` in frontend/); add 2 JSON-serialisation regression tests. AC: `GET /v2/collections/{id}/data-objects/{appId}` response body has none of the 7 numeric id fields; appId-keyed replacements all present; `mvn verify -pl backend` + frontend typecheck green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-199, PR #2073 `8262380a`) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/dataobject/io/DataObjectDetailV2IO.java:37`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/dataobject/io/DataObjectListItemV2IO.java:31`; apisimp-sweep-2026-06-24 §MAJOR-1 |

All 3 still flow through the v2 collection list/get responses. Fix: extend to `@JsonIgnoreProperties({"id", "dataObjectIds", "incomingIds", "defaultFileContainerId"})`; coordinate with any v2 client that might still read `dataObjectIds` (the comment at CollectionV2Rest:188 suggests it was already flagged as legacy — confirm no FE usage); add 1 JSON-serialisation regression test. | **APISIMP-DO-ROOT-PATH-INCONSISTENCY** | `DataObjectV2Rest` CRUD is at `/v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects` but the batch endpoint (`DataObjectBatchV2Rest`) is at `/v2/data-objects/batch` and the RDF endpoint (`DataObjectRdfRest`) is at `/v2/data-objects/{appId}/rdf`. The RDF endpoint operates on a single DataObject by appId and has no reason to be at a different root than the CRUD surface. Fix: (a) add a sibling route at `/v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects/{appId}/rdf` on `DataObjectRdfRest` (or a redirect to the flat path); (b) add a one-line Javadoc comment to `DataObjectBatchV2Rest` stating the cross-collection design intent. AC: `GET /v2/collections/{cId}/data-objects/{appId}/rdf` responds identically to the flat path; batch Javadoc explains the root-level choice; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-203, PR #2077 → commit `ee06695fc`) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/dataobject/resources/DataObjectRdfRest.java`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/dataobject/resources/DataObjectBatchV2Rest.java`; apisimp-sweep-2026-06-24 §MINOR-1 |
| **APISIMP-TOMBSTONE-REMOVAL-WINDOW** | Four tombstone REST classes return 410 Gone with no removal target release tracked: `v2/file/resources/FileReferenceV2Rest.java`, `plugins/video/.../VideoStreamReferenceV2Rest.java`, `plugins/git/.../GitReferenceRest.java`, `plugins/wiki-writer/.../WikiWriterTombstoneRest.java`. Tombstones that live indefinitely register JAX-RS routes, consume classpath scanning time, and confuse contributors who grep for the path. The 410 body should include a `Location` header pointing to the replacement path so clients can auto-migrate. Fix: for each tombstone, add a `Location: <replacement-path>` header to the 410 response; file a removal target (suggest: next minor after current); delete the class at that release. AC: 410 responses carry `Location` header; removal dates recorded in `aidocs/34` compat ledger. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-201, PR #2075 `80b0c8d`) | `FileReferenceV2Rest.java`, `VideoStreamReferenceV2Rest.java`, `GitReferenceRest.java`, `WikiWriterTombstoneRest.java`; apisimp-sweep-2026-06-24 §MINOR-2 |
| **APISIMP-PROVENANCE-CURSOR-UNDOCUMENTED** | `ProvenanceRest` uses time-cursor pagination (`since`/`until` epoch ms + `pageSize` cap) instead of the `page`+`pageSize` convention used by every other v2 list endpoint. The design is correct for an append-only event stream (cursor avoids pagination drift) but is not documented as intentionally different. A caller who supplies `?page=0&pageSize=20` gets no error — the `page` param is silently ignored and the endpoint returns the full window. Fix: add an `@Operation` note stating "This endpoint uses time-cursor pagination (`since`/`until` epoch ms) rather than page-offset — page-offset pagination would produce inconsistent results under concurrent activity writes."; optionally return 400 if `page` is supplied. AC: OpenAPI `@Operation` note documents the cursor convention; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ done (PR #2076, merged 2026-06-24) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/provenance/resources/ProvenanceRest.java`; apisimp-sweep-2026-06-24 §MINOR-3 |

Fix: add @Parameter + import where missing across 4 files; add 1 reflection test per file (CollectionV2RestTest, ProvenanceRestParamAnnotationTest extended, UnhideFeedRestTest extended, new SpatialPromoteRestTest). AC: OpenAPI documents all 10 params; `mvn verify -pl backend -pl plugins/spatiotemporal -pl plugins/unhide` green. | **APISIMP-CROSS-DO-BULK-N-PLUS-ONE** | `CrossDoBulkDataRest.POST /v2/data-objects/cross-timeseries-bulk` — for each of up to 100 DataObject appIds the handler serially calls: (1) `permissionsService.isAccessAllowedForDataObjectAppId` (Neo4j), (2) `dataObjectDAO.findByAppId` (Neo4j), (3) `crossDoChannelResolver.resolveChannelsByPredicate` (Neo4j+Postgres), then (4) LTTB query per resolved channel (TimescaleDB). 100 DOs → potentially 400 serial DB round-trips before any data is returned. Fix: replace serial (1)+(2) with two batch calls — `permissionsService.filterAllowedDataObjectAppIds` (1 Neo4j round-trip) + `dataObjectDAO.findNamesByAppIds` (1 Neo4j round-trip). Note: unknown appIds (not in collection graph) silently dropped (consistent with permission semantics — can't grant access to non-existent DOs). |

| **APISIMP-PAGINATION-PARAM-STYLE** | Cross-cutting: prior pagination-add fires added `page`/`pageSize` to all unbounded list endpoints but used two different annotation styles. Some endpoints use the validated form (`@DefaultValue("0") @PositiveOrZero int page`); others use nullable `Integer page` (no default, no validation — silently accepts negative values, adds null-branch service code). Remaining nullable-style sites: `ContainersV2Rest` (after APISIMP-CONTAINERS-LIST-NO-PAGINATION); `CollectionLabJournalEntriesRest` (bulk-mode backward-compat — convert to documented optional-paginated, deprecate null=bulk). Fix: standardize all v2 list `@QueryParam` page/pageSize to `@DefaultValue("0") @PositiveOrZero int page` / `@DefaultValue("50") @Min(1) @Max(200) int pageSize`; propagate non-null primitives through service layers (no logic change needed); add 1 negative-value 400-rejection test per converted endpoint. AC: `?page=-1` → 400 on all converted endpoints; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ shipped 2026-06-26 (PR #2101 → fdf32556) | apisimp-sweep-2026-06-26-fire231 §Gap1 |
| **APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE** | Cross-cutting: three different pagination response shapes coexist across v2 list endpoints: (A) structured `{items: [], total: N, page: 0, pageSize: 50}` (SnapshotListRest — most discoverable), (B) plain array + `X-Total-Count` header (ContainersV2Rest, NotificationRest, ProjectsRest, CollectionWatchesRest, CollectionWatchersRest, CollectionDQRRest, OntologyGitSourceRest — partially discoverable), (C) plain array no total (CollectionV2Rest + some annotation endpoints — least discoverable). Forces clients to use three parsing patterns for functionally equivalent list responses. Fix: introduce shared `PagedResponseIO<T> {items: List<T>, total: long, page: int, pageSize: int}` record; migrate all shape-B and shape-C list endpoints to return it; keep `X-Total-Count` header in parallel for one minor-version deprecation window. AC: all v2 list endpoints return `{items, total, page, pageSize}`; header also present during deprecation window; `mvn verify -pl backend` green; frontend composable updated to read `response.items` not `response.data`. | M | ✅ **fully shipped** (ENVELOPE-1 #2102 → dd051adc; ENVELOPE-2-1 #2103 → 3da2c993; ENVELOPE-2-2 #2104 → 43cc4d8d; ENVELOPE-2-3 #2105 → 715748eb4) — all shape-B + shape-C endpoints migrated to PagedResponseIO; both FE annotation composables defensive `page.items ?? page`. | apisimp-sweep-2026-06-26-fire231 §Gap2 |
| **APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE-2** | Shape-C migration follow-on: `CollectionV2Rest` and annotation list endpoints that return a plain array with no total or pagination envelope (shape-C). Migrate each to return `PagedResponseIO<T>` (same record introduced in APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE-1). Frontend composable update: change all callers that read `.data` on list responses to `.items`. AC: all v2 list endpoints return `{items, total, page, pageSize}`; `X-Total-Count` header removed (deprecation window closed) in the same PR; `mvn verify -pl backend` green; frontend composable updated. | M | ✅ **fully shipped** (2-1 #2103 → 3da2c993; 2-2 #2104 → 43cc4d8d; 2-3 #2105 → 715748eb4). | APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE-1 is the prerequisite |
| **APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE-2-2** | Annotation list endpoints + frontend composable: migrate `SemanticAnnotationV2Rest.list()` and `find()` from plain array to `PagedResponseIO<T>`; add `countFiltered`/`countTextSearch` to `SemanticAnnotationV2DAO`; update `frontend/composables/annotated.ts` to read `page.items`; update `backend-client` with `PagedResponseAnnotationV2` model. | **APISIMP-FLAT-PUBS-NO-PAGINATION** | `FlatPublicationsRest.list()` at `v2/publish/resources/FlatPublicationsRest.java:84` returns a plain `List<PublicationIO>` with no `page`/`pageSize` params, no `X-Total-Count` header, and no `PagedResponseIO` envelope. Publications per entity are expected small (<10 typical) but grow unboundedly with each publish/retire cycle. Inconsistent with the paginated envelope now standard across all v2 list endpoints. Fix: add `@QueryParam("page") @DefaultValue("0") @PositiveOrZero int page` and `@QueryParam("pageSize") @DefaultValue("50") @Min(1) @Max(200) int pageSize`; change response body to `new PagedResponseIO<>(slice, total, page, pageSize)`; add `X-Total-Count` header during deprecation window. | **APISIMP-PUBS-ALIAS-HARDCODED-LIMIT** | `PublicationsListRest` (deprecated `GET /v2/{kind}/{appId}/publications`) called `publicationDAO.findByEntityAppId(appId, 0, 1000)` with hardcoded SKIP 0 LIMIT 1000 and returned a bare `List<PublicationIO>` with no `PagedResponseIO` envelope — inconsistent with the canonical flat alias `GET /v2/publications` (which already uses `PagedResponseIO` after APISIMP-FLAT-PUBS-NO-PAGINATION). The `countByEntityAppId` and bounded `findByEntityAppId(String, int, int)` DAO methods already existed (added in fire-421 / PR #2306 for the flat alias). Fix: add `@QueryParam("page") @DefaultValue("0") @PositiveOrZero int page` and `@QueryParam("pageSize") @DefaultValue("50") @Min(1) @Max(500) int pageSize` (note: max 500 to match the deprecated endpoint's prior 1000-cap spirit while enforcing a bound); replace hardcoded call with `countByEntityAppId` + bounded `findByEntityAppId(appId, skip, pageSize)`; wrap in `PagedResponseIO`; add `X-Total-Count` header. | **APISIMP-SEARCH-PAGESIZE-HEADER** | `SearchV2Rest.java:102` `GET /v2/search` — two inconsistencies found in fire-270 sweep: (1) `pageSize` param uses `@PositiveOrZero` (allows 0) instead of `@Min(1) @Max(200)` — the `@Schema(minimum="1",maximum="200")` and `@Parameter` docs say 1–200 but bean-validation allows 0; runtime guard `Math.min(Math.max(pageSize, 1), 200)` clamps silently; the REST contract should reject 0 with 400, consistent with every other paginated endpoint; (2) no `X-Total-Count` response header — `Response.ok(new SearchV2ResultIO(...)).build()` at line 147 never calls `.header("X-Total-Count", total)`; every other paginated v2 list endpoint sets this header; generic pagination clients must special-case search. Fix: replace `@PositiveOrZero` with `@Min(1) @Max(200)` on `pageSize` (line 102); add `.header("X-Total-Count", total)` to the `Response.ok(...)` builder (line 147); add 2 regression tests (`search_pageSize0_returns400`, `search_xTotalCountHeader_isSet`). AC: `?pageSize=0` returns 400; `X-Total-Count` header present on 200; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. Note: `SearchV2ResultIO` intentionally stays (not `PagedResponseIO`) to carry the `query` field. | XS | ✅ **merged fire-272 (PR #2137 → 5b88d198)** | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/search/resources/SearchV2Rest.java:102,147`; apisimp-sweep-2026-06-27-fire270 §Finding1+2 |

| **APISIMP-USERGROUP-Q-FILTER** | `GET /v2/user-groups` lacked a `?q` name-filter for UI pickers and typeahead. When `?q` is absent the endpoint paginates normally; when `?q` is set, all groups are fetched via `service.getAllUserGroups(new QueryParamHelper())` (preserves caller-permission filtering via `CypherQueryHelper.getReadableByQuery`), filtered in-memory by case-insensitive substring, and returned as `PagedResponseIO(items, items.size(), 0, items.size())`. Fix: add 5th `@QueryParam("q") String q` parameter + `@Parameter(name="q", description="Optional name filter...")` annotation to `listUserGroups()`; add in-memory filter branch. Tests: 2 new unit tests (`withQ_returnsPagedEnvelope`, `withQ_noMatches_returnsEmptyPagedEnvelope`); 3 existing call-site invocations updated for the extra null arg; 2 reflection tests updated for `String.class` 5th param. | **APISIMP-AAS-SHELLS-LIST-ENVELOPE** | `AasShellsRest.listShells()` (`GET /v2/aas/shells`) accepts `?page`/`?pageSize` query params (wired to `QueryParamHelper` at the DAO level) but returns a plain `List<AasShellIO>` via `Response.ok(shells).build()` — no `PagedResponseIO` envelope, no `total`, no page echo. An operator with > 100 Collections receives a silently truncated list at the default `pageSize=100` with no signal that more pages exist. Inconsistent with the v2 pagination contract established by APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE. Fix: add `collectionDAO.countAllCollectionsByShepardId(username)` count overload; wrap response in `new PagedResponseIO<>(shells, total, safePage, safeSize)`. | **APISIMP-TEMPLATE-TAGS-CAP** | `ShepardTemplateRest.tags()` at `v2/template/resources/ShepardTemplateRest.java:302` calls `dao.listDistinctTags(kind)` which executes `MATCH (t:ShepardTemplate) WHERE ... UNWIND coalesce(t.tags, []) AS tag RETURN DISTINCT tag ORDER BY tag` with no `LIMIT` clause. The endpoint is used by the picker UI's tag-autocomplete. No server cap means a large deployment with many templates returns an arbitrarily-sized tag list. Fix: add `LIMIT 500` to the Cypher string in `ShepardTemplateDAO.listDistinctTags()` (line 238) — tags are deduplicated vocabulary strings, hard cap is appropriate (no pagination params needed; 500 distinct tags would be extraordinary). | **APISIMP-NOTIF-TRANSPORT-LIST-ENVELOPE** | `NotificationTransportRest.list()` (`GET /v2/admin/notifications/transports`) returns a bespoke `NotificationTransportListIO` wrapper (`transports: List<NotificationTransportReadIO>`) instead of the standard `PagedResponseIO<NotificationTransportReadIO>`. `NotificationTransportListIO` predates the pagination-envelope wave (APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE, 2026-06-26) and has no `total`, `page`, or `pageSize` fields. Transport count is small in practice (O(1–2) per instance) but the inconsistency forces OpenAPI clients to import a one-off list shape. | **APISIMP-PROV-ISO8601-TIMESTAMPS** | `ProvenanceRest.java` — 8 paginated list endpoints (`listActivities`, `listEntityActivities`, `countActivities`, and PROV-N/JSON-LD variants) accept `@QueryParam("since") Long` and `@QueryParam("until") Long` as millisecond epoch timestamps. These are **timestamps** (not Neo4j IDs) so they technically are exempt from the "appId/UUID only" rule, but they are numerics on the v2 REST surface while the sibling `InstanceAdminRest.permissionAuditLog()` `from`/`to` params already use ISO 8601 string semantics (per APISIMP-INSTANCE-ADMIN-AUDIT-FILTER-PARAMS-UNDOCUMENTED, shipped). Inconsistency: two provenance-adjacent admin endpoints use different time-range input conventions. Fix: accept EITHER an ISO 8601 instant string (`?since=2026-01-01T00:00:00Z`) OR the legacy epoch-ms Long at the REST layer; parse with `Instant.parse()` / `Long.parseLong()` heuristic (contains '-' → ISO 8601, else epoch-ms); update `@Parameter` descriptions to document both; add 1 regression test for each format. Alternative: document the epoch-ms choice as a known inconsistency (acceptable, since `APISIMP-PROVENANCE-CURSOR-UNDOCUMENTED` already documented the cursor design). AC: `?since=2026-01-01T00:00:00Z` returns 200; `?since=1751299200000` (epoch ms) also returns 200; `@Parameter` description updated; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. No frontend change (the frontend doesn't render since/until inputs). | XS | **✓ shipped fire-313** | branch `APISIMP-PROV-ISO8601-TIMESTAMPS-1`; heuristic: `contains('T')` → `Instant.parse()`, else `Long.parseLong()`; 9 endpoints updated (all since/until String params); `parseTimestamp` static helper + `badTimestamp` 400 response; 11-test `ProvenanceRestTimestampTest`; 40/40 provenance tests green. `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/provenance/resources/ProvenanceRest.java`. |
| **APISIMP-PAGESZ-VALIDATION-INCONSISTENCY** | `CollectionV2Rest.listCollections()` at `v2/collection/resources/CollectionV2Rest.java:165,167-168` uses `@PositiveOrZero` on `pageSize` (allows 0) plus runtime clamping: `int safeSize = Math.min(Math.max(pageSize, 1), 200)`. `DataObjectV2Rest.listDataObjects()` at `v2/dataobject/resources/DataObjectV2Rest.java:197` uses `@PositiveOrZero` without clamping (docs say "clamped to [1,200]" but the raw value is passed downstream). Every other v2 list endpoint uses `@Min(1) @Max(200)` with no runtime clamping. Fix: (1) Change `@PositiveOrZero` to `@Min(1) @Max(200)` on both params; (2) remove lines 167-168 (`safePage` + `safeSize` clamp in `CollectionV2Rest`); (3) pass `page` and `pageSize` directly to the downstream computation; (4) add `import jakarta.validation.constraints.Max` + `import jakarta.validation.constraints.Min` if not already imported; (5) update any tests that assert on 0-valued pageSize behaviour. `ContainersV2Rest.listChannels()` line 640 (`@PositiveOrZero`, default 200) is acceptable as-is — its semantics ("all channels in one page") differ from the standard list contract. AC: `GET /v2/collections?pageSize=0` returns 400 (Bean Validation); `GET /v2/collections?pageSize=201` returns 400; `mvn verify -pl backend` green; no frontend change. | XS | **✓ shipped fire-314** | branch `APISIMP-PAGESZ-VALIDATION-INCONSISTENCY`; `@Min(1) @Max(200)` on `pageSize` in both files; removed runtime clamp; replaced `listClampsOversizePageSizeToMax200` with two annotation-presence tests; 91/91 tests green. |
| **APISIMP-ANNOTATION-MERGE-PATCH-HTTP** | `SemanticAnnotationV2Rest.update()` at `v2/annotations/resources/SemanticAnnotationV2Rest.java:459` is annotated `@PUT` but its `@Operation` description explicitly states "RFC 7396 merge-patch: only non-null fields in the request body are applied" and the implementation at line 528 carries the comment `// Apply merge-patch` with null-guarded field assignments. `PUT` means full-replacement per RFC 7231 §4.3.4; `PATCH` (RFC 5789) is the correct HTTP method for merge-patch (RFC 7396). API consumers and caching proxies that honour HTTP semantics will be confused. Fix: (1) change `@PUT` to `@PATCH` on line 459; (2) update `@Operation` to remove the ambiguity (the description body already says merge-patch — just remove the `PUT` implication from the generated OpenAPI operationId `updateAnnotation` if desired); (3) optionally add `@Consumes({"application/json", "application/merge-patch+json"})` to signal the RFC 7396 media type alongside `application/json` for untyped clients; (4) update any tests that send `PUT /v2/annotations/{appId}` to send `PATCH` instead. AC: `PATCH /v2/annotations/{appId}` returns 200 with partial-update semantics; `PUT /v2/annotations/{appId}` returns 405 Method Not Allowed; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | **✓ shipped fire-315** | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/annotations/resources/SemanticAnnotationV2Rest.java`; `backend/src/test/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/annotations/resources/SemanticAnnotationV2RestTest.java`; branch `APISIMP-ANNOTATION-MERGE-PATCH-HTTP`; `@PUT` → `@PATCH`; method-level `@Consumes({"application/merge-patch+json", MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON})`; 2 new reflection tests (`update_methodAnnotationIsPatch_notPut`, `update_consumesIncludesMergePatchJson`); `recordAnnotationActivity()` summary updated to `"PATCH /v2/annotations/{appId}"`; `aidocs/34` + `aidocs/44` updated. |
| **APISIMP-SNAPSHOT-LIST-ENVELOPE** | `SnapshotListRest.java:99-104` declares a bespoke inner record `SnapshotListPageIO(items, total, page, pageSize)` instead of the standard `PagedResponseIO<SnapshotListItemIO>`. The field layout is identical to `PagedResponseIO` but the type is different — the OpenAPI generator emits a distinct `SnapshotListPageIO` schema. `CollectionSnapshotRest` (the sibling per-collection snapshot list) correctly uses `PagedResponseIO<SnapshotListItemIO>`. This is the last bespoke list envelope on the v2 surface. Fix: (1) import `de.dlr.shepard.v2.collection.io.PagedResponseIO` (or whichever package holds it); (2) replace `new SnapshotListPageIO(filtered, total, safePage, safeSize)` at line 184 with `new PagedResponseIO<>(filtered, total, safePage, safeSize)`; (3) delete the inner `SnapshotListPageIO` record at lines 99-104; (4) update `@APIResponse` `@Schema(implementation = SnapshotListPageIO.class)` to `@Schema(implementation = PagedResponseIO.class)`; (5) update any tests asserting on `SnapshotListPageIO` type. AC: `GET /v2/snapshots` returns standard `PagedResponseIO` envelope; OpenAPI schema is `PagedResponseIO` not `SnapshotListPageIO`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-317, PR pending) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/snapshot/resources/SnapshotListRest.java`; apisimp-sweep-2026-06-30-fire316 §Finding1 |
| **APISIMP-REFS-FAKE-PAGINATION** | `ReferencesV2Rest.listReferences()` (`GET /v2/references?kind=...&dataObjectAppId=...`) loads the entire reference list for a DataObject into memory and wraps it in `PagedResponseIO` with `total=refs.size(), page=0, pageSize=refs.size()`. No `page` or `pageSize` query params are accepted. The endpoint looks paginated to consumers (the envelope is present) but performs no actual cursor/offset pagination — a DataObject with 1,000 references returns all 1,000 in one response body. Fix: (1) add `@QueryParam("page") @DefaultValue("0") @PositiveOrZero int page` and `@QueryParam("pageSize") @DefaultValue("50") @Min(1) @Max(200) int pageSize`; (2) thread them to service/DAO to fetch only the requested slice; (3) pass the unfiltered total count to `PagedResponseIO.total`. AC: `GET /v2/references?kind=FileReference&dataObjectAppId=X&page=0&pageSize=10` returns 10 items max; `total` reflects the full count; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | M | ✅ FIXED fire-320 | `ReferencesV2Rest.list()`: added `page`/`pageSize` `@QueryParam`s (`@DefaultValue("0")`/`@DefaultValue("50")`); in-memory slice via `subList(from, to)`; `total` is the pre-slice full count; manual range validation (page<0→400; pageSize∉[1,200]→400). 5 new test cases (`list_paginationSlicesCorrectly`, `list_pageOutOfBoundsReturnsEmpty`, `list_returns400WhenPageNegative`, `list_returns400WhenPageSizeZero`, `list_returns400WhenPageSizeTooLarge`); 2 new reflection tests for `page`/`pageSize` `@Parameter` annotations. SPI interface and DAO unchanged — in-memory slicing is a safe first pass for typical reference lists. |
| **APISIMP-REFS-LIST-NO-XTOTALCOUNT** | `ReferencesV2Rest.list()` (`GET /v2/references?kind=...&dataObjectAppId=...`) returns `PagedResponseIO` envelope body but omits the `X-Total-Count` response header present on every other paginated v2 list endpoint (added by fire-320 / APISIMP-REFS-FAKE-PAGINATION which added real pagination, but did not wire the header). Fix: (1) add `.header("X-Total-Count", total)` to the `Response.ok(new PagedResponseIO<>(...)).build()` return in `list()`; (2) update `@APIResponse` description to match the standard "Header X-Total-Count = total count before paging (kept during deprecation window, APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE)." pattern; (3) add `list_returnsXTotalCountHeader` test. AC: `GET /v2/references?kind=file&dataObjectAppId=X` response carries `X-Total-Count` header; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ shipped fire-473 | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/resources/ReferencesV2Rest.java`; `backend/src/test/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/resources/ReferencesV2RestTest.java` |
| **APISIMP-DO-RELATIONSHIP-LISTS-UNBOUND** | `DataObjectV2Rest` endpoints `GET /v2/data-objects/{appId}/predecessors`, `…/successors`, and `…/children` return bare `List<DataObjectSummaryIO>` with no `PagedResponseIO` envelope and no pagination parameters. For DataObjects with deep lineage chains (LUMEN TR-004 investigation sub-tree, MFFD AFP→NDT→Rework DAG) the service traverses the entire OGM lazy relationship set and returns all items in a single response. Fix: (1) add `page`/`pageSize` `@QueryParam`s; (2) return `PagedResponseIO<DataObjectSummaryIO>` with `total` from a count query; (3) add service/DAO slice methods. AC: each of the three endpoints accepts `?page=0&pageSize=20`; `total` reflects the full count; bare list callers get 400 if they pass unsupported pagination shape; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. Note: whether to page graph traversals or apply a bounded depth limit deserves a brief ADR. | M | ✅ merged (fire-324, PR #2201, sha: 66c3fcbc) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/dataobject/resources/DataObjectV2Rest.java`; apisimp-sweep-2026-06-30-fire316 §Finding3 |
| **APISIMP-DO-V2-STALE-SPEC-TEXT** | `DataObjectV2Rest.getDataObject()` `@Operation(description=...)` references `referenceIds[]` and `childrenIds[]` as if they appear in the JSON response body. `DataObjectDetailV2IO` suppresses these fields via `@JsonIgnoreProperties` — they are never emitted. A consumer reading the OpenAPI spec will expect fields that never arrive. Fix: remove or correct the description passage mentioning `referenceIds[]` and `childrenIds[]`; verify no other operation descriptions reference suppressed fields. AC: OpenAPI description for `GET /v2/data-objects/{appId}` does not mention `referenceIds` or `childrenIds`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✓ shipped fire-319 | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/dataobject/resources/DataObjectV2Rest.java`; removed stale referenceIds/childrenIds passage from both `listDataObjects` and `getDataObject` @Operation; fixed "Next step" to point to `/v2/references?dataObjectAppId=` instead of `/shepard/api/…`. |
| **APISIMP-FILEREF-V2-TOMBSTONE-DELETE** | `FileReferenceV2Rest.java` is a "retired" class where every endpoint unconditionally returns HTTP 410 Gone. It is still compiled, loaded by Quarkus, and included in the OpenAPI document, inflating the API surface with defunct endpoints. The per-kind file reference path has been superseded by the generic `GET /v2/references/{appId}` surface. Fix: delete `FileReferenceV2Rest.java` and its test counterpart; verify no other class imports or references it; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | **APISIMP-CHANNEL-PAGESZ-MAX** | `ContainersV2Rest.listChannels()` at line 640 accepts `@QueryParam("pageSize") @DefaultValue("200") @PositiveOrZero int pageSize` with no upper cap. A caller can request millions of rows from the channel list. The main container list at line 638 uses `@Max(200)`. Fix: add `@Max(500)` (higher than the standard 200 since channel lists legitimately want "all channels at once" for dense timeseries containers); update `@Parameter(description=...)` to document the cap. This is the only v2 list exception. The frontend `useListDataObjects.ts` composable must handle the response differently from all peers. | **APISIMP-MISSING-OPERATIONID** | 129 HTTP endpoint methods across 60 v2 REST resource files lack `@Operation(operationId="...")`, causing auto-derived fragile names (e.g. `getV2AdminConfig_0`) in the generated OpenAPI spec — breaking TS client generation, monitoring, and API doc tools. Fix: add `operationId = "javaMethodName"` as first field of every multi-line `@Operation(` block that lacks one. Naming convention: use the Java method name verbatim (`list*`/`create*`/`get*`/`delete*`/`patch*`). AC: all `@GET`/`@POST`/`@PUT`/`@DELETE`/`@PATCH` methods in `de.dlr.shepard.v2.*` carry an operationId; generated spec contains only human-readable names; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ merged (fire-341, PR #2213, sha: f73f7cab) | `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-2026-07-01.md` §F1 |
| **APISIMP-MISSING-401-RESPONSES** | 10 authenticated v2 REST resources protected by `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")` or `@Authenticated` declare no `@APIResponse(responseCode = "401")` entry. The actual Quarkus behavior is correct (401 returned for unauthenticated requests) but the generated OpenAPI spec omits the 401 response code, misleading API consumers and SDK generators. Affected files: `AdminConfigRest.java`, `LedgerAnchorRest.java`, `MffdProcessChainMappingRest.java`, `FileMigrationRest.java`, `StorageAdminRest.java`, `AdminFeaturesRest.java`, `InstanceAdminRest.java`, `NotificationAdminRest.java`, `NotificationTransportRest.java`, `PersonalVocabularyRest.java`. Fix: add `@APIResponse(responseCode = "401", description = "Request is not authenticated.")` to each protected method (or at class level where uniformly authenticated). Annotation-only; zero wire change. AC: all 10 files document 401 in the OpenAPI spec; generated spec shows 401 for all protected endpoints; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-345, PR #2217, sha: 1ae2c3c5) | `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire342-2026-07-01.md` §F1 |
| **APISIMP-EXPORT-URL-EXCEPTION-SHAPE** | `CollectionExportUrlRest` (at `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/export-url`) throws `InternalServerErrorException` (lines 151, 162) and `ServiceUnavailableException` (lines 141, 166) from the Jakarta WS RS API. The Resteasy default exception mapper produces a non-`application/problem+json` body for these, bypassing the RFC 7807 envelope. The class already has a `problem()` helper at line 180–183; the four throw sites need conversion to `return problem(...)`. Fix: replace each `throw new *Exception(...)` with `return problem(PT_..., "...", Response.Status.*, ...)`. Define two `PT_` constants. No logic change. AC: all error paths on `CollectionExportUrlRest` return `Content-Type: application/problem+json`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ shipped APISIMP-EXCEPTION-SHAPE-1 | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/collection/resources/CollectionExportUrlRest.java` — removed `InternalServerErrorException`/`ServiceUnavailableException` imports; added `PT_EXPORT_FAILED`/`PT_STORAGE_UNAVAILABLE` constants; 4 throw→return conversions; added `@APIResponse(responseCode="500")`. |
| **APISIMP-DATAOBJECT-LIST-RUNTIME-EX** | `DataObjectV2Rest.listDataObjects()` (`GET /v2/data-objects`) throws `new RuntimeException("Failed to serialise DataObject list response", e)` at line 334 when JSON serialization fails. This is the only non-exception-mapper error path in that class. The Resteasy default mapper for `RuntimeException` produces a non-ProblemJson 500, bypassing RFC 7807. `ProblemJson` is already imported. Fix: replace `throw new RuntimeException(...)` with a structured `Response.serverError().type("application/problem+json").entity(new ProblemJson(...)).build()` return with an `ERROR`-level log. AC: serialization failure returns `Content-Type: application/problem+json` 500; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ shipped APISIMP-EXCEPTION-SHAPE-1 | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/dataobject/resources/DataObjectV2Rest.java` — added `PROBLEM_TYPE_INTERNAL` constant; converted `throw new RuntimeException(...)` to `return problem(PROBLEM_TYPE_INTERNAL, ...)`. |
| **APISIMP-SEMANTIC-ADMIN-ILLEGAL-ARG** | `SemanticAdminRest.java:557` throws `new IllegalArgumentException("metadata part is required")` and `:568` throws `new IllegalArgumentException(ex.getMessage(), ex)` during multipart ontology upload validation. The JAX-RS default mapper for `IllegalArgumentException` produces a `400 Bad Request` with a plain-text or non-ProblemJson body, bypassing RFC 7807. Affects `POST /v2/admin/semantic/ontologies`. Fix: add a private `problem()` helper and replace both throws with `return problem(PT_BAD_REQUEST, "Bad Request", Response.Status.BAD_REQUEST, message)`. AC: bad multipart upload returns `Content-Type: application/problem+json` 400; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-345, PR #2216, sha: 1e921a93) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/admin/semantic/SemanticAdminRest.java:557,568`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire342-2026-07-01.md` §F4 |
| **APISIMP-TERM-SEARCH-BARE-LIST** | `GET /v2/semantic/terms/search` accepts `@QueryParam("pageSize")` but returns a bare `List<TermSuggestionIO>` JSON array (`SemanticTermSearchRest.java:232`). Every other paginated list endpoint on the v2 surface returns `PagedResponseIO<T>` with `{items, total, page, pageSize}`. Fix: wrap `results` in `new PagedResponseIO<>(results, results.size(), 0, effectivePageSize)`. Update `@APIResponse` schema annotation from `SchemaType.ARRAY` to `implementation = PagedResponseIO.class`. `PagedResponseIO` is already imported throughout the v2 layer. AC: endpoint returns `{items:[...], total:N, page:0, pageSize:M}`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-345, PR #2215, sha: c18ab2c4) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/semantic/resources/SemanticTermSearchRest.java:232`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire342-2026-07-01.md` §F5 |
| **APISIMP-COLLECTION-CREATE-LONG-INPUT** | `CollectionIO.java:34` declares `private Long defaultFileContainerId = null;` — a raw Neo4j node id — as an accepted input field on `POST /v2/collections` (create, `CollectionV2Rest.java:279`) and `PATCH /v2/collections/{appId}` (update, `:354`). The generated OpenAPI input schema exposes `"defaultFileContainerId": {"type":"integer","format":"int64"}`. The operation description (`:250`) explicitly calls this a "legacy long id." GET responses correctly suppress the field via `@JsonIgnoreProperties`; only the input is affected. This violates the v2 contract that all entity identifiers must be `appId` (UUID v7) strings. Fix: create `CreateCollectionV2IO` / `UpdateCollectionV2IO` records replacing `defaultFileContainerId: Long` with `defaultFileContainerAppId: String`; resolve the appId to the Neo4j entity in `CollectionService.create()` / `update()` via a new `FileContainerService.findByAppId(String)` method. Document the input-shape change in `aidocs/34` (pre-existing "legacy" field removal — no caller should be using a numeric id on the v2 surface). AC: `POST /v2/collections` accepts `defaultFileContainerAppId` (string); `defaultFileContainerId` (Long) no longer in OpenAPI input schema; `CollectionService` resolves by appId; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | M | ✅ merged (fire-346, PR #2218, sha: 9675c12a) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/context/collection/io/CollectionIO.java:34`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/collection/resources/CollectionV2Rest.java:250,279,354`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire342-2026-07-01.md` §F6 |
| **APISIMP-MISSING-401-RESPONSES-P2** | 3 method sites across 3 v2 resources are authenticated but lack `@APIResponse(responseCode = "401")` documentation. Fire-342's APISIMP-MISSING-401-RESPONSES used a per-file scan that passed a file if ANY method documented 401 — these gaps escaped because sibling PATCH methods already carried 401 doc. Affected sites: (1) `InstanceRegistryRest.java` `PATCH /v2/admin/instances` — `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")` with only `@APIResponse("403")`, no 401; (2) `CollectionPublicationStateRest.java` `GET /v2/collections/{id}/publication-state` — class `@Authenticated`, GET has 200+404 only; (3) `ContainerPublicationStateRest.java` `GET /v2/containers/{id}/publication-state` — class `@Authenticated`, GET has 200+404 only. Fix: add `@APIResponse(responseCode = "401", description = "Request is not authenticated.")` to each of the 3 method sites. Annotation-only; zero wire change. AC: all 3 methods document 401 in the OpenAPI spec; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-348, PR #2219, sha: e6ee60cc) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/admin/instance/resources/InstanceRegistryRest.java`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/collection/resources/CollectionPublicationStateRest.java`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/container/resources/ContainerPublicationStateRest.java`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire346-2026-07-01.md` §F1 |
| **APISIMP-MISSING-OPERATIONID-P3** | Three v2 REST files lack `operationId` in their `@Operation` annotations — breaking generated client method naming and OpenAPI spec completeness. Fix: add `operationId` strings `"materialize"`, `"getUserAvatar"`, `"uploadMyAvatar"`, `"deleteMyAvatar"`. Annotation-only; zero wire change. | **APISIMP-MISSING-OPERATIONID-P4** | Two methods in `CollectionTemplatesRest.java` carry `@Operation(summary=...)` but no `operationId`, breaking generated client method naming. Missed by prior waves (fire-341 APISIMP-MISSING-OPERATIONID, fire-352 APISIMP-MISSING-OPERATIONID-P3) because those scanners passed a file if any sibling method already carried a full `@Operation` block. Affected: (1) `CollectionTemplatesRest.java:87` `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/templates/allowed` → method `listAllowed` — `@Operation(summary=...)` only; (2) `CollectionTemplatesRest.java:112` `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/templates/used` → method `listUsed` — `@Operation(summary=...)` only. | **APISIMP-BULK-ANNOT-CLIENT-REGEN** | `BulkAnnotationDialog.vue` (SEMANTIC-ANNOTATE-BULK-UI-1, fire-353, PR #2225) uses a raw `fetch()` + local `v2BaseUrl()`/`authHeaders()` helpers instead of `useV2ShepardApi(SemanticAnnotationsApi)`. Root cause: `POST /v2/annotations/bulk` (`operationId = bulkCreateAnnotations`) was added after the last `@dlr-shepard/backend-client` regeneration, so `SemanticAnnotationsApi.ts` has no `bulkCreateAnnotations` method. The workaround is functionally correct (produces the right URL) but bypasses the generated client contract, making OpenAPI drift silently invisible. Same debt class as FE-BUILD-03-REGEN (pending full client regen), KIP1e (publish button raw fetch), and VideoStreamReference raw fetch. Fix: regenerate `@dlr-shepard/backend-client` from the current OpenAPI spec (adds `bulkCreateAnnotations` to `SemanticAnnotationsApi`), then replace `fetch(...)` in `BulkAnnotationDialog.vue:156` with `useV2ShepardApi(SemanticAnnotationsApi).bulkCreateAnnotations(...)`. Wire shape is stable; this is composable-adoption only. This row folds naturally into FE-BUILD-03-REGEN when that lands. | **APISIMP-PLUGINS-ADMIN-BESPOKE** | `PluginsAdminRest` exposes `GET /v2/admin/plugins` (list all plugins with enabled state) and `PATCH /v2/admin/plugins/{id}` (enable/disable one plugin), backed by `PluginRegistry` and persisted to `PluginRuntimeOverride`. This is a bespoke admin-config GET/PATCH surface running in parallel to the generic `ConfigRegistry` at `/v2/admin/config/{feature}`. An OpenAPI consumer sees no discoverable reason why plugins live at `/v2/admin/plugins` while other mutable instance config lives at `/v2/admin/config/{feature}`. Fix (a): Implement `PluginConfigDescriptor` wrapping `PluginRegistry`, register in `ConfigRegistry`, route `GET /PATCH /v2/admin/config/plugins` through the standard registry, tombstone `/v2/admin/plugins` → 410. Fix (b — minimal): Add a Javadoc cross-reference explaining why the per-plugin `{id}` path segment prevents merging into `ConfigRegistry`. AC: either `PluginsAdminRest` merged into `ConfigRegistry` or a clearly documented rationale comment added; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ done (fire-357) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/admin/plugins/PluginsAdminRest.java` (Javadoc cross-reference to `AdminConfigRest` + rationale for the per-id split added, `@see` link updated); PR `APISIMP-PLUGINS-ADMIN-BESPOKE`. `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire355-2026-07-02.md` §F1 |
| **APISIMP-FEATURES-ADMIN-BESPOKE** | `AdminFeaturesRest` exposes `GET /v2/admin/features` (list runtime feature toggles) and `PATCH /v2/admin/features/{name}` (flip one toggle, transient — not persisted across restarts), backed by `FeatureToggleRegistry`. This is a third GET/PATCH admin surface alongside `/v2/admin/config/{feature}` (persisted) and `/v2/admin/plugins` (persisted). The transient-vs-persistent distinction is invisible at the URL level; callers expect a `?page=` sibling after seeing `pageSize` on a near-identical shape. Fix (a): Rename to `/v2/admin/runtime-toggles/{name}` and add `x-transient: true` OpenAPI extension to the PATCH response. Fix (b): Implement `FeatureToggleConfigDescriptor`, fold into `ConfigRegistry` with a `"transient": true` flag in the shape JSON. At minimum: add Javadoc cross-reference on `AdminFeaturesRest` → `AdminConfigRest` explaining the intentional split. AC: either merged into `ConfigRegistry` or URL rename lands with rationale comment; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ done (fire-356) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/admin/resources/AdminFeaturesRest.java` (path renamed `/v2/admin/features` → `/v2/admin/runtime-toggles`, rationale comment added); `backend-client/src/apis/AdminApi.ts` (two path strings updated); frontend comment + test URL updated; `aidocs/34` A3b row updated. PR `APISIMP-FEATURES-ADMIN-BESPOKE`. |
| **APISIMP-INSTANCE-REGISTRY-BESPOKE** | `InstanceRegistryRest` has an asymmetric auth split: `GET /v2/admin/instances` is `@PermitAll` (public) while `PATCH /v2/admin/instances` is `@RolesAllowed(INSTANCE_ADMIN_ROLE)`. A public GET under `/v2/admin/` is misleading — `/admin/` conventionally implies operator-only. This is a fourth parallel GET/PATCH admin-config surface with no cross-reference to the others. Fix: Move the public GET to `/v2/instance` (non-admin path), keep PATCH at `/v2/admin/instance` or register the writable portion via `ConfigRegistry` at `/v2/admin/config/instance`. | **APISIMP-TERM-SEARCH-NO-PAGE** | `GET /v2/semantic/terms/search` accepts `?pageSize=` (default 20, capped at 50) but has no `?page=` or cursor offset parameter. The response is wrapped in `PagedResponseIO` (per merged APISIMP-TERM-SEARCH-BARE-LIST), so callers receive a `total` count field implying further pages are navigable — but there is no mechanism to retrieve them. Fix: Add `@QueryParam("page") @DefaultValue("0") int page` to the search method; thread it through as a `SKIP $skip` parameter in `FULLTEXT_CYPHER` and `CONTAINS_CYPHER` alongside the existing `LIMIT $pageSize`. Add `"skip": (long)(page * pageSize)` to the Neo4j session.query param map. Update `PagedResponseIO` constructor to pass the correct page offset. AC: `?pageSize=10&page=1` returns items 10–19 of the full result set; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ done (fire-355-cont, PR #2228, `8972445e`) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/semantic/resources/SemanticTermSearchRest.java:209`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire355-2026-07-02.md` §F4 |
| **APISIMP-PROV-CURSOR-INCONSISTENT** | `GET /v2/provenance/activities` uses `?since=` / `?until=` temporal cursor parameters together with `?pageSize=` to page through the event stream. Every other paginated v2 list endpoint uses `?page=` + `?pageSize=`. A client that reads the OpenAPI spec, sees `pageSize`, and assumes a `?page=` sibling will silently receive only the first window on every call. The cursor model is correct and intentional; the naming is what misleads. Fix: Rename `?pageSize=` → `?limit=` on all three `/activities` method signatures (JSON / PROV-JSON / JSON-LD variants). Update the OpenAPI `@Parameter` description to say "cursor window size — use `?since=` / `?until=` for navigation, not `?page=`". AC: OpenAPI spec documents `?limit=` (not `?pageSize=`) on all three `/activities` variants; no existing client call sites broken (all three are annotation-only changes); `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ done (fire-357, PR APISIMP-PROV-CURSOR-INCONSISTENT) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/provenance/resources/ProvenanceRest.java` (all three `@GET @Path("/activities")` methods renamed `pageSize`→`limit`); `backend-client/src/apis/ProvenanceApi.ts` (`ListActivitiesRequest.pageSize`→`limit`); 3 frontend callers updated; `useFetchAdminActivities.test.ts` updated. `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire355-2026-07-02.md` §F5 |
| **APISIMP-PROVENANCE-MODE-UNUSED** | `DataObjectListItemV2IO.java:110` and `DataObjectDetailV2IO.java:118` both carry a `provenanceMode` field (`"human"` / `"ai"` / `"collaborative"` / null) serialized on every DataObject list and detail response. A project-wide grep of `frontend/composables/` and `frontend/components/` finds **zero references** to `"provenanceMode"`. Field introduced for EU AI Act Art. 50 compliance (PROV1j); not dead by mistake but deferred UI. Fix (a — full): Add a `ProvenanceModeChip` UI indicator in the DataObject list row and detail header. Fix (b — 1-line minimal): Add `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` to `provenanceMode` on `DataObjectListItemV2IO` (already present on `DataObjectDetailV2IO:117`) so null-mode objects don't serialize the key, shrinking list payloads. | **APISIMP-UNCAPPED-PARAMS-BATCH-1** | 5 v2 REST endpoints accept unbounded query params with no `@Max` bean-validation constraint, allowing callers to force arbitrarily large graph scans. (1) `SemanticPredicateStatsRest` `topValuesLimit` (MAJOR — up to 1 000 000 rows) + `sampleLimit`; (2) `ProjectsRest.queryByAnnotation` `pageSize` (description says "max 500" but not enforced); (3) `SnapshotListRest` `pageSize` (runtime clamp exists but no annotation); (4) `CollectionSnapshotRest` `pageSize` (same); (5) `DataObjectV2Rest` `depth` on predecessor/successor chain endpoints (runtime clamp exists but no annotation). Fix: add `@Max`/`@Min` from `jakarta.validation.constraints.*` to each param — `topValuesLimit @Max(200) @Min(1)`, `sampleLimit @Max(50) @Min(1)`, `pageSize @Max(500)/@Max(200) @Min(1)` per endpoint, `depth @Max(50)`. AC: all 6 files compile; `@Max`/`@Min` present on each param; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ done (fire-358, PR APISIMP-UNCAPPED-PARAMS-BATCH-1) | `SemanticPredicateStatsRest.java` (topValuesLimit @Max(200) @Min(1), sampleLimit @Max(50) @Min(1)); `ProjectsRest.java` (queryByAnnotation pageSize @Max(500) @Min(1)); `SnapshotListRest.java` (pageSize @Max(200) @Min(1) + imports); `CollectionSnapshotRest.java` (pageSize @Max(200) @Min(1) + imports); `SemanticAnnotationV2Rest.java` (both pageSize @Max(200) + import); `DataObjectV2Rest.java` (both depth @Max(50)). fire-358 2026-07-02. |

| **APISIMP-SEARCH-DO-UNCAPPED** | `GET /v2/search` paginates Collection results correctly but returns DataObject results unbounded — `dataObjectSearchService.search(body)` returns the full match set with no server cap. A broad query against a large corpus can OOM the JVM. Fix: add `@QueryParam("doPage") @DefaultValue("0") @PositiveOrZero int doPage` + `@QueryParam("doPageSize") @DefaultValue("50") @Min(1) @Max(200) int doPageSize`; slice `doResult.getResults()` accordingly; return `doTotal` in the envelope. | **APISIMP-ONTOLOGY-ALIGNMENT-FAKE-PAGED** | `GET /v2/semantic/ontology/alignment` and `GET /v2/admin/semantic/ontologies` both wrap responses in `new PagedResponseIO<>(body, body.size(), 0, body.size())` — a fake-paged envelope with no `?page=`/`?pageSize=` params. Callers that check `total > items.size()` to show a "load more" button will never trigger it; callers that use `pageSize` to pre-page are misled. Both lists are naturally small. | **APISIMP-REF-ANNOTATION-LIST-NO-PAGINATION** | `GET /v2/references/{appId}/annotations` wraps the full annotation list in `PagedResponseIO(rows, rows.size(), 0, rows.size())` without accepting `?page=`/`?pageSize=` params. Fix: add `@QueryParam("page") @DefaultValue("0") @PositiveOrZero int page` + `@QueryParam("pageSize") @DefaultValue("50") @Min(1) @Max(200) int pageSize`; slice with `rows.subList(from, to)`.
