| **APISIMP-PROJECT-BY-ANNOTATION-IRI-PATH** | `GET /v2/projects/{appId}/by-annotation/{predicate}/{value}` accepts IRI predicate and value as raw URL-encoded path segments. IRIs can contain `/` characters that nginx/Caddy reject when percent-encoded as `%2F`. Inconsistent with `SemanticPredicateStatsRest`'s Base64 URL-safe encoding pattern. Fix (preferred): move to `@QueryParam` — `?predicate=<iri>&value=<iri>` — sidestepping encoding ambiguity entirely. AC: `GET /v2/projects/{appId}/by-annotation?predicate=urn:shepard:mffd:layer&value=AFP` returns matching projects; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ shipped (fire-367, PR #2242) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/project/resources/ProjectsRest.java`; `frontend/composables/context/useProjectByAnnotation.ts`; apisimp-sweep-2026-07-02 §APISIMP-PROJECT-BY-ANNOTATION-IRI-PATH |
| **APISIMP-GIT-SOURCE-DUAL-AUTH** | `OntologyGitSourceRest` carries both `@RolesAllowed(INSTANCE_ADMIN_ROLE)` at class level and a per-method `guardAdmin(securityContext)` call — making the per-method guard appear to be dead code. The identical pattern is explicitly justified in `SemanticAdminRest:136–145` as defence-in-depth for Quarkus test-harness paths that bypass the JAX-RS filter chain. | XS | ⛔ parked — by design (same rationale as `SemanticAdminRest`; Quarkus test harness bypasses `@RolesAllowed`; `OntologyGitSourceRest` should carry the same `// Defence-in-depth` comment for clarity if it doesn't already) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/admin/semantic/OntologyGitSourceRest.java`; apisimp-sweep-2026-07-02 §APISIMP-GIT-SOURCE-DUAL-AUTH |
| **APISIMP-AAS-SHELLS-SOFT-CAPS** | `AasShellsRest.listShells()`, `AasShellsRest.listSubmodels()`, and `AasRegistrationAdminRest.listRegistrations()` use `Math.max(page, 0)` / `Math.min(Math.max(pageSize, 1), 200)` soft-clamping instead of JSR-380 `@PositiveOrZero` / `@Min(1)` / `@Max(200)` annotations. Invalid params (`page=-1`, `pageSize=0`, `pageSize=9999`) silently succeed with HTTP 200 instead of returning 400 — inconsistent with all other paginated v2 list endpoints. Fix: add `@PositiveOrZero` to `page` params; add `@Min(1) @Max(200)` to `pageSize` params; remove `safePage`/`safeSize` soft-clamp locals; add Jakarta Validation constraint imports. AC: `?page=-1` → 400; `?pageSize=0` → 400; `?pageSize=201` → 400; valid `?page=0&pageSize=50` → 200; `mvn verify -pl plugins/aas` green. | XS | ✅ shipped (fire-374) | `plugins/aas/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/plugins/aas/v2/resources/AasShellsRest.java`; `plugins/aas/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/plugins/aas/admin/resources/AasRegistrationAdminRest.java`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire374-2026-07-02.md §F1` |
| **APISIMP-SNAPSHOT-MANIFEST-FAKE-PAGED** | Added `?page=` (default 0, `@PositiveOrZero`) + `?pageSize=` (default 200, `@Min(1) @Max(1000)`) to `GET /v2/snapshots/{snapshotAppId}/manifest`; real in-memory slicing via `subList(from, to)` with correct `total`; 4 new pagination regression tests (`manifest_paginatesCorrectly_firstPage`, `_secondPage`, `_beyondLastPage`). | S | ✅ shipped (fire-370, PR APISIMP-SNAPSHOT-MANIFEST-PAGED) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/snapshot/resources/SnapshotRest.java` |
| **APISIMP-DQR-EVALUATE-BARE-LIST** | `POST /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/dqr/evaluate` returns a bare `List<DQRResultIO>` with no size cap. A collection with 10 000 DataObjects and 5 enabled DQRs generates 50 000 rows in a single synchronous response. Fix: add server-side cap (default 5 000); return a `DQRResultsIO` envelope `{ results, truncated, total }`. AC: evaluate on 10 000 DOs + 5 DQRs returns ≤ 5 000 results and `truncated: true`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ shipped (fire-371, branch APISIMP-DQR-EVALUATE-BARE-LIST) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/quality/resources/CollectionDQRRest.java`; apisimp-sweep-fire367-2026-07-02 §F2 |
| **APISIMP-IMPORT-DIAG-EVENTS-BARE-LIST** | `GET /v2/import/diagnostics/{runId}` returns `Response.ok(events).build()` — a bare `List<EventIO>` with no size cap or `?limit=` param. Fix: add `@QueryParam("limit") @DefaultValue("5000") @Min(1) @Max(10000) int limit`; slice events; add `X-Truncated: true` header when full list exceeds limit. AC: `?limit=500` returns exactly 500 items + `X-Truncated: true`; default returns at most 5 000; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ shipped (fire-369, PR APISIMP-FAKE-PAGED-BATCH-3) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/importer/resources/ImportDiagnosticsV2Rest.java`; apisimp-sweep-fire367-2026-07-02 §F3 |
| **APISIMP-NOTEBOOK-LIST-FAKE-PAGED** | `GET /v2/data-objects/{dataObjectAppId}/notebooks` wraps all `.ipynb` FileReferences in `new PagedResponseIO<>(result, result.size(), 0, result.size())` — no `?page=`/`?pageSize=` params. Notebooks per DataObject are naturally small (< 20). The `PagedResponseIO` shape implies navigable pages that don't exist. Fix: drop the wrapper; return `List<NotebookReferenceIO>` with `SchemaType.ARRAY`. AC: response is a plain JSON array; OpenAPI schema type is `array`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ shipped (fire-368, PR APISIMP-DROP-FAKE-PAGED-WRAPPERS) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/labjournal/resources/NotebookRest.java`; apisimp-sweep-fire367-2026-07-02 §F4 |
| **APISIMP-ADMIN-CONFIG-LIST-BARE** | `GET /v2/admin/runtime-toggles` (`AdminFeaturesRest.list()`) returned `new PagedResponseIO<>(result, result.size(), 0, result.size())` — fake-paged on a bounded in-memory list. Fixed: dropped the fake-paged wrapper; returns `Response.ok(result).build()` with `@Schema(type = SchemaType.ARRAY, implementation = FeatureToggleIO.class)`. (`AdminConfigRest.listFeatures()` was already a bare list — no change needed there.) | XS | ✅ shipped (fire-369, PR APISIMP-FAKE-PAGED-BATCH-3) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/admin/resources/AdminFeaturesRest.java`; apisimp-sweep-fire367-2026-07-02 §F5 |
| **APISIMP-FEATURE-TOGGLES-CLIENT-STALE** | After fire-369 dropped the `PagedResponseIO` wrapper from `GET /v2/admin/runtime-toggles`, the TypeScript client (`AdminApi.ts`) and frontend composable (`useFetchFeatureToggles.ts`) were left deserialising the response as `PagedResponse` (calling `PagedResponseFromJSON`). Fixed: `listFeatureTogglesRaw` return type → `Promise<runtime.ApiResponse<Array<FeatureToggle>>>`, deserialiser → `jsonValue.map(FeatureToggleFromJSON)`, `listFeatureToggles` return type → `Promise<Array<FeatureToggle>>`; `useFetchFeatureToggles.ts` drops the stale `.items` unwrap; 5 Vitest tests added (`useFetchFeatureToggles.test.ts`). | XS | ✅ shipped (fire-375, PR APISIMP-FEATURE-TOGGLES-CLIENT-STALE) | `backend-client/src/apis/AdminApi.ts`; `frontend/composables/context/admin/useFetchFeatureToggles.ts`; `frontend/tests/unit/useFetchFeatureToggles.test.ts` (new) |
| **APISIMP-INSTANCE-ADMIN-FAKE-PAGED** | `GET /v2/admin/instance-admins` and `GET /v2/admin/permission-audit` both use `new PagedResponseIO<>(items, items.size(), 0, items.size())` with no `?page=`/`?pageSize=` params. Fixed: both methods drop the fake-paged wrapper and return `Response.ok(list).build()` with `@Schema(type = SchemaType.ARRAY, ...)`. (`permissionAuditLog()` uses real pagination and was left unchanged.) | XS | ✅ shipped (fire-369, PR APISIMP-FAKE-PAGED-BATCH-3) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/admin/resources/InstanceAdminRest.java`; apisimp-sweep-fire367-2026-07-02 §F6 |
| **APISIMP-GIT-CRED-ADMIN-FAKE-PAGED** | `GET /v2/admin/users/{username}/git-credentials` returns `new PagedResponseIO<>(items, items.size(), 0, items.size())` with no `?page=`/`?pageSize=` params. A user's credential list is naturally small and bounded. Fix: drop the fake-paged wrapper; return `List<AdminGitCredentialListItemIO>` with `SchemaType.ARRAY`. AC: response is a plain JSON array; OpenAPI schema type is `array`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ shipped (fire-368, PR APISIMP-DROP-FAKE-PAGED-WRAPPERS) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/admin/users/AdminUserGitCredentialRest.java`; apisimp-sweep-fire367-2026-07-02 §F7 |
| **APISIMP-IMPORT-RUNS-FAKE-PAGED** | `GET /v2/import/runs` returns `new PagedResponseIO<>(runs, runs.size(), 0, runs.size())` on a transient in-memory list evicted after 24 h. `PagedResponseIO` implies paginable data that cannot exist; `total` always equals `pageSize`. Fix: `Response.ok(runs).build()` with `SchemaType.ARRAY`. AC: response is a plain JSON array; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ shipped (fire-368, PR APISIMP-DROP-FAKE-PAGED-WRAPPERS) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/importer/resources/ImportDiagnosticsV2Rest.java`; apisimp-sweep-fire367-2026-07-02 §F8 |
| **APISIMP-SNAPSHOT-PINNED-DO-UNCAPPED** | `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/snapshots/{snapshotAppId}/data-objects` embeds the full `List<String>` of DataObject appIds in `SnapshotDataObjectsIO` with no cap. A 50 000-DataObject snapshot serialises ~3 MB of UUID strings per call. Fix: add `?page=`/`?pageSize=` (default 500, max 2000); slice; add `totalDataObjects` + pagination fields to `SnapshotDataObjectsIO`. AC: `?pageSize=500` returns exactly 500 appIds with correct `total`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ shipped (fire-372, PR #2247) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/snapshot/resources/SnapshotPinnedReadRest.java`; apisimp-sweep-fire367-2026-07-02 §F9 |
| **APISIMP-SNAPSHOT-DIFF-UNCAPPED** | `GET /v2/snapshots/{aAppId}/diff/{bAppId}` returns `added` (`List<String>`), `removed` (`List<String>`), and `changed` (`List<DiffEntry{entityAppId, revA, revB}>`) without any server-side cap. For a 50 000-entity collection where all entities differ between snapshots, `changed` alone serialises ~2.6 MB of UUIDs + revision pairs in a single synchronous response. Fix: add `@QueryParam("maxItems") @DefaultValue("5000") @Min(1) @Max(20000) int maxItems`; cap each of the three lists at `maxItems/3` entries (rounded up); add `truncated: boolean` + `totalAdded`/`totalRemoved`/`totalChanged` counts to `SnapshotDiffIO` so callers know the untruncated sizes. AC: `?maxItems=100` returns ≤100 total diff items across all three lists with `truncated: true` and correct per-list totals; `?maxItems=20001` → 400; default 3-entry diff → `truncated: false`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ shipped (fire-374, PR #2248) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/snapshot/resources/SnapshotDiffRest.java`; apisimp-sweep-fire373-2026-07-02 §F1 |
| **APISIMP-INSTANCE-CAPABILITIES-DUPLICATE-200** | `InstanceCapabilitiesRest.getCapabilities()` carries two `@APIResponse(responseCode = "200")` annotations — OpenAPI disallows duplicate status codes; SmallRye OpenAPI silently overwrites one. Fix: remove the duplicate; move descriptive text into `@Operation(description=...)`. AC: single `@APIResponse(responseCode = "200")` on the method; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ done (fire-361, PR #2235, merged SHA `55ea4be4`) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/instance/InstanceCapabilitiesRest.java`; apisimp-sweep-2026-07-02 §APISIMP-INSTANCE-CAPABILITIES-DUPLICATE-200 |
| **APISIMP-INDEPENDENCE-PROOF-INPUT-CAP** | `POST /v2/quality/independence-proof` accepts `setA: List<String>` and `setB: List<String>` in `IndependenceProofRequestIO` with no `@Size(max=...)` constraint. The service runs three expensive Neo4j operations from the full unbounded lists: a 10-hop `has_successor` ancestor-walk from every appId in both sets (`SHARED_ANCESTORS` Cypher), plus two full `MATCH (do:DataObject) WHERE do.appId IN $appIds` attribute-map fetches. A caller providing 5 000 appIds per set drives 15 000+ Neo4j traversal threads. Fix: add `@NotNull @Size(min=1, max=500)` to both `setA` and `setB`; add `@Valid` to the POST body param in the REST resource. AC: body with 501 elements in either set → 400 RFC 7807; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ shipped (PR #2251, merged fire-377) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/quality/io/IndependenceProofRequestIO.java`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/quality/resources/IndependenceProofRest.java`; apisimp-sweep-fire376-2026-07-03 §APISIMP-INDEPENDENCE-PROOF-INPUT-CAP |
| **APISIMP-CROSS-TIMELINE-UNCAPPED-COLLECTIONS** | `GET /v2/collections/timeline?collections=` accepts an unbounded multi-value `@QueryParam("collections") List<String>` with no size cap. The handler loops over all supplied appIds performing `entityIdResolver.resolveLong()` + `permissionsService.isAccessTypeAllowedForUser()` (two Neo4j lookups) per Collection before calling `timelineDAO.aggregateMulti(collectionAppIds)` with the full list. A caller supplying 1 000 Collection appIds drives 2 000 serial Neo4j queries before the aggregation. The feature is designed for 2–5 Collections (swimlane comparison). Fix: after `parseIds()`, add an early-reject guard: `if (collectionAppIds.size() > 20) return problem(PT_BAD_REQUEST, "Too many collections", 400, "at most 20")`. Add `MAX_COLLECTIONS = 20` constant. AC: 21 collections → 400 RFC 7807; 20 collections → 200; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ shipped (PR #2252, fire-377) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/collection/resources/CollectionCrossTimelineRest.java`; apisimp-sweep-fire376-2026-07-03 §APISIMP-CROSS-TIMELINE-UNCAPPED-COLLECTIONS |
| **APISIMP-USERGROUP-PATCH-UNCAPPED** | `PATCH /v2/user-groups/{appId}` (`patchUserGroup`) accepts `usernames: List<String>` and `PATCH /v2/user-groups/{appId}/permissions` (`patchUserGroupPermissions`) accepts `reader`, `writer`, `manager`, `readerGroupIds`, `writerGroupIds` — all as unbounded arrays with no `@Size(max=...)` constraint. A caller can supply 50 000 usernames, driving an oversized Neo4j write. Fix: add `MAX_MEMBERS = 500` constant; return RFC 7807 400 when any array exceeds 500 elements. AC: array with 501 elements → 400 RFC 7807; 500 → 200; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✓ shipped (PR #2253, fire-378) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/users/resources/UserGroupV2Rest.java`; apisimp-sweep-fire378-2026-07-03 |
| **APISIMP-AAS-GET-SHELL-UNCAPPED** | `GET /v2/aas/shells/{aasId}` (`getShell`) calls `dataObjectDAO.findTopLevelByCollectionAppId(appId)` (2-arg, no pagination) and embeds all DataObjects as inline submodel references in the Shell body via `mappingService.toShell(collection, dataObjects)`. A Collection with 10 000 top-level DataObjects returns a 10 000-reference response body with no guard. The sibling `listSubmodels()` was fixed by APISIMP-AAS-SUBMODELS-UNBOUNDED (fire-281, PR #2147) but `getShell()` was not updated. Fix: add `static final int SHELL_MAX_SUBMODELS = 500`; cap to first 500 after the DAO call; add `X-Shepard-Truncated: true` + `X-Shepard-Truncated-At: 500` response headers when cap fires. AC: 501-DO collection → 200 with exactly 500 submodel refs + `X-Shepard-Truncated: true`; 10-DO collection → 200, no truncation header; `mvn verify -pl plugins/aas` green. | XS | ✅ shipped (PR #2254, fire-380) | `plugins/aas/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/plugins/aas/v2/resources/AasShellsRest.java:151`; apisimp-sweep-fire378-2026-07-03 §Finding4 |
| **APISIMP-REF-ANNOTATION-LIST-UNCAPPED** | `ReferenceAnnotationRest.list()` (`GET /v2/references/{appId}/annotations`) calls `r.handler().listAnnotations(appId)` and wraps all rows in a fake-page `PagedResponseIO(rows, rows.size(), 0, rows.size())` — no `@QueryParam("page")` / `@QueryParam("limit")` params exist and no cap is applied. A reference with thousands of annotations (auto-annotated MFFD/AFP channels) dumps the full unbounded list. The file also has unresolved merge conflict markers from commit 642be5a and will not compile. Fix: (1) resolve conflict markers favouring HEAD pagination params (`@QueryParam("page") @DefaultValue("0") @Min(0) int page`, `@QueryParam("limit") @DefaultValue("200") @Min(1) @Max(1000) int limit`); (2) also resolve all other conflict markers in the file (operationId, tag name, operation descriptions); (3) ensure the slice logic `from=Math.min((long)page*limit, total)` is retained; (4) add `X-Total-Count` header; (5) `mvn verify -pl backend` green. AC: `GET /v2/references/{appId}/annotations?page=0&limit=50` returns `{items, total, page, pageSize}` envelope with ≤ 50 rows; `X-Total-Count` header present; file compiles cleanly. | XS | ✅ shipped (PR #2255, fire-381) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/resources/ReferenceAnnotationRest.java:149`; apisimp-sweep-fire379-2026-07-03 §MINOR-1 |
| **APISIMP-CONTAINER-CHANNEL-ANNOTATIONS-UNCAPPED** | `ContainersV2Rest.listChannelAnnotations()` (`GET /v2/containers/{appId}/channels/{channelShepardId}/annotations`) returns the handler result directly with no `page`/`pageSize` params accepted at the REST layer and no cap. Fix: accept `@QueryParam("page") @DefaultValue("0") @PositiveOrZero int page` and `@QueryParam("pageSize") @DefaultValue("50") @Min(1) @Max(200) int pageSize`; thread them to the handler signature or slice the returned list. AC: endpoint accepts `?page=0&pageSize=50`; result is bounded; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ shipped (PR #2256, fire-381) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/containers/resources/ContainersV2Rest.java:~1004`; apisimp-sweep-fire379-2026-07-03 §MINOR-2 |
| **APISIMP-CONTAINER-TEMPORAL-ANNOTATIONS-UNCAPPED** | `ContainersV2Rest.listTemporalAnnotations()` (`GET /v2/containers/{appId}/temporal-annotations`) returns the handler result directly with no cap. Temporal annotations (event markers) can grow unboundedly in long-running MFFD process chains. Fix: same pattern as APISIMP-CONTAINER-CHANNEL-ANNOTATIONS-UNCAPPED. AC: endpoint accepts `?page=0&pageSize=50`; result is bounded; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ shipped (PR #2257, fire-382) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/containers/resources/ContainersV2Rest.java:~1099`; apisimp-sweep-fire379-2026-07-03 §MINOR-3 |
| **APISIMP-PAGINATION-ANNOTATION-GAP-P2** | `SemanticAnnotationV2Rest` (`list()` at line ~176, `search()` at line ~234) and `ReferencesV2Rest` (`list()` at line ~469) accepted `page`/`pageSize` via manual if-guards only — no JSR-380 bean-validation annotations. Missing: `@PositiveOrZero` on `page` (both files); `@Min(1)` on `pageSize` (both files); `@Max(200)` was present on `SemanticAnnotationV2Rest` but absent from `ReferencesV2Rest`. Fix: add `@PositiveOrZero` / `@Min(1)` / `@Max(200)` annotations; keep if-guards as defence-in-depth (unit tests call Java methods directly, bypassing JAX-RS interceptors). AC: `@PositiveOrZero` on `page`, `@Min(1) @Max(200)` on `pageSize` in both files; 99 unit tests green. | XS | ✅ shipped (PR #2258, fire-382) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/annotations/resources/SemanticAnnotationV2Rest.java:176,234`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/resources/ReferencesV2Rest.java:469-471` |
| **APISIMP-MCP-LIST-ANNOTATIONS-UNCAPPED** | `ContentMcpTools.list_annotations` called `getAllAnnotationsByShepardId()` without any page/size cap, returning the full annotation list in one MCP response. For MFFD AI-annotated DataObjects this could be hundreds of rows, exceeding MCP payload limits. Fix: add optional `page`/`pageSize` params (`Integer`; default 0 / 50; capped at 200) with in-memory slicing via `subList(from, to)`. Tool description updated to document pagination. AC: 5 unit tests green (3 existing updated to `null, null`; 2 new: page1of2, capped-pageSize). | XS | ✅ shipped (PR #2259, fire-383) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/mcp/ContentMcpTools.java:~199`; `backend/src/test/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/mcp/ContentMcpToolsTest.java` |
| **APISIMP-REF-ANNOTATION-PAGINATION-PARAM** | `ReferenceAnnotationRest.java:157` uses `@QueryParam("limit")` as its page-size parameter, making `GET /v2/references/{appId}/annotations` the only paginated v2 endpoint using `?page=` + `?limit=`. Every other paginated endpoint across the v2 surface (37+ endpoints) uses `?page=` + `?pageSize=`. The inconsistency arose when APISIMP-REF-ANNOTATION-LIST-UNCAPPED (fire-381, PR #2255) resolved merge-conflict markers keeping `?limit=` from the HEAD branch rather than standardising to `?pageSize=`. No frontend caller passes either param (both composables use the default); rename is safe. Fix: in `ReferenceAnnotationRest.java` line 157 rename `@QueryParam("limit")` → `@QueryParam("pageSize")` and local variable `limit` → `pageSize` (4 occurrences in the method body); update `@Parameter` description to say `?page=0&pageSize=200`; annotation-only — zero wire change for callers using the default. AC: `GET /v2/references/{appId}/annotations?page=0&pageSize=200` works; `?limit=` no longer accepted; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ shipped (PR #2263, fire-388) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/resources/ReferenceAnnotationRest.java:157`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire387-2026-07-03.md` §F1 |
| **APISIMP-RECENT-COLLECTIONS-V2** | Migrate `useFetchRecentCollections` from v1 `CollectionApi.getAllCollections()` to v2 `CollectionsApi.listCollections({ pageSize: 200 })` + client-side sort by `updatedAt` desc (nulls → 0 = oldest), take first 30. Helper signatures changed from `(c: Collection)` to structural `(c: { status?: string | null })` so neither helper imports the v1 generated type. Test mock updated from `useShepardApi`/`getAllCollections` to `useV2ShepardApi`/`listCollections`; `coll()` factory typed as `CollectionV2`. AC: composable no longer imports `useShepardApi`; `npm run typecheck` + `npm run test` green. | XS | ✅ done (fire-398, PR #2283, sha: afdfed7f) | `frontend/composables/context/useFetchRecentCollections.ts`; `frontend/tests/unit/useFetchRecentCollections.test.ts` |
| **APISIMP-STATS-NUMERIC-ID** | `FileContainerStatsRest.java` and `StructuredDataContainerStatsRest.java` (added by UI21-SIZEBAR-DATA) fail three APISIMP axes: (1) Standalone `@Path("/v2/file-containers")` / `@Path("/v2/structured-data-containers")` bypass the unified `ContainersV2Rest` kind dispatcher (Axis 2). (2) `@PathParam("containerId") long containerId` exposes a Neo4j numeric id on the public wire (Axis 4). (3) Both `@Operation` annotations carry only `summary`+`description`, no `operationId` (Axis 6). Fix: (a) Re-key from `long containerId` to `String containerAppId`; resolve numeric id internally via `entityIdResolver.resolveLong(containerAppId)`. (b) Add `operationId = "getFileContainerStats"` / `operationId = "getStructuredDataContainerStats"`. (c) Either move into `ContainersV2Rest` as kind-dispatched methods (preferred), or add a `// V2-EXCEPTION:` Javadoc explaining the standalone path. (d) Update frontend caller to pass appId string. AC: both endpoints accept UUID string path param; both have operationId; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ done (fire-404, PR #2288) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/filecontainer/resources/FileContainerStatsRest.java:32,40,56`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/structureddatacontainer/resources/StructuredDataContainerStatsRest.java:32,40,56`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-2026-07-04.md §F1` |
| **APISIMP-SHAPES-PREDICATES-FULL-PAGINATION** | `ShapesPredicatesRest.java:99` (`GET /v2/shapes/predicates`) uses `@QueryParam("limit")` and wraps response in `PagedResponseIO` — but has no `?page=` param, so multi-page navigation is impossible. The `PagedResponseIO` shape implies navigable pages that don't exist (callers reading `total > items.size()` as "there are more pages" have no way to retrieve them). Introduced by APISIMP-SHAPES-PREDICATES-BARE-LIST (fire-361); the `?limit=` naming was inconsistent from day one but not caught by prior sweeps. Fix (option A — preferred): add `@QueryParam("page") @DefaultValue("0") @PositiveOrZero int page`; rename `@QueryParam("limit")` → `@QueryParam("pageSize")`; use `subList(page * pageSize, Math.min((page+1)*pageSize, total))` slicing. Fix (option B): drop `PagedResponseIO`, return plain array + `X-Truncated` header (consistent with ImportDiagnosticsV2Rest `?limit=` cap pattern). AC: either (A) `?page=1&pageSize=50` returns items 50–99; or (B) endpoint returns plain array with `X-Truncated` header; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ done (fire-403, PR #2287) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/shapes/resources/ShapesPredicatesRest.java:99`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-2026-07-04.md §F2` |
| **APISIMP-SEMANTIC-CONTENT-TYPE** | Six local `problem()` helper methods across v2 semantic, provenance, and dataobject resources build RFC 7807 error bodies but emit `Content-Type: application/json` instead of `Content-Type: application/problem+json`. Clients checking the media type to distinguish error envelopes from success bodies will misclassify these. Files + lines: `SemanticSparqlRest.java:480`, `SemanticTermSearchRest.java:340`, `VocabularyBrowseRest.java:222`, `SemanticPredicateStatsRest.java:140`, `ProvenanceRest.java:638`, `DataObjectV2Rest.java:1079`. All have the same pattern: `.type(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)`. Fix: change each to `.type("application/problem+json")`. Optionally extract into a shared `ProblemResponseHelper.problem()` in `de.dlr.shepard.v2.common` to prevent recurrence. Annotation-only; zero wire-shape change. AC: all six paths return `Content-Type: application/problem+json` on error; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ done (fire-404, PR #2285) | `SemanticSparqlRest.java:480`; `SemanticTermSearchRest.java:340`; `VocabularyBrowseRest.java:222`; `SemanticPredicateStatsRest.java:140`; `ProvenanceRest.java:638`; `DataObjectV2Rest.java:1079`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-2026-07-04.md §F3`; PR #2285 |
| **APISIMP-DELETE-REFS-V2** | `useDeleteReferences.ts` has `deleteUriReferenceV2(appId: string)` calling `DELETE /v2/references/{appId}` (v2 ✅) but `deleteCollectionReference(collectionReferenceId: number)` and `deleteDataObjectReference(dataObjectReferenceId: number)` still use v1 `useShepardApi(CollectionReferenceApi)` / `useShepardApi(DataObjectReferenceApi)` with numeric ids. `DELETE /v2/references/{appId}` is a unified kind-dispatched endpoint that works for URI, Collection, and DataObject references. The `information.referenceAppId` field is already populated for all three kinds in `RelationshipTableElement` (confirmed via `readReferenceAppId()` in `relationshipTableElementMappingUtil.ts`). Fix: add `deleteReferenceV2(appId: string)` function using `DELETE /v2/references/${encodeURIComponent(appId)}` (same pattern as existing `deleteUriReferenceV2`); update `DeleteRelationshipDialog.vue` `case "Collection Reference"` and `case "Data Object Reference"` to call `deleteReferenceV2(props.tableElement.information.referenceAppId)` when `referenceAppId` is defined, with numeric-id v1 fallback only when `referenceAppId` is absent. AC: collection/dataobject reference rows delete via v2 when `referenceAppId` present; `npm run typecheck` + `npm run test` green. | XS | ✅ done (fire-402, PR #2286, sha: 97cc0f80) | `frontend/composables/references/useDeleteReferences.ts`; `frontend/components/context/display-components/relationships/delete-dialog/DeleteRelationshipDialog.vue`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-2026-07-04.md §F4` |
| **APISIMP-CREATE-REFS-V2** | `useCreateReferences.ts` still calls v1 for URI reference creation (`addUriReference` at lines 27–45 uses `useShepardApi(UriReferenceApi).value.createUriReference()`). The collection/dataobject create path is already v2-first in `AddRelationshipDialog.vue` via `useCreateReferencesV2.ts`, but URI create has no v2 equivalent there. `UriReferenceKindHandler` (kind=`uri`) exists in the backend and accepts `name`, `uri`, `relationship` in the POST body — the gap is purely frontend. Fix: (1) add `addUriReferenceV2(uri, name, relationship)` to `useCreateReferencesV2.ts` using `POST /v2/references?kind=uri&dataObjectAppId=...` (same fetch pattern as existing `addCollectionReference`/`addDataObjectReference` in that file); (2) update `AddRelationshipDialog.vue` URI case to call `addUriReferenceV2` when `props.dataObjectAppId` is present, fall back to `addUriReference` for legacy DataObjects. AC: URI references create via v2 when `dataObjectAppId` is present; `npm run typecheck` + `npm run test` green. Note: v1 fallback paths in `useCreateReferences.ts` for collection/dataobject remain as necessary fallback until L2 migration confirms all DataObjects have an `appId`. | S | ✅ done (fire-404, PR #2289, sha: 3390d2b) | `frontend/composables/references/useCreateReferences.ts:27–45`; `frontend/composables/references/useCreateReferencesV2.ts`; `frontend/components/context/display-components/relationships/add-dialog/AddRelationshipDialog.vue:175–185`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire404-2026-07-04.md §F1` |
| **APISIMP-SUMMARY-IO-NUMERIC-ID** | `DataObjectSummaryIO.java:19` declares `private Long id` (Neo4j numeric OGM node id) serialised in the v2 wire response for all predecessor/successor summaries (`GET /v2/collections/{cid}/data-objects/{did}/predecessors|successors`). The field's Javadoc said _"kept for backward-compat delete flows until appId-keyed delete ships"_ — that condition was met by APISIMP-DELETE-REFS-V2 (PR #2286, fire-402), making the justification stale. The field violates the v2 "no numeric internal ids on wire" contract. Fix: (1) remove `private Long id` and its `@Schema(readOnly = true)` annotation from `DataObjectSummaryIO`; (2) audit all frontend consumers referencing `.id` on a DataObjectSummary-shaped object (grep `frontend/` for `.id` in predecessor/successor list context); (3) switch display to `appId`. AC: serialised `DataObjectSummaryIO` has no `id` key; `mvn verify -pl backend` green; `npm run typecheck` green. Note: M-sized due to frontend consumer audit across ~10 components before the backend field is safely removed. | M | ✅ done (fire-407, PR #2291, sha: f2afcb19) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/dataobject/io/DataObjectSummaryIO.java:17–21`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire404-2026-07-04.md §F2`; PR #2291 |
| **APISIMP-PAGE-VALIDATION-GUARDS** | Four v2 list endpoints declare `@DefaultValue("0") int page` without a `@PositiveOrZero` (or `@Min(0)`) guard, and `SemanticTermSearchRest.java` also lacks `@Min`/`@Max` on `pageSize`. All endpoints already do server-side clamping so there is no runtime-behaviour gap — the missing annotations are a contract-documentation gap: OpenAPI clients cannot derive the valid range from the generated spec. Files: `SnapshotListRest.java:132`, `CollectionSnapshotRest.java:187`, `SemanticTermSearchRest.java:215`, `ProjectsRest.java:230`. Fix: add `@PositiveOrZero` after `@DefaultValue("0")` on each `page` param; for `SemanticTermSearchRest.java:211` add `@Min(1) @Max(50)` on `pageSize`. AC: all four `page` params annotated with `@PositiveOrZero`; `SemanticTermSearchRest.pageSize` annotated with `@Min(1) @Max(50)`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green; no wire-shape change; no frontend change needed. | XS | ✅ merged fire-409 (PR #2292, sha: a20e6d67) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/snapshot/resources/SnapshotListRest.java:132`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/snapshot/resources/CollectionSnapshotRest.java:187`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/semantic/resources/SemanticTermSearchRest.java:215`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/project/resources/ProjectsRest.java:230`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire407-2026-07-04.md §F1` |
| **APISIMP-GIT-SOURCE-IN-MEMORY-PAGING** | `GET /v2/admin/semantic/ontology/git-sources` (`OntologyGitSourceRest.list()`) accepts `?page=`/`?pageSize=` via `@QueryParam` but calls `gitSourceDAO.listAll()` — loading every row — and slices in Java with `all.subList(from, to)`. The DAO round-trip scales linearly with total rows even when `pageSize=10`. Fix: push `SKIP page*pageSize LIMIT pageSize` to `OntologyGitSourceDAO`; add a paired `count()` Cypher query for the `total` envelope field. AC: `?page=0&pageSize=2` returns exactly 2 rows and correct `total`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green; no wire-shape change. | XS | ✅ merged fire-411 (PR #2294, sha: 6b4175d) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/admin/semantic/OntologyGitSourceRest.java:113`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/context/semantic/daos/OntologyGitSourceDAO.java`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire409-2026-07-04.md §F1` |
| **APISIMP-VOCAB-PREDICATES-IN-MEMORY-PAGING** | `GET /v2/semantic/vocabularies/{vocabId}/predicates` (`VocabularyBrowseRest.listPredicatesForVocabulary()`) accepts `?page=`/`?pageSize=` but calls `predicateDAO.listByVocabulary(vocabId)` (no args) then slices in Java. Large vocabularies (PROV-O, IAO) have hundreds of predicates; loading all before slicing wastes DB I/O on every page-2+ request. Fix: add `listByVocabulary(String vocabId, int skip, int limit)` to `PredicateDAO`; delegate `count(vocabId)` to a `COUNT(*)` Cypher query. AC: `?page=1&pageSize=10` issues a single bounded Cypher call; returns correct `total`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ shipped (fire-410, PR #2293, sha: 9f059e1d) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/semantic/resources/VocabularyBrowseRest.java:151`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire409-2026-07-04.md §F2` |
| **APISIMP-ALIGNMENT-UNBOUNDED** | `GET /v2/semantic/ontology/alignment` (`OntologyAlignmentRest.list()`) calls `ontologyAlignmentDAO.findAll()` and returns a bare `List<OntologyAlignmentIO>` — no pagination params, no `?limit=`, no size cap. The registry is small today (≤ ~50 rows) but carries no enforcement. Fix (preferred): add server-side cap `MAX_ALIGNMENT_ROWS = 500`; return first 500 + `X-Truncated: true` header if exceeded; annotate response `@Schema(type = SchemaType.ARRAY)` (the `@APIResponse` content already carries this annotation). | **APISIMP-VOCAB-LIST-UNBOUNDED** | `GET /v2/semantic/vocabularies` (`VocabularyBrowseRest.listVocabularies()`) calls `vocabularyDAO.listAll()` with no cap. An operator seeding the full OBO Foundry slice (300+ vocabularies) gets all in one response. Fix: add `?page=`/`?pageSize=` (default 50, max 200); push `SKIP/LIMIT` to `VocabularyDAO`; return `PagedResponseIO<VocabularyIO>`. | **APISIMP-CONTAINERS-FAKE-PAGINATION** | `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/containers` (`CollectionContainersRest.list()`) accepts no `?page=`/`?pageSize=` params but wraps the result in `new PagedResponseIO<>(containers, containers.size(), 0, containers.size())`. The envelope `page=0`, `pageSize=N`, `total=N` implies navigable pages that don't exist; callers reading `total > items.size()` as "there are more pages" have no mechanism to retrieve them. Fix: drop the `PagedResponseIO` wrapper; return `Response.ok(containers).build()` with `@Schema(type = SchemaType.ARRAY, implementation = ContainerV2IO.class)`; update frontend `CollectionContainersPane.vue` caller to read the raw array (currently unwraps `.items`). AC: response is a plain JSON array; `npm run typecheck` + `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged fire-415 (PR #2297, sha: 5fa1960) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/collection/resources/CollectionContainersRest.java:78`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire409-2026-07-04.md §F5` |
| **APISIMP-IO-STALE-TS-REF-PATHS** | 4 Javadoc/comment strings in the anomaly detection IO + handler still reference the old per-kind `timeseries-references` path prefix abolished by V2CONV-A2. `AnomalyDetectRequestIO.java:9` (`POST /v2/timeseries-references/{refAppId}/detect-anomalies` → `/v2/references/{appId}/detect-anomalies`); `AnomalyDetectResultIO.java:8` (same); `TimeseriesReferenceKindHandler.java:37` class Javadoc (`PATCH /v2/timeseries-references/{appId}` → `PATCH /v2/references/{appId}`); `TimeseriesReferenceKindHandler.java:162` inline comment (same). Wire shape is correct; Javadoc/comments mislead future readers. Counterpart to APISIMP-IO-STALE-PATH-REFS (fire-325, PR #2203) which fixed container-family paths; this covers reference-family paths missed by that pass. Fix: update 4 `@code`/prose references to the unified `/v2/references/...` path. | **APISIMP-COLLECTIONS-IN-MEMORY-PAGING** | `CollectionV2Rest.list()` (`GET /v2/collections`) accepts `page`/`pageSize` but calls `collectionService.getAllCollections(params)` which loads **all** caller-visible Collections into memory before Java `subList`. The inline comment acknowledges `APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE-1` but no row was ever filed. Fix (pragmatic): pre-fetch the permission-filtered list of collection appIds (PermissionsService cache is warm), then issue a bounded Cypher `MATCH (c:Collection) WHERE c.appId IN $ids … SKIP $skip LIMIT $limit`; carry `total = count(filtered-ids)`. Very low urgency (publications per entity are typically 1–5 history rows). AC: bounded Cypher issued; `mvn verify -pl backend` green; no wire-shape change. | XS | ✅ shipped fire-421 (PR #2306) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/publish/resources/FlatPublicationsRest.java`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/publish/resources/PublicationsListRest.java`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/publish/daos/PublicationDAO.java`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire420-2026-07-05.md §F3` |
| **APISIMP-SNAPSHOT-MANIFEST-IN-MEMORY-PAGING** | `SnapshotRest.manifest()` (`GET /v2/snapshots/{appId}/manifest`) declared `page`/`pageSize` params but called `snapshotService.findEntries(id)` — unbounded load of ALL SnapshotEntry nodes — then sliced in Java with `allEntries.subList(from, to)`. Critical for large Collections (manifests can reach 10k+ entries). Fix: add `countEntriesBySnapshot(long)` (COUNT Cypher) + `findEntriesBySnapshot(long, int skip, int limit)` (SKIP/LIMIT Cypher) to `SnapshotDAO`; add `countEntries(long)` + `findEntriesPage(long, int, int)` delegates to `SnapshotService`; update `manifest()` to push SKIP/LIMIT to DB. AC: bounded Cypher issued; all 25 `SnapshotRestTest` tests pass; no wire-shape change. | XS | ✅ shipped fire-422 (PR #2308) | `SnapshotDAO.java` — `countEntriesBySnapshot` + overloaded `findEntriesBySnapshot(long,int,int)`; `SnapshotService.java` — `countEntries` + `findEntriesPage` delegates; `SnapshotRest.java` — `manifest()` rewritten; `SnapshotRestTest.java` — 4 pagination tests updated. `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire422-2026-07-05.md §F1` |
| **APISIMP-REFERENCES-LIST-IN-MEMORY-PAGING** | `ReferencesV2Rest.listReferences()` (`GET /v2/dataobjects/{appId}/references`) declares `page`/`pageSize` but calls an unbounded service method then slices with `all.subList(from, to)` at line 500. Medium urgency — DataObjects with large reference sets (e.g. large FileBundles) load all references. Fix: add `countByDataObjectAppId(String)` + `findByDataObjectAppId(String, int skip, int limit)` bounded DAO pair; update service + REST handler. AC: bounded Cypher; tests green; no wire-shape change. | S | ✅ merged fire-425 (PR #2310) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/resources/ReferencesV2Rest.java:500`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire422-2026-07-05.md §F2` |
| **APISIMP-CONTAINERS-LIST-IN-MEMORY-PAGING** | `ContainersV2Rest.listContainers()` (`GET /v2/dataobjects/{appId}/containers`) declares `page`/`pageSize` but calls unbounded DAO then slices with `all.subList(from, to)` at line 472. Fix: add `countContainersByDataObjectAppId(String)` + bounded `findContainersByDataObjectAppId(String, int skip, int limit)` to ContainerDAO; update service + REST. AC: bounded Cypher; tests green; no wire-shape change. | S | ✅ merged fire-425 (PR #2311 `a45da22`) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/containers/resources/ContainersV2Rest.java:472`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire422-2026-07-05.md §F3` |
| **APISIMP-DATAOBJECT-PREDECESSORS-IN-MEMORY-PAGING** | `DataObjectV2Rest.listPredecessors()` (`GET /v2/dataobjects/{appId}/predecessors`) and `listSuccessors()` + `listChildren()` (lines 679, 793, 842) all load the full related-entity set then Java-paginate with `subList`. Fix: add bounded DAO methods with SKIP/LIMIT Cypher for each relationship direction; update service + three REST handlers. AC: bounded Cypher; tests green; no wire-shape change. | M | ✅ merged fire-427 (PR #2312 squash `4499d72`) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/dataobject/resources/DataObjectV2Rest.java:679,793,842`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire422-2026-07-05.md §F4` |
| **APISIMP-VOCABULARY-BROWSE-IN-MEMORY-PAGING** | `VocabularyBrowseRest.browseTerms()` (`GET /v2/vocabularies/{vocabId}/terms`) and `PersonalVocabularyRest.listTerms()` (`GET /v2/personal-vocabularies/{appId}/terms`) both load all terms then slice at lines 172 and 206. Low urgency (vocabularies are typically <500 terms) but violates bounded-DAO contract. Fix: add `countTermsByVocabulary` + bounded `findTermsByVocabulary` DAO pair for each; update REST handlers. AC: bounded Cypher; tests green. | XS | ✅ done fire-428 (PR #2313, sha: 49f7647) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/semantic/resources/VocabularyBrowseRest.java:172`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/vocabularies/resources/PersonalVocabularyRest.java:206`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire422-2026-07-05.md §F5` |
| **APISIMP-UNHIDE-FEED-IN-MEMORY-PAGING** | `UnhideFeedService.buildFeed()` (`GET /v2/unhide/feed.jsonld`) calls `collectionDAO.findAll()` at line 122 — loading ALL Collection entities from Neo4j — then applies three Java transforms (delete filter + KG opt-out filter via N+1 `collectionPropertiesDAO` calls + sort) before slicing with `subList(from, to)`. Every feed page request is O(N) on the full Collection graph. Fix: push the filter+sort+pagination into one Cypher query with `OPTIONAL MATCH (c)-[:HAS_PROPERTIES]->(p:CollectionProperties) / WHERE p IS NULL OR p.publishToHelmholtzKG <> false / ORDER BY c.createdAt ASC, c.appId ASC / SKIP $skip LIMIT $limit` + a companion COUNT query for `totalEntries`. Remove Java filter + subList from `buildFeed()`. AC: single bounded Cypher issued per page request; `totalEntries` reflects filtered count; `mvn verify -pl backend` green; no wire-shape change on the feed endpoint. | M | ✅ merged fire-429 (PR #2314, sha: 7b8ee82) | `plugins/unhide/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/plugins/unhide/services/UnhideFeedService.java:122–153`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire428-2026-07-05.md §F1` |
| **APISIMP-USER-SEARCH-NO-PAGINATION** | `UserSearchV2Rest.searchUsers()` (`GET /v2/users?q=`) returns a bare `List<UserIO>` JSON array — no `page`/`pageSize` params, no `PagedResponseIO` envelope, no `X-Total-Count` header. The underlying `UserSearchService.search()` issues an unbounded Cypher query via `SearchDAO.findUsers()` with no `LIMIT` clause. Fix: (1) add `@QueryParam("page") @DefaultValue("0") @PositiveOrZero int page` and `@QueryParam("pageSize") @DefaultValue("20") @Min(1) @Max(100) int pageSize` to `searchUsers()`; (2) add bounded DAO overloads `findUsers(String query, int skip, int limit)` + `countUsers(String query)` (or slice at the service layer); (3) return `new PagedResponseIO<>(users, total, page, pageSize)`; (4) add 2 regression tests. AC: `GET /v2/users?q=alice&page=0&pageSize=10` returns `{"items":[…],"total":N,"page":0,"pageSize":10}` envelope; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged fire-437 (PR #2325, sha: cc6635f9) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/users/resources/UserSearchV2Rest.java:67`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire435-2026-07-06.md §F1` |
| **APISIMP-AAS-SHELL-DO-LOAD-CAP** | `AasShellsRest.getShell()` (`GET /v2/aas/shells/{aasId}`) calls `dataObjectDAO.findTopLevelByCollectionAppId(appId)` with no LIMIT, loads the full top-level DataObject list for the Collection into the JVM heap, then caps at `SHELL_MAX_SUBMODELS = 500` via in-memory `subList(0, 500)`. A Collection with 50 k DataObjects materialises the full Neo4j result set and discards 99% of it. Fix: pass `SHELL_MAX_SUBMODELS + 1` as a hard cap to the DAO so at most 501 rows are hydrated; derive `truncated` from `size > SHELL_MAX_SUBMODELS`. AC: `getShell()` never hydrates more than `SHELL_MAX_SUBMODELS + 1` nodes; `X-Shepard-Truncated` semantics unchanged; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ shipped (fire-442, PR #2358, sha: dade8c52) | `plugins/aas/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/plugins/aas/v2/resources/AasShellsRest.java:157–159`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire437-2026-07-06.md §F1` |
| **APISIMP-REFANNOT-IN-MEMORY-PAGING** | `ReferenceAnnotationRest.listAnnotations()` (`GET /v2/references/{appId}/annotations`) calls `handler.listAnnotations(appId)` which loads all annotations from the store then pages in Java via `rows.subList(from, to)`. `TimeseriesReferenceKindHandler.listAnnotations()` issues an unbounded SQL/Cypher query. Fix: replace `ReferenceKindHandler.listAnnotations(refAppId)` SPI default with `listAnnotations(refAppId, skip, limit)` whose override pushes SKIP+LIMIT to SQL/Cypher; REST layer passes `skip = page * pageSize`, `limit = pageSize`. AC: DB delivers only the requested slice; in-memory `subList` removed; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | M | ✅ shipped (fire-442, PR #2359, sha: d927b56) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/resources/ReferenceAnnotationRest.java:160–168`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/spi/ReferenceKindHandler.java:250`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire437-2026-07-06.md §F2` |
| **APISIMP-TS-CONT-ANNOT-IN-MEMORY-PAGING** | `TimeseriesContainerKindHandler.listChannelAnnotations()` and `listTemporalAnnotations()` (timeseries-container annotation endpoints) fetch the full annotation list with no DB-level bounds then page in Java via `all.subList(from, to)`. Fix: replace both service/DAO call sites with paginated variants accepting `skip`/`limit` that translate to SQL `OFFSET`/`FETCH`. AC: DB delivers only the requested slice for both endpoints; in-memory `subList` removed; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | M | ✅ shipped (fire-442, PR #2360, sha: c38aec3) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/containers/handlers/TimeseriesContainerKindHandler.java:518–530, 557–568`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire437-2026-07-06.md §F3` |
| **APISIMP-COLL-CONTAINERS-BARE-LIST** | `CollectionContainersRest.listReferencedContainers()` (`GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/referenced-containers`) returns a raw `List<ContainerSummaryIO>` via `Response.ok(containers)` — no pagination envelope, no `pageSize` cap, no `X-Total-Count` header. Underlying DAO issues an unbounded Cypher walk (`Collection → DataObject → Reference → Container`). Fix: add `@QueryParam("page") @DefaultValue("0") @PositiveOrZero int page` and `@QueryParam("pageSize") @DefaultValue("50") @Min(1) @Max(200) int pageSize`; add `SKIP $skip LIMIT $limit` + `COUNT` query to the DAO; wrap response in `PagedResponseIO`. AC: `GET …/referenced-containers?page=0&pageSize=20` returns `{"items":[…],"total":N,"page":0,"pageSize":20}`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ merged (fire-439, PR #2356, sha: 3d00af5) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/collection/resources/CollectionContainersRest.java`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/collection/daos/CollectionContainersDAO.java` |
| **APISIMP-DQR-LIST-IN-MEMORY-PAGING** | `CollectionDQRRest.list()` (`GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/dqr`) accepts `page`/`pageSize` (`@Max(200)`) but calls `service.list()` → `dao.findByCollectionAppId()` with no SKIP/LIMIT, then pages in-memory via `all.subList(from, to)`. Fix: add paginated DAO overload `findByCollectionAppId(appId, skip, limit)` pushing SKIP+LIMIT to Cypher; wire `skip = page * pageSize` and `limit = pageSize` through service and REST layer; remove in-memory `subList`. AC: in-memory `subList` removed; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ shipped (fire-439, PR #2357, sha: pending merge) | `DataQualityRequirementDAO.findByCollectionAppId(String,int,int)` + `countByCollectionAppId(String)` added; `DataQualityRequirementService.list()` → `listPaged()` + `count()`; `CollectionDQRRest.list()` removes in-memory `subList`. |
| **APISIMP-TSCHANNEL-CONTAINER-ID-WIRE** | `TimeseriesChannelV2IO` exposes `long containerId` — a Postgres serial FK — on the `/v2/` wire. Field carries `@Schema(deprecated=true)` but is NOT `@JsonIgnore`d, so it serialises in every channel listing response, leaking the internal Postgres container row PK. Fix (after TS-IDb/c): annotate `containerId` with `@JsonIgnore`; add `containerAppId` (UUID) to the wire. AC: `containerId` absent from response; `containerAppId` present. **Blocked on TS-IDb/c migration.** | M | blocked (TS-IDb/c) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/timeseriescontainer/io/TimeseriesChannelV2IO.java:48–49`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire437-2026-07-06.md §F6` |
| **APISIMP-USERGROUP-SEARCH-FAKE-PAGED** | `UserGroupV2Rest.listUserGroups()` (`GET /v2/user-groups?q=`) wraps the full search result in a fake-paged envelope: `new PagedResponseIO<>(items, count, 0, count == 0 ? 1 : (int) count)` — `page` hardcoded to `0`, `pageSize` equals the total count. The caller's `?page=` / `?pageSize=` params are silently ignored on the `?q=` path. User-groups are admin-bounded (tens to hundreds) so no heap hazard; the violation is the structurally misleading envelope. Fix: replace with `Response.ok(items).build()` (plain JSON array) on the `?q=` path; update `@APIResponse` to `SchemaType.ARRAY`. `GET /v2/user-groups` (no `q`) continues to return the proper paged envelope. AC: `GET /v2/user-groups?q=eng` returns plain JSON array; `GET /v2/user-groups` returns paged envelope; OpenAPI shows `type: array` for search path; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged fire-444 (PR #2361, sha: 4318a9d) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/users/resources/UserGroupV2Rest.java:114`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire442-2026-07-06.md §F1` |
| **APISIMP-WIKI-NUMERIC-ID-WIRE** | `WikiWriteResponseIO.labJournalEntryId` (line 25) — a Neo4j OGM numeric ID — is serialized on the v2 wire even though `labJournalEntryAppId` (UUID v7) is present on line 32. Field carries `@Deprecated` Javadoc but is NOT `@JsonIgnore`d, so the internal Neo4j node ID appears in every `POST /v2/data-objects/{appId}/wiki-write` response. Fix: add `@JsonIgnore` to `labJournalEntryId`. AC: `labJournalEntryId` absent from response; `labJournalEntryAppId` present; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged fire-444 (PR #2362) | `plugins/wiki-writer/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/plugins/wikiwriter/io/WikiWriteResponseIO.java:25`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire444-2026-07-06.md §F2` |
| **APISIMP-BUNDLE-FILES-NO-DEFAULTVALUE** | `FileBundleReferenceRest.listGroupFiles()` declares `@QueryParam("page") Integer page` and `@QueryParam("pageSize") Integer pageSize` without `@DefaultValue`, so the OpenAPI spec shows no default for either param even though the implementation applies defaults of 0 and 200. Fix: add `@DefaultValue("0")` and `@DefaultValue("200")` plus `schema = @Schema(...)` to document defaults. Keep `Integer` type to preserve intentional clamping behavior. AC: OpenAPI spec shows `defaultValue: 0` / `defaultValue: 200`; behavior unchanged; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged fire-444 (PR #2362) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/bundle/resources/FileBundleReferenceRest.java:450–453`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire444-2026-07-06.md §F3` |
| **APISIMP-PAGESIZE-CAP-UNDOCUMENTED** | `listGroupFiles` (cap 1000), `SnapshotPinnedReadRest.getDataObjects` (cap 2000), `listChannelAnnotations` and `listTemporalAnnotations` (cap 500) all enforce non-standard `pageSize` maxima without documenting them via `schema = @Schema(maximum = "…")` in `@Parameter`, so OpenAPI consumers discover the cap only via a runtime error. `SnapshotPinnedReadRest` also lacks `@Parameter` entirely on page/pageSize. Fix: add `schema = @Schema(minimum, maximum, defaultValue)` to all four endpoints; add `@Parameter` import to `SnapshotPinnedReadRest.java`. AC: OpenAPI spec documents the cap for all four endpoints; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged fire-444 (PR #2362) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/bundle/resources/FileBundleReferenceRest.java:450–453`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/snapshot/resources/SnapshotPinnedReadRest.java:124–125`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/containers/resources/ContainersV2Rest.java:1011–1013,1123–1125`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire444-2026-07-06.md §F4` |
| **APISIMP-CHANNEL-ANNOTATION-MISSING-SCHEMA** | `listChannelAnnotations` and `listTemporalAnnotations` in `ContainersV2Rest.java` have `@APIResponse(responseCode = "200")` without `content = @Content(schema = @Schema(implementation = PagedResponseIO.class))`, so the OpenAPI spec documents these 200 responses with no body schema. Every other list endpoint on the same class documents its schema correctly. Fix: add `content = @Content(schema = @Schema(implementation = PagedResponseIO.class))` to both 200 annotations. AC: OpenAPI spec shows `PagedResponseIO` schema for both 200 responses; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged fire-444 (PR #2362) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/containers/resources/ContainersV2Rest.java:1001–1002,1115–1116`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire444-2026-07-06.md §F5` |
| **APISIMP-SEARCH-SPLIT-PAGINATION** | `SearchV2Rest.search()` (`GET /v2/search`) exposes two parallel pagination vocabularies — `page`/`pageSize` (Collections) and `doPage`/`doPageSize` (DataObjects) — making it the only v2 endpoint that cannot be driven with a single standard page-cursor. Fix: unify into a single `PagedResponseIO` whose `items[]` interleaves both kinds; if per-kind totals are needed, expose them as named fields on a typed `SearchV2ResultIO`. AC: `GET /v2/search?q=mffd&page=0&pageSize=20` returns a single paged envelope; `doPage`/`doPageSize` removed; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ merged fire-445 (PR #2363, sha: d6b1a79) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/search/resources/SearchV2Rest.java:113,118`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire444-2026-07-06.md §F6` |

REST layer then slices with `subList(from, to)` at line 159. A paged overload `findEntriesBySnapshot(id, skip, limit)` exists (line 194) but is never called on this path. Fix: migrate list/GET/create/PATCH/DELETE to `ReferencesV2Rest` `kind=file-bundle` handler; nest the groups sub-resource as `/v2/references/{appId}/groups`; tombstone `/v2/bundles` with 301/410. | **APISIMP-ANNOTATION-ALIAS-FIELDS** | `AnnotationIO` serializes both v6 canonical fields (`predicateIri`, `predicateLabel`, `objectLiteral`, `objectIri`) and four legacy aliases (`propertyName`, `propertyIri`, `valueName`, `valueIri`) in every annotation response. Audit the 11 `useShepardApi(AnnotationApi)` call sites for which field names are actually read; then mark the four aliases `@Deprecated` + `@Schema(deprecated=true)` and schedule removal. AC: OpenAPI marks the four alias fields deprecated; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | **APISIMP-CROSS-DO-BULK-N-PLUS-ONE** | `CrossDoBulkDataRest.POST /v2/data-objects/cross-timeseries-bulk` — for each of up to 100 DataObject appIds the handler serially calls: (1) `permissionsService.isAccessAllowedForDataObjectAppId` (Neo4j), (2) `dataObjectDAO.findByAppId` (Neo4j), (3) `crossDoChannelResolver.resolveChannelsByPredicate` (Neo4j + Postgres), then (4) LTTB query per resolved channel (TimescaleDB). 100 DOs → potentially 400 serial DB round-trips before any data is returned. `PermissionsService.filterAllowedForUser(ids, ...)` already exists and is used by `SqlTimeseriesRest`. Fix: call `permissionsService.filterAllowedForUser(allDoIds, AccessType.Read, caller)` once; call `dataObjectDAO.findByAppIds(allowedIds)` once; then resolve channels + fetch LTTB per DO. AC: request with 20 DOs issues ≤ 3 DB calls before per-DO LTTB queries; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | M | ✅ merged (fire-455, PR #2380, SHA bcd942d) — duplicate row of the entry above | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/timeseries/resources/CrossDoBulkDataRest.java:132–181`; apisimp-sweep-fire450-2026-07-06.md §F8-1 |
| **APISIMP-CROSS-TIMELINE-PERM-BATCH** | `CollectionCrossTimelineRest.GET /v2/collections/timeline` — before the correctly-batched `timelineDAO.aggregateMulti(collectionAppIds)`, each collection appId is individually `entityIdResolver.resolveLong` + `permissionsService.isAccessTypeAllowedForUser` in serial — up to `MAX_COLLECTIONS (20)` × 2 Neo4j calls. APISIMP-CROSS-TIMELINE-UNCAPPED-COLLECTIONS (✅ fire-377) added the size cap; this is the orthogonal serial permission loop inefficiency on the still-capped path. Fix: replace the serial loop with `permissionsService.filterAllowedForUser(allCollectionLongIds, AccessType.Read, caller)`; fail-fast if any supplied Collection is not in the allowed set (consistent with the cross-DO "silently drop" policy — pick one and document it). | **APISIMP-TOMBSTONE-BARE-STRING** | Five 410 Gone tombstone methods across five files return `.entity(plainString).build()` instead of `.type("application/problem+json").entity(new ProblemJson(…)).build()`. The class-level `@Produces(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)` causes JAX-RS to set `Content-Type: application/json` with a bare JSON string body (not a JSON object, not RFC 7807). Correct tombstone pattern established by `WikiWriterTombstoneRest` + `GitReferenceRest` (fire-444/455): `Response.status(GONE).type("application/problem+json").header("Location","…").entity(new ProblemJson(…)).build()`. Affected files: (1) `FileContainerStatsRest.java:39` — no Location header; (2) `StructuredDataContainerStatsRest.java:39` — no Location header; (3) `AdminFeaturesRest.java:41` — has Link header but bare string body; (4) `DataciteAdminRest.java:69,83` (minter-datacite plugin) — bare string, no Location; (5) `EpicAdminRest.java:69,83` (minter-epic plugin) — bare string, no Location. Fix: in each file replace `private static final String GONE_MSG/GONE_DETAIL` + bare `.entity(...)` with `new ProblemJson("urn:shepard:error:gone","Gone",410,GONE_MSG,null)` + `.type("application/problem+json").header("Location","…")`. Add `Location` header to the two container-stats tombstones pointing to `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/stats`. AC: `GET /v2/file-containers/{id}/stats` → 410 with `Content-Type: application/problem+json` and JSON object body; same for the four other paths; `mvn verify -pl backend,plugins/minter-datacite,plugins/minter-epic` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-463, PR #2389, SHA 12580e01a) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/filecontainer/resources/FileContainerStatsRest.java:39`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/structureddatacontainer/resources/StructuredDataContainerStatsRest.java:39`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/admin/resources/AdminFeaturesRest.java:41`; `plugins/minter-datacite/.../DataciteAdminRest.java:69,83`; `plugins/minter-epic/.../EpicAdminRest.java:69,83`; aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-2026-07-07.md §F1 |
| **APISIMP-BUNDLE-GROUP-FILES-INTEGER-PARAMS** | `BundleGroupsV2Rest.listGroupFiles()` (`GET /v2/bundles/{bundleAppId}/groups/{groupAppId}/files`) declares `@QueryParam("page") @DefaultValue("0") Integer page` and `@QueryParam("pageSize") @DefaultValue("200") Integer pageSize` — boxed types with manual null-clamping (`pageIdx = page == null || page < 0 ? 0 : page`; `effectiveSize = pageSize == null ? DEFAULT : Math.min(MAX, Math.max(1, pageSize))`). The `@DefaultValue` annotations make the null branches unreachable. Every other v2 paginated endpoint uses primitive `int` + `@PositiveOrZero`/`@Min(1)`/`@Max(n)` Bean Validation constraints. Fix: change `Integer page` → `@PositiveOrZero int page`; `Integer pageSize` → `@Min(1) @Max(1000) int pageSize`; add `import jakarta.validation.constraints.PositiveOrZero`; remove manual null-clamp block; use `page`/`pageSize` directly in the method body. AC: method uses primitive int with Bean Validation; manual clamp removed; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-464, PR #2390, SHA d6bc03ba9) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/bundle/resources/BundleGroupsV2Rest.java:354–372`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire463-2026-07-07.md §F1` |
| **APISIMP-CROSS-DO-BULK-NO-ENVELOPE** | `CrossDoBulkDataRest.POST /v2/data-objects/cross-timeseries-bulk` (line 177) returns `Response.ok(out).build()` where `out` is `List<CrossDoSeriesIO>` — a bare array with no `PagedResponseIO` wrapper and no `total` count. The endpoint already bounds input to 100 DataObject appIds (`MAX_DATA_OBJECTS = 100`), but callers cannot distinguish "all results" from "got 100 but maybe 200 channels matched." Fix: wrap response in `PagedResponseIO<CrossDoSeriesIO>` with `total = out.size(), page = 0, pageSize = out.size()`; update `@APIResponse` schema annotation from `SchemaType.ARRAY` to `implementation = PagedResponseIO.class`. AC: response shape is `{items, total, page, pageSize}`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ done (fire-466, PR #2393) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/timeseries/resources/CrossDoBulkDataRest.java:177`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire464-2026-07-07.md §B` |
| **APISIMP-SQL-TIMESERIES-NUMERIC-CONTAINER-ID-FALLBACK** | `SqlTimeseriesRest` (`POST /v2/sql/timeseries`) accepts a `container_id_in` request field that takes a list of numeric Neo4j Long container IDs as a fallback path (line 195: "Prefer container_app_id_in (UUID v7); fall back to deprecated container_id_in (numeric)"). The `container_app_id_in` appId variant already exists. The numeric fallback causes the SQL timeseries query shape to expose substrate-internal IDs on the v2 wire, violating the "v2 never exposes numeric IDs" invariant. Fix: remove `container_id_in` from the request IO and handler; add a deprecation-period tombstone that returns 400 with a `Location` hint pointing to `container_app_id_in`. AC: request body with `container_id_in` returns 400 with clear migration guidance; `container_app_id_in` continues to work; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ done (fire-466, PR #2394) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/sql/resources/SqlTimeseriesRest.java:195–208`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire464-2026-07-07.md §D` |
| **APISIMP-CONTAINERS-CHANNELS-LIST-BARE-ARRAY** | `ContainersV2Rest.listChannels()` (`GET /v2/containers/{appId}/channels`, line 638) accepts `page`/`pageSize` Bean Validation params but the `ContainerKindHandler` SPI returns `Optional<List<TimeseriesChannelV2IO>>` — a bare list. The REST layer passes it through as `Response.ok(result.get()).build()` with no `PagedResponseIO` wrapper; `@APIResponse` schema is `SchemaType.ARRAY`. Callers receiving a full page cannot distinguish "exactly pageSize channels" from "no more pages." Fix: change `ContainerKindHandler.listChannels` interface return to `Optional<PagedResponseIO<TimeseriesChannelV2IO>>`; update `TimeseriesContainerKindHandler` to push SKIP/LIMIT to the timeseries DAO and wrap in `PagedResponseIO`; update `@APIResponse` schema annotation. AC: `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/channels?page=0&pageSize=10` returns `{items, total, page, pageSize}`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | S | ✅ done (fire-467, PR #2395) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/containers/resources/ContainersV2Rest.java:638–659`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/containers/spi/ContainerKindHandler.java:286`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/containers/handlers/TimeseriesContainerKindHandler.java:243`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire464-2026-07-07.md §E` |

| **APISIMP-PROVENANCE-LIMIT-BOXED** | `ProvenanceRest` has six `GET /v2/provenance/activities*` / `GET /v2/admin/provenance/activities*` overloads (lines 129, 192, 239, 291, 332, 368) each declaring `@QueryParam("limit") Integer limit` — boxed — and then defensively defaulting via `int eff = limit == null ? 100 : limit;`. The `@Parameter` description documents the default (100) and cap (1000) correctly, but Bean Validation could enforce the cap declaratively and eliminate the null-check. Fix: change each `Integer limit` to `@DefaultValue("100") @Min(1) @Max(1000) int limit`; remove the `int eff = limit == null ? 100 : limit;` line; use `limit` directly. AC: passing `?limit=-1` returns 400; passing nothing uses 100; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-465, PR #2392) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/provenance/resources/ProvenanceRest.java:129,192,239,291,332,368`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire464-2026-07-07.md §G` |
| **APISIMP-CONTAINERS-THUMBNAIL-SIZE-BOXED** | `ContainersV2Rest.getThumbnail()` (`GET /v2/containers/{appId}/thumbnail`, line 1463) declares `@QueryParam("size") Integer size` — boxed — with no `@DefaultValue`, no `@Min`, no `@Max`. The `@Parameter` annotation (fixed separately in APISIMP-CONTAINERS-THUMBNAIL-SIZE-PARAM, ✅ fire-141) documents the allowed range, but Bean Validation is not enforced: callers can pass `size=-1` without a 400. Fix: add `@DefaultValue("200") @Min(64) @Max(2048)` and change to primitive `int size`; remove any manual null-check in the method body. AC: `?size=-1` returns 400; `?size=200` continues to work; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-465, PR #2392) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/containers/resources/ContainersV2Rest.java:1463`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire464-2026-07-07.md §H` |

The method body presumably null-defaults these before passing to the DAO. | **APISIMP-SEMANTIC-ONTOLOGIES-BARE-ARRAY** | `SemanticAdminRest.listOntologies()` (`GET /v2/admin/semantic/ontologies`, line 260) returns a bare `List<OntologyBundleIO>` (SchemaType.ARRAY in `@APIResponse`, line 256). The list size is bounded by the number of seeded + uploaded bundles (typically < 20), but the response is inconsistent with every other v2 admin list endpoint that uses `PagedResponseIO`. Fix: wrap in `PagedResponseIO<OntologyBundleIO>` with `total = items.size(), page = 0, pageSize = items.size()`; update `@APIResponse` schema annotation. AC: response shape is `{items, total, page, pageSize}`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ done (fire-466, PR #2393) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/admin/semantic/SemanticAdminRest.java:254–260`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire464-2026-07-07.md §J` |
| **APISIMP-V1STATS-TOPN-BOXED** | `LegacyV1StatsAdminRest` (v1-compat plugin, `GET /v2/admin/v1compat/stats`, line 70) declares `@QueryParam("topN") Integer topN` — boxed — and then manually clamps: `int effective = topN == null ? LegacyV1StatsService.DEFAULT_TOP_N : Math.min(Math.max(1, topN), MAX_TOP_N)`. Fix: add `@DefaultValue("50") @Min(1) @Max(1000) int topN`; remove the clamp line and use `topN` directly; add `import jakarta.validation.constraints.Min; import jakarta.validation.constraints.Max`. AC: `?topN=0` returns 400; `?topN=1001` returns 400; no `topN` uses 50; `mvn verify -pl plugins/v1-compat` green. | XS | ✅ done (fire-466, PR #2393) | `plugins/v1-compat/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/plugins/v1compat/resources/LegacyV1StatsAdminRest.java:70–71`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire464-2026-07-07.md §M` |
| **APISIMP-SSE-SILENT-403-404** | `CollectionEventsRest.subscribe()` (`GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/events`, line 96) is the sole SSE endpoint in the codebase. When the requested collection does not exist (`ogmId == null`, line 97) or the caller lacks Read permission (line 102), both branches call `sink.close(); return;`. In Quarkus/RESTEasy, a `void` SSE method that closes the sink without throwing commits HTTP 200 with an empty/immediately-closed event stream — callers see `EventSource.readyState = CLOSED` with no error code and cannot distinguish "not found" from "forbidden" from "no events yet." The `@Authenticated` annotation already handles 401 at the framework level, making the null-caller guard at lines 91–94 dead code. Fix: replace `sink.close(); return;` in the not-found branch with `throw new NotFoundException("No Collection with appId '" + collectionAppId + "'")` and in the forbidden branch with `throw new ForbiddenException()`; remove dead-code null-caller guard (lines 91–94). Both exception types are already used elsewhere; RESTEasy maps them to 404/403 before the SSE handshake is established. AC: `GET /v2/collections/{nonexistent}/events` → 404; `GET /v2/collections/{existing}/events` (no permission) → 403; authenticated subscriber still receives SSE stream; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ done (fire-469, PR #2396, SHA 24585b2) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/events/CollectionEventsRest.java:91–104`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire468-2026-07-08.md §A1` |
| **APISIMP-USERGROUP-LIST-SCHEMA-MISMATCH** | `UserGroupV2Rest.listUserGroups()` (`GET /v2/user-groups`, line 95) declares `@APIResponse` schema as `SchemaType.ARRAY + UserGroupV2IO.class` but both code paths (with and without `?q=`) return `PagedResponseIO<UserGroupV2IO>` (lines 114, 122). The generated OpenAPI spec describes a bare `array[UserGroupV2IO]` while the wire shape is `{items, total, page, pageSize}`. Root cause: fire-444 (PR #2361) correctly set `SchemaType.ARRAY` when `?q=` returned a bare list; fire-456 (PR #2381) reverted `?q=` to `PagedResponseIO` but forgot to revert the annotation. Fix: change `@Schema(implementation = UserGroupV2IO.class, type = SchemaType.ARRAY)` to `@Schema(implementation = PagedResponseIO.class)`; remove unused `SchemaType` import (line 39). No runtime change — annotation only. AC: OpenAPI spec for `GET /v2/user-groups` shows `PagedResponseIO` as the 200 schema; `SchemaType` import absent from file; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-470, PR #2397, SHA d990d2b) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/users/resources/UserGroupV2Rest.java:95`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire469-2026-07-08.md §A1` |
| **APISIMP-DEAD-SCHEMATYPE-IMPORTS** | Eight v2 REST resources import `org.eclipse.microprofile.openapi.annotations.enums.SchemaType` but have zero body references to `SchemaType.*` — the import is dead code left over from earlier refactors that migrated the endpoint to `PagedResponseIO` or removed the `SchemaType.ARRAY` annotation. Files: (1) `CollectionWatchesRest.java:29` — list now uses `PagedResponseIO`; (2) `CollectionLabJournalEntriesRest.java:32`; (3) `NotificationRest.java:29`; (4) `NotificationTransportRest.java:29`; (5) `ProjectsRest.java:26`; (6) `OntologyGitSourceRest.java:38`; (7) `BundleGroupsV2Rest.java:48`; (8) `CollectionV2Rest.java:49`. Fix: remove the unused `import` line from each file. No runtime change. AC: `grep -r "import.*SchemaType" v2/` matches only files that genuinely use `SchemaType.*` in their body; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-470, PR #2398, SHA a75903f8) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/watches/resources/CollectionWatchesRest.java:29`; `backend/.../v2/labjournal/resources/CollectionLabJournalEntriesRest.java:32`; `backend/.../v2/notifications/resources/NotificationRest.java:29`; `backend/.../v2/notifications/transport/resources/NotificationTransportRest.java:29`; `backend/.../v2/project/resources/ProjectsRest.java:26`; `backend/.../v2/admin/semantic/OntologyGitSourceRest.java:38`; `backend/.../v2/bundle/resources/BundleGroupsV2Rest.java:48`; `backend/.../v2/collection/resources/CollectionV2Rest.java:49`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire470-2026-07-08.md §A1` |
| **APISIMP-REFANNO-LIST-NO-SCHEMA** | `ReferenceAnnotationRest.listReferenceAnnotationsV2()` (`GET /v2/references/{appId}/annotations`, line 151) annotates its 200 response with `@APIResponse(responseCode = "200", description = "Paged envelope...")` but omits `content = @Content(schema = @Schema(implementation = PagedResponseIO.class))`. The method body at line 165 returns `Response.ok(new PagedResponseIO<>(slice, total, page, pageSize))`, so the OpenAPI spec misrepresents the wire shape. Fix: expand the single-line `@APIResponse` to include `content = @Content(schema = @Schema(implementation = PagedResponseIO.class))`; add `import org.eclipse.microprofile.openapi.annotations.media.Content` and `import org.eclipse.microprofile.openapi.annotations.media.Schema`. Annotation-only; no runtime change. AC: OpenAPI spec for `GET /v2/references/{appId}/annotations` 200 body shows `PagedResponseIO`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-471, PR #2399, SHA a9b41009) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/resources/ReferenceAnnotationRest.java:151`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire471-2026-07-08.md §A1` |
| **APISIMP-VOCAB-BROWSE-SCHEMA-MISMATCH** | `VocabularyBrowseRest` three list endpoints (`GET /v2/semantic/vocabularies`, `GET /v2/semantic/vocabularies/used-by/{entityAppId}`, `GET /v2/semantic/vocabularies/{vocabId}/predicates`) annotate 200 responses with item-type schemas instead of `PagedResponseIO.class`. Fix: expand each `@APIResponse` to include `content = @Content(schema = @Schema(implementation = PagedResponseIO.class))`. Annotation-only; no runtime change. AC: OpenAPI spec shows `PagedResponseIO` for all three endpoints; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-473, PR #2400, SHA 9794f1ae) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/semantic/resources/VocabularyBrowseRest.java:199,252,316`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire472-2026-07-08.md §A1` |
| **APISIMP-PAGE-PARAM-MIN0** | Seven v2 REST endpoints declare `@Min(0) int page` instead of the standard `@PositiveOrZero int page` used by the majority of v2 list endpoints. Both annotations enforce `>= 0` on primitive `int`, but `@PositiveOrZero` is semantically richer (communicates zero-indexed intent) and matches the fork convention. Affected: `ReferenceAnnotationRest.list()`, `ShapesPredicatesRest.predicates()`, `ContainersV2Rest.listChannelAnnotations()`, `ContainersV2Rest.listTemporalAnnotations()`, `BundleGroupsV2Rest.listGroups()`, `ShepardTemplateRest.list()`, `ShepardTemplateRest.tags()`. Fix: swap annotation + add `PositiveOrZero` import where missing. Annotation-only; no runtime change. AC: zero `@Min(0) int page` in v2 resources; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-475, PR #2401, SHA af5d320) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/resources/ReferenceAnnotationRest.java:164`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/shapes/resources/ShapesPredicatesRest.java:101`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/containers/resources/ContainersV2Rest.java`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/bundle/resources/BundleGroupsV2Rest.java:133`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/template/resources/ShepardTemplateRest.java:92,302`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire473-2026-07-08.md §A1` |
| **APISIMP-XTOTALCOUNT-BATCH** | 21 v2 list endpoints compute a real `total` count and return `PagedResponseIO<T>` but omit the `X-Total-Count: <total>` response header that every other paginated `/v2/` list endpoint emits. Fix: add `.header("X-Total-Count", total)` before `.build()` in each of the 21 return statements across 12 files. For dump-all endpoints (no pagination) use the `items.size()` cast to `long`. For `UserSearchV2Rest:87` use `paged.total()` (record accessor). AC: all 21 endpoints emit `X-Total-Count`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. Wire-safe (additive response header). Files: `SemanticAnnotationV2Rest.java:202,258`; `DataObjectV2Rest.java:701,810,854`; `CollectionSnapshotRest.java:207`; `SnapshotListRest.java:180`; `SnapshotRest.java:173`; `CollectionTemplatesRest.java:109,134`; `ShepardTemplateRest.java:100`; `UserGroupV2Rest.java:113,121`; `UserSearchV2Rest.java:87`; `BundleGroupsV2Rest.java:145`; `ShapesPredicatesRest.java:114`; `VocabularyBrowseRest.java:169`; `InstanceAdminRest.java:123`; `SemanticAdminRest.java:275`; `PluginsAdminRest.java:131`; `NotificationTransportRest.java:88`. | S | ✅ merged (fire-475, PR #2403, SHA 6996e9b) | `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire474-2026-07-08.md §A1` |
| **APISIMP-GIT-CRED-PAGED** | `GET /v2/admin/users/{username}/git-credentials` (`AdminUserGitCredentialRest.java:236`) returns a bare `List<AdminGitCredentialListItemIO>` — the only admin list endpoint not wrapped in `PagedResponseIO`. Fix: wrap in `PagedResponseIO<>(items, items.size(), 0, items.size())`, add `.header("X-Total-Count", (long) items.size())`; update `@APIResponse` at line 216 from `SchemaType.ARRAY + AdminGitCredentialListItemIO.class` to `implementation = PagedResponseIO.class`; remove dead `SchemaType` import. Admin-only endpoint; wire-safe for this fork's callers. AC: response shape is `{items, total, page, pageSize}`; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-475, PR #2403, SHA 6996e9b) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/admin/users/AdminUserGitCredentialRest.java:216,236`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire474-2026-07-08.md §A2` |
| **APISIMP-MISSING-401-RESPONSES-P5** | Six method sites across two v2 backend REST classes are protected by `@RolesAllowed(INSTANCE_ADMIN_ROLE)` but lack `@APIResponse(responseCode = "401")` documentation. Missed by prior waves (fire-342, fire-348) because those scanners passed a file if any sibling method already documented 401. Affected: (1) `SemanticAdminRest.java:278` `POST /v2/admin/semantic/ontologies/{bundleId}/disable`; (2) `SemanticAdminRest.java:299` `POST /v2/admin/semantic/ontologies/{bundleId}/enable`; (3) `SemanticAdminRest.java:360` `POST /v2/admin/semantic/ontologies`; (4) `SemanticAdminRest.java:488` `DELETE /v2/admin/semantic/ontologies/{bundleId}`; (5) `ShepardTemplateRest.java:186` `PATCH /v2/templates/{appId}`; (6) `ShepardTemplateRest.java:263` `DELETE /v2/templates/{appId}`. Fix: add `@APIResponse(responseCode = "401", description = "Authentication required.")` to each of the 6 method sites. Annotation-only; zero wire change. AC: all 6 methods document 401 in the generated OpenAPI spec; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-477, PR #2404, SHA 8789a00) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/admin/semantic/SemanticAdminRest.java:278,299,360,488`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/template/resources/ShepardTemplateRest.java:186,263`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire475-2026-07-08.md §A1` |
| **APISIMP-MAXPOINTS-BOXED** | `ContainersV2Rest.java:733` declares `@QueryParam("maxPoints") Integer maxPoints` — boxed with no `@Min` or `@Max`. The handler (`TimeseriesContainerKindHandler.java:323`) already clamps to `[1, 5000]` via `Math.min(Math.max(maxPoints, 1), HARD_MAX_POINTS)`, but the REST layer accepts any value including negative, letting invalid inputs silently reach the handler. Note: `maxPoints` is intentionally nullable (`null` = absent = use handler's default of 2000), so `@DefaultValue` is incorrect here. Fix: change to `@QueryParam("maxPoints") @Min(1) @Max(5000) Integer maxPoints`. AC: `?maxPoints=0` returns 400; `?maxPoints=5001` returns 400; `?maxPoints` absent passes null through; `?maxPoints=500` returns data; `mvn verify -pl backend` green. | XS | ✅ merged (fire-477, PR #2406, SHA df9b99d) | `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/containers/resources/ContainersV2Rest.java:733`; `aidocs/agent-findings/apisimp-sweep-fire475-2026-07-08.md §A2` |

### UIVERIFY — feature verification + dual-doc program (2026-06-13)

SSOT inventory: `aidocs/agent-findings/ui-feature-inventory-2026-06-13.md`.
Four row families per feature: `PLACEHOLDER-*` (replace stub with real UI),
`UIVERIFY-*` (Playwright 4K+1920 e2e + screenshots proving it works),
`DOC-BASIC-*` (`docs/help/<f>.md`, researcher/basic mode), `DOC-ADV-*`
(`docs/reference/<f>.md`, inner workings + API/advanced mode). Hourly dispatcher
picks these up. Terse by design.

| ID | Scope | Size | First-reference |
