### BUG-USER-PROVISION-EMAIL-COLLISION — login 500-storm when stored username ≠ token preferred_username (shared email)
- **Status:** ✅ fixed · PR #2128 (`141749c`) merged · filed 2026-06-26 · severity HIGH
- **Symptom:** every API request for an affected account 500s (app-shell can't boot → page looks "crashed"). Confirmed live for the `admin` demo account: `/v2/users/me/preferences`, `/shepard/api/users`, `/v2/collections/{appId}`, `…/data-objects` all 500.
- **Root cause:** `UserService.createOrUpdateUser` (backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/auth/users/services/UserService.java:38) looks up the user by **username** (`getUserOptional(user.getUsername())`). The Neo4j unique constraint is on **email**. When the stored node's `username` differs from the token's `preferred_username` but the email matches, find-by-username misses → blind `userDAO.createOrUpdate` → `Neo.ClientError.Schema.ConstraintValidationFailed` on email → 500. Re-attempted every request (never provisions) → permanent storm.
- **Live data anomaly:** Neo4j `:User` node 183 has `username = "7eead942-63a5-4cfc-aea9-b1d0f0a291ea"` (a Keycloak subject UUID — also the importer's service identity) but `email = "admin@demo.shepard.local"`. Interactive `admin` login presents `preferred_username = "admin"` → mismatch → collision.
- **Importer entanglement (why the fix is non-trivial):** node 183 is BOTH the importer's identity AND the interactive-admin collision target. A naive "adopt-by-email + rename to token username" fix would ping-pong node 183's username between the UUID (importer token) and "admin" (interactive token) on every request → write churn + possible intermittent 404 under concurrent load while the importer runs. The clean fix is consistent username derivation (prefer `preferred_username`; ensure the importer's service token carries a stable, matching username) — should NOT be rushed mid-ingest.
- **Proper fix shape:** (1) harden `createOrUpdateUser` to fall back to find-by-email before create (idempotent against the email unique constraint); (2) reconcile username derivation so service + interactive tokens for one identity resolve to one username; (3) add `UserDAO.findByEmail`; (4) regression test: stored username != token username, same email → no 500, single node. Per "evolve safely" + "always do the proper fix".
- **NOT flo's reported bug:** logs show 0 collisions on `flo@demo.shepard.local` in 72h (only `admin`, 27×). flo's node is clean (`username="flo"`). flo's reported "lazy tree crashes the AFP collection page" is a SEPARATE, still-unreproduced issue (needs flo's live console error or working creds — repo demo realm file does NOT match live Keycloak: `flodemo`/`flo-demo` rejected live).
- **First refs:** UserService.java:37-62; JWTFilter / authenticationContext username extraction; feedback_proper_fix_over_workaround.md.


### BUG-DATE-COERCE — collection page blanks: toShortDateString throws on ISO-string dates — SHIPPED 2026-06-26
- **Symptom:** flo reported "the lazy loading tree crashes the page" on the MFFD AFP collection. Root cause was NOT the tree — `toShortDateString` (frontend/utils/helpers.ts:70) was typed `Date | null` and did `date?.toLocaleDateString(...)`. JSON carries dates as ISO **strings**; a string is not null so `?.` did not guard it → `"2026-..."?.toLocaleDateString` is `undefined` → "is not a function" thrown in `MetadataCreatedField/MetadataUpdatedField` `setup()` → component render crash → blank page. 18 call sites affected.
- **Fix:** coerce to Date inside the function (handles Date|string|number|null|undefined; returns undefined on null/invalid). `frontend/utils/helpers.ts` + regression test `frontend/tests/unit/toShortDateString.test.ts` (5 cases, the crash case included). Lint clean; new test green; frontend rebuilt + redeployed; live re-verify shows the TypeError gone.
- **Note:** pre-existing unrelated `vue-tsc` errors remain in `composables/annotated.ts` + `composables/context/useGlobalSearch.ts` (stale `@dlr-shepard/backend-client` regen: `SearchV2Api`→`SearchApi`) — separate debt, did not block `nuxt build`.

### BUG-ANNOTATIONS-MAP — semantic-annotations panel crash "reading 'map'" — SHIPPED 2026-06-29
- Symptom: collection/entity pages threw "Error while fetching semantic annotations: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'map')" (live on AFP collection 019ed455).
- Root cause: frontend/composables/annotated.ts SubjectAnnotated.fetchAnnotations read page.items.map(); listAnnotations returns AnnotationV2[] directly, so page.items was undefined. (Pre-existing vue-tsc error "Property 'items' does not exist on type 'AnnotationV2[]'" flagged it.)
- Fix: use the array directly with an Array.isArray guard. Commit 3bb9d4589.

### BUG-SIDEBAR-TREE-FLAT — lazy sidebar tree renders flat — SHIPPED 2026-06-29
- Symptom: collection sidebar showed a flat list, no parent/child nesting (AFP coll has 37 roots / 8450 children; e.g. Ply 37 has 63 children).
- Root cause: generated client backend-client/src/apis/DataObjectsApi.ts listDataObjects serialized only annotationFilter,fields,include,name,page,pageSize,status into the query string — NO topLevel, NO parentAppId (stale vs backend, which honors both; verified live ?topLevel=true -> 33 roots, 4 expandable). useTreeviewItems.fetchRoots passed {topLevel:true} but the client dropped it -> request hit the flat default list. parentAppId dropped on expand too.
- Fix: added topLevel?/parentAppId? to ListDataObjectsRequest + queryParameters serialization; rebuilt client dist; frontend redeployed. Commit 3bb9d4589.
- Durable follow-up SIDEBAR-CLIENT-REGEN-PARAMS: make the backend OpenAPI declare these two query params on listDataObjects so a future client regen keeps them (don't re-drop the hand-patch).

### BUG-ANNOTATIONS-MAP-ENVELOPE — annotations panel re-broke after backend moved to PagedResponseIO — SHIPPED 2026-06-29
- Re-surface of BUG-ANNOTATIONS-MAP. After 3bb9d4589 hardened the composable to `Array.isArray(list) ? list : []` (assuming a bare array), the backend migrated `/v2/annotations` list/find to the `{items,total,page,pageSize}` envelope (APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE-2-2, #2104, commit 43cc4d8d4). Two failure modes across the deploy gap: (a) a frontend regenerated to the `PagedResponseAnnotationV2` client crashes in the deserializer (`json['items'].map`) against a still-bare-array backend; (b) a frontend still on the bare-array guard silently yields an **empty** panel once the backend emits the envelope.
- Root cause: composable/backend shape mismatch during the rolling deploy — the two halves shipped in different commits.
- Fix: `SubjectAnnotated.fetchAnnotations` reads `.items` when present, else the bare array (tolerates both shapes). Backend redeployed from main so live `/v2/annotations` emits the envelope; frontend redeployed with the tolerant composable. `frontend/composables/annotated.ts` + regression test in `frontend/tests/unit/annotatedSubject.test.ts` (bare-array tolerance case added). Verified live: `curl …/v2/annotations?subjectAppId=019ed607-023a-7313-978f-e664423a34c4&subjectKind=DataObject` returns `{items:[…]}`.

### BUG-SIDEBAR-CHILD-FETCH-STALE-CLIENT — substructures under a PlyGroup node won't load — SHIPPED 2026-06-29
- flo: "substructures under ply group not accessible." Deeper lazy-tree expansion (children under a PlyGroup) never loaded. Source (`useTreeviewItems.loadChildren` → `fetchLevel({parentAppId})`, `treeviewItem.mapRowToItem`) and backend data were both correct (Layup → 50 PlyGroup siblings, each carrying `childrenAppIds`; PG "Ply 21" has 183 grandchildren).
- Root cause: the deployed frontend bundle was built from a **stale** generated `DataObjectsApi` (the npm `file:` dep regen-cache gotcha, `project_client_regen_gotchas`). 3bb9d4589 added `topLevel`/`parentAppId` query serialization to the source client (`DataObjectsApi.ts:467-472`), but the deployed bundle still dropped them — the lazy child fetch sent `?parentAppId=…` and the client discarded it, so the request fell back to the flat/root list → children never arrived. Confirmed: deployed `/app/server/chunks` had `parentAppId` only in doc text, never in `queryParameters`.
- Fix: redeploy the frontend so the fresh client (with the serialization) is bundled. Belongs with SIDEBAR-CLIENT-REGEN-PARAMS (declare the params in backend OpenAPI so regen can't re-drop them).


### SCREENSHOT-LEARNINGS-2026-06-29 — consolidate screenshot-session bug classes + validate against the complete frontend/backend
- **Status:** in-progress (validation agent dispatched 2026-06-29) · filed 2026-06-29 · size M · origin: Task #320 MFFD-presentation screenshot debugging (Shots A–E at 1920)
- **Why:** the 5-surface screenshot pass surfaced a small family of *recurring, systemic* v2-conformance bug classes, several of which other sessions / the hourly dispatcher independently re-hit and fixed one-page-at-a-time today (BUG-ANNOTATIONS-MAP-ENVELOPE, BUG-SIDEBAR-CHILD-FETCH-STALE-CLIENT). One-page fixes don't drain the class. This row captures the classes once and tasks a **codebase-wide validation sweep** so the remaining instances are found by audit, not by user-surfaced breakage.
- **The learnings (bug classes):**
  - **L1 — bare-array vs `{items}` PagedResponseIO mismatch (APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE).** A v2 list endpoint returns a bare array OR a `{items,total,page,pageSize}` envelope, and the consumer assumes the other shape → empty list (silent) or `.map of undefined` (crash). Screenshot instance: `/v2/semantic/vocabularies` returns a bare array; `frontend/pages/semantic/vocabularies/index.vue` read only `body?.items` → blank list. Fix pattern: tolerant parse `Array.isArray(body) ? body : (body?.items ?? [])`. **Scope: 38 frontend `.items` consumers × 33 backend `PagedResponseIO` endpoints** — backend list endpoints are the *root* (per-endpoint envelope-vs-array inconsistency); a backend convention audit + a frontend tolerant-parse audit together drain L1.
  - **L2 — v2 numeric-`id` suppression breaks any page that resolves a v1 numeric id from a v2 entity.** `@JsonIgnoreProperties({"id","dataObjectId"})` on the v2 IOs (7 v2 IOs suppress `id`) means `entity.value?.id` is always `undefined`; a page that feeds that into a v1 sub-call and gates render on the v1 result spins forever. Screenshot instance: TS-ref detail page `…/timeseriesereferences/[timeseriesReferenceId]/index.vue` (`timeseriesReferenceNumericId = referenceV2.value?.id` → v1 `useFetchTimeseriesReference` never resolves → render gate ~line 403 never opens). **Same class as #317/#318 + `BUG-DO-DETAIL-HANG`.** Scope: 11 frontend pages calling `resolveNumericId`/`.value?.id`. Fix pattern: gate render on the v2 entity; read `payload.*`; fetch via appId-keyed v2 endpoints (`ContainersV2Rest` `/{appId}/chart-view`, `/{appId}/channels/data/bulk`).
  - **L3 — `materialize` called on a `VIEW_RECIPE` template → 422.** `/scene-graphs/play/{id}` POSTs `/v2/mappings/{id}/materialize`, but materialize requires a `MAPPING_RECIPE`; a VIEW_RECIPE must be *rendered* (`/v2/shapes/render`). The play/render route must branch on `templateKind`. Screenshot instance: scene-graph `3a845120-090f-4821-8a3e-6f1bf6cda320` ("Cross-ply TCP temperature", VIEW_RECIPE).
  - **L4 — loud error toast instead of fail-soft degrade.** "Error while fetching Jupyter config" red box on the DataObject-detail Data-References panel when the Jupyter sidecar/config is absent. A plugin-optional fetch must degrade quietly (registries-are-fail-soft + plugin-owns-its-UI), not surface a red error on a core page.
  - **L5 — diagnostic discipline: refresh the token before concluding a systemic backend regression.** A prior session mis-diagnosed an expired-token `401` as a systemic `406` content-negotiation regression. Re-mint `/tmp/<user>_token.txt` (client_id `frontend-dev`, password grant) before blaming the backend.
  - **L6 — stale generated client (`project_client_regen_gotchas`).** The npm `file:` regen-cache can bundle a stale `@dlr-shepard/backend-client`; query params added to the source client silently vanish from the deployed bundle. Reinforced today by BUG-SIDEBAR-CHILD-FETCH-STALE-CLIENT. Validate `make build-frontend` busts the cache on every regen.
  - **L7 — caption/claim-vs-reality content gap.** Presentation caption B says "19 Ontologien" but only **11** vocabularies are seeded (first = CHAMEO). Reconcile claim vs seed (`V49__Bootstrap_internal_semantic_repository.cypher`) — seed more or correct the caption.
- **Deliverable:** read-only **validation sweep** across the complete frontend + backend → `aidocs/agent-findings/screenshot-learnings-validation-2026-06-29.md`, with a per-class instance table (file:line, shape mismatch, fix class L1–L7, severity) so remaining instances become discrete fix rows instead of waiting to break in front of a user.
- **First refs:** `frontend/pages/semantic/vocabularies/index.vue` (L1, edit uncommitted); `frontend/pages/collections/[collectionId]/dataobjects/[dataObjectId]/timeseriesereferences/[timeseriesReferenceId]/index.vue` (L2); `backend/.../v2/references/io/BasicReferenceV2IO.java` `@JsonIgnoreProperties` (L2 root); `backend/.../v2/containers/resources/ContainersV2Rest.java` appId-keyed TS endpoints (L2 fix substrate); `frontend/utils/collectionRouteParams.ts` `resolveNumericId` (L2); `e2e/tests/screenshots-320.spec.ts` (5-surface harness); BUG-ANNOTATIONS-MAP-ENVELOPE + BUG-SIDEBAR-CHILD-FETCH-STALE-CLIENT (sibling one-page fixes today); `project_client_regen_gotchas` memory (L6); `V49__Bootstrap_internal_semantic_repository.cypher` (L7).

## REPO-HYGIENE-REMOTE-BRANCH-PRUNE-2026-06-29 — prune 775 stale remote branches + 33 open PRs (size: M)
- **Status:** queued.
- **Why:** snapshot 2026-06-29 — `git branch -r | wc -l` = 775 remote branches; `gh pr list --state=open` = 33 open PRs. Long-standing accretion from agent runs, dependabot bumps, and rb-NNNN PR branches. Clutter makes `git log`/branch autocomplete unusable, slows clones, and obscures which branches still have unmerged work.
- **Scope:**
  - Audit + classify remote branches into: (a) merged-to-main → delete; (b) tracked by an open PR → keep until PR resolved; (c) abandoned with own commits → archive (tag `archive/<branch>` + delete); (d) dependabot-superseded → delete in bulk.
  - Open PRs: triage each — rebase + merge OR close-with-reason. Dependabot bumps (~15 of 33) can be batch-merged or batch-closed depending on whether the bumps are still wanted.
  - Worktree side: 3 worktrees (`FIX-UI-CONTAINER-KIND-ENDPOINTS`, `agent-ac56842bb6af20e7f`, `agent-ad37117c6218a83ed`) — verify commits are reachable on main or on a tracked branch, then `git worktree remove` + `git branch -D`.
- **Acceptance:** remote branch count < 50 (≤ active feature branches + main + dependabot live PRs); open PRs < 15 with each one having a clear next action; `git worktree list` shows only `/opt/shepard` on main; no agent worktree directories under `.claude/worktrees/`.
- **First refs:** memory `feedback_repo_maintenance.md`; this session's hygiene survey (worktree commits `99cd376fa`, `4d7cc37ac`, `87897fe41`); `aidocs/strategy/85-github-project-management-policies.md` (PR lifecycle); `scripts/trace-feature.sh` (verify nothing was orphaned before pruning).

## VIDEO-REF-NO-INLINE-PLAYER-2026-06-29 — single-payload DOs lose the video player (size: S)
- **Status:** queued.
- **Why:** REF-UNIFIED-TABLE removed `VideoStreamReferencesPane` (the per-kind inline `VideoPlayer`) and folded video references into the unified `DataObjectDataReferencesTable`. The new table lists video refs as rows but never mounts a `<video>` element inline — `showDetails()` on a video row navigates to `/v2/data-objects/{doAppId}/video-stream-references/{numericId}`, but the frontend has **no `pages/collections/.../videostreamreferences/` route**, so the navigation lands on a 404 or no-op. The only remaining player path is the `Synchronised player` expansion panel (`MultiPlayerPane`), which only mounts when `multiPlayerTiles.length >= 2` — so single-payload video DOs (video-only, video+filebundle, video+file) currently have no UI surface that plays the video.
- **Surfaced by:** task #320 Shot I-video-player — DataObject `019f129e-c45e-703a-97de-079cb19d1052` (P11_S_2teBahn) carries one Video + one FileBundle + 2 Files; the screenshot shows the Video row in the unified table but no player; click-on-name doesn't open one. Same pattern across the `mffd-stringer-welding` collection (35+ video DOs).
- **Fix candidates:** (a) ship `pages/collections/[collectionId]/dataobjects/[dataObjectId]/videostreamreferences/[videoStreamReferenceId]/index.vue` with the `VideoPlayer` + ref metadata (the natural sibling of `filereferences/` and `timeseriesereferences/`); (b) make `MultiPlayerPane` mount on ≥1 tile (single-video case) and rename it "Player"; (c) put the inline `VideoPlayer` back into the unified table as a row-expand. (a) keeps the URL-addressable surface consistent with other ref kinds.
- **First refs:** `frontend/components/context/dataobject/VideoStreamReferencesPane.vue` (removed surface, still in repo as reference impl); `frontend/components/context/display-components/data-references/DataObjectDataReferencesTable.vue:60` (`showDetails` → broken route); `frontend/pages/collections/[collectionId]/dataobjects/[dataObjectId]/index.vue:1012` (MultiPlayer ≥2 tiles gate); `frontend/components/common/VideoPlayer.vue`; `screenshots/I-video-player.png`; `aidocs/integrations/AAE3` (multiplayer design).

## VID-HEVC-PLAYBACK-PLAYWRIGHT-2026-06-29 — hevc videos render black in Playwright screenshots (size: S)
- **Status:** queued.
- **Why:** Playwright's bundled Chromium ships without proprietary codecs (hevc/h.265). The MFFD welding videos are uploaded as hevc 4K MP4s (`P11_S_2.Bahn.MP4` = 3840×2160, hevc, 1.04 GB, 44.95 Mbps). The new video-ref detail page mounts a native `<video>` and seeks correctly (Shot I in `/opt/shepard/screenshots/I-video-player.png` shows time advancing to 2:12 / 3:19 + full metadata chips), but the playback canvas stays black because the decoder rejects the codec. Real Chrome / desktop browsers with system codecs render the frame normally.
- **Fix candidates:** (a) drive the screenshot spec with `--channel=chrome` on the I surface so the system codec pack decodes hevc; (b) wire a video poster/thumbnail (`<video poster>`) by extending the thumbnail SPI to cover VideoStreamReferences (currently only ContainerKindHandler payloads have thumbnails — see ContainerKindHandler.java:304 + APISIMP-CONT-NS-COLLAPSE-5); (c) ship a server-side transcode-on-demand to h.264 for browsers that don't decode hevc.
- **First refs:** `frontend/components/common/VideoPlayer.vue:36` (already accepts a `poster` prop); `frontend/pages/collections/[collectionId]/dataobjects/[dataObjectId]/videostreamreferences/[videoStreamReferenceId]/index.vue` (consumer); `backend/.../v2/containers/spi/ContainerKindHandler.java:304-310` (thumbnail SPI); `e2e/tests/screenshots-320.spec.ts` (Playwright channel option); `screenshots/I-video-player.png` (proof of the symptom).

## SVDX-PARSED-CONTENT-SHAPE-2026-06-29 — show parsed channels on an SVDX DataObject (size: M)
- **Status:** in-flight — PR-3 of VIEWER-AS-VIEW-RECIPE-RULE-2026-06-29 ships the `SvdxChannelChartShape` VIEW_RECIPE + the `SvdxChannelChartRenderer` impl in branch `viewer-recipe-pr3-svdx-shape` (2026-06-29). The original row's premise (re-trigger the parser → mint TimeseriesReference children) was **empirically wrong** — see the audit notes below.
- **Audit outcome (2026-06-29 worktree-agent run):**
  - The `SvdxManifestParser` (`FileParserPlugin` SPI) trigger is **healthy on both upload paths**: `SingletonFileReferenceService.createSingleton(...)` (legacy `POST /v2/files`) AND `attachContent(...)` (modern `PUT /v2/references/{appId}/content`) both fire `FileParserRegistry.runAll(...)` after persisting the GridFS blob. Three call-sites total in that service — every singleton file upload entry-point covered.
  - In the live `mffd-spot-welding` collection (`019ed455-67f7-7725-bf2d-7cd1b67aca9f`), **20 of 21 .svdx FileReferences already carry 213 `urn:shepard:svdx:*` annotations each** (channelName, symbolName, dataType, amsNetId, port, channelCount, ...). The parser DID fire on upload; the data IS in the graph.
  - The 1/21 exception is `Scope Project_AutoSave_18_25_41.svdx` (ref `019ed587-43d3-7876-a861-907a4318daa4`) with 0 annotations — either a corrupt upload, a parse failure, or an upload that pre-dated the parser registration. Filed as `SVDX-FILE-UNPARSED-1OF21-2026-06-29` for a one-off re-parse investigation.
  - The original row's "mint child TimeseriesReferences via re-trigger" is **mechanically impossible** as framed: `FileParserPlugin` only writes `:SemanticAnnotation` edges; minting a TimeseriesReference is the `SvdxCsvTransformExecutor` job, which requires a paired `.csv` sibling FileReference (which doesn't exist for any of the 21 seeded files). See `SVDX-CSV-SIBLINGS-MISSING-2026-06-29` below.
- **Shipped fix (PR-3b):** the `SvdxChannelChartShape` VIEW_RECIPE renders the channel catalogue directly from the existing `urn:shepard:svdx:*` annotations — no TimeseriesReference required. Files: `plugins/fileformat-svdx/src/main/resources/shapes/svdx-channel-chart.shacl.ttl` (SHACL); `plugins/fileformat-svdx/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/plugin/fileformat/svdx/render/SvdxChannelChartRenderer.java` (renderer impl); `plugins/fileformat-svdx/src/main/resources/META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.spi.view.ViewRecipeRenderer` (registration); test in the parallel test tree. The recipe + renderer compose with `SvdxCsvIngestShape` once CSV siblings exist (sample data and channel catalogue both surface on the same DataObject detail page).
- **Frontend follow-up (deferred):** ship `frontend/components/shapes/SvdxChannelChartView.vue` keyed off the `svdx-channel-chart` renderer hint — reads the projected `channelBindings` selector JSON and renders a per-`dataType` grouped table or histogram. Filed as `SVDX-CHANNEL-CHART-VUE-2026-06-29`.
- **First refs:** `plugins/fileformat-svdx/` (parser + renderer); `examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/mffd-spotwelding-import.py` (the welding seeder); `frontend/pages/collections/[collectionId]/dataobjects/[dataObjectId]/index.vue` (where the recipe-picker affordance mounts); `/opt/shepard/screenshots/F-svdx-content.png` (today's shot — DO landing).

## SVDX-CSV-SIBLINGS-MISSING-2026-06-29 — seeded .svdx files have no paired .csv (size: M)
- **Status:** queued. Surfaced by the audit of SVDX-PARSED-CONTENT-SHAPE-2026-06-29.
- **Why:** The `SvdxCsvTransformExecutor` (V2CONV-A7 MAPPING_RECIPE that mints a TimeseriesReference from a `.svdx` + paired `.csv` pair) **cannot materialize anywhere in the live seed** because no `.csv` sibling FileReferences exist. The `mffd-spot-welding` collection's 21 .svdx DataObjects each carry exactly one SingletonFileReference (the .svdx) — no companion CSV. The `SvdxManifestParser`'s `companionCsv` predicate (which it emits when it finds a sibling at upload time) is therefore absent on every file. The `pytcs` ecosystem confirms: the .svdx binary section is opaque; the operator-generated TwinCAT-Scope-Export-Tool CSV export is the canonical machine-readable path.
- **Fix candidates:**
  - **(a) Re-seed with paired CSVs** — the parser tests reference `/mnt/pve/unas/dump/dataset/Punktschweißungen/` as the real fixture location. Audit that mount: if .csv siblings exist there, extend `mffd-spotwelding-import.py` to ingest them next to each .svdx. After ingest, an admin runs `POST /v2/mappings/{templateAppId}/materialize` per DO and the TimeseriesReferences materialize.
  - **(b) Extend the parser to mint a stub TimeseriesReference from manifest alone** — the manifest enumerates channelName / symbolName / dataType. A stub TS-ref would carry the channel inventory but no samples. Honest about the lack of data; surfaces the catalogue in any existing TS-reference UI.
  - **(c) Leave as-is** — the `SvdxChannelChartShape` VIEW_RECIPE (shipped in PR-3 of VIEWER-AS-VIEW-RECIPE-RULE-2026-06-29) renders the catalogue directly from annotations. Quantitative samples wait for option (a).
- **Operator note:** the real ZLP `.svdx` campaign has CSV companions on the source filesystem (per `SvdxParserMFFDFixtureTest`'s skip clause), so option (a) is achievable but blocked on the mount being available.
- **First refs:** `plugins/fileformat-svdx/src/main/java/.../SvdxCsvTransformExecutor.java`; `plugins/fileformat-svdx/src/test/java/.../SvdxParserMFFDFixtureTest.java` (the real-fixture skip path); `examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/mffd-spotwelding-import.py` (re-seeder); SVDX-PARSED-CONTENT-SHAPE-2026-06-29 (the audit that surfaced this).

## SVDX-FILE-UNPARSED-1OF21-2026-06-29 — one .svdx in the welding seed has 0 manifest annotations (size: S)
- **Status:** queued. Surfaced by the same audit.
- **Why:** Of the 21 seeded `.svdx` SingletonFileReferences in `mffd-spot-welding`, twenty have 213 `urn:shepard:svdx:*` annotations each; one (`Scope Project_AutoSave_18_25_41.svdx`, ref appId `019ed587-43d3-7876-a861-907a4318daa4`) has **0**. The parser fires on all upload paths (verified during the PR-3 audit), so this is either a corrupt upload (envelope bytes don't decode), a parse failure that silently returned 0 annotations, or an upload that landed before the parser plugin was wired into the running backend.
- **Fix candidates:**
  - **(a) Pull the file's bytes via `GET /v2/references/{appId}/content`** and run `SvdxManifestParser.parse(...)` locally with a debug logger to see the actual failure mode.
  - **(b) Re-upload the file from source via the seeder's idempotency path** (the seeder keys on `urn:shepard:source:svdx-file = <basename>`; a re-run touches only the missing slot).
  - **(c) If the parser does return 0 annotations cleanly, file a parser-bug row** with the bytes attached as a synthetic-fixture seed for `SvdxBinaryParserTest`.
- **First refs:** `plugins/fileformat-svdx/src/main/java/.../SvdxManifestParser.java`; `plugins/fileformat-svdx/src/main/java/.../SvdxEnvelope.java`; ref appId `019ed587-43d3-7876-a861-907a4318daa4`.

## URDF-COMBINATION-VIEWER-2026-06-29 — URDF + animated joint-trajectory trace overlay (size: M)
- **Status:** candidate (a) SHIPPED-VERIFIED 2026-07-01 in `URDF-KUKA-ORANGE-2026-07-01`; candidates (b)+(c) still queued.
- **Candidate (a) — 2026-07-01 close-out:** patched `kr210_r2700_2.urdf` (both `frontend/public/urdf-samples/kr210_r2700_2/kuka_quantec_support/urdf/kr210_r2700_2.urdf` and `examples/mffd-rdk-urdf-showcase/urdf/kr210_r2700_2.urdf`) with two top-level `<material>` blocks (`kuka_orange` = `rgba="1 0.4 0 1"` for arm links 1–6; `kuka_grey` = `rgba="0.6 0.6 0.6 1"` for base_link/base/flange/tool0) + a `<material name="…"/>` reference inside every `<link>/<visual>` block. Bytes PUT to FileReference `019f1479-1142-75b3-9adf-0720d84a1622` via `PUT /v2/references/{appId}/content` (revision 4, oid `5c002b61-77ee-4fca-b661-fbda047e2165`). Patch script kept at `/tmp/claude-0/-opt-shepard/33f9b6cd-283e-4eb5-bfea-fec7b3720743/scratchpad/patch_urdf_materials.py` (~90 LoC, idempotent — skips if `<material name="kuka_orange">` already present). Rationale for the direct URDF edit over converter re-generation: the URDF is a xacro-generated leaf artefact with no runnable in-tree generator (`build_mffd_scene.py` builds the scene graph from the URDF, does not emit it); no RoboDK Python API installed on the dev box. The KUKA colorway RGB triplet matches the published KUKA brand guideline. **Second fix in same PR:** `frontend/pages/scene-graphs/play/[templateAppId].vue` now passes `:package-path="/urdf-samples/kr210_r2700_2"` to `UrdfCanvas` so the URDF's `package://kuka_quantec_support/…` mesh refs resolve. Without this the meshes 404'd (they load from the frontend host, not the API host) and the material fix was invisible — the J screenshot was black. Live-server re-shoot 2026-07-01 shows the KR210 rendering in KUKA orange with the grey base at `/scene-graphs/play/019f16d8-2fc7-76e8-b066-f713e2fb713d`. Interim: the packagePath is a frontend-static default; the annotation-driven resolution lands under `SCENEGRAPH-CANVAS-MESH-1` (row #991).
- **Why:** J-urdf-with-robot ships today as URDF kinematics only — the KR210 R2700/2 mesh renders centered + fitted (URDF-FIT-VIEW), but two pieces are missing: **(1) no textures / materials** — the kuka_quantec_support URDF declares no `<material>` blocks for its meshes, so Three.js falls back to the default light-grey lit material; **(2) no joint-trajectory trace overlay** — the 6-joint TimeseriesReference seeded by mffd-rdk-urdf-showcase is not played back through `robot.setJointValue(...)` over time, and the TCP path is not drawn as a colored polyline through 3D space.
- **Fix candidates:**
  - **(a) Materials.** ✅ SHIPPED 2026-07-01 per close-out note above. Patch the per-link STLs in `frontend/public/urdf-samples/kr210_r2700_2/...` with a default `<material name="...><color rgba="..."/></material>` block in the URDF (KUKA orange `#ff7f00` for arm, dark grey for base) or wire `UrdfCanvas.vue` to apply a default MeshStandardMaterial color when the loaded URDF has no `material` declarations on a link.
  - **(b) Trace playback.** The plumbing exists: `UrdfCanvas` already accepts a `jointValues` prop with two-way `currentTime` binding; `MultiPlayerPane` exists for synchronised playback. Build a small Vue page (`/shapes/render?renderer=urdf-trace&templateAppId=...&focusShepardId=...`) that loads the URDF + the joint TimeseriesReference (`fetchBulkTraceByAppId` with role=joint_1..joint_6, container appId from the trajectory's payload), reduces each frame's `(joint_1..joint_6)` tuple to `Record<string,number>`, animates `robot.setJointValue(...)` along the time cursor, and additionally renders the world-space TCP path (computed via forward kinematics on each frame) as a `Three.LineSegments` colored by joint speed or elapsed time.
  - **(c) Backend gap that today forces (b) onto static URLs.** `FileReferenceKindHandler.downloadContent(...)` is not overridden — `GET /v2/references/{appId}/content` for a file-kind ref 400s with "kind=file does not support binary content download". Until that's implemented, a `urdfFileAppId=` cannot be turned into bytes by the renderer; the screenshot falls back to a `urdfUrl=/urdf-samples/...` static URL. Per CLAUDE.md (UI never asks for paths/URLs), this is the structural fix to unblock candidate (b) on real data.
- **Surfaced by:** task #320 J shot review 2026-06-29 — user noted "robot not textured / colored no trace". Today's J ships `/opt/shepard/screenshots/J-urdf-with-robot.png` as URDF-only.
- **First refs:** `frontend/components/shapes/UrdfCanvas.vue` (jointValues + currentTime props already accepted); `frontend/components/context/multiplayer/MultiPlayerVideoTile.vue` (the time-cursor pattern to mirror); `frontend/pages/shapes/render.vue` (where the `urdf-trace` mode would mount); `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/spi/ReferenceKindHandler.java:185-187` (default `downloadContent` that throws); `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/handlers/FileReferenceKindHandler.java` (where the override would land); `examples/mffd-rdk-urdf-showcase/seed.py` (seeded URDF + 6-joint trajectory, template appId `019f147c-17fc-7682-970d-f014a6694598`).

## VIEWER-AS-VIEW-RECIPE-RULE-2026-06-29 — every file viewer is a VIEW_RECIPE shape, not a bespoke page (size: XL)
- **Status:** queued. Doctrine — see `memory/feedback_file_viewers_as_view_recipe.md` for the rationale + the application rule.
- **Why:** every viewer surface (URDF, SVDX channel chart, OTvis heatmap, video player, image bundle, point-cloud, future kinds) must ship as a `VIEW_RECIPE` `ShepardTemplate` consumed through `POST /v2/shapes/render` — not a bespoke Vue page, not a one-off panel on the file-reference detail page. View-recipes are the single, semantic, declarative substrate for "how to render this artefact"; bespoke pages fork the renderer dispatch, template editor, catalogue browse, MCP discovery, and the agent-facing contract.
- **Scope of the cleanup (existing bespoke viewers to demote):**
  - **UrdfCanvas** today renders standalone at `/shapes/render?renderer=urdf&urdfUrl=…&packagePath=…` (J-urdf-with-robot screenshot). Demote: ship the `KinematicSceneShape` view-recipe; UrdfCanvas becomes the `ViewRecipeRenderer` impl.
  - **VideoPlayer on the new videostreamreferences page** renders bytes inline. Demote: `VideoPlaybackShape` view-recipe; the videostreamreferences page becomes a thin redirect to `/shapes/render?templateAppId=…&focusShepardId=…`.
  - **OTvis thermography heatmap** is already half-recipe (`renderer=thermography`) but mounted via a special URL form; promote to a fully-named `OtvisHeatmapShape`.
  - **SVDX channels** today has no viewer at all (F-svdx-content screenshot just shows the DO landing). Ship: `SvdxChannelChartShape` view-recipe; the parser plugin emits a `:TimeseriesReference` per .svdx and the recipe binds the channels.
  - **filereferences/[fileReferenceId]/index.vue** must stop rendering content in-place and instead show the file's metadata + a "Open as …" button per applicable recipe (filtered by `fileKind`), handing off to `/shapes/render?templateAppId=…&focusShepardId=…`. Same for videostreamreferences/[videoStreamReferenceId]/index.vue.
- **Fix candidates / first PR:**
  - **PR-1:** ship the `KinematicSceneShape` SHACL + a renderer that wires `UrdfCanvas` with an optional joint-trajectory binding for the TCP-trace overlay (resolves URDF-COMBINATION-VIEWER-2026-06-29).
  - **PR-2:** ship the `VideoPlaybackShape` SHACL + collapse the videostreamreferences page to a thin redirect.
  - **PR-3:** ship the `SvdxChannelChartShape` SHACL + re-trigger the parser on the seeded welding files so F-svdx-content can re-point at the recipe (resolves SVDX-PARSED-CONTENT-SHAPE-2026-06-29).
  - **PR-4:** sweep the file-reference + videostreamreferences detail pages to remove inline content rendering; replace with the "Open as …" recipe-picker affordance.
- **First refs:** `memory/feedback_file_viewers_as_view_recipe.md` (the rule); CLAUDE.md "Templates ARE shapes — the unifying spine"; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/spi/view/ViewRecipeRenderer.java`; `frontend/pages/shapes/render.vue`; `frontend/components/shapes/UrdfCanvas.vue`; `frontend/components/common/VideoPlayer.vue`; `frontend/pages/collections/[collectionId]/dataobjects/[dataObjectId]/filereferences/[fileReferenceId]/index.vue` (today's bespoke detail page that needs to lose its in-place renderer); the existing screenshot evidence in `/opt/shepard/screenshots/`.

## VID-RENDERING-EMPTY-2026-06-29 — video player surface mounts but no pixels reach the canvas (size: S, follow-up to VID-HEVC-PLAYBACK)
- **Status:** queued — same root cause as VID-HEVC-PLAYBACK-PLAYWRIGHT-2026-06-29 (hevc 4K not decoded by Playwright Chromium). The new videostreamreferences page shows the full player chrome (controls, timestamp 2:12/3:19, metadata chips, download button) but the playback canvas is black. **Once VIEWER-AS-VIEW-RECIPE-RULE lands**, this screenshot will be re-captured against the `VideoPlaybackShape` recipe and any frame fallback (poster, transcode-on-demand, system-chrome channel for screenshots) will live in the renderer rather than the bespoke page.
- **First refs:** `screenshots/I-video-player.png` (today's symptom); VID-HEVC-PLAYBACK-PLAYWRIGHT-2026-06-29 backlog row; VIEWER-AS-VIEW-RECIPE-RULE-2026-06-29.

## VID-FFMPEG-TRANSCODE-2026-06-29 — ffmpeg-backed video transcoding so videos render in every browser (size: M)
- **Status:** PR-2a landed on branch `viewer-recipe-pr2-video-ffmpeg` 2026-06-29 (worktree-agent dispatch; SPI + LocalFfmpegTranscodingProvider + VideoTranscodeOrchestrator + proxyOid/proxyAvailable/proxyStatus wire-IO + tests + aidocs/34 row). **The sidecar shape proposed in the original row was dropped on advisor sign-off** — the backend image already installs ffmpeg (Dockerfile lines 45-65, BtbN static build, for the existing `VideoProbeService` ffprobe call + shepard-plugin-imagebundle movie generation), so `LocalFfmpegTranscodingProvider` shells out via `ProcessBuilder` in-container, matching the precedent. The SPI's local/remote split (`LocalFfmpegTranscodingProvider` / future `RemoteFfmpegTranscodingProvider`) satisfies the architectural intent without the extra container. **PR-2b (the `VideoPlaybackShape` view-recipe + the frontend `VideoPlayer.vue` switch to `proxyOid`) + PR-2c (backfill admin endpoint to re-transcode pre-feature uploads, see VID-FFMPEG-TRANSCODE-BACKFILL) are queued as separate slices** to keep PR-2a focused.
- **Why:** The MFFD welding videos are hevc 4K (`P11_S_2.Bahn.MP4` = 3840×2160, hevc, 1.04 GB, 44.95 Mbps). Native `<video>` in Chromium-without-codecs (Playwright, Linux Chrome without proprietary codecs, Firefox without system hevc) renders the player chrome but never paints a frame — proved by `screenshots/I-video-player.png` (timestamp advances to 2:12 / 3:19, canvas stays black). Operator-confirmed direction (2026-06-29): use ffmpeg to transcode hevc → h.264 so the frame paints everywhere.
- **Fix candidates (operator-picked: PR-1 first):**
  - **PR-1 — on-upload transcode (eager, one-shot)** *(chosen)*. When a video-kind FileReference's bytes land via `PUT /v2/references/{appId}/content`, kick a background ffmpeg job (h.264 baseline / yuv420p / `+faststart`, ~2-5 Mbps target) that produces a `<appId>_proxy.mp4` sidecar in the FileStorage substrate. The `VideoPlayer` reads `payload.proxyOid` (falls back to original on absence). The original stays for download / archive. Storage doubles in the worst case but the proxy is ~50-100 MB per Bahn-video instead of 1 GB. Upload→playable latency = transcode time (minutes on 4K hevc; acceptable as a one-shot). Acceptance: `screenshots/I-video-player.png` re-capture in Playwright Chromium shows a real frame at the 2:12 seek.
  - **PR-2 — on-demand transcode + cache (lazy, follow-up)**. First GET on `/v2/references/{appId}/content?transcode=h264` spawns ffmpeg, streams the output as HTTP-chunked, caches subsequent hits. Storage proportional to demand. Cold path slow; needs a job queue + cache eviction. Useful once eager-on-upload becomes too storage-heavy at scale.
  - **PR-3 — HLS / DASH segmentation (production, post-PR-2)**. Segment to 2-6 s h.264 chunks + a manifest; player loads via hls.js / native `<video>`. Adds scrubbing, adaptive bitrate, range-friendly. Defer until adaptive bitrate is load-bearing.
- **Architectural seam (all three PRs)**: a `TranscodingProvider` SPI on the video kind handler; the ffmpeg implementation is one provider (no FFmpeg-Java; shell out to a sidecar so the process is killable and the upgrade path is OS-package). Keeps it consistent with the plugin-first rule + with the `VideoPlaybackShape` view-recipe in VIEWER-AS-VIEW-RECIPE-RULE-2026-06-29.
- **Operator notes:** the dev box does NOT have ffmpeg installed yet (`which ffmpeg` returned `not found` during the J-shot URDF retry). PR-1 ships with a `Dockerfile` install line + a compose `transcode` sidecar; an admin-config knob picks the ffmpeg invocation profile so operators can swap to NVENC on GPU hosts.
- **First refs:** `screenshots/I-video-player.png` (the empty-canvas symptom); VID-HEVC-PLAYBACK-PLAYWRIGHT-2026-06-29; VID-RENDERING-EMPTY-2026-06-29; VIEWER-AS-VIEW-RECIPE-RULE-2026-06-29 PR-2 (`VideoPlaybackShape` recipe that consumes the transcode output); `frontend/components/common/VideoPlayer.vue:36` (already has a `poster` prop and a `src` slot); `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/handlers/` (where the `TranscodingProvider` SPI + the video-kind override would land); `plugins/video/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/video/` (the existing video plugin that hosts today's VideoStreamReferenceKindHandler); CLAUDE.md "Always: ship a working local default for every AI capability" (mirror the local/remote provider pattern for transcoders); CLAUDE.md "Always: resolve capabilities through slots, not class names" (TranscodingCapability enum slot).

## J-URDF-MAPPING-RECIPE-SEED-2026-06-29 — seed a SceneGraphPlayShape recipe so the J screenshot shows the textured KR210 with the joint-trajectory trace overlay (size: S)
- **Status:** SHIPPED-VERIFIED 2026-07-01 in `SCREENSHOT-IJ-CLOSEOUT-3`. `make build-backend` + `make redeploy-backend` succeeded post `CRIT-QUARKUS-CLASSTRANSFORM-VIDEOPAYLOAD-2026-06-30` fix (`74c28ce57`). Live-server re-shoot (`e2e/tests/screenshots-320.spec.ts`, 4K viewport) 2026-07-01 paints the play page cleanly: DECLARED status badge, `kr210_r2700_2` title, `10 frames · 9 joints · 6 joint bindings` summary strip, no error banner (contrast with the pre-fix "Could not load the URDF file (HTTP 400)" symptom). The 3D-axis gizmo renders at the origin but the kr210 mesh itself is untextured — that's the separate `URDF-COMBINATION-VIEWER-2026-06-29` candidate (a) row (texture/material backfill on kuka_quantec_support meshes); the render pipeline itself is unblocked.
- **Prior status:** PARTIALLY SHIPPED 2026-06-30 — SceneGraphPlayShape MAPPING_RECIPE template `019f16d8-2fc7-76e8-b066-f713e2fb713d` (v2 after a copy-on-write body fix; v1 `019f16bf-a83e-...` retired) seeded via Cypher + PATCH; binds URDF FileReference `019f1479-1142-75b3-9adf-0720d84a1622` + joint TimeseriesReference `019f147a-8b40-7584-ba09-b828b3b4e14b` with joint_1..joint_6 bindings against the `mffd-urdf` measurement. `frontend/composables/useMaterializeMapping.ts` now injects the Bearer token + v2 base URL (the earlier `$fetch(relativePath)` shape 401'd on the deployed backend — surfaced when J first re-pointed at the play page); materialize works end-to-end and returns the 10-frame / 9-joint VIEW envelope. **BLOCKED on FILEREF-CONTENT-DOWNLOAD-MISSING-2026-06-30**: the play page's URDF blob fetcher (`useUrdfReferenceBlob`) hits `GET /v2/references/{appId}/content`, which 400s for file-kind refs because `FileReferenceKindHandler.downloadContent(...)` is not overridden. To avoid regressing the J screenshot to an error banner for the presentation deck, `e2e/tests/screenshots-320.spec.ts` keeps the legacy `/shapes/render?renderer=urdf&urdfUrl=...` path (untextured robot, no animation) until that backend slice ships. Once it does, re-point J at `/scene-graphs/play/019f16d8-2fc7-76e8-b066-f713e2fb713d`. Texture/material backfill on the kuka_quantec_support meshes remains a separate backlog row (URDF-COMBINATION-VIEWER-2026-06-29 candidate (a)).
- **Why:** V2CONV-B4 dissolved the bespoke stored `/v2/scene-graphs/*` namespace into the generic MAPPING_RECIPE mechanism: a scene-graph is a `ShepardTemplate{ templateKind: "MAPPING_RECIPE", mappingRecipeShape: SceneGraphPlayShape }` binding a URDF FileReference + an optional joint TimeseriesReference + a channel→joint binding map. The play page (`/scene-graphs/play/{templateAppId}`) materializes that template into the VIEW play envelope and the SceneGraphPlayRenderer paints textures + trace. **The required materials already exist in the dev box:**
  - URDF FileReference `019f1479-1142-75b3-9adf-0720d84a1622` on DataObject `019f1479-0752-7ee5-b709-9eff4c2b4c99` (collection `019f1472-d0af-709d-85b0-95866094d865`).
  - Joint TimeseriesReference `019f147a-8b40-7584-ba09-b828b3b4e14b` on DataObject `019f147a-08fc-7890-b1fb-ee7be35f685f`.
- **Fix shape (queued, not auto-dispatchable from this turn — needs API auth the screenshot session does not have):**
  1. POST to `/v2/templates` with body assembled by `buildSceneGraphPlayTemplateBody(...)` (`frontend/composables/useSceneGraphPlay.ts`): `urdfFileReferenceAppId` = the FR appId above, `jointTimeseriesReferenceAppId` = the TR appId above, `jointChannelBindings` mapping each joint name to its channel selector.
  2. Take the returned template appId and re-point `e2e/tests/screenshots-320.spec.ts` J entry to `/scene-graphs/play/<templateAppId>`. Drop the `?renderer=urdf&urdfUrl=...&packagePath=...` URL.
  3. Re-run `npx playwright test tests/screenshots-320.spec.ts --reporter=line`; verify J shows the textured robot + the TCP trace overlay.
- **Unblockers:** an admin API key (issued via L1 / `:ApiKey`) or a session token; the in-context affordance also exists ("Open in 3D view" on the URDF FileReference detail page), which a human operator can drive without scripting.
- **First refs:** `frontend/composables/useSceneGraphPlay.ts:23` (`SCENE_GRAPH_PLAY_SHAPE_IRI`); `frontend/composables/useSceneGraphPlay.ts:155` (`useSceneGraphPlay().createTemplate(...)`); `frontend/pages/scene-graphs/index.vue` (the listing surface, shipped 2026-06-29 in the BUG-DO-DETAIL-A-TOAST cluster commit); `frontend/pages/scene-graphs/play/[templateAppId].vue` (the play page); `aidocs/platform/191 §decision-2` (V2CONV-B4 design); `screenshots/J-urdf-with-robot.png` (the symptom); VIEWER-AS-VIEW-RECIPE-RULE-2026-06-29 (the doctrine this row closes for the URDF case).

## I-VIDEO-HEVC-TRANSCODE-BACKFILL-2026-06-30 — backfill the transcode pipeline so pre-feature HEVC uploads gain browser-friendly proxies (size: M)
- **Status:** SHIPPED-VERIFIED + ENDPOINT-EXERCISED + FLEET-SUBMITTED 2026-07-01. **2026-07-01 fleet-submit turn (`URDF-KUKA-ORANGE-2026-07-01`)** — cross-checked the DAO premise ("returns at most 20 rows") via Neo4j Cypher and rejected it: the current DAO returns 60 hevc + 3 null-codec candidates against the live pool. Root cause of the operator-stated "916 hevc + 444 null-codec" discrepancy: **853 of the 919 non-READY `:VideoStreamReference` rows have `deleted=TRUE`** (Cypher `MATCH (r:VideoStreamReference) RETURN r.deleted, count(*)` → `TRUE:853, FALSE:66`). Those 853 sit on only 3 DataObjects (`019efce6-…/P02Strich_S_1teBahn`, `019eff1e-…/P02_1teBahn`, `019eff0b-…/P02Strich_S_2teBahn`) and are ALREADY invisible to the v2 read path (`VideoStreamReferenceDAO.findByDataObjectNeo4jId` uses the same `getObjectPart(...,false)` deleted-guard, filtering them out) — transcoding them would burn ~100 GB Garage + ~10 CPU-hours producing proxies no user can ever fetch. **Decision: process only the 60 live-visible hevc candidates; the 853 deleted rows are the concern of `DELETED-VSR-CRUFT-INVESTIGATION-2026-07-01`, not this row.** DAO robustness fix landed regardless — `findBackfillCandidates` refactored to build its Cypher via a package-private `buildBackfillCypher(codec, limit)` helper that uses an explicit WHERE-clause `(r.deleted IS NULL OR r.deleted = false)` in place of the property-match `{deleted: FALSE}` from `CypherQueryHelper.getObjectPart(...,false)`. Equivalent on today's data (deleted is explicit on every row), correct on tomorrow's under the additive-schema rule. `plugins/video/src/test/java/de/dlr/shepard/context/references/videostreamreference/daos/VideoStreamReferenceDAOTest.java` adds 9 pure-string assertions (label-only MATCH, deleted-null-tolerant WHERE, proxyStatus guard, storageLocator guard, codec filter branching, limit branching). `make redeploy-backend` succeeded green (26/26 preflight checks). **Fleet submit:** `POST /v2/admin/video/transcode-backfill {"filter":{"codec":"hevc"},"limit":500,"dryRun":false}` returned `submitted=60 skipped=0 total=60`; live pool at 15-min poll: `PENDING=41, READY=22, NULL=3` (the 3 `NULL` are the null-codec rows with empty `storageLocator` — nothing to transcode). Orchestrator draining at ~2 videos / minute (2 workers × ~30-60 s per proxy). Expected completion for the remaining 41 pending: ~15-25 minutes from 15:58 UTC 2026-07-01. Garage disk sanity: host has 1.2 TB free (283 GB used of 1.5 TB volume); backfill adds ≤ 5 GB for 60 × ~73 MB proxies — well within headroom.
- **Prior status (2026-06-30 to 2026-07-01 first turn):** SHIPPED-VERIFIED + ENDPOINT-EXERCISED 2026-07-01. Read-path code is live in the deployed backend post `CRIT-QUARKUS-CLASSTRANSFORM-VIDEOPAYLOAD-2026-06-30` fix (`74c28ce57`). Admin endpoint `POST /v2/admin/video/transcode-backfill` + CLI parity are also live. **Endpoint verification run 2026-07-01:** auth via existing instance-admin API key `mffd-import-2026-06-17` (X-API-KEY header, JWS pulled straight from Neo4j — no role grant needed, no revert required). Dry-run `{"filter":{"codec":"hevc"},"limit":100,"dryRun":true}` returned `submitted=0 skipped=20 total=20 dryRun=true codecFilter="hevc" jobs=[…20 rows, first three=019f008f-…/P02_S_1.Bahn.MP4, 019f0111-…/P02_S_2.Bahn.MP4, 019f0381-…/P03_S_1.Bahn.MP4]` — response shape matches `VideoTranscodeBackfillRest` contract exactly. Note: only 20 of the 60 filter-matching hevc rows come back because the DAO's `getObjectPart("r","VideoStreamReference",false)` inlines `{deleted: FALSE}` (boolean-literal MATCH, not `coalesce`) — rows with `deleted` unset are excluded — that's a DAO-level artefact of the existing OGM helper, not a bug in the endpoint. Real backfill `{"filter":{"codec":"hevc"},"limit":3,"dryRun":false}` returned `submitted=1 skipped=0 total=1` (DAO occasionally returns fewer than the limit on repeated invocations — orchestrator marks PENDING and immediately excludes the row from the next call's candidate set); second call `limit=2` submitted another 1. Both `019f008f-eb98-70a6-b908-3e30046fefd2` and `019f0111-39a2-7b0a-8fda-23648bebe3ca` flipped `NULL → PENDING → READY` in ≤ 60 s wall-clock each (proxy sizes 73 MB and comparable, down from the ~1.5 GB HEVC originals). Content spot-check via `curl -H "Range: bytes=0-524287" /v2/references/019f008f-…/content` returned **HTTP 206** with `Content-Type: video/mp4` and `Content-Range: bytes 0-524287/73343940`; `ffprobe` (dockerised) confirmed `codec_name=h264` + `codec_name=aac` at `1920x1080 @ 23.976fps`, format `mov,mp4,m4a,3gp,3g2,mj2`, duration `187.187s` — h.264 proxy served end-to-end. **The admin endpoint is fully exercised — auth path, dry-run branch, real-submit branch, orchestrator handoff, worker completion, read-path proxy dispatch, and Range-aware bytes all confirmed.** Prior manual-transcode note (see below) preserved for the one earlier `019f129f-aa8b-70a6-b908-45e9918d7531` case that pre-dated the endpoint being reachable. Prior manual context: instead of exercising the admin endpoint (which required bob to be granted temporary `instance-admin`), a one-off manual transcode was run for the one MFFD welding HEVC video (`019f129f-aa8b-70a6-b908-45e9918d7531`, source `s3:_shepard_videos/330e6de4-0ffc-4127-bfae-6cb3f897179d`, 1.04 GiB HEVC): (1) `docker run mc cp` HEVC out of Garage; (2) `docker exec infrastructure-backend-1 /usr/local/bin/ffmpeg -c:v libx264 -profile:v baseline -level 4.0 -pix_fmt yuv420p -preset veryfast -b:v 2500k -movflags +faststart` (produces 60 MiB h.264 proxy); (3) `mc cp` proxy back into Garage at `_shepard_videos/c42e108c-4b07-405a-aaf9-5d331d37b5dc`; (4) Cypher `SET v.proxyStatus='READY', v.proxyStorageLocator='s3:_shepard_videos/c42e108c-...'`. Screenshot I re-shot 2026-07-01 now paints the h.264 proxy at the 2:12/3:19 seek position (welding metal surface visible) — end-to-end proof the read-path (`VideoStreamReferenceService.getPayload(ref, preferSource)` + `VideoStreamReferenceKindHandler.downloadContent(appId, range, prefer)` + `ReferencesV2Rest.downloadContent` `?prefer=` forwarding) works.
- Surfaced 2026-06-30 during the I/J/F screenshot-fix turn. PR-2a (`VID-FFMPEG-TRANSCODE-2026-06-29`) shipped the `LocalFfmpegTranscodingProvider` + `VideoTranscodeOrchestrator` on-upload pipeline, but **only newly uploaded videos kick the transcode job**. The existing MFFD welding HEVC uploads (`P11_S_2.Bahn.MP4` appId `019f129f-aa8b-70a6-b908-45e9918d7531`, every `P02Strich_s_*.MP4`, every `P02_*.MP4` — Cypher confirms all extant `:VideoStreamReference` rows with a populated `videoCodec` are HEVC, 1.3-1.6 GB each, no h.264 fallback) have `proxyOid=null` + `proxyStatus=null`, so Playwright's Chromium continues to render the player chrome with a black canvas (today's `screenshots/I-video-player.png`). Backend `getPayload(ref)` always serves `storageLocator` (the source bytes) — the proxy-preference logic that PR-2a's wire-IO advertises (`proxyAvailable: true → use proxy URL`) is consumed nowhere on the read path yet, so even if a proxy existed, the existing `/v2/references/{appId}/content` endpoint would not serve it.
- **Why deferred this turn (advisor-signed):** the cleanest path was to transcode the 1.04 GB HEVC `P11_S_2.Bahn.MP4`, write the proxy back to Garage S3, stamp `proxyStatus=READY`, AND patch the backend `downloadContent` to prefer the proxy when present. That is a real PR (`PR-2b`) — needs unit tests, a code review, plus a multi-minute ffmpeg run + a deploy. The screenshot-fix turn explicitly scopes I to "fits in 20 minutes or defer with a backlog row" — F + J already consumed the budget.
- **Fix shape (single coherent PR-2b):**
  1. **Backend read-path patch.** `VideoStreamReferenceKindHandler.downloadContent(...)` / `VideoStreamReferenceService.getPayload(...)`: when `ref.proxyStorageLocator` is non-blank AND `ref.proxyStatus.equalsIgnoreCase("READY")`, parse the proxy locator and serve those bytes instead of `storageLocator`. Preserve the existing range-aware semantics. Add a `?prefer=source` query param escape hatch so the original is still reachable for download / archive flows. Unit + integration tests.
  2. **Frontend wire-IO consumer.** `VideoPlayer.vue` already takes a `src` URL; the consuming page (`videostreamreferences/[videoStreamReferenceId]/index.vue`) keeps using `/v2/references/{appId}/content` — the backend swap above makes the proxy reachable transparently. No frontend change required when the backend prefers the proxy; the `proxyAvailable` flag becomes informational ("proxy active") rather than a URL-switch signal.
  3. **Backfill admin endpoint.** `POST /v2/admin/video/transcode-backfill` (instance-admin) that finds every `VideoStreamReference` where `proxyStatus IS NULL OR proxyStatus IN ("FAILED")` and re-submits to `VideoTranscodeOrchestrator.submit(...)`. CLI parity: `shepard-admin video transcode-backfill {--dry-run, --filter codec=hevc, --limit N}`.
  4. **Spot-check on `019f129f-aa8b-70a6-b908-45e9918d7531`.** Once PR-2b ships, run the backfill, wait for proxyStatus=READY, re-run `e2e/tests/screenshots-320.spec.ts`. The Playwright `seek to 2/3 of duration` block in the spec is already in place — the h.264 proxy should paint at the seek.
- **Try-list (what the 2026-06-30 turn tested and rejected, so the next agent doesn't repeat):**
  - **Alt 1: replace I's target with an h.264 video** — Cypher confirms zero non-HEVC `:VideoStreamReference` rows on the dev box (all extant videos are MFFD welding 4K HEVC). Rejected — no fallback target exists.
  - **Alt 2: manually transcode + stamp** — would need PR-2b's backend read-path patch anyway, so it would land as half a feature with no test coverage. Rejected as scope-creep.
- **First refs:** `screenshots/I-video-player.png` (the empty-canvas symptom — STILL BROKEN as of 2026-06-30); `plugins/video/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/video/handlers/VideoStreamReferenceKindHandler.java:220` (the `getPayload(ref)` call that today always serves `storageLocator`); `plugins/video/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/context/references/videostreamreference/services/VideoStreamReferenceService.java:395` (`getPayload` source — the read-path that needs the proxy-preference branch); `plugins/video/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/plugins/video/transcode/VideoTranscodeOrchestrator.java` (the on-upload orchestrator that the backfill admin endpoint will re-use); VID-FFMPEG-TRANSCODE-2026-06-29 (PR-2a parent row, mentions PR-2b + PR-2c slices); VID-HEVC-PLAYBACK-PLAYWRIGHT-2026-06-29 (root cause); VID-RENDERING-EMPTY-2026-06-29 (sibling symptom row).

## FILEREF-CONTENT-DOWNLOAD-MISSING-2026-06-30 — `GET /v2/references/{appId}/content` 400s for file-kind references, blocking SceneGraphPlay (size: S)
- **Status:** SHIPPED-VERIFIED 2026-07-01 in `SCREENSHOT-IJ-CLOSEOUT-3`. `FileReferenceKindHandler.downloadContent(appId, rangeHeader)` overrides the SPI default (`ReferenceKindHandler:185-187`); resolves `:SingletonFileReference → ShepardFile.oid` via `SingletonFileReferenceService.getPayload(appId)`, honours `Range: bytes=START-END` (206 Partial Content), sets `Content-Disposition` + `Accept-Ranges` headers identical to the video handler's shape. 3 new unit tests (`ReferenceKindHandlersTest`): full-body 200 + headers, missing oid 404, range request 206 with Content-Range. Deployed 2026-07-01 after `CRIT-QUARKUS-CLASSTRANSFORM-VIDEOPAYLOAD-2026-06-30` was fixed on origin (`74c28ce57`). J screenshot re-shoot 2026-07-01 shows the play page loading successfully (no "HTTP 400" error banner, kr210_r2700_2 title paints, DECLARED status badge visible, `10 frames · 9 joints · 6 joint bindings` summary strip renders). The kr210 mesh itself is untextured — that's the separate `URDF-COMBINATION-VIEWER-2026-06-29` candidate (a) row; the FileReference content download path itself is confirmed working end-to-end.
- Surfaced 2026-06-30 during the J/MAPPING_RECIPE seed turn. With the J URL now correctly pointing at `/scene-graphs/play/019f16d8-2fc7-76e8-b066-f713e2fb713d`, the play page successfully calls `POST /v2/mappings/{appId}/materialize` (returns the 10-frame / 9-joint VIEW envelope with the URDF FileReference appId — "kr210_r2700_2 DECLARED Bind channels 10 frames · 9 joints · 6 joint bindings"), but `useUrdfReferenceBlob.resolve(urdfFileReferenceAppId)` then hits `GET /v2/references/{appId}/content` and **400s with "kind=file does not support binary content download"**. The screenshot today (post-fix) shows the play page header + "Could not load the URDF file (HTTP 400)" error banner.
- **Why:** `FileReferenceKindHandler.downloadContent(...)` is not overridden — the SPI default in `ReferenceKindHandler:185-187` returns 400. The existing file content reach (download buttons, viewers) goes through a different endpoint (`/v2/files/{appId}/content`), which is the singleton-file shape; the SceneGraphPlay renderer is correctly using the per-reference `/v2/references/{appId}/content` shape per the kind-handler dispatch contract. The override is a small kind-handler method that reuses the same `FileService.getPayload(...)` the singleton endpoint uses, gated on `ref.fileOid IS NOT NULL`.
- **Fix shape:** override `FileReferenceKindHandler.downloadContent(String appId, String rangeHeader)` to resolve `:FileReference` → its `fileOid` → `FileService.getPayload(oid)` → return the bytes (range-aware, same Content-Disposition + Accept-Ranges headers as the singleton endpoint). Tests: kind-handler unit test that 200s for a singleton file ref, 404s for a missing oid, supports range requests. Pairs with `URDF-COMBINATION-VIEWER-2026-06-29 candidate (c)` — same root cause, this row is the focused implementation slice for the SceneGraphPlay flow.
- **Symptom matrix:**
  - **Today:** J screenshot shows the play page chrome + "Could not load the URDF file (HTTP 400)" error — confirms materialize works but the FileReference download fallback doesn't exist.
  - **Post-fix:** the renderer paints the kr210_r2700_2 kinematic tree (10 frames, 9 joints), then the joint-channel bindings drive `robot.setJointValue(...)` over the AFP layup trajectory window.
- **First refs:** `screenshots/J-urdf-with-robot.png` (today's symptom — error banner + 10/9/6 metadata strip); `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/spi/ReferenceKindHandler.java:185-187` (default 400-returning method); `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/handlers/FileReferenceKindHandler.java` (where the override lands); `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/references/handlers/VideoStreamReferenceKindHandler.java:213` (a working sibling override that shows the shape); `frontend/composables/useUrdfReferenceBlob.ts` (the caller that hits this 400); `frontend/pages/scene-graphs/play/[templateAppId].vue:88` (the `await resolveBlob(...)` site); URDF-COMBINATION-VIEWER-2026-06-29 candidate (c) (parent row with broader context).

## DELETED-VSR-CRUFT-INVESTIGATION-2026-07-01 — 853 `:VideoStreamReference` rows marked `deleted=TRUE` under 3 live DataObjects (size: S, data-integrity triage)
- **Status:** queued.
- **Why:** During the `URDF-KUKA-ORANGE-2026-07-01` fleet-backfill submission we observed a large `deleted=TRUE` pool that skewed the operator's expected candidate count (task premise: "916 hevc + 444 null-codec" → actual: 60 hevc live). Cypher `MATCH (r:VideoStreamReference) RETURN r.deleted, count(*) ORDER BY r.deleted` → `TRUE:853, FALSE:66`. All 853 sit on **only 3 DataObjects**: `019efce6-9a65-77b2-a793-edb4e4a30859` (`P02Strich_S_1teBahn` → 288 refs), `019eff1e-44bd-7122-9c82-eebd3afb7297` (`P02_1teBahn` → 368 refs), `019eff0b-8601-7123-91c4-845e39b4cb99` (`P02Strich_S_2teBahn` → 197 refs). None of the 3 parent DataObjects are themselves `deleted=TRUE`. Cross-tab confirms: `MATCH (d:DataObject)-[:has_reference]->(r:VideoStreamReference {deleted:TRUE}) WHERE d.deleted <> TRUE OR d.deleted IS NULL RETURN count(*) → 853, count(DISTINCT d) → 3`. Zero of these VSRs have an attached `:Version` node.
- **Fix candidates (investigate before deciding):**
  - **(a) Bulk cleanup — orphan-relationship prune.** If these are re-ingest cruft from an earlier importer bug (a fresh ingest wrote 3 new VSRs per Bahn while marking the previous 853 as deleted), the Cypher fix is `MATCH (d)-[hr:has_reference]->(r:VideoStreamReference {deleted:TRUE}) DELETE hr` + a follow-up `DELETE r` after confirming no `:Version` / `:Activity` / `:SemanticAnnotation` edges. Requires an operator-approved cleanup script + a snapshot pair before running.
  - **(b) Investigate the ingest path.** Grep the video plugin for the code path that stamps `deleted=TRUE` on VSR creation collisions — if a fresh `PUT /v2/references/{appId}/content` writes a new VSR + soft-deletes the old (per the append-only history rule), that's working-as-intended and the 853 rows are the audit trail of 288+368+197 upload revisions on those 3 DataObjects. Confirm before proposing (a).
  - **(c) Leave the crud in place, ship a semantic annotation flag.** If (b) confirms these are legitimate history, add a `urn:shepard:audit:supersededBy` predicate pointing each deleted VSR to its live successor, and add a UI filter panel that surfaces the history if the operator wants it. Preserves the "audit trail is a graph, not a log" invariant.
- **What NOT to do:** transcode-backfill these 853 rows. They are invisible on the v2 read path (`VideoStreamReferenceDAO.findByDataObjectNeo4jId` uses `getObjectPart(...,false)` which inlines `{deleted: FALSE}`), so any h.264 proxy produced would be unreachable via the UI — burning ~100 GB Garage disk + ~10 CPU-hours for zero user-facing value.
- **First refs:** Cypher probes above (rerun on-demand); `plugins/video/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/context/references/videostreamreference/daos/VideoStreamReferenceDAO.java:33-46` (the read-path deleted-guard); `plugins/video/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/context/references/videostreamreference/services/VideoStreamReferenceService.java` (VSR creation + soft-delete path — where the ingest-time behaviour lives); `I-VIDEO-HEVC-TRANSCODE-BACKFILL-2026-06-30` (parent row where the discrepancy was surfaced); `URDF-KUKA-ORANGE-2026-07-01` (the turn that filed this).

## CRIT-QUARKUS-CLASSTRANSFORM-VIDEOPAYLOAD-2026-06-30 — `mvn quarkus:build` fails with `NoClassDefFoundError: VideoPayload` on the dev box (size: M, env-blocker)
- **Status:** ✅ FIXED fire-320 — root cause identified and resolved. Root cause: `VideoStreamReference` (in plugin, `provided`-scope backend dep) declared `implements VideoPayload` (backend interface). Quarkus's `ClassTransformingBuildStep` augments indexed-dependency plugin classes via a classloader that excludes `provided`-scope deps; therefore ASM's `ClassWriter.getCommonSuperClass()` could not load `VideoPayload` during frame computation → `NoClassDefFoundError`. Fix: converted `VideoPayload` from a marker **interface** to a `@Retention(RUNTIME)` **annotation** (annotation types are stored as descriptor strings in the class attributes table, never loaded during frame computation). `VideoStreamReference` now carries `@VideoPayload` instead of `implements VideoPayload`. Backend detection via `ref.getClass().isAnnotationPresent(VideoPayload.class)` preserves full extensibility (any future video reference class can carry the annotation). 4 files changed: `VideoPayload.java`, `VideoStreamReference.java`, `DataObjectIO.java`, `DmpSnippetService.java`. PR: CRIT-VIDEOPAYLOAD-FIX-fire320.
- **Root cause:** `VideoStreamReference` (plugin, `provided`-scope dep) declared `implements VideoPayload` (backend interface). Quarkus `ClassTransformingBuildStep` uses a classloader that excludes `provided`-scope deps when augmenting indexed plugin classes → ASM `ClassWriter.getCommonSuperClass` threw `NoClassDefFoundError: VideoPayload`.
- **First refs:** `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/context/references/videostreamreference/VideoPayload.java`; `plugins/video/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/context/references/videostreamreference/model/VideoStreamReference.java`.

## CHANNELS-PAGESIZE-500-FIX-2026-07-02 — frontend channel fetches clamped to the server @Max(500) (size: XS, SHIPPED)
- **Status:** ✅ SHIPPED 2026-07-02 in the MFFD showcase verification sweep. `APISIMP-CHANNEL-PAGESZ-MAX` (PR #2196, `e5fae68e4`) added `@Max(500)` to `listChannels pageSize`, but four frontend callers still requested 1000/2000 → `400 Constraint Violation` → empty channel lists. Live symptoms on the deployed MFFD deck surfaces: Trace3D C+G ("Channel 'x' returned 0 points"), TS container F ("Error while fetching timeseries channels: HTTP 400" + empty inventory table), TS-ref D silent channel auto-populate failure. Fix: clamp `frontend/utils/shapesRenderChannels.ts`, `frontend/composables/container/TimeseriesContainerAccessor.ts` (`CHANNEL_PAGE_SIZE`), `frontend/composables/container/useFetchV2Channels.ts`, `frontend/pages/.../timeseriesereferences/[...]/index.vue` to 500; unit-test expectation updated. Gates: lint/vitest(2526)/typecheck ✅, `make redeploy-frontend` smoke 26/26 ✅, live recheck renders C/G traces + F channel table.
- **First refs:** `aidocs/agent-findings/mffd-showcase-verification-2026-07-02.md`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/containers/resources/ContainersV2Rest.java:644` (the `@Max(500)`).

## TRACE3D-E2E-SMOKE-2026-07-02 — e2e smoke for the Trace3D render happy path + shared channel pageSize constant (size: S)
- **Status:** 🔄 PR open (fire-449, PR #2369).
- **Why:** the `@Max(500)` cap silently broke three presentation-deck surfaces for ~1 day because no e2e covers `/shapes/render?renderer=trace-3d` end-to-end and the channels listing is consumed in four places with three different hand-rolled page sizes. A backend param-contract change had no failing signal anywhere.
- **Fix shape:** (a) one Playwright smoke that loads the C-shot URL and asserts a canvas + no `.v-alert.error` (data-independent variant: any seeded container); (b) extract `MAX_CHANNEL_PAGE_SIZE = 500` into a shared frontend constant (or read the generated client's documented cap) and use it at all four call sites.
- **First refs:** `CHANNELS-PAGESIZE-500-FIX-2026-07-02`; `e2e/tests/screenshots-320.spec.ts` (the C/G URLs); `frontend/utils/shapesRenderChannels.ts`.
- **Shipped:** `frontend/utils/channelConstants.ts` (new shared constant `MAX_CHANNEL_PAGE_SIZE = 500`); updated 5 call sites (`shapesRenderChannels.ts`, `TimeseriesContainerAccessor.ts`, `useFetchV2Channels.ts`, `useChannelsFromTimeseriesRef.ts`, `timeseriesereferences/[id]/index.vue`); `e2e/tests/uiverify-trace3d-smoke.spec.ts` (two tests: live data-gated happy-path + structural data-independent).

## VIDEO-EMPTY-UPLOAD-ROWS-2026-07-02 — 3 live `:VideoStreamReference` rows with no bytes (no storageLocator, no probe metadata) (size: XS, data triage)
- **Status:** queued.
- **Why:** surfaced by the 2026-07-02 MFFD verification sweep. `MATCH (v:VideoStreamReference) WHERE v.proxyStatus IS NULL AND v.deleted=false` → 3 rows: `019efda5-68e7-7d55-b219-34d3153bdb75` (`P02Strich_s_1.Bahn.MP4`, DO `019efce6-9a65…`), `019effef-ac01-74ff-9e5b-20f419d61cdf` (`P02Strich_s_2.Bahn.MP4`, DO `019eff0b-8601…`), `019effef-b875-7d6b-aff0-badc852abd68` (`P02_1.Bahn.MP4`, DO `019eff1e-44bd…`). All three have `storageLocator=NULL`, no `videoCodec/width/height/duration` — aborted/incomplete uploads, not transcode stragglers (the backfill endpoint correctly excludes them via its `storageLocator IS NOT NULL` guard; dry-run finds 0 candidates). Their ref detail pages render a player with no playable source.
- **Fix shape:** decide with `DELETED-VSR-CRUFT-INVESTIGATION-2026-07-01` (same 3 parent DataObjects!): either re-upload the source MP4s for these 3 Bahnen, or soft-delete the empty rows with a PROV-O Activity documenting the cleanup. Do NOT hard-delete (referenced-data retention rule).
- **First refs:** `aidocs/agent-findings/mffd-showcase-verification-2026-07-02.md §Video backfill fleet state`; `plugins/video/.../VideoStreamReferenceDAO.java:127-133` (`buildBackfillCypher` guard).

## TSREF-CONTAINER-NAME-2026-07-02 — TS reference detail header shows "Container: unknown name (ID: 221633)" (size: XS)
- **Status:** ✅ shipped fire-383 (PR #2260, merged 2026-07-03).
- **Why:** on `/collections/.../timeseriesereferences/019efae9-6084…` (MFFD deck shot D) the header rendered `Container: unknown name` plus the raw numeric Neo4j id.
- **Fix:** resolved container via `GET /v2/containers/{appId}` and renders name + appId-routed link; numeric `(ID: …)` display dropped.
- **First refs:** `frontend/pages/collections/[collectionId]/dataobjects/[dataObjectId]/timeseriesereferences/[timeseriesReferenceId]/index.vue`.

## SEMANN-CONTAINER-PERM-2026-07-02 — container Semantic Annotations panel toasts "Caller 'bob' lacks Read permission on the subject entity" (size: S)
- **Status:** 🔄 PR open (SEMANN-CONTAINER-PERM-2026-07-02-1).
- **Why:** surfaced by the 2026-07-02 MFFD verification recheck on `/containers/timeseries/019ede2a-60ec-7ac1-899d-3fe4c6263cbb` as user bob (Reader on the collection). The page itself gates fine (listChannels 200, header + channel table render), but the Semantic Annotations panel's fetch 403s and surfaces an error toast. Either (a) the annotations endpoint checks Read on a different subject entity than the container the page already gated (e.g. per-channel `AnnotatableTimeseries` subjects owned by Demo Admin), or (b) the container's `:Permissions` node lacks bob while the collection grants Reader. Toast on a read-only panel for a Reader persona is a demo-visible wart.
- **Fix shape:** align the annotations-read gate with the container Read gate (if the caller can read the container, they can read its annotations), or degrade silently to an empty panel on 403 (WARN, no toast) per the fail-soft rule.

## APISIMP-TERMSEARCH-NO-XCOUNT — SemanticTermSearchRest list response missing X-Total-Count header (size: XS, sweep: fire-475)
- **Status:** ✅ merged (fire-475, PR #APISIMP-XCOUNT-BATCH-2)
- **Why:** `GET /v2/semantic/terms/search` returned a `PagedResponseIO` body but no `X-Total-Count` header. All v2 paginated list endpoints must emit `X-Total-Count: <total>` for clients that prefer the header over parsing the envelope. `SemanticTermSearchRest.java:244`.
- **Fix:** chain `.header("X-Total-Count", (long) results.size())` on the `Response.ok(...)` builder. Note: `total = results.size()` is architectural — the n10s fulltext index cannot do a count-only query, so the total reflects only the page window (same as the `PagedResponseIO.total` field).
- **First refs:** `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/semantic/resources/SemanticTermSearchRest.java:244`; apisimp-sweep-fire475-2026-07-08.md §F1

## APISIMP-SNAP-DO-XCOUNT — SnapshotPinnedReadRest list response missing X-Total-Count header (size: XS, sweep: fire-475)
- **Status:** ✅ merged (fire-475, PR #APISIMP-XCOUNT-BATCH-2)
- **Why:** `GET /v2/collections/{cid}/snapshots/{sid}/data-objects` returned a `SnapshotDataObjectsIO` body but no `X-Total-Count` header. `total` was already computed from `snapshotService.countDataObjectAppIds(snapshot)` — purely a missing header chain.
- **Fix:** chain `.header("X-Total-Count", total)` on the `Response.ok(...)` builder at line 160.
- **First refs:** `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/snapshot/resources/SnapshotPinnedReadRest.java:160`; apisimp-sweep-fire475-2026-07-08.md §F2

## APISIMP-AAS-SHELLS-NO-XCOUNT — AasShellsRest list endpoints missing X-Total-Count header (size: XS, sweep: fire-475)
- **Status:** ✅ merged (fire-475, PR #APISIMP-XCOUNT-BATCH-2)
- **Why:** Two list endpoints in the AAS plugin — `GET /v2/aas/shells` (listShells) and `GET /v2/aas/shells/{aasId}/submodels` (listSubmodels) — returned `PagedResponseIO` bodies without the `X-Total-Count` header. Both already computed `total` correctly; the header chain was missing.
- **Fix:** chain `.header("X-Total-Count", total)` on both response builders (`AasShellsRest.java:121` and `AasShellsRest.java:210`).
- **First refs:** `plugins/aas/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/plugins/aas/v2/resources/AasShellsRest.java:121,210`; apisimp-sweep-fire475-2026-07-08.md §F3

## APISIMP-AAS-REG-SCHEMA-DRIFT — AasRegistrationAdminRest @APIResponse schema annotation drift + missing X-Total-Count (size: XS, sweep: fire-475)
- **Status:** ✅ merged (fire-475, PR #APISIMP-XCOUNT-BATCH-2)
- **Why:** `GET /v2/admin/aas/registrations` declared `@Schema(implementation = AasRegistrationIO.class)` in its 200 `@APIResponse`, but the actual return type is `PagedResponseIO<AasRegistrationIO>`. OpenAPI spec therefore described a bare object instead of the paged envelope. Also missing `X-Total-Count` header.
- **Fix:** change annotation to `@Schema(implementation = PagedResponseIO.class)`; chain `.header("X-Total-Count", total)` on response builder at line 84.
- **First refs:** `plugins/aas/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/plugins/aas/admin/resources/AasRegistrationAdminRest.java:70,84`; apisimp-sweep-fire475-2026-07-08.md §F4

## APISIMP-BUNDLE-FILES-PAGEFMT — BundleGroupsV2Rest uses bespoke PagedFilesIO envelope instead of standard PagedResponseIO (size: M, sweep: fire-475, DEFERRED)
- **Status:** 🔴 deferred — wire-breaking change; requires design doc + migration window before touching.
- **Why:** `GET /v2/references/{appId}/groups/files` (and related BundleGroupsV2Rest list endpoints at line 376) return a `PagedFilesIO` body with non-standard field names (`size` instead of `pageSize`, `totalElements` instead of `total`). Every other v2 list endpoint uses `PagedResponseIO` with canonical `{items, total, page, pageSize}`. Any client built against the current bespoke shape will break if we rename the fields.
- **Fix shape (future):** introduce `PagedResponseIO<FileGroupIO>` alongside the existing shape; gate behind a `?envelope=v2` param or a deprecation header; remove `PagedFilesIO` after a deprecation window. Requires a versioning/migration design before landing.
- **First refs:** `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/bundle/resources/BundleGroupsV2Rest.java:376`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/bundle/io/PagedFilesIO.java`; apisimp-sweep-fire475-2026-07-08.md §F5

## APISIMP-PROV-CURSOR-PAGED-WRAP — ProvenanceRest cursor-based paged endpoints use misleading PagedResponseIO fields (size: S, sweep: fire-475)
- **Status:** ✅ merged (fire-477, PR #2408, SHA 6e45bece).
- **Why:** `GET /v2/collections/{cid}/activities` and `GET /v2/dataobjects/{did}/activities` use time-cursor pagination (`since`/`until` epoch ms) but wrap results in `PagedResponseIO` with `total=rows.size()`, `page=0`, `pageSize=rows.size()`. These fields are semantically wrong for cursor pagination: `total` is the window size not the true count, `page` is always 0, `pageSize` is the window. No `X-Total-Count` header emitted. A caller using the standard envelope contract to drive offset-based paging will silently get confused. Also missing `X-Next-Cursor` / `X-Has-More` response headers that cursor clients expect.
- **Fix shape:** either (a) emit the actual window limit in `pageSize`, document `total=null` to signal "cursor mode", and add `X-Has-More: true/false` + `X-Next-Cursor: <unix_ms>` headers; or (b) introduce a dedicated `CursorPagedResponseIO` type and update the 200 schema annotation to match.
- **First refs:** `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/provenance/resources/ProvenanceRest.java:160,312`; apisimp-sweep-fire475-2026-07-08.md §F6

## APISIMP-SHAPES-BUILD-400-NOTRFC7807 — ShapesBuildRest 400 uses bespoke ShapeBuildResponseIO instead of ProblemJson (size: S, sweep: fire-475)
- **Status:** ✅ merged (fire-476, PR #2407).
- **Why:** `POST /v2/shapes/build` returns HTTP 400 with `Content-Type: application/problem+json` but the body is a `ShapeBuildResponseIO` record (`{shapeIri, shapeGraph, error}`) — not a RFC7807 Problem Detail (`{type, title, status, detail, instance}`). Clients that parse `application/problem+json` expecting the standard fields receive `null` for all of them. The 200 response body also uses `ShapeBuildResponseIO` — the single-shape approach conflates success and failure bodies.
- **Fix shape:** on 400, return a proper `ProblemJson(type="/problems/shapes.invalid-dsl", title="Invalid Shape DSL", status=400, detail=ex.getMessage(), instance=null)`; on 200, keep `ShapeBuildResponseIO` for the success body (only `shapeIri` + `shapeGraph` needed). Update `@APIResponse` schema annotations accordingly.
- **First refs:** `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/shapes/resources/ShapesBuildRest.java:105-120`; `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/shapes/io/ShapeBuildResponseIO.java`; apisimp-sweep-fire475-2026-07-08.md §F7

## APISIMP-IMPORT-RUNS-BARE — ImportDiagnosticsV2Rest listRuns returns bare List without PagedResponseIO envelope (size: XS, sweep: fire-475)
- **Status:** ⛔ deferred — contradicts fire-368 decision. `APISIMP-IMPORT-RUNS-FAKE-PAGED` (fire-368, PR `APISIMP-DROP-FAKE-PAGED-WRAPPERS`) explicitly removed `PagedResponseIO` from this endpoint because `total` always equals `pageSize` (transient in-memory list, evicted after 24h, not paginable). Fire-475 sweep re-found the inconsistency without knowing the prior intentional decision. The bare-array shape is correct for this endpoint; adding a fake wrapper again would re-introduce the same anti-pattern. Leave bare.
- **Why:** `GET /v2/import/diagnostics/runs` at `ImportDiagnosticsV2Rest.java:192` returns `Response.ok(runs).build()` — a bare `List<RunSummaryIO>` with no `PagedResponseIO` envelope and no `X-Total-Count` header. The companion events endpoint at line 154 also returns a bare `List<EventIO>` (though that one has a `limit` cap). Inconsistent with every other v2 list endpoint.
- **First refs:** `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/importer/resources/ImportDiagnosticsV2Rest.java:154,192`; apisimp-sweep-fire475-2026-07-08.md §F8

## APISIMP-MAXPOINTS-BOXED — ContainersV2Rest maxPoints query param missing @Min/@Max validation (size: XS, sweep: fire-475)
- **Status:** ✅ merged (fire-477, PR #2406, SHA df9b99d).
- **Why:** `ContainersV2Rest.java:733` declares `@QueryParam("maxPoints") Integer maxPoints` with no `@Min` or `@Max` constraint at the REST layer. The handler (`TimeseriesContainerKindHandler.java:323`) already clamps to `[1, HARD_MAX_POINTS=5000]` via `Math.min(Math.max(maxPoints, 1), 5000)`, but a caller passing `?maxPoints=0` or `?maxPoints=99999` receives silently clamped data with no HTTP 400, violating the principle of fail-fast at the boundary. Note: `maxPoints` is intentionally nullable (`null` = absent = handler default 2000); `@Min`/`@Max` from Jakarta Bean Validation do not fire on `null`, so null still passes through correctly.
